## TASK TYPE: T176 — Cross-Flow Analytics Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:  ORCHESTRATION
ENTRY:      Fires on mart.refreshed events from QUEUE FABRIC (emitted by T174) when
            multiple mart types are available. Also triggered on schedule (weekly cross-flow
            analytics) or manually via admin. Requires minimum 2 mart types populated.
PURPOSE:    Join data across warehouse marts to produce the three business flows described
            in the specification: Lead→Delivery (Sales→Work), Plan→Profit (Work→Finance),
            Support→Resolution (Support→Work). These cross-flow joins produce composite
            analytics that no single mart can provide alone.
DISTINCT FROM: T174 (Mart Build — builds individual marts; T176 joins across marts)
               T160 (FLOW-13 Period Close — finance-specific orchestration; T176 is analytics)
               T40 (Three-Way Join Gate — merges data streams; T176 joins analytical marts)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F451: IMartBuilderService              — read mart data for cross-flow joins
  F450: IFactWriterService               — write cross-flow composite facts
  F455: ISemanticQueryService            — execute cross-mart KPI queries
  F460: ILineageTrackerService           — record cross-flow lineage

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F451 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (marts — read)
  F450 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (cross-flow facts — write)
  F455 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (mart queries) + Redis (semantic cache)
  F460 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (lineage_graph)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generates CrossFlowAnalyticsService extending MicroserviceBase
  AF-2 Planning:    Decomposes into: check-mart-availability → lead-to-delivery-join →
                    plan-to-profit-join → support-to-resolution-join → write-composite-facts →
                    refresh-cross-flow-KPIs → record-lineage
  AF-3 Prompt Lib:  Retrieves "cross-flow-analytics", "multi-mart-join", "composite-kpi-computation"
  AF-4 RAG:         Searches SK-96 (KPI Registry + Semantic Query), SK-92 (Multi-Zone Pipeline)
  AF-5 Multi-model: Parallel: Claude (join SQL) + GPT (KPI formula validation)
  AF-6 Code Review: Join correctness (shared dim keys), null handling for missing mart data,
                    partial mart availability (graceful degradation)
  AF-7 Compliance:  DNA-1–DNA-9; verifies cross-flow facts append-only, tenantId everywhere
  AF-8 Security:    No cross-tenant mart joins, PII already masked in mart layer (DR-63)
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates IR-176-1 through IR-176-8 + QG-176-1 through QG-176-6
  AF-10 Merge:      Selects best join strategy for each business flow
  AF-11 Feedback:   Stores cross-flow join match rate, composite KPI coverage

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-197: Mart dependency chain — cross-flow reads from marts only, never raw/staging/core directly
  CF-201: Cross-flow vs FLOW-10 commerce data — if tenant uses FLOW-10, commerce mart available for join
  CF-208: Cross-flow vs FLOW-13 finance data — finance mart consumes FLOW-13 GL data when enabled

MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:  Cross-flow join logic:
            Lead→Delivery: Sales mart (dim_account, deal_won_date) JOIN Delivery mart (dim_project,
            project_start_date) ON dim_account.businessKey → handover_velocity = start_date - won_date
            Plan→Profit: Delivery mart (dim_project, time_cost) JOIN Sales mart (deal_value)
            → profitability = (deal_value - time_cost) / deal_value
            Support→Resolution: Support mart (dim_account, ticket_count) JOIN Delivery mart
            (dim_project, feature_id) → feature_support_load = tickets per feature
  FREEDOM:  Business flows enabled per tenant, join keys configuration, composite KPI formulas,
            minimum mart types required for join (default 2), graceful degradation policy
            (skip unavailable marts vs fail)

IRON RULES (IR-176-1 to IR-176-8):
  IR-176-1: Cross-flow joins MUST read from mart layer ONLY — never raw/staging/core (CF-197)
  IR-176-2: tenantId MUST appear on every mart read and composite fact write (DNA-5)
  IR-176-3: Composite facts MUST be append-only — same pattern as core facts (CF-196)
  IR-176-4: All composite records stored as dict[str, Any] — no typed models (DNA-1)
  IR-176-5: If required mart is unavailable, graceful degradation — DataProcessResult.Partial, not failure
  IR-176-6: Lineage MUST record which marts contributed to each composite fact (F460)
  IR-176-7: Cross-flow KPIs MUST reference definitions from F454 — no hardcoded formulas
  IR-176-8: DataProcessResult[T] on all paths — join failures wrapped, never thrown (DNA-3)

QUALITY GATES (QG-176-1 to QG-176-6):
  QG-176-1: Lead→Delivery join produces handover_velocity for all accounts with both deal and project
  QG-176-2: Plan→Profit join produces profitability for all projects with both time and deal data
  QG-176-3: Support→Resolution join produces feature_support_load where ticket+feature data exists
  QG-176-4: Graceful degradation verified: missing mart → partial result, not failure
  QG-176-5: Composite KPI values match metric definitions from F454
  QG-176-6: No cross-tenant data in any join result — isolation verified by AF-8
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T177 — Reverse ETL Activation Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:  ACTIVATION
ENTRY:      Fires on mart.refreshed + cross-flow KPI computed events (from T174/T176).
            F456 IReverseEtlService evaluates thresholds against KPI results. Threshold
            breach → activation event published to QUEUE FABRIC.
PURPOSE:    Push computed insights back to external SaaS platforms (reverse ETL). When a
            KPI crosses a configured threshold, trigger an action in the source system:
            create ClickUp task, update Zoho CRM deal field, change Zoho Desk priority.
            All outbound pushes go through QUEUE FABRIC (DR-64), never direct HTTP.
DISTINCT FROM: T169 (Webhook Ingestion — inbound from external SaaS; T177 is outbound TO external SaaS)
               T168 (Incremental Sync — inbound polling; T177 is outbound push)
               T88 (if prior ACTIVATION archetype existed — T177 is warehouse-specific with KPI triggers)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F427: ICredentialVaultService          — OAuth tokens for external API writes
  F430: IRateLimitGuardService           — rate limit before external push
  F434: IApiPollingService               — reused for external API write calls (HTTP PUT/POST)
  F456: IReverseEtlService              — threshold evaluation + activation dispatch
  F459: IWarehouseAuditService           — audit every outbound push

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F427 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (credential vault — read)
  F430 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase) — cache) → Redis (rate limit)
  F434 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase) — MicroserviceBase HTTP) — reused for outbound API calls
  F456 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) → Redis Streams (activation.triggered/completed/failed)
         + DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (activation job state)
  F459 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (WORM audit)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generates ReverseEtlActivationService extending MicroserviceBase
  AF-2 Planning:    Decomposes into: evaluate-thresholds → filter-triggered → check-cooldown →
                    publish-activation-event → [consumer]: rate-limit-check → fetch-credential →
                    build-API-request → execute-push → log-audit → ack-or-DLQ
  AF-3 Prompt Lib:  Retrieves "reverse-etl-activation", "threshold-evaluation",
                    "external-api-write-pattern"
  AF-4 RAG:         Searches SK-97 (Reverse ETL with Threshold Activation), SK-89 (OAuth Lifecycle),
                    SK-90 (API Polling — reused for write calls)
  AF-5 Multi-model: Parallel: Claude (threshold logic) + GPT (external API contract generation)
  AF-6 Code Review: Idempotency of external pushes, cooldown enforcement, DLQ handling,
                    rate limit integration, credential refresh during int activation batches
  AF-7 Compliance:  DNA-1–DNA-9; verifies DR-64 (event-based, never direct HTTP), no provider SDK
  AF-8 Security:    Credential from vault only, TLS on external calls, no PII in activation payload
                    without tenant consent, audit before push
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates IR-177-1 through IR-177-8 + QG-177-1 through QG-177-6
  AF-10 Merge:      Selects best threshold evaluation and cooldown strategy
  AF-11 Feedback:   Stores activation success rate, external API response times, cooldown effectiveness

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-210: Rate limit MUST be checked before every outbound API call
  CF-213: Reverse ETL MUST NOT push to locked records/periods (FLOW-13 fiscal locks)
  CF-204: Activation scoped to tenant — never push data to another tenant's external system

MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:  Threshold evaluation (metric_value [operator] threshold_value), cooldown enforcement
            (last_triggered_at + cooldown_minutes > now → skip), idempotency key per activation
            (tenantId.metricId.thresholdId.date), QUEUE FABRIC event publishing
  FREEDOM:  Activation definitions: { metricId, operator (>, <, ==, >=, <=), threshold,
            targetProvider (clickup/zoho_crm/zoho_desk), targetAction (create_task, update_field,
            send_notification, change_priority), cooldownMinutes, enabled },
            activation batch size, DLQ retry limit, target API endpoint patterns per provider

IRON RULES (IR-177-1 to IR-177-8):
  IR-177-1: Outbound push MUST go through QUEUE FABRIC as event — NEVER direct HTTP (DR-64)
  IR-177-2: Rate limit (F430.CheckAsync) MUST pass before every external API call (CF-210)
  IR-177-3: Credentials MUST come from F427 vault — never hardcoded or cached outside vault
  IR-177-4: CF-213: MUST NOT push to records in locked fiscal periods (FLOW-13 integration)
  IR-177-5: Cooldown MUST be enforced — repeated threshold breaches within cooldown = skip
  IR-177-6: Idempotency key MUST be on every activation event — duplicate pushes prevented
  IR-177-7: Audit record MUST be written to F459 BEFORE external API call executes
  IR-177-8: Provider SDK MUST NOT be imported — external calls via CORE FABRIC HTTP (F434 reuse)

QUALITY GATES (QG-177-1 to QG-177-6):
  QG-177-1: Threshold breach correctly detected — metric value crosses configured boundary
  QG-177-2: Cooldown prevents rapid-fire triggers — same metric+threshold only once per cooldown window
  QG-177-3: Activation event reaches external API and receives success response
  QG-177-4: Failed pushes route to DLQ with full context (tenantId, activation, error, retry count)
  QG-177-5: DataProcessResult[T] on all paths — activation failures wrapped, never thrown (DNA-3)
  QG-177-6: Audit trail shows complete activation history: threshold → event → push → result
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T178 — Warehouse Tenant Provision Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:  PROVISIONING
ENTRY:      Fires when a tenant is onboarded to the warehouse module OR when admin requests
            warehouse provisioning via DynamicController. Also fires when isolation tier
            upgrade requested (pool→bridge→silo). Consumes tenant context from FLOW-08.
PURPOSE:    Provision the warehouse infrastructure for a new tenant: create zone-specific
            isolation (raw/staging/core/mart per DR-65), generate RLS policies, set quotas,
            configure retention policies, seed dim_date, and run smoke tests. Zero code
            changes between pool/bridge/silo — fabric handles routing.
DISTINCT FROM: T166 (FLOW-13 Tenant Finance Provision — provisions finance isolation
               T178 provisions warehouse zone isolation)
               T167 (Connector Registration — provisions a connector; T178 provisions the
               warehouse storage that connectors feed into)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F453: IRetentionPolicyService          — set default retention per zone
  F461: ITenantWarehouseIsolationService — set isolation tier per zone
  F463: IRowLevelSecurityService         — create RLS policies per zone
  F465: IWarehouseQuotaService           — set quota limits per tenant tier
  F449: IDimensionLoaderService          — seed dim_date for new tenant

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F453 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (retention policy config)
  F461 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase) — MicroserviceBase + Config)
         + MULTI-TENANT ISOLATION FABRIC (Skill 11)
  F463 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (RLS policy management)
  F465 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Redis (quota counters)
  F449 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (dim_date seed)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generates WarehouseTenantProvisionService extending MicroserviceBase
  AF-2 Planning:    Decomposes into: validate-tenant-context → set-isolation-tier-per-zone →
                    create-schemas/indices → create-RLS-policies → set-quotas →
                    set-retention-policies → seed-dim-date → run-smoke-tests → mark-active
  AF-3 Prompt Lib:  Retrieves "warehouse-tenant-provisioning", "rls-policy-generation",
                    "zone-isolation-pattern"
  AF-4 RAG:         Searches SK-92 (Multi-Zone Pipeline), SK-95 (SCD-2 Dimension Loader — for dim_date)
  AF-5 Multi-model: Parallel: Claude (provision orchestration) + GPT (RLS policy generation)
  AF-6 Code Review: RLS policy correctness, quota initialization, isolation tier validation,
                    idempotent re-provisioning, smoke test completeness
  AF-7 Compliance:  DNA-1–DNA-9; verifies DR-65 (zone-level isolation), no cross-tenant leakage
  AF-8 Security:    RLS policies prevent cross-tenant access, quotas prevent resource exhaustion,
                    isolation validated via smoke test (attempt cross-tenant read → zero rows)
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates IR-178-1 through IR-178-8 + QG-178-1 through QG-178-6
  AF-10 Merge:      Selects best provisioning strategy for each isolation tier
  AF-11 Feedback:   Stores provisioning success rate, smoke test pass rate, provision duration

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-204: No cross-tenant data bleed during provisioning — smoke test validates
  CF-205: Warehouse schema isolation — bridge/silo tenants get dedicated schemas/databases
  CF-206: RLS policy per zone per tenant — pool tenants get RLS, bridge/silo get schema isolation
  CF-199: Warehouse provision vs FLOW-13 finance provision — compatible tier models (DR-65 ↔ DR-56)

MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:  RLS SQL generation (CREATE POLICY, ALTER TABLE ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY),
            schema creation for bridge (CREATE SCHEMA tenant_{id}_{zone}),
            index creation for silo (raw_{tenantId}_{provider}_{entity}_YYYYMM),
            smoke test (cross-tenant read attempt → must return zero rows),
            dim_date seed (10-year range pre-populated)
  FREEDOM:  Isolation tier per zone per tenant (matrix), quota limits per tenant tier
            (free/standard/enterprise), retention days per zone, dim_date year range,
            auto-graduation thresholds (data volume → upgrade tier), provisioning template

IRON RULES (IR-178-1 to IR-178-8):
  IR-178-1: Isolation tier MUST be set for EVERY zone before any warehouse data operation (DR-65)
  IR-178-2: RLS policies MUST be created for pool-tier zones — no unprotected tables
  IR-178-3: Quotas MUST be set before warehouse is marked ACTIVE — prevent unbounded usage
  IR-178-4: Retention policies MUST be set per zone before ACTIVE — prevent unbounded storage growth
  IR-178-5: dim_date MUST be seeded before any fact write (facts reference dim_date FK)
  IR-178-6: Smoke test MUST pass — cross-tenant read returns zero rows — before ACTIVE
  IR-178-7: Provisioning MUST be idempotent — re-provisioning same tenant = no-op, no duplicate schemas
  IR-178-8: Provisioning event MUST be emitted to QUEUE FABRIC on completion

QUALITY GATES (QG-178-1 to QG-178-6):
  QG-178-1: RLS policies correctly generated — AF-8 validates SQL syntax
  QG-178-2: Smoke test: cross-tenant query returns zero rows for ALL zone tables
  QG-178-3: Quota counters initialized in Redis for all quota types
  QG-178-4: dim_date has 10 years of records (365×10 = 3,650+ rows)
  QG-178-5: DataProcessResult[T] on all paths — provision failure must not leave partial config (DNA-3)
  QG-178-6: Re-provisioning same tenant produces no changes — idempotency verified (IR-178-7)
```

## FLOW TEMPLATES 33–35 (DAG JSON Definitions)

### FLOW TEMPLATE 33 — ingest-pipeline-v1

```
FLOW: FLOW-14
TEMPLATE: 33
VERSION: 1.0.0
DESCRIPTION: Data Warehouse Ingestion Pipeline — Connect, Poll, Webhook, Backfill → Raw Zone
ORCHESTRATOR: IFlowOrchestrator (SK-392 (RagStrategyRegistry))
ENTRY_EVENT: WarehouseIngestionRequested
TENANT_SCOPED: true
```

```json
{
  "flowId": "flow-14-ingest",
  "templateId": 33,
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Data Warehouse Ingestion Pipeline — connect → poll → webhook → backfill → raw",
  "entryEvent": "WarehouseIngestionRequested",
  "tenantScoped": true,
  "steps": [
    {
      "stepId": "step-route",
      "taskType": "ROUTE",
      "description": "Route to ingestion sub-flow based on ingestType",
      "conditions": [
        {"if": "event.ingestType == 'REGISTER_CONNECTOR'", "goto": "step-register"},
        {"if": "event.ingestType == 'INCREMENTAL_SYNC'", "goto": "step-sync"},
        {"if": "event.ingestType == 'WEBHOOK'", "goto": "step-webhook"},
        {"if": "event.ingestType == 'BACKFILL'", "goto": "step-backfill"}
      ]
    },
    {
      "stepId": "step-register",
      "taskType": "T167",
      "description": "Connector Registration — OAuth + vault + health probe",
      "factories": ["F426","F427","F428","F430","F431"],
      "onSuccess": "step-init-sync",
      "onFailure": "DEAD_LETTER"
    },
    {
      "stepId": "step-init-sync",
      "taskType": "T168",
      "description": "First incremental sync after connector registration",
      "factories": ["F430","F432","F433","F434","F437","F438"],
      "freedom": {"syncFrequency": "${config.flow14.{tenantId}.sync_cron}"},
      "onSuccess": "END",
      "onFailure": "RETRY:3:EXPONENTIAL"
    },
    {
      "stepId": "step-sync",
      "taskType": "T168",
      "description": "Scheduled incremental sync — cursor-based polling",
      "factories": ["F430","F432","F433","F434","F437","F438"],
      "onSuccess": "END",
      "onFailure": "RETRY:3:EXPONENTIAL"
    },
    {
      "stepId": "step-webhook",
      "taskType": "T169",
      "description": "Webhook event ingestion — HMAC verify → dedupe → raw land",
      "factories": ["F429","F435","F438","F440"],
      "onSuccess": "END",
      "onFailure": "DEAD_LETTER"
    },
    {
      "stepId": "step-backfill",
      "taskType": "T170",
      "description": "Historical backfill saga — date-range slicing with EP-4",
      "factories": ["F430","F434","F436","F437","F438"],
      "freedom": {"sliceSize": "${config.flow14.{tenantId}.backfill_slice_days}"},
      "onSuccess": "END",
      "onFailure": "RETRY:1:EXPONENTIAL"
    }
  ]
}
```

### FLOW TEMPLATE 34 — transform-pipeline-v1

```
FLOW: FLOW-14
TEMPLATE: 34
VERSION: 1.0.0
DESCRIPTION: Data Warehouse Transform Pipeline — Raw → Staging → Core → Marts
ORCHESTRATOR: IFlowOrchestrator (SK-392 (RagStrategyRegistry))
ENTRY_EVENT: RawDataLanded
TENANT_SCOPED: true
```

```json
{
  "flowId": "flow-14-transform",
  "templateId": 34,
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Data Warehouse Transform Pipeline — raw → staging → core → marts",
  "entryEvent": "RawDataLanded",
  "tenantScoped": true,
  "steps": [
    {
      "stepId": "step-schema-check",
      "taskType": "T172",
      "description": "Schema drift detection before transform",
      "factories": ["F438","F439","F443","F445","F458"],
      "onSuccess": "step-transform",
      "onFailure": "step-quarantine-alert",
      "onDrift": "step-drift-review"
    },
    {
      "stepId": "step-drift-review",
      "taskType": "T172",
      "description": "Schema drift admin review — block mart promotion for new fields",
      "factories": ["F439"],
      "freedom": {"autoAcceptFieldAdded": "${config.flow14.{tenantId}.schema_auto_accept}"},
      "onApprove": "step-transform",
      "onReject": "step-quarantine-alert"
    },
    {
      "stepId": "step-transform",
      "taskType": "T171",
      "description": "Raw-to-Staging transform — normalize, identity resolve, quarantine",
      "factories": ["F438","F442","F443","F444","F445","F446","F447","F448"],
      "onSuccess": "step-identity",
      "onFailure": "step-quarantine-alert"
    },
    {
      "stepId": "step-identity",
      "taskType": "T175",
      "description": "Cross-system identity join — probabilistic matching",
      "factories": ["F440","F446","F449","F459"],
      "freedom": {"autoMergeThreshold": "${config.flow14.{tenantId}.identity_threshold}"},
      "onSuccess": "step-model",
      "onFailure": "RETRY:2:EXPONENTIAL"
    },
    {
      "stepId": "step-model",
      "taskType": "T173",
      "description": "Dimension/Fact refresh — SCD-2 dims + append-only facts + snapshots",
      "factories": ["F444","F449","F450","F452","F460"],
      "onSuccess": "step-mart",
      "onFailure": "RETRY:2:EXPONENTIAL"
    },
    {
      "stepId": "step-mart",
      "taskType": "T174",
      "description": "Mart build + KPI refresh — PII gate → aggregate → cache refresh",
      "factories": ["F449","F450","F451","F454","F455","F460","F462"],
      "freedom": {"martTypes": "${config.flow14.{tenantId}.enabled_marts}"},
      "onSuccess": "END",
      "onFailure": "RETRY:1:EXPONENTIAL"
    },
    {
      "stepId": "step-quarantine-alert",
      "taskType": "ALERT",
      "description": "Quarantine threshold exceeded or schema drift rejected — notify admin",
      "factories": ["F445","F457"],
      "onSuccess": "END"
    }
  ]
}
```

```json
      "onSuccess": "END",
      "onFailure": "DEAD_LETTER"
    },
    {
      "stepId": "step-provision",
      "taskType": "T178",
      "description": "Warehouse tenant provisioning — isolation + RLS + quotas + dim_date",
      "factories": ["F449","F453","F461","F463","F465"],
      "freedom": {"isolationMatrix": "${config.flow14.{tenantId}.zone_isolation}"},
      "onSuccess": "END",
      "onFailure": "DEAD_LETTER"
    }
  ]
}
```

## PHASE 4 — ARCHETYPE REGISTRY UPDATE (Complete FLOW-14)

| Archetype | First Introduced | FLOW-14 Usage | Key Characteristic |
|---|---|---|---|
| PROVISIONING | FLOW-10 | T167, T178 | Setup lifecycle with health validation + smoke test |
| DURABLE_SAGA | FLOW-13 | T168, T170 | EP-4 crash recovery, checkpoint-per-step |
| **INGESTION** | **FLOW-14** | T169 | **External push-based event capture + HMAC** |
| **TRANSFORM** | **FLOW-14** | T171 | **Multi-factory zone-crossing pipeline** |
| VALIDATION | Various | T172 | Rule-based validation with routing |
| **MODELING** | **FLOW-14** | T173, T174 | **Warehouse model refresh: SCD-2 + mart aggregation** |
| JOIN_GATE | Various | T175 | Probabilistic scoring with confidence threshold |
| ORCHESTRATION | Various | T176 | Cross-mart analytical joins |
| **ACTIVATION** | **FLOW-14** | T177 | **Outbound push to external SaaS via QUEUE FABRIC** |

**4 new archetypes: INGESTION, TRANSFORM, MODELING, ACTIVATION**

## SAVE POINT: FLOW14:P4:COMPLETE ✅

### Recovery Commands
```
"Load P4"                → This file (FLOW14_P4_TASK_TYPES.md)
"Start P5"               → Generate CF-192–CF-213 + ST-92–ST-103
"Merge P4"               → Append FLOW14_P4_TASK_TYPES.md → TASK_TYPES_CATALOG_MERGED.md
"Resume from P4"         → Load P0 + P1 + P2 + P3 + this file + basic_prompt.txt
```

# PART 1 — TASK TYPES T179–T185 (Families 60–63)

## New Archetypes Introduced (P4A)
| Archetype | First Task Type | Description |
|-----------|----------------|-------------|
| TEMPLATE | T180 | NLP-driven spec generation from natural language into versioned app spec |
| SCAFFOLDING | T182 | AI-powered full-stack scaffold generation from validated spec |
| SPEC | T183 | Spec validation with multi-rule engine + AI review |

---

## TASK TYPE: T179 — App Workspace Provision Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** PROVISIONING
**ENTRY:** Fires on WorkspaceCreateRequested event
**PURPOSE:** Provision a new app workspace with tenant-scoped storage, configuration defaults, and collaboration channels — the entry point for the MVP builder pipeline.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T88 (Managed Primitive Provisioner, FLOW-08) — T88 provisions DB/storage/auth primitives for a running app. T179 provisions the workspace container that holds apps before they're built. Different lifecycle stage (pre-build vs runtime).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F466, F467, F468, F477 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F466 IAppWorkspaceService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Workspace metadata + ownership |
| F467 IAppProjectService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | App project initialization |
| F468 IAppConfigService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) | Default config provisioning |
| F477 ICollaborationService | DATABASE FABRIC (Redis + ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Collaboration channel setup |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates workspace provisioner + config initializer + collaboration channel creator |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: validate tenant → create workspace → init config → create collab channel → emit WorkspaceReady |
| AF-3 | `workspace-provisioning`, `tenant-scope-initialization`, `collaboration-setup` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-30 (Managed Primitive Factory), SK-31 (MT Pooled/Silo Graduation) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for workspace template generation |
| AF-6 | Verify workspace isolation — no cross-tenant data leakage |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Tenant isolation verified, no credential leakage in workspace config |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-179-1 through IR-179-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Workspace provision success rate + latency |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-214 (Workspace indices vs FLOW-08 builder session indices)

### IRON RULES (IR-179-1 through IR-179-8)
1. Workspace MUST be scoped to exactly 1 tenantId — multi-tenant workspace = BUILD FAILURE
2. Workspace creation MUST be idempotent (same request = same workspace, not duplicate)
3. Config defaults MUST come from FREEDOM layer (ES) — never hardcoded
4. Collaboration channel MUST be tenant-scoped (DNA-5)
5. WorkspaceReady event MUST fire only after ALL sub-provisions succeed
6. Workspace quota MUST be checked before creation (F508 paywall gate if applicable)
7. All workspace data stored as dict[str, Any] via parse_document (DNA-1)
8. Workspace creation MUST emit event via transactional outbox (DNA-8)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-179-1 through QG-179-6)
1. Isolation: workspace from tenant A cannot read tenant B workspace data
2. Idempotency: same create request 100× → exactly 1 workspace
3. Config: workspace has all default config keys populated
4. Collaboration: collab channel active and tenant-scoped
5. Event: WorkspaceReady event contains workspaceId + tenantId
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Workspace = 1 tenant | — | No |
| MACHINE | Idempotent creation | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Max apps per workspace | 10 | Yes — per tier |
| FREEDOM | Default collaboration mode | real-time | Yes — per tenant |

---

## TASK TYPE: T180 — NLP Spec Intake Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** TEMPLATE (NEW)
**ENTRY:** Fires on UserMessageReceived in app creation context
**PURPOSE:** Parse natural language user intent into structured app specification diffs, using AI engine + RAG for domain knowledge, producing versioned spec documents.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T84 (Intent-to-Spec Compiler, FLOW-08) — T84 operates within a conversation session for research synthesis. T180 operates in the app creation context, generating full app specs (not research bundles). Different input (app description vs research topic) and output (app spec DAG vs research consensus).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F470, F471, F472, F473, F469 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F470 INlpSpecIntakeService | AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC + RAG FABRIC | NLP parsing + spec generation |
| F471 ISpecValidationService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC + QUEUE FABRIC | Spec validation |
| F472 ISpecDiffService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC | Diff computation |
| F473 IVersioningService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Version creation |
| F469 IAppSchemaService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Schema extraction from spec |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates NLP spec intake pipeline + validation + versioning chain |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: receive message → parse intent (AI) → generate spec diff → validate → create version → emit SpecVersionCreated |
| AF-3 | `nlp-spec-generation`, `app-spec-validation`, `version-management` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-29 (NLP-to-Spec Compilation), SK-99 (Workspace-Scoped Spec Chain) |
| AF-5 | 3+ models for spec generation (Claude primary, GPT-4 + Gemini review) |
| AF-6 | Verify spec is valid JSON DAG, all entities resolvable |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 (spec stored as Dictionary, never typed SpecModel) |
| AF-8 | Prompt injection prevention in user message → AI pipeline |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-180-1 through IR-180-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge multi-model spec outputs |
| AF-11 | Spec generation accuracy + user satisfaction rate |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-215 (NLP spec vs FLOW-08 conversation builder — separate pipelines)

### IRON RULES (IR-180-1 through IR-180-8)
1. Spec MUST be stored as dict[str, Any] — never typed SpecModel (DNA-1)
2. AI calls MUST go through IAiProvider.GenerateAsync() — never direct SDK (Fabric)
3. RAG retrieval MUST go through IRagService.SearchAsync() — never direct search
4. Spec diff MUST be computed and stored before version creation
5. Validation MUST pass before SpecVersionCreated event fires
6. User input MUST be sanitized before AI prompt injection
7. Spec version MUST be immutable once published (DR-24 pattern)
8. All operations MUST include tenantId + appId scope (DNA-5)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-180-1 through QG-180-6)
1. Accuracy: spec generated from "build a todo app" contains tasks entity + CRUD
2. Safety: prompt injection attempt → rejected, no spec corruption
3. Versioning: 10 spec iterations → 10 distinct immutable versions
4. Isolation: spec from app A cannot reference entities from app B
5. Multi-model: 3 models produce compatible spec outputs (consensus)
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

## TASK TYPE: T181 — Visual Editor Render Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** SCAFFOLDING (NEW)
**ENTRY:** Fires on SpecVersionPublished for apps with visual editing enabled
**PURPOSE:** Generate the visual editor component tree from a validated spec version, rendering drag-drop-capable UI components with responsive breakpoints.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T87 (Code Generation Judgment Gate, FLOW-08) — T87 validates generated code at the AF-7/8/9 level. T181 produces the visual representation (component tree + layout) that users interact with. Different output (code vs visual tree) and different consumer (developer vs business user).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F478, F479, F480, F481, F484 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F478 IVisualEditorService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) + QUEUE FABRIC + AI ENGINE FABRIC | Editor state + AI layout |
| F479 IComponentLibraryService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) | Component catalog |
| F480 IDragDropLayoutService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) | Layout + grid |
| F481 IStyleThemeService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC | Theme generation |
| F484 IDesignTokenService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Design tokens |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates visual editor renderer + component tree builder + layout engine |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: load spec → resolve components → build tree → apply theme → generate layout → emit EditorReady |
| AF-3 | `visual-editor-render`, `component-resolution`, `responsive-layout` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-100 (Visual Editor Component Resolution), SK-29 (NLP-to-Spec for component mapping) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for layout suggestion + theme generation |
| AF-6 | Verify component tree renders without errors on 3 viewports |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | No XSS vectors in component properties |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-181-1 through IR-181-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Render success rate + first-render latency |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-216 (Editor state indices vs FLOW-08 builder session state)

### IRON RULES (IR-181-1 through IR-181-8)
1. Component tree MUST be stored as dict[str, Any] (DNA-1) — never typed ComponentModel
2. Editor state MUST be tenant+app scoped (DNA-5)
3. Component library MUST resolve via F479 — never hardcoded component list
4. Theme MUST be applied from F481 — never inline styles in generated code
5. Layout MUST support 3+ responsive breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop)
6. Real-time collaboration cursor data MUST use Redis (not ES) for latency
7. Component properties MUST be sanitized (no script injection)
8. EditorReady event MUST fire only after all components resolve successfully

### QUALITY GATES (QG-181-1 through QG-181-6)
1. Render: component tree renders on mobile, tablet, desktop viewports
2. Performance: first render < 2s for 50-component tree
3. Collaboration: 3 users editing simultaneously — no state corruption
4. Theme: theme change propagates to all components within 1s
5. Isolation: editor state from app A inaccessible from app B
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

## TASK TYPE: T182 — Template Scoring & Selection Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** TEMPLATE
**ENTRY:** Fires on TemplateSearchRequested or AppCreatedFromTemplate event
**PURPOSE:** Score and rank app templates using AI-assisted quality analysis, matching user intent to best-fit templates for quick start.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T85 (Deep Research Consensus Gate, FLOW-08) — T85 uses multi-model consensus for knowledge synthesis. T182 uses AI to evaluate template quality/fit. Different subject (research vs templates) and different output (consensus score vs template ranking).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F482, F485, F489, F470 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F482 ITemplateScoringService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC + RAG FABRIC | AI scoring |
| F485 ITemplateRegistryService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Template catalog |
| F489 ITemplateMarketplaceService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC | Marketplace search |
| F470 INlpSpecIntakeService | AI ENGINE FABRIC + RAG FABRIC | Intent parsing for template matching |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates template scorer + recommender + marketplace query engine |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: parse intent → search templates → score matches → rank → return top N |
| AF-3 | `template-scoring`, `intent-template-matching`, `marketplace-ranking` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-101 (Template Quality Scoring), SK-29 (NLP-to-Spec) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for quality assessment + intent matching |
| AF-6 | Verify ranking stability (same input → same top-3) |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | No bias injection in AI scoring |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-182-1 through IR-182-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Template selection success rate + user click-through |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-217 (Template search vs FLOW-08 spec search — separate ES indices)

### IRON RULES (IR-182-1 through IR-182-8)
1. Template scores MUST be stored as dict[str, Any] (DNA-1)
2. Scoring MUST use AI ENGINE FABRIC — never hardcoded scoring algorithm
3. Template catalog is global; scoring results are tenant-scoped
4. Rankings MUST be deterministic for identical inputs
5. Template metadata MUST never contain user PII from other tenants
6. Marketplace search MUST use build_search_filter (DNA-2) for query construction
7. Template selection event MUST be recorded for feedback (AF-11)
8. All scores MUST return DataProcessResult[T] (DNA-3)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-182-1 through QG-182-6)
1. Relevance: "build a todo app" → todo-template in top 3
2. Determinism: same query 10× → identical top-3 ranking
3. Isolation: tenant A scoring cannot influence tenant B rankings
4. Performance: search + score < 3s for 1000-template catalog
5. Accuracy: AI score correlates with human rating (>0.7 correlation)
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

## TASK TYPE: T183 — Scaffold Generation Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** SCAFFOLDING
**ENTRY:** Fires on SpecValidated event (after T180 spec intake + T182 template selection)
**PURPOSE:** Generate full-stack application scaffold from validated spec — services, APIs, UI components, database schema, and configuration — all sitting on fabric interfaces.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T86 (Agent Task Graph Fork, FLOW-08) — T86 decomposes a spec into parallel agent tasks. T183 generates the actual scaffold artifacts (code, schema, config) from the decomposed tasks. T86 = decomposition; T183 = generation.

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F488, F469, F471, F473, F250 (FLOW-08) — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F488 IScaffoldGeneratorService | AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC + RAG FABRIC | AI scaffold generation |
| F469 IAppSchemaService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Schema generation |
| F471 ISpecValidationService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC + QUEUE FABRIC | Post-scaffold validation |
| F473 IVersioningService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Scaffold version snapshot |
| F250 ICodegenPipelineService | AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) | Code generation pipeline (FLOW-08) |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates scaffold builder + schema generator + code gen + config writer |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: load spec → resolve template base → generate schema → generate services → generate UI → generate config → validate → snapshot |
| AF-3 | `scaffold-generation`, `schema-from-spec`, `fabric-first-codegen` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-102 (Scaffold Generation Pipeline), SK-30 (Managed Primitives) |
| AF-5 | 3+ models for code generation (Claude + GPT-4 + Gemini) |
| AF-6 | Verify generated code compiles + passes DNA checks |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 (ALL generated code MUST use fabric interfaces) |
| AF-8 | No secrets in generated code, no direct provider imports |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-183-1 through IR-183-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge multi-model code outputs (best-of-N selection) |
| AF-11 | Scaffold build success rate + generated code quality score |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-218 (Generated scaffold indices vs existing FLOW-08 build artifact indices)

### IRON RULES (IR-183-1 through IR-183-8)
1. ALL generated services MUST extend MicroserviceBase (DNA-4)
2. ALL generated services MUST use fabric interfaces — no direct provider imports
3. ALL data access MUST use dict[str, Any] via parse_document (DNA-1)
4. ALL queries MUST use build_search_filter (DNA-2)
5. ALL operations MUST return DataProcessResult[T] (DNA-3)
6. ALL queries MUST include tenantId scope (DNA-5)
7. Generated scaffold MUST be version-snapshotted via F473 before deployment
8. Generated controllers MUST use DynamicController pattern (DNA-6) — no entity-specific controllers

### QUALITY GATES (QG-183-1 through QG-183-6)
1. Build: scaffold compiles without errors
2. DNA: all 9 DNA patterns pass on every generated service
3. Fabric: zero direct provider imports in generated code
4. Schema: generated schema matches spec entities
5. Multi-model: 3 models produce mergeable outputs
6. Rollback: scaffold can be rolled back to prior version via F473

## TASK TYPE: T184 — App Preview Deploy Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** PROVISIONING
**ENTRY:** Fires on ScaffoldGenerated event
**PURPOSE:** Deploy generated app scaffold to an ephemeral preview environment with time-bounded TTL, providing a shareable preview URL for stakeholder review.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T88 (Managed Primitive Provisioner, FLOW-08) — T88 provisions runtime primitives for production. T184 provisions temporary preview slots for pre-production review. Different lifecycle (ephemeral vs persistent) and purpose (review vs runtime).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F474, F475, F476 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F474 IAppPreviewService | CORE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Preview slot management |
| F475 IAppDeployService | QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) | Deploy pipeline |
| F476 IAppStatusService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) | Health monitoring |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates preview deployer + health checker + TTL manager |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: allocate preview slot → deploy scaffold → health check → generate URL → start TTL timer → emit PreviewReady |
| AF-3 | `preview-deployment`, `ephemeral-slot-management`, `health-check` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-32 (Immutable Snapshot + EP-2 Rollback), SK-30 (Managed Primitive) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for deployment validation |
| AF-6 | Verify preview URL resolves and returns 200 |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Preview URL must not expose internal infrastructure details |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-184-1 through IR-184-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Preview deploy success rate + first-load latency |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-219 (Preview slots vs FLOW-08 managed primitive provisioning — separate resource pools)

### IRON RULES (IR-184-1 through IR-184-8)
1. Preview MUST have TTL — no permanent previews (FREEDOM: TTL configurable, default 24h)
2. Preview MUST be tenant+app scoped (DNA-5) — no shared preview slots
3. Preview URL MUST NOT expose internal hostnames or infrastructure
4. Preview slot MUST be destroyed on TTL expiry (EP-2 durable timer)
5. Max concurrent previews MUST be enforced per tier (paywall gate via F508)
6. Preview health check MUST pass before PreviewReady event fires
7. Preview data MUST be isolated from production data
8. Preview deploy MUST use QUEUE FABRIC events — not direct HTTP calls

### QUALITY GATES (QG-184-1 through QG-184-6)
1. Access: preview URL returns 200 within 5s of PreviewReady
2. TTL: preview auto-destroyed after configured TTL
3. Isolation: preview from app A cannot access app B preview data
4. Quota: exceeding max previews → soft block + upgrade prompt
5. Health: unhealthy preview → alert + auto-destroy
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

---

## TASK TYPE: T185 — GitHub Sync Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** INTEGRATION
**ENTRY:** Fires on GitHubConnectRequested or AppCodeChanged events
**PURPOSE:** Bidirectional sync between generated app code and external GitHub repository, with conflict detection, cursor-based incremental sync, and branch management.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T186 (Code Export Gate) — T186 produces a one-time standalone export. T185 maintains ongoing bidirectional sync. Different lifecycle (continuous vs one-shot) and different output (synced repo vs download package).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F491, F492, F473, F475 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F491 IGitHubSyncService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) + QUEUE FABRIC + CORE FABRIC (HTTP) | Sync engine |
| F492 IGitBranchService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Branch management |
| F473 IVersioningService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Version tracking |
| F475 IAppDeployService | QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) | Auto-deploy after sync |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates bidirectional sync engine + conflict resolver + branch manager |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: connect repo → establish cursor → watch for changes → push/pull delta → resolve conflicts → update cursor → emit SyncCompleted |
| AF-3 | `github-sync`, `bidirectional-sync`, `conflict-resolution` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-89 (Incremental Cursor, FLOW-14), SK-103 (Git Bidirectional Sync) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for conflict resolution suggestions |
| AF-6 | Verify sync cursor advances correctly, no data loss |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | No GitHub tokens in logs or generated code; HMAC webhook verification |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-185-1 through IR-185-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Sync success rate + conflict rate + sync latency |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-220 (GitHub sync vs FLOW-14 webhook ingestion — separate webhook endpoints)

### IRON RULES (IR-185-1 through IR-185-8)
1. Sync cursor MUST persist to PostgreSQL (not Redis) — survives restarts
2. GitHub tokens MUST be stored in encrypted credential vault — never in logs
3. Webhook HMAC MUST be verified before processing (DR-59 pattern)
4. Conflict detection MUST create conflict markers — never silently overwrite
5. Sync MUST be tenant+app scoped (DNA-5) — no cross-tenant repo access
6. Auto-deploy after sync MUST go through deploy pipeline (F475) — never direct
7. Branch operations MUST emit events via QUEUE FABRIC
8. All sync records MUST be stored as dict[str, Any] (DNA-1)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-185-1 through QG-185-6)
1. Sync: push 10 files → all appear in GitHub repo
2. Pull: change 5 files in GitHub → all reflected in app
3. Conflict: simultaneous edit on same file → conflict marker created
4. Cursor: sync interrupted + resumed → no duplicate or missed changes
5. Security: GitHub token not in any log entry or generated code
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

```json
{
  "templateId": "mvp-app-creation-v1",
```

## T186 — Code Export Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** INTEGRATION
**ENTRY:** Fires on CodeExportRequested event
**PURPOSE:** Package generated application code into a standalone, runnable project with all dependencies, documentation, and configuration — ready for developer handoff outside the platform.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T185 (GitHub Sync Gate) — T185 synchronizes ongoing changes bidirectionally. T186 produces a one-time standalone export package. Different lifecycle (continuous sync vs one-shot export).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F493, F491, F245, F251 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F493 ICodeExportService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Export packaging + job queue |
| F491 IGitHubSyncService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) | Repo initialization for export |
| F245 ISpecRegistryService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Current app spec (FLOW-08) |
| F251 IBuildArtifactService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Build artifacts to include |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates code export packager + dependency resolver + documentation generator |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: resolve spec → collect artifacts → strip platform deps → inject standalone config → generate README → package |
| AF-3 | `code-export-packaging`, `standalone-project-structure`, `dependency-resolution` |
| AF-4 | Matches SK-104 (GitHub Bidirectional Sync), SK-29 (NLP-to-Spec for documentation) |
| AF-5 | 2+ models for documentation generation |
| AF-6 | Verify exported project builds independently |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | No secrets, credentials, or platform-internal URLs in export |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-186-1 through IR-186-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Export success rate + build-test pass rate |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-224 (Export artifacts vs FLOW-14 data export)

### IRON RULES (IR-186-1 through IR-186-8)
1. Export MUST NOT contain platform secrets, API keys, or internal URLs
2. Export MUST include all dependencies with pinned versions (reproducible build)
3. Export MUST include generated README with setup instructions
4. Export MUST produce a project that builds without platform connectivity
5. Export MUST strip fabric-specific code and replace with standard implementations
6. Export artifacts MUST be content-addressable (hash-verified integrity)
7. Export MUST respect tenant scope (only export own tenant's code)
8. Export MUST emit CodeExportCompleted with package manifest and download URL

### QUALITY GATES (QG-186-1 through QG-186-6)
1. Standalone build: exported project builds without platform
2. No secrets: scan export for API keys, tokens, passwords → zero found
3. Completeness: export includes all generated services, flows, configs
4. Documentation: README contains setup steps, environment variables, run commands
5. Integrity: download hash matches generation hash
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Export must strip fabric code | — | No |
| MACHINE | Secret scan mandatory | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Target framework | react-typescript | Yes — per tenant |
| FREEDOM | Include test suite | true | Yes — per export |
| FREEDOM | README template | default | Yes — per tenant |

## T187 — Plugin Installation Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** PROVISIONING
**ENTRY:** Fires on PluginInstallRequested event
**PURPOSE:** Validate plugin dependencies, provision isolated sandbox with resource quotas, wire plugin to tenant's managed primitives (F252-F256), and activate within the app.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T88 (Primitive Provisioning, FLOW-08) — T88 provisions infrastructure primitives. T187 provisions plugin sandboxes WITHIN existing primitives. Different isolation layer (infrastructure vs plugin).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F496, F497, F498, F499, F500, F252, F253, F254 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F496 IPluginRegistryService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) + QUEUE FABRIC | Plugin catalog + install records |
| F497 IPluginInstallerService | QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) | Install pipeline orchestration |
| F498 IPluginSandboxService | CORE FABRIC (ResourceQuota) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) | Sandbox isolation |
| F499 IPluginMarketplaceService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC | Browse + recommend |
| F500 IPluginConfigService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Per-install FREEDOM config |
| F252 IManagedDatabaseService | DATABASE FABRIC | Plugin data access (FLOW-08) |
| F253 IManagedStorageService | DATABASE FABRIC | Plugin file access (FLOW-08) |
| F254 IManagedAuthService | DATABASE FABRIC | Plugin auth context (FLOW-08) |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates plugin sandbox provisioning + resource quota enforcement code |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: check deps → validate tier → provision sandbox → wire primitives → init → activate |
| AF-3 | `plugin-sandbox-provisioning`, `resource-quota-enforcement`, `primitive-wiring` |
| AF-4 | Matches SK-30 (Managed Primitive Factory Set), SK-105 (Plugin Sandbox Resource Quota) |
| AF-5 | 2+ models for sandbox isolation code |
| AF-6 | Verify sandbox boundary enforcement |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Plugin code injection prevention, resource escape detection, cross-tenant access prevention |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-187-1 through IR-187-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Installation success rate + sandbox breach attempts |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-220 (Plugin Sandbox vs Warehouse Isolation), CF-225 (Plugin resources vs tenant quotas)

### IRON RULES (IR-187-1 through IR-187-8)
1. Plugin sandbox MUST have CPU, memory, storage, API call limits enforced
2. Plugin MUST NOT access other tenants' data (DNA-5 + sandbox boundary)
3. Plugin installation MUST be idempotent (re-install = no-op or update)
4. Plugin sandbox timeout MUST be enforced (max 30s per action, FREEDOM: configurable)
5. Plugin MUST NOT access host filesystem or network beyond allowed endpoints
6. Uninstall MUST clean up ALL sandbox resources (no orphaned compute/storage)
7. Plugin config schema MUST be validated before activation (invalid config = install failure)
8. Plugin activation MUST verify managed primitive access works (F252-F254 connectivity test)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-187-1 through QG-187-6)
1. Sandbox isolation proven: plugin A cannot read plugin B's data within same tenant
2. Resource quota proven: plugin exceeding CPU limit → throttled, not crashed
3. Install/uninstall idempotency proven
4. Timeout proven: int-running plugin action → killed at timeout
5. Managed primitive wiring: plugin can access F252-F254 through sandbox boundary
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Sandbox isolation enforced | — | No |
| MACHINE | Cross-tenant access blocked | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Sandbox timeout (seconds) | 30 | Yes — per plugin |
| FREEDOM | CPU limit (millicores) | 500 | Yes — per tier |
| FREEDOM | Memory limit (MB) | 256 | Yes — per tier |
| FREEDOM | Storage limit (MB) | 100 | Yes — per tier |

## T188 — Plugin Marketplace Discovery

**ARCHETYPE:** RECOMMENDATION
**ENTRY:** Fires on PluginSearchRequested or PluginRecommendRequested event
**PURPOSE:** Search, filter, and AI-recommend plugins from the global catalog based on app context, user behavior, and installed plugin complement. Surface ratings, reviews, and compatibility.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T183 (Template Scaffolding) — T183 scaffolds entire apps from templates. T188 recommends individual plugins to add to existing apps. Different granularity (full app vs single component).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F499, F496, F482 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F499 IPluginMarketplaceService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC | Search + recommend |
| F496 IPluginRegistryService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Plugin metadata + reviews |
| F482 ITemplateScoringService | AI ENGINE FABRIC | Reuse scoring algorithm (FLOW-15 Fam 62) |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates search ranking + AI recommendation engine code |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: query → search → filter → rank → AI enhance → surface results |
| AF-3 | `search-ranking-patterns`, `recommendation-algorithms`, `plugin-compatibility-check` |
| AF-4 | Matches SK-105 (Plugin Sandbox), RAG FABRIC strategies for semantic search |
| AF-5 | 2+ models for recommendation quality |
| AF-6 | Verify recommendation relevance, no sponsored manipulation |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Review authenticity validation, no rating manipulation by publishers |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-188-1 through IR-188-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Recommendation click-through + install conversion rates |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-218 (Plugin search vs FLOW-08 spec search — different ES indices)

### IRON RULES (IR-188-1 through IR-188-8)
1. Plugin search MUST use DATABASE FABRIC (ES) — never in-memory filtering of full catalog
2. AI recommendations MUST go through AI ENGINE FABRIC (never direct provider call)
3. Plugin ratings MUST NOT be modifiable by the plugin publisher
4. Search results MUST be scoped to compatible plugins (version, platform, dependencies)
5. Marketplace MUST NOT favor paid/sponsored plugins without transparency
6. Reviews MUST be tenant-scoped (one review per tenant per plugin)
7. Compatibility check MUST verify plugin dependencies against installed plugins
8. Search MUST support pagination with stable cursor (no result drift)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-188-1 through QG-188-6)
1. Search relevance: query "chatbot" → chatbot plugins ranked first
2. Recommendation quality: AI suggests plugins matching app type and missing capabilities
3. Compatibility: incompatible plugin → flagged in search results
4. Rating integrity: publisher cannot modify their own plugin's ratings
5. Pagination stability: page 2 results consistent across requests
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Search via ES (not in-memory) | — | No |
| MACHINE | Rating immutability | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Results per page | 20 | Yes — per tenant |
| FREEDOM | AI recommendation model | claude-sonnet | Yes — per config |
| FREEDOM | Compatibility strictness | strict | Yes — strict/warn/ignore |

## T189 — Sandbox Fork & Commit Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** SANDBOX (NEW)
**ENTRY:** Fires on SandboxForkRequested or SandboxCommitRequested event
**PURPOSE:** Fork entire app state into isolated sandbox for non-destructive experimentation, then atomically commit changes to live or discard. Includes AI-powered diff preview.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T82 (Scheduled Rebalancer, FLOW-08) — T82 rebalances system state on schedule. T189 forks/commits app state on demand for experimentation. Different trigger and purpose.

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F501, F502, F503, F504, F245, F257 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F501 IDiscussionSessionService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) + QUEUE FABRIC | Session lifecycle |
| F502 ISandboxForkService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) | Copy-on-write fork |
| F503 IDiffPreviewService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC | Structural diff + AI summary |
| F504 ISandboxCommitService | QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) | Atomic commit/discard |
| F245 ISpecRegistryService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Live app spec to fork (FLOW-08) |
| F257 IVersionControlService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Snapshot before commit (FLOW-08) |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates copy-on-write fork engine + atomic commit + diff calculator |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: fork live state → sandbox indices → apply changes → compute diff → AI summary → commit/discard |
| AF-3 | `copy-on-write-patterns`, `atomic-commit-strategies`, `diff-computation` |
| AF-4 | Matches SK-106 (Copy-on-Write Sandbox Fork), SK-32 (Immutable Snapshot), SK-110 (Diff Preview) |
| AF-5 | 2+ models for diff summarization |
| AF-6 | Verify sandbox isolation, no live state contamination |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Sandbox escape prevention, commit injection prevention |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-189-1 through IR-189-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Sandbox commit vs discard ratio, diff accuracy |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-216 (Sandbox state vs live state indices), CF-228 (Sandbox commit vs version snapshots)

### IRON RULES (IR-189-1 through IR-189-8)
1. Fork MUST create isolated copy (NOT reference to live state — true copy-on-write)
2. Changes in sandbox MUST NOT affect live app until explicit commit
3. Commit MUST be atomic (all-or-nothing application of sandbox changes)
4. Commit MUST create version snapshot via F257 BEFORE applying changes (rollback safety)
5. Commit MUST trigger BFA validation BEFORE applying changes (conflict detection)
6. Discard MUST clean up ALL sandbox indices (no orphaned data)
7. Sandbox TTL MUST auto-expire and cleanup (default: 48h, FREEDOM: configurable)
8. Sandbox MUST inherit live tenant scope but use sandbox-prefixed indices (DNA-5)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-189-1 through QG-189-6)
1. Isolation proven: changes in sandbox NOT visible in live app
2. Commit atomicity: commit → all changes applied; fail mid-commit → none applied
3. Rollback safety: commit creates snapshot → rollback restores pre-commit state
4. Auto-expiry: sandbox expires after TTL → indices cleaned up
5. Diff accuracy: AI-generated summary matches actual structural differences
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Snapshot before commit | — | No |
| MACHINE | BFA validation before commit | — | No |
| MACHINE | Sandbox index prefix isolation | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Sandbox TTL (hours) | 48 | Yes — per tenant |
| FREEDOM | Max concurrent sandboxes | 3 | Yes — per tier |
| FREEDOM | AI diff model | claude-sonnet | Yes — per config |

---

## T190 — Subscription Lifecycle Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** BILLING
**ENTRY:** Fires on SubscriptionCreateRequested, SubscriptionUpgradeRequested, SubscriptionCancelRequested events
**PURPOSE:** Manage subscription state machine (trial→active→suspended→canceled) with prorated billing, entitlement updates, and graceful degradation on payment failure.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T88 (Primitive Provisioning, FLOW-08) — T88 provisions infrastructure. T190 manages billing subscriptions and entitlements. Different business domain (infrastructure vs billing).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F505, F506, F507, F508, F509 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F505 ISubscriptionManagerService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Subscription state (ACID) |
| F506 IUsageMeteringService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) + QUEUE FABRIC | Usage tracking |
| F507 IInvoicingService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Invoice generation |
| F508 IPaywallGateService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) | Entitlement enforcement |
| F509 IBillingWebhookService | QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) | Payment callback processing |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates subscription state machine + proration engine + entitlement cache |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: validate plan → compute proration → update state → sync entitlements → generate invoice → emit events |
| AF-3 | `subscription-state-machine`, `proration-calculation`, `entitlement-management` |
| AF-4 | Matches SK-107 (Subscription State Machine), SK-108 (Usage Metering), SK-109 (Paywall Gate) |
| AF-5 | 3+ models for billing logic accuracy |
| AF-6 | Verify proration calculations, state machine correctness |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Payment amount validation, float-charge prevention |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-190-1 through IR-190-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Subscription conversion + churn rates |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-217 (Billing vs FLOW-08 Managed Payments), CF-229 (Billing events vs platform events)

### IRON RULES (IR-190-1 through IR-190-8)
1. Subscription state transitions MUST follow defined state machine (invalid transition = rejection)
2. Proration MUST be computed to-the-second for upgrade/downgrade (no rounding errors)
3. Payment failure MUST trigger grace period then suspension (NOT immediate cancellation)
4. Entitlement cache (F508) MUST update within 300s of subscription change
5. Cancel MUST honor paid-through date (no early feature revocation)
6. Trial expiry MUST be enforced via EP-2 durable timer (not cron)
7. Duplicate subscription creation MUST be prevented (one active sub per tenant per plan type)
8. All billing amounts MUST use integer cents (no floating point — financial precision)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-190-1 through QG-190-6)
1. State machine proven: valid transitions succeed, invalid transitions rejected
2. Proration accuracy: upgrade mid-month → correct prorated amount computed
3. Grace period: payment fails → tenant active for grace period → then suspended
4. Entitlement sync: upgrade → features available within 300s
5. Trial expiry: trial ends → no conversion → access revoked (per plan rules)
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Integer cents for amounts | — | No |
| MACHINE | State machine transition rules | — | No |
| MACHINE | EP-2 for trial expiry | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Grace period (days) | 7 | Yes — per plan |
| FREEDOM | Trial duration (days) | 14 | Yes — per plan |
| FREEDOM | Entitlement sync SLA (seconds) | 300 | Yes — per tier |
| FREEDOM | Proration granularity | to-the-second | Yes — second/day/none |

---

## T191 — Usage Metering Aggregation

**ARCHETYPE:** METERING (NEW)
**ENTRY:** Fires on BillingPeriodEndReached or UsageThresholdExceeded event
**PURPOSE:** Aggregate raw usage events (API calls, storage, compute, AI tokens) into billing-ready metered quantities, apply tiered pricing, detect anomalies, and feed into invoicing pipeline. The metering aggregation is the bridge between real-time usage tracking and periodic billing cycles.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T190 (Subscription Lifecycle Gate) — T190 manages subscription state machine and entitlements. T191 aggregates raw usage into billable quantities for consumption-based billing. Different concern (subscription state vs usage quantification).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F506, F507, F508, F509, F505 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F506 IUsageMeteringService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) + QUEUE FABRIC | Raw event ingestion + aggregation windows |
| F507 IInvoicingService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Aggregated usage → line items on invoice |
| F508 IPaywallGateService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) | Real-time quota enforcement during aggregation |
| F509 IBillingWebhookService | QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) | External billing provider sync |
| F505 ISubscriptionManagerService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Plan limits for threshold computation |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates time-window aggregation engine + tiered pricing calculator + anomaly detector |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: collect raw events → window aggregate → apply pricing tiers → detect anomalies → generate usage summary → feed invoicing → update quotas |
| AF-3 | `time-window-aggregation`, `tiered-pricing-calculation`, `usage-anomaly-detection` |
| AF-4 | Matches SK-108 (Usage Metering Aggregation), SK-89 (Incremental Cursor — reuse cursor pattern for metering windows) |
| AF-5 | 2+ models for anomaly detection logic |
| AF-6 | Verify aggregation accuracy: sum(raw events) = aggregated total (reconciliation) |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Prevent usage inflation attacks, validate metering event authenticity |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-191-1 through IR-191-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Aggregation accuracy rate + anomaly detection precision |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-230 (Metering aggregation vs FLOW-14 warehouse metrics — different ES indices), CF-231 (Usage thresholds vs subscription entitlements — must agree)

### IRON RULES (IR-191-1 through IR-191-8)
1. Usage aggregation MUST be idempotent (re-aggregating same period = same result)
2. All metering amounts MUST use integer units (no floating point — API calls as count, storage as bytes, tokens as count)
3. Aggregation window boundaries MUST be deterministic (UTC midnight for daily, UTC hour-start for hourly)
4. Raw usage events MUST be immutable after ingestion (append-only — DR-58 pattern reuse)
5. Aggregated totals MUST reconcile with raw event sum (drift > 0.01% = aggregation failure)
6. Tiered pricing MUST apply in order (tier 1 filled before tier 2 charges begin)
7. Usage threshold alerts MUST fire BEFORE quota exhaustion (at 80% and 95% thresholds, FREEDOM: configurable)
8. Metering MUST emit UsageAggregationCompleted with period, tenant, quantities, and computed charges

### QUALITY GATES (QG-191-1 through QG-191-6)
1. Idempotency proven: aggregate same period twice → identical results
2. Reconciliation proven: sum(raw events) matches aggregated total within 0.01% tolerance
3. Tiered pricing proven: 1000 API calls with 500-free-tier → charges only for 500
4. Threshold alerts: usage at 80% → alert fires; usage at 100% → enforcement triggers
5. Anomaly detection: 10× spike in usage → flagged for review (not auto-billed)
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Integer units for quantities | — | No |
| MACHINE | Idempotent aggregation | — | No |
| MACHINE | Immutable raw events | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Aggregation window | hourly | Yes — hourly/daily/monthly |
| FREEDOM | Alert thresholds (%) | 80, 95 | Yes — per plan |
| FREEDOM | Anomaly spike multiplier | 10× | Yes — per tenant |
| FREEDOM | Pricing tiers | plan-defined | Yes — per plan |

---

## T192 — Paywall Enforcement Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** ENFORCEMENT (NEW)
**ENTRY:** Fires on FeatureAccessRequested or ApiCallIntercepted event
**PURPOSE:** Real-time entitlement check at the point of feature access. Intercepts every gated action, validates against current subscription tier and usage quotas, and either allows, soft-blocks (with upgrade prompt), or hard-blocks access. Acts as the runtime enforcement layer that makes T190 subscriptions and T191 metering meaningful.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T190 (Subscription Lifecycle Gate) — T190 manages subscription state transitions. T192 enforces entitlements at access time. Different timing (lifecycle event vs every request). T191 (Usage Metering) — T191 aggregates usage for billing. T192 blocks access when quotas are exceeded. Different action (count vs block).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F508, F505, F506, F500 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F508 IPaywallGateService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) | Entitlement cache + quota counters |
| F505 ISubscriptionManagerService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Subscription tier for entitlement source of truth |
| F506 IUsageMeteringService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) + QUEUE FABRIC | Current usage for quota check |
| F500 IPluginConfigService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Plugin-specific entitlements (if plugin has premium features) |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates entitlement cache + quota counter + enforcement middleware + upgrade prompt generator |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: intercept request → check entitlement cache → verify quota → decide (allow/soft-block/hard-block) → log decision → return response |
| AF-3 | `entitlement-enforcement`, `quota-checking`, `graceful-degradation`, `upgrade-prompt-patterns` |
| AF-4 | Matches SK-109 (Paywall Gate Enforcement), SK-107 (Subscription State Machine for tier resolution) |
| AF-5 | 2+ models for upgrade prompt personalization |
| AF-6 | Verify enforcement correctness: free tier blocked from premium features, premium tier allowed |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Bypass prevention (no client-side enforcement only), cache poisoning detection |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-192-1 through IR-192-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Block rate + upgrade conversion rate + false-positive rate |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-232 (Paywall enforcement vs FLOW-08 auth gate — different concerns: billing vs identity), CF-233 (Quota counters vs metering counters — must use same source)

### IRON RULES (IR-192-1 through IR-192-8)
1. Enforcement MUST check server-side entitlement (client-side checks are UX hints only — never authoritative)
2. Entitlement cache MUST have TTL ≤ 300s (stale cache = revenue leakage or false blocks)
3. Cache miss MUST fall back to F505 subscription source of truth (never default to "allow")
4. Quota check MUST use F506 current-period counters (not aggregated totals from prior periods)
5. Soft-block (upgrade prompt) MUST NOT prevent access to already-paid features (only new features beyond tier)
6. Hard-block MUST return standardized PaywallBlockResponse with reason, current tier, and upgrade options
7. Enforcement decision MUST be logged (tenantId, featureId, decision, timestamp) for audit and analytics
8. Grace period active (from T190 payment failure) MUST allow continued access (suspended ≠ blocked)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-192-1 through QG-192-6)
1. Tier enforcement proven: free user → blocked from premium feature; premium user → allowed
2. Quota enforcement proven: API calls at limit → hard-block; below limit → allow
3. Cache consistency: subscription upgrade → entitlement cache updated within 300s → access granted
4. Grace period respected: payment failed but in grace → access continues
5. Upgrade prompt: soft-block returns actionable upgrade path (not just "denied")
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Server-side enforcement mandatory | — | No |
| MACHINE | Cache miss → deny (not allow) | — | No |
| MACHINE | Audit logging of every decision | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Entitlement cache TTL (seconds) | 300 | Yes — per tier |
| FREEDOM | Soft-block vs hard-block threshold | plan-defined | Yes — per feature |
| FREEDOM | Upgrade prompt content | default | Yes — per tenant |
| FREEDOM | Feature gating granularity | per-feature | Yes — per-feature/per-module |

```
FLOW: FLOW-15
TEMPLATE: 38
VERSION: 1.0.0
DESCRIPTION: MVP Builder Monetization Pipeline — Subscription → Metering → Enforcement → Export
ORCHESTRATOR: IFlowOrchestrator (SK-392 (RagStrategyRegistry))
ENTRY_EVENT: SubscriptionCreateRequested OR BillingPeriodEndReached OR FeatureAccessRequested OR CodeExportRequested OR PluginInstallRequested OR SandboxForkRequested
TENANT_SCOPED: true
```

```json
{
  "flowId": "flow-15-monetization",
  "templateId": 38,
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "MVP Builder Monetization + Plugins + Sandbox + Export pipeline",
  "entryEvent": "MULTI_EVENT_ROUTER",
  "tenantScoped": true,
  "steps": [
    {
      "stepId": "step-route",
      "taskType": "ROUTE",
      "description": "Route based on entry event type",
      "conditions": [
        {"if": "event.type == 'SubscriptionCreateRequested' || event.type == 'SubscriptionUpgradeRequested' || event.type == 'SubscriptionCancelRequested'", "goto": "step-subscription"},
        {"if": "event.type == 'BillingPeriodEndReached' || event.type == 'UsageThresholdExceeded'", "goto": "step-metering"},
        {"if": "event.type == 'FeatureAccessRequested' || event.type == 'ApiCallIntercepted'", "goto": "step-paywall"},
        {"if": "event.type == 'CodeExportRequested'", "goto": "step-export"},
        {"if": "event.type == 'PluginInstallRequested'", "goto": "step-plugin-install"},
        {"if": "event.type == 'PluginSearchRequested' || event.type == 'PluginRecommendRequested'", "goto": "step-plugin-marketplace"},
        {"if": "event.type == 'SandboxForkRequested' || event.type == 'SandboxCommitRequested'", "goto": "step-sandbox"}
      ]
    },
    {
      "stepId": "step-subscription",
      "taskType": "T190",
      "description": "Subscription lifecycle — state machine + proration + entitlements",
      "factories": ["F505", "F506", "F507", "F508", "F509"],
      "freedom": {

        "trialDurationDays": "${config.flow15.{tenantId}.trial_duration_days}"
      },
      "onSuccess": "step-entitlement-sync",
      "onFailure": "RETRY:3:EXPONENTIAL"
    },
    {

      "factories": ["F508", "F505"],
```
