# FAMILY 68 — CUSTOM DOMAINS & PUBLISHING
# Task Types T193–T196

---

## T193 — Domain Provision Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** PUBLISHING ← NEW ARCHETYPE
**ENTRY:** Fires on CustomDomainRegistered event (from F510.RegisterDomainAsync)
**PURPOSE:** Orchestrate the end-to-end domain provisioning sequence: domain registration → DNS challenge generation → ownership verification → SSL certificate provisioning → CDN configuration push → PublishGate pre-check. Produces a fully provisioned, SSL-secured, CDN-accelerated custom domain ready for app deployment.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T88 (Managed Primitive Provisioning, FLOW-08) — T88 provisions DB/Storage/Auth infrastructure primitives for an app. T193 provisions the external-facing publishing layer (DNS + SSL + CDN). Different infrastructure plane (internal backend vs public-facing edge). T193 coordinates F510–F514 in a sequential saga; T88 provisions F252–F256 independently.

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F510, F511, F512, F514, F517 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F510 ICustomDomainService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Domain record + ownership + lifecycle events |
| F511 ISslCertService | CORE FABRIC (HTTP cert API) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) | SSL issuance via ACME/Let's Encrypt |
| F512 ICdnConfigService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | CDN edge config push after SSL |
| F514 IPublishGateService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) + QUEUE FABRIC | Pre-publish gate checks |
| F517 IDomainVerificationService | CORE FABRIC (DNS resolver) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | DNS TXT/CNAME challenge verification |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates domain provisioning saga: F510 register → F517 challenge → F517 verify → F511 provision → F512 push → F514 gate |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: register domain → emit challenge → poll DNS verification (max 48h) → issue SSL → push CDN config → run publish gate → emit DomainProvisionCompleted |
| AF-3 | `domain-provisioning-saga`, `ssl-cert-lifecycle`, `cdn-config-push`, `dns-challenge-pattern` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-30 (Managed Primitive Factory Set — cert as managed primitive), SK-111 (Custom Domain + SSL Provision) |
| AF-5 | 2+ models for DNS verification polling logic and SSL saga orchestration |
| AF-6 | Verify saga idempotency: re-run at any stage produces same final state |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 (domain config as Dictionary), DNA-2 (build_search_filter on domain queries), DNA-3 (DataProcessResult), DNA-4 (MicroserviceBase), DNA-5 (tenantId on all records), DNA-6 (DynamicController), DNA-7, DNA-8, DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | No private key exposure in events, no cross-tenant domain registration, ACME challenge token entropy validation |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-193-1 through IR-193-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge provisioning saga code from multi-model outputs |
| AF-11 | Provision success rate + DNS verification time + SSL issuance latency |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-234 (Domain DNS must not collide with existing tenant domains — unique domain registry check)

### IRON RULES (IR-193-1 through IR-193-8)
1. Domain MUST NOT be registered if it already exists for any tenant — global uniqueness enforced via F510 registry check (CF-234)
2. SSL MUST NOT be provisioned before domain verification passes (F517 status = VERIFIED) — ordering enforced
3. CDN config MUST NOT be pushed before SSL cert is ACTIVE (F511 status = ACTIVE)
4. PublishGate (F514) MUST run after CDN config propagates — all 5 gate checks must pass
5. Domain provisioning MUST be a resumable saga — each step emits a queue event; crash recovery resumes from last successful step
6. Private keys MUST be stored encrypted via F511 (PG encrypted columns) — never in queue events or ES
7. tenantId MUST be on every domain record, cert record, CDN config record (DNA-5)
8. DomainProvisionCompleted MUST be emitted only after ALL steps pass (no partial-provision live routing)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-193-1 through QG-193-6)
1. E2E provision succeeds: domain registered → DNS verified → SSL active → CDN propagated in under 60 min
2. Idempotency: provision triggered twice for same domain → second run detects existing record and no-ops cleanly
3. Uniqueness: same domain attempted by two tenants simultaneously → exactly one succeeds, other receives DataProcessResult.Failure
4. SSL verification: provisioned cert passes chain validation, not self-signed, not expiring within 30 days
5. CDN propagation: config push confirmed across edge locations before DomainProvisionCompleted emitted
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns verified in generated saga code

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Domain uniqueness enforced globally | — | No |
| MACHINE | SSL before CDN before Gate ordering | — | No |
| MACHINE | Private key encrypted storage | — | No |
| FREEDOM | SSL cert authority | Let's Encrypt | Yes — custom CA for enterprise |
| FREEDOM | DNS verification method | TXT record | Yes — CNAME for enterprise |
| FREEDOM | CDN provider | Config-driven | Yes — swap via FREEDOM config |
| FREEDOM | Max provisioning retry attempts | 3 | Yes — admin configurable |

---

## T194 — SSL Certificate Lifecycle Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** PUBLISHING
**ENTRY:** Fires on CertRenewalScheduled (from F511 scheduler) OR CertExpiring event (< 30 days TTL) OR CertRevoked event
**PURPOSE:** Manage the complete SSL certificate lifecycle beyond initial provision: scheduled renewal before expiry, emergency renewal on near-expiry detection, revocation on domain removal, and chain re-validation after renewal. Ensures zero-downtime cert rotation via queue-ordered CDN config update.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T193 (Domain Provision Gate) — T193 provisions a domain from scratch including initial SSL. T194 manages the ongoing lifecycle of an already-provisioned cert (renewal, rotation, revocation). Different trigger (T193 = on-demand provision; T194 = scheduled/event-driven maintenance) and different factory set (T194 focuses on F511 + F512 rotation; T193 involves F510, F517 additionally).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F511, F512, F514 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F511 ISslCertService | CORE FABRIC (HTTP cert API) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) | Cert renewal + revocation |
| F512 ICdnConfigService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | CDN cert reference update after renewal |
| F514 IPublishGateService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) + QUEUE FABRIC | Validate renewed cert passes gate before CDN update |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates cert renewal pipeline: detect expiry → renew → validate → update CDN → confirm rotation |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: check cert expiry → issue renewal via ACME → validate new cert chain → update CDN config reference → confirm propagation → archive old cert → emit CertRotationCompleted |
| AF-3 | `cert-renewal-lifecycle`, `zero-downtime-rotation`, `cdn-cert-update` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-111 (Custom Domain + SSL Provision), SK-32 (Immutable Snapshot — old cert archived) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for renewal timing logic and zero-downtime rotation sequence |
| AF-6 | Verify no downtime window during cert rotation; verify old cert archived not deleted |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | No cert private key in queue events; renewal must use fresh key pair (no key reuse) |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-194-1 through IR-194-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Renewal success rate + days-before-expiry at renewal + rotation downtime (target: 0s) |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-235 (SSL cert renewal must not overlap active deploy pipeline — F511 renewal vs F513 deploy timing)

### IRON RULES (IR-194-1 through IR-194-8)
1. Cert renewal MUST be initiated at least 30 days before expiry (FREEDOM: configurable threshold, MACHINE: must run)
2. Emergency renewal MUST be triggered at 7-day threshold regardless of schedule
3. New cert MUST be validated (chain check via F511.ValidateCertChainAsync) before CDN reference update
4. CDN cert update MUST be queued (async QUEUE FABRIC) — never synchronous blocking during renewal
5. Old cert MUST be archived (not deleted) for 30 days post-rotation
6. Cert rotation MUST NOT cause a CDN downtime window — new cert active before old cert removed from CDN
7. Revocation MUST trigger CDN config removal within 1 queue processing cycle
8. tenantId MUST be on all cert lifecycle events (DNA-5)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-194-1 through QG-194-6)
1. Renewal: new cert issued, chain-valid, expiry > 89 days from issuance
2. Zero-downtime: traffic monitor shows no SSL errors during cert rotation
3. CDN update: new cert reflected in CDN edge config within 5 min of renewal completion
4. Emergency trigger: cert at 7-day threshold → emergency renewal fires within 1 queue cycle
5. Archive: old cert retrievable from PG for 30 days post-rotation
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Renewal at 30-day threshold mandatory | — | No |
| MACHINE | Emergency renewal at 7-day threshold | — | No |
| MACHINE | Old cert archived not deleted | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Renewal trigger threshold (days) | 30 | Yes — can set earlier |
| FREEDOM | Cert validity period | 90 days (Let's Encrypt) | Yes — varies by CA |

---

## T195 — Deploy Pipeline Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** PUBLISHING
**ENTRY:** Fires on DeployTriggered event (from F513.TriggerDeployAsync) OR PromoteToProductionRequested
**PURPOSE:** Execute the full application deploy pipeline: source artifact collection → lint → test → build → PublishGate pre-checks → production promotion → post-deploy health validation → blue-green cutover → DeployCompleted. Coordinates F513, F514, F553, F556 in a staged, rollback-aware deployment flow.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T86 (Agent Orchestrator, FLOW-08) — T86 orchestrates spec-to-service generation. T195 deploys already-generated artifacts to a live environment. Different domain (code generation vs deployment pipeline). T195 also differs from T193 (provision) — T193 sets up the domain/SSL/CDN once; T195 runs on every deploy.

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F513, F514, F516, F553, F556 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F513 IDeployPipelineService | QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) | Pipeline stage orchestration + logs |
| F514 IPublishGateService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) + QUEUE FABRIC | Pre-production gate checks |
| F516 IRollbackService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Rollback point recording + auto-rollback |
| F553 IDeployHealthService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Post-deploy smoke tests |
| F556 IBlueGreenService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Blue-green slot management + traffic shift |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates deploy pipeline orchestrator: artifact → gate → green slot → health check → traffic shift → promote |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: collect artifacts → run gate (F514) → prepare green slot (F556) → run smoke tests (F553) → shift traffic 10%→50%→100% → record rollback point (F516) → emit DeployCompleted |
| AF-3 | `deploy-pipeline-orchestration`, `blue-green-deploy`, `post-deploy-smoke-test`, `rollback-on-failure` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-112 (Blue-Green Deploy Gate), SK-32 (Immutable Snapshot for rollback), SK-30 (Managed Primitive deploy pattern) |
| AF-5 | 2+ models for pipeline stage sequencing and auto-rollback trigger logic |
| AF-6 | Verify pipeline stages are in correct order; verify smoke test failure triggers rollback |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Artifact integrity verification (hash check), no secrets in build logs, tenant-scoped deploy records |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-195-1 through IR-195-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Deploy success rate + mean time to deploy + rollback rate |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-235 (Deploy pipeline must not run during SSL cert renewal — timing guard)

### IRON RULES (IR-195-1 through IR-195-8)
1. PublishGate (F514) MUST pass before any production traffic shift — gate failure = deploy abort
2. Blue-green traffic shift MUST be gradual (10% → 50% → 100%) with health check between each step
3. Post-deploy smoke tests (F553) MUST run after green slot is live at 10% traffic
4. Failed smoke tests MUST trigger auto-rollback to blue slot via F516 (if auto-rollback enabled)
5. Each pipeline stage MUST emit a queue event on enter AND exit (audit trail + recovery)
6. Rollback point MUST be recorded in F516 before promoting to 100% traffic
7. Build artifacts MUST be hash-verified (content-addressable integrity check)
8. tenantId MUST be on all pipeline run records, stage records, and deploy events (DNA-5)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-195-1 through QG-195-6)
1. Gate enforcement: deploy without passing F514 → blocked at gate, never reaches green slot
2. Blue-green isolation: blue and green slots serve separate traffic pools simultaneously without interference
3. Auto-rollback: smoke test failure at 10% → auto-rollback fires within 1 queue processing cycle
4. Traffic shift: canary at 10% shows healthy metrics → promoted to 50% then 100% with no errors
5. Rollback point integrity: rollback to last good state produces identical running version
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | PublishGate required before production | — | No |
| MACHINE | Smoke test runs after green slot live | — | No |
| MACHINE | Rollback point recorded before 100% shift | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Deploy strategy | blue-green | Yes — rolling or canary per tenant |
| FREEDOM | Smoke test suite | default healthcheck | Yes — custom test suite per app |
| FREEDOM | Auto-rollback | true | Yes — disable for manual-control tenants |
| FREEDOM | Traffic shift increments | 10/50/100 | Yes — configurable percentages |

---

## T196 — Preview Environment Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** PUBLISHING
**ENTRY:** Fires on PreviewSpawnRequested event (from F515.SpawnPreviewAsync) OR BranchPushDetected (from GitHub webhook via F491)
**PURPOSE:** Provision an ephemeral, isolated preview environment for a feature branch: spawn isolated k8s namespace, deploy branch artifacts, generate preview URL, enforce TTL-based auto-termination, and notify requester. Enables "preview before merge" workflow without touching production.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T195 (Deploy Pipeline Gate) — T195 deploys to production with blue-green, gate checks, and rollback. T196 deploys to ephemeral preview slots with no gate checks (intentional — previews are for pre-production review). Different safety model: T196 isolates from production entirely; T195 goes to production.

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F513, F515, F491 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F513 IDeployPipelineService | QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) | Build artifacts for preview |
| F515 IPreviewEnvironmentService | CORE FABRIC (infra) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) | Preview slot lifecycle |
| F491 IGitHubSyncService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) | Branch artifact source |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates preview spawn + deploy + TTL enforcement service |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: receive branch push → build artifacts (F513) → spawn preview slot (F515) → deploy to slot → generate URL → notify requester → schedule auto-terminate |
| AF-3 | `ephemeral-environment-provisioning`, `preview-url-generation`, `ttl-auto-termination` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-30 (Managed Primitive Factory — preview slot as managed primitive), SK-112 (Blue-Green pattern — preview isolation model) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for preview isolation and TTL enforcement logic |
| AF-6 | Verify preview slot is isolated from production; verify TTL enforced |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Preview URL requires auth (no anonymous public access), no production DB access from preview |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-196-1 through IR-196-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Preview spawn time + TTL compliance rate + preview-to-merge conversion rate |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-228 (Preview environment state vs live app state — from P4B, must be separate DB prefix)

### IRON RULES (IR-196-1 through IR-196-8)
1. Preview slot MUST be isolated from production (separate namespace + separate DB prefix)
2. Preview URL MUST require authentication — no anonymous public access
3. Preview MUST auto-terminate after TTL without manual intervention (FREEDOM: TTL configurable; MACHINE: auto-terminate enforced)
4. Max concurrent previews per app MUST be enforced via F515 (FREEDOM: configurable; MACHINE: limit enforced)
5. Preview MUST NOT access production data — only preview-scoped DB seed or empty DB
6. Preview spawn MUST be idempotent — spawn for same branchId twice = return existing preview URL
7. Preview termination MUST clean up ALL resources (namespace, DB prefix, artifacts) — no orphaned infra
8. tenantId + branchId on all preview records (DNA-5)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-196-1 through QG-196-6)
1. Isolation: preview slot cannot read production DB records (query isolation verified)
2. Auth gate: preview URL accessed without token → 401 response
3. TTL enforcement: preview not interacted with for TTL duration → auto-terminated and resources cleaned
4. Idempotency: spawn for same branch twice → same preview URL returned, no duplicate slot
5. Concurrent limit: spawning beyond max concurrent previews → graceful rejection with DataProcessResult.Failure
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Preview isolation from production | — | No |
| MACHINE | Auth required on preview URL | — | No |
| MACHINE | Auto-terminate enforced | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Preview TTL | 24h | Yes — per tenant, max 72h |
| FREEDOM | Max concurrent previews | 3 per app | Yes — per subscription tier |
| FREEDOM | Auto-spawn on branch push | false | Yes — per app workflow |

# FAMILY 69 — ANALYTICS & OBSERVABILITY
# Task Types T197–T200

---

## T197 — Analytics Event Ingestion Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** INGESTION  (reuses FLOW-14 T169 archetype — same push-based immutable capture pattern)
**ENTRY:** Fires on AppAnalyticsEventReceived (from any tenant's generated app DynamicController via QUEUE FABRIC)
**PURPOSE:** Accept high-volume tenant-scoped analytics events, validate against registered schema (F525), deduplicate via Redis SETNX, write to immutable raw analytics zone (F518 ES), and enqueue for metric rollup (F519). Sustains high throughput without blocking the originating app.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T169 (Webhook Ingestion Gate, FLOW-14) — T169 captures events from EXTERNAL systems with HMAC verification. T197 captures events from INTERNAL generated apps with schema validation. Different trust boundary (external untrusted vs internal tenant-scoped), different dedup strategy (HMAC vs schema + SETNX), different storage (FLOW-14 raw warehouse vs analytics raw zone). T197 also differs from billing metering (T191, F506) — analytics events drive dashboards; metering events drive invoicing. Separate pipelines enforced by CF-237.

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F518, F519, F523, F525 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F518 IAnalyticsIngestionService | QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) | Event receive, dedup, raw write |
| F519 IMetricRollupService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) | Rollup queue target after raw write |
| F523 IRetentionPurgeService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | TTL enforcement for raw events |
| F525 ICustomEventSchemaService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Schema validation for incoming events |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates analytics event receiver: consumer group on QUEUE FABRIC → schema validate → Redis dedup → ES raw write → enqueue rollup |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: dequeue event batch → validate schema (F525) → check dedup (F518 Redis SETNX) → write to ES raw (F518) → enqueue to rollup topic (F519) → emit AnalyticsEventLanded |
| AF-3 | `analytics-event-capture`, `immutable-raw-zone`, `redis-dedup-setnx`, `schema-validation-gate` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-92 (Raw Landing Pattern, FLOW-14 — immutable zone), SK-108 (Usage Metering Aggregation — windowed rollup), SK-114 (Analytics Immutable Raw Zone — new in P6C) |
| AF-5 | 2+ models for dedup + schema validation code |
| AF-6 | Verify raw zone immutability (no update/delete operations generated), verify dedup collision correctness |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 (event payload as Dictionary — never typed EventModel), DNA-2, DNA-3, DNA-4, DNA-5 (tenantId required), DNA-6, DNA-7, DNA-8, DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | PII scrub check (F521 integration point for PII-sensitive fields), tenant cross-contamination prevention |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-197-1 through IR-197-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Ingest throughput (target: 50k events/min sustained) + dedup collision rate + schema rejection rate |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-236 (Analytics raw zone must be separate from FLOW-14 DWH raw zone — different ES indices)
CF-237 (Analytics ingestion pipeline must not feed billing metering pipeline)

### IRON RULES (IR-197-1 through IR-197-8)
1. Analytics raw zone MUST be immutable (no updates/deletes after write) — enforced via F518 ES index config (DR-58 pattern)
2. Every event MUST include tenantId (DNA-5) — missing tenantId = immediate DLQ, not retry
3. Deduplication MUST use Redis SETNX with eventId as key (24h window, FREEDOM: configurable)
4. Schema validation MUST use F525 (ICustomEventSchemaService) — never inline schema in generated code
5. F519 rollup enqueue MUST occur AFTER F518 raw write succeeds — write-first ordering
6. Event payload MUST be stored as dict[str, Any] via parse_document (DNA-1) — never typed EventModel
7. Ingestion throughput MUST be sustained via backpressure queue (F555) — never block originating app
8. Retention policy MUST be driven by F523 policy document — never hardcoded TTL in ingestion code

### QUALITY GATES (QG-197-1 through QG-197-6)
1. Immutability: 10k events written → zero update/delete operations generated by AF-1 for raw zone
2. Dedup: same eventId delivered 100× → exactly 1 record in ES raw zone
3. Tenant isolation: eventId from tenant A cannot overwrite tenant B's event (separate dedup keyspace)
4. Schema gate: malformed event (missing required field) → rejected to DLQ; valid event → accepted and stored
5. Throughput: 50k events/min sustained without memory leak or queue depth growth
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns verified

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Raw zone immutable | — | No |
| MACHINE | tenantId required | — | No |
| MACHINE | Rollup enqueue after raw write | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Dedup window | 24h | Yes — per tenant |
| FREEDOM | Event retention TTL | 90 days | Yes — per tenant |
| FREEDOM | Schema validation mode | strict | Yes — lenient per app |
| FREEDOM | Batch size | 1000 events | Yes — admin configurable |

---

## T198 — Metric Rollup Cycle

**ARCHETYPE:** TRANSFORM  (reuses FLOW-14 T171 archetype — multi-step pipeline across data zones)
**ENTRY:** Fires on AnalyticsEventLanded (from T197) OR RollupWindowClosed (scheduler, per window config)
**PURPOSE:** Aggregate raw analytics events into windowed metric buckets (1min, 5min, 1hr, 1day) stored in PostgreSQL, update Redis rolling counters, and emit MetricRollupCompleted for dashboard queries and alerting evaluation. Makes raw events queryable at dashboard speed without scanning raw ES on every request.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T191 (Usage Metering Aggregation, FLOW-15 P4B) — T191 aggregates billing-relevant usage for invoicing. T198 aggregates analytics events for dashboard display. Different destination (T191 → invoicing F507; T198 → dashboard F520), different granularity (T191 per billing period; T198 per time window), and different pipeline (CF-237 explicitly separates them).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F519, F520, F522 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F519 IMetricRollupService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) | Windowed bucket write + rolling counter |
| F520 IDashboardQueryService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) | Cache invalidation after rollup |
| F522 IAlertingService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Alert evaluation trigger on new rollup |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates windowed aggregation worker: consume raw events → compute window buckets → write PG → update Redis counters → invalidate dashboard cache → trigger alert evaluation |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: dequeue from rollup topic → read raw events (F518 ES) → compute 1m/5m/1h/1d buckets → upsert PG rollup tables → HINCRBY Redis counters → invalidate F520 Redis cache for affected metrics → emit MetricRollupCompleted → trigger F522 alert evaluation |
| AF-3 | `windowed-metric-aggregation`, `redis-counter-increment`, `dashboard-cache-invalidation` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-108 (Usage Metering Aggregation — same window pattern), SK-114 (Analytics Immutable Raw Zone), SK-115 (Windowed Metric Rollup — new in P6C) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for window computation and PG upsert idempotency |
| AF-6 | Verify rollup is idempotent (re-run produces same bucket values), verify no billing pipeline contamination |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Tenant scope on all rollup records, no cross-tenant bucket aggregation |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-198-1 through IR-198-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Rollup latency (target: < 30s from event landing to rollup available) + rollup accuracy vs raw count |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-237 (Rollup tables must not aggregate billing metering data — separate PG tables enforced)

### IRON RULES (IR-198-1 through IR-198-8)
1. Rollup MUST NOT read from or write to F506 billing metering tables (CF-237 enforcement)
2. Each rollup window computation MUST be idempotent (reprocessing same window produces same result)
3. Redis counter updates MUST use atomic HINCRBY — no read-modify-write race conditions
4. Dashboard cache (F520 Redis) MUST be invalidated after each rollup completes
5. Alert evaluation (F522) MUST be triggered after rollup completes — never before
6. tenantId MUST be on all rollup records and Redis counter keys (DNA-5)
7. PG rollup tables MUST use upsert semantics (not insert — enables idempotent recompute)
8. Rollup worker MUST acknowledge queue message only after all windows successfully written

### QUALITY GATES (QG-198-1 through QG-198-6)
1. Accuracy: rollup count for 1h window matches raw event count for same period ± 0%
2. Idempotency: rollup triggered twice for same window → same PG values, no duplicates
3. Separation: rollup worker generates zero reads from F506 billing tables
4. Cache invalidation: after rollup, dashboard query returns new data (not stale cache)
5. Latency: 99th percentile rollup time < 30s after AnalyticsEventLanded
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | No billing table reads | — | No |
| MACHINE | Idempotent upsert semantics | — | No |
| MACHINE | Alert evaluation triggered post-rollup | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Rollup windows | 1m, 5m, 1h, 1d | Yes — custom windows per metric |
| FREEDOM | Rollup function | sum, count, avg, p95 | Yes — per metric definition |
| FREEDOM | Retention per window | 7d/30d/1yr | Yes — admin configurable |

---

## T199 — Error Tracking Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** VALIDATION
**ENTRY:** Fires on AppErrorOccurred (from any generated app's error boundary via QUEUE FABRIC)
**PURPOSE:** Capture application errors from generated apps, fingerprint and group them by stack hash, scrub PII before storage, surface to the error tracking index, and notify via alerting. Enables developers to identify and triage errors across their generated apps without requiring custom error infrastructure.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T161 (FLOW-13 validation archetype instance) — T161 validates data pipeline records. T199 captures runtime application errors from generated app code. Different domain (pipeline data quality vs runtime error observability). T199 also differs from T200 (Alerting) — T199 captures and groups errors; T200 evaluates threshold rules across any metric including error rates.

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F521, F522 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F521 IErrorTrackingService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Error capture, fingerprint grouping |
| F522 IAlertingService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Alert on error rate threshold |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates error capture service: receive error event → PII scrub → fingerprint → upsert error group → trigger alert evaluation |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: dequeue AppErrorOccurred → scrub PII (F462 integration) → compute stack fingerprint → upsert ES error group → increment occurrence count → check alert thresholds (F522) → emit ErrorGroupUpdated |
| AF-3 | `error-fingerprinting`, `pii-scrub-before-storage`, `error-grouping` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-01 (MicroserviceBase DataProcessResult error chain — errors as DataProcessResult), SK-114 (Analytics Immutable Raw Zone — error events also immutable) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for fingerprinting algorithm and PII scrub pattern |
| AF-6 | Verify PII scrub fires before storage, verify fingerprint is deterministic |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | PII scrub mandatory before ES write, stack traces must not contain auth tokens |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-199-1 through IR-199-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Error capture latency + grouping accuracy + PII scrub coverage rate |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-236 (Error events stored in analytics ES indices, not FLOW-14 warehouse)

### IRON RULES (IR-199-1 through IR-199-8)
1. PII scrub MUST run before any ES write — no PII in stored error records (F462 integration point)
2. Fingerprinting MUST be deterministic — same error stack always produces same fingerprintId
3. Error grouping MUST use upsert (same fingerprint = increment existing group, not new document)
4. Error rate threshold evaluation MUST use F522 (IAlertingService) — never inline threshold check
5. Error events MUST be stored as dict[str, Any] via parse_document (DNA-1)
6. tenantId MUST be on all error records — no cross-tenant error group aggregation (DNA-5)
7. Error event MUST be acknowledged from queue only after ES write succeeds
8. Error tracking MUST NOT interfere with the originating app's response time (fully async capture)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-199-1 through QG-199-6)
1. PII scrub: 100 error events with known PII patterns → zero PII present in ES stored records
2. Grouping: 50 identical errors sent → exactly 1 error group with occurrence count = 50
3. Determinism: same error payload processed twice → same fingerprintId both times
4. Async: error capture measured to add < 5ms to originating app request path (queue-offloaded)
5. Alert integration: error rate exceeds threshold → F522 alert fires within 1 rollup cycle
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | PII scrub before storage | — | No |
| MACHINE | Deterministic fingerprinting | — | No |
| MACHINE | Fully async capture | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Error grouping algorithm | stack-hash | Yes — message-hash or custom |
| FREEDOM | Error retention | 30 days | Yes — per tenant |
| FREEDOM | Auto-resolve stale groups | 14 days | Yes — per tenant |

---

## T200 — Alerting Evaluation Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** OBSERVABILITY ← NEW ARCHETYPE
**ENTRY:** Fires on MetricRollupCompleted (from T198) OR ErrorGroupUpdated (from T199) OR AlertRuleRegistered (on first evaluation)
**PURPOSE:** Evaluate registered alert rules against the latest metric rollup snapshot, detect threshold breaches, emit AlertFired events (with dedup/cooldown to prevent storm), and route alert notifications through registered channels (Slack F534, email F533, queue events). Provides the reactive observability layer for tenant-operated apps.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T199 (Error Tracking Gate) — T199 captures and groups individual errors. T200 evaluates threshold rules across any metric series (error rate, latency, throughput, custom events) and routes notifications. T200 is triggered by T199's completion event (not by raw errors). Different abstraction level (error capture vs threshold rule engine).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F522, F519, F520, F533, F534 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F522 IAlertingService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Alert rule evaluation + fire/resolve |
| F519 IMetricRollupService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) | Metric values for threshold evaluation |
| F520 IDashboardQueryService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) | Query aggregated metrics for rules |
| F533 IEmailNotificationService | QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Email alert channel |
| F534 ISlackConnectorService | QUEUE FABRIC | Slack alert channel |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates alert evaluation engine: load rules → query latest metrics → threshold check → dedup (cooldown) → route to channels |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: consume MetricRollupCompleted → load active alert rules (F522 ES) → query metric values (F519/F520) → evaluate each rule → if breach: check cooldown (Redis) → emit AlertFired → route to channels (F533/F534 via QUEUE FABRIC) → persist alert history |
| AF-3 | `alert-rule-evaluation`, `threshold-breach-detection`, `alert-dedup-cooldown`, `multi-channel-routing` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-115 (Windowed Metric Rollup — T200 consumes T198's output), SK-121 (Circuit Breaker State Machine — alert cooldown has similar state transitions) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for rule evaluation and cooldown logic |
| AF-6 | Verify cooldown prevents alert storm, verify channel routing is async |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Alert rules must not leak cross-tenant metric data, channel credentials via F427 vault |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-200-1 through IR-200-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Alert latency (from MetricRollupCompleted to AlertFired) + false positive rate + cooldown effectiveness |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-237 (Alert evaluation must not query billing metering tables — only analytics rollup tables)

### IRON RULES (IR-200-1 through IR-200-8)
1. Alert evaluation MUST be async — never block the metric rollup pipeline
2. Alert channel delivery MUST go through QUEUE FABRIC — never synchronous HTTP in evaluation path
3. Alert cooldown MUST be enforced via Redis to prevent notification storms (FREEDOM: cooldown duration configurable)
4. tenantId MUST scope all alert rule queries — no cross-tenant rule evaluation (DNA-5)
5. Alert rules MUST be loaded from F522 (ES) — never hardcoded in evaluation code
6. AlertFired event MUST include: ruleId, metricId, threshold, actual value, tenantId, timestamp
7. Alert resolution MUST be auto-detected (metric returns below threshold) and emit AlertResolved
8. Channel credentials (Slack tokens, SMTP passwords) MUST come from FLOW-14 F427 vault — never config file

### QUALITY GATES (QG-200-1 through QG-200-6)
1. Latency: AlertFired emitted within 60s of MetricRollupCompleted that triggered breach
2. Cooldown: same rule breached 100 times within cooldown window → exactly 1 AlertFired emitted
3. Auto-resolve: metric returns to healthy → AlertResolved emitted within 1 evaluation cycle
4. Cross-tenant: tenant A's alert rule cannot evaluate tenant B's metrics
5. Channel async: Slack/email delivery measured as non-blocking to evaluation pipeline
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Async channel delivery | — | No |
| MACHINE | Cooldown enforced | — | No |
| MACHINE | Channel credentials via vault | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Alert cooldown duration | 5 min | Yes — per rule |
| FREEDOM | Alert channels | Queue event | Yes — Slack, email, webhook |
| FREEDOM | Max alerts per hour | 10 | Yes — per tenant |

---

### FLOW TEMPLATE 40 — analytics-observability-pipeline-v1

```json
{
  "template_id": "analytics-observability-pipeline-v1",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "flow_type": "OBSERVABILITY",
  "description": "End-to-end analytics: event ingestion → rollup → error tracking → alerting",
  "steps": [
    {
      "step_id": "analytics-ingest",
      "task_type": "T197",
      "factory_refs": ["F518", "F519", "F523", "F525"],
      "trigger": "AppAnalyticsEventReceived",
      "on_success": "metric-rollup",
      "on_failure": "ingestion-dlq"
    },
    {
      "step_id": "metric-rollup",
      "task_type": "T198",
      "factory_refs": ["F519", "F520", "F522"],
      "trigger": "AnalyticsEventLanded",
      "on_success": "alert-evaluation",
      "on_failure": "rollup-retry"
    },
    {
      "step_id": "error-tracking",
      "task_type": "T199",
      "factory_refs": ["F521", "F522"],
      "trigger": "AppErrorOccurred",
      "on_success": "error-indexed",
      "on_failure": "error-capture-dlq",
      "parallel": true
    },
    {
      "step_id": "alert-evaluation",
      "task_type": "T200",
      "factory_refs": ["F522", "F519", "F520", "F533", "F534"],
      "trigger": "MetricRollupCompleted OR ErrorGroupUpdated",
      "on_success": "alerts-routed",
      "on_failure": "alert-evaluation-failed"
    }
  ],
  "dna_compliance": ["DNA-1", "DNA-2", "DNA-3", "DNA-4", "DNA-5", "DNA-6", "DNA-7", "DNA-8", "DNA-9"],
  "bfa_rules": ["CF-236", "CF-237"],
  "fabric_dependencies": {
    "DATABASE FABRIC": ["ES", "PG", "Redis"],
    "QUEUE FABRIC": ["Redis Streams"],
    "AI ENGINE FABRIC": []
  }
}
```

# FAMILY 70 — EXTERNAL INTEGRATIONS
# Task Types T201–T205

---

## T201 — Integration Install Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** PROVISIONING  (reuses FLOW-08 T88 archetype — same check-provision-activate pattern)
**ENTRY:** Fires on IntegrationInstallRequested event
**PURPOSE:** Validate the integration is compatible with the tenant's app stack and subscription tier, install it via the catalog (F527), provision any required OAuth flow or webhook endpoint, and activate the integration. Differs from plugin install (T187) — integrations are external SaaS connectors; plugins are sandboxed code add-ons.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T187 (Plugin Installation Gate, FLOW-15 P4B) — T187 provisions sandboxed code plugins with ResourceQuota enforcement. T201 installs external SaaS connectors (Zapier, Drive, Slack) that communicate via OAuth/webhooks — no sandbox needed. Different isolation model: plugins run inside the platform; integrations are external services talking to the platform via APIs.

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F527, F528, F529, F535, F536 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F527 IIntegrationCatalogService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Catalog lookup + install record |
| F528 IOAuthExchangeService | CORE FABRIC (HTTP) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) | OAuth2 PKCE flow initiation |
| F529 IWebhookRelayService | QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Webhook endpoint registration |
| F535 IIntegrationHealthService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Initial health check after install |
| F536 IIntegrationLogService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Audit log for install action |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates integration install flow: catalog check → tier validation → OAuth initiation or webhook registration → health check → activate |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: check catalog (F527) → validate tier + max integrations → initiate OAuth (F528) OR register webhook (F529) → run health check (F535) → log install (F536) → emit IntegrationInstalled |
| AF-3 | `integration-install-flow`, `oauth-initiation`, `webhook-endpoint-registration` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-116 (OAuth2 PKCE Exchange — new in P6C), SK-117 (Integration Webhook Relay + HMAC) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for install flow branching (OAuth path vs webhook path) |
| AF-6 | Verify install is idempotent, verify max integration limit enforced |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | No credentials stored outside F427 vault, no install bypasses tier check |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-201-1 through IR-201-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Install success rate + OAuth completion rate + health check pass rate |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-238 (OAuth tokens must go to FLOW-14 F427 vault, not F528 direct storage)

### IRON RULES (IR-201-1 through IR-201-8)
1. Tier check MUST pass before install — tenant at free tier cannot install enterprise-only integrations
2. Max integrations limit MUST be enforced via F527 — no bypass
3. Install MUST be idempotent — re-installing same integration = return existing install record
4. OAuth tokens MUST be stored in FLOW-14 F427 (ICredentialVaultService) — never in F528 or F527 directly (CF-238)
5. Webhook endpoint registration MUST include HMAC secret generation (F529)
6. Initial health check (F535) MUST run after install — install marked ACTIVE only on health pass
7. Install action MUST be logged in F536 (audit trail)
8. tenantId MUST scope all install records (DNA-5)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-201-1 through QG-201-6)
1. Idempotency: install requested twice → second returns same install record, no duplicate
2. Tier enforcement: free-tier tenant attempts enterprise integration → blocked with DataProcessResult.Failure
3. Token vault: installed OAuth token retrievable from F427, not from F527/F528 tables
4. Health check: integration marked ACTIVE only after F535 health check passes
5. Audit: install event present in F536 log with full metadata
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Token storage via F427 vault | — | No |
| MACHINE | Health check before ACTIVE status | — | No |
| MACHINE | Idempotent install | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Max integrations per tenant | 10 | Yes — per tier |
| FREEDOM | Webhook HMAC algorithm | SHA-256 | Yes — admin configurable |

---

## T202 — OAuth Exchange Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** OAUTH ← NEW ARCHETYPE
**ENTRY:** Fires on OAuthCallbackReceived (from F528 redirect handler) OR TokenRefreshRequired (from F428 FLOW-14 refresh scheduler)
**PURPOSE:** Complete the OAuth2 PKCE authorization code exchange, validate state parameter, store tokens securely in FLOW-14 F427 vault, and emit OAuthConnected. Also handles proactive token refresh before expiry. Reuses FLOW-14's token refresh pattern (F428) for ongoing maintenance.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T201 (Integration Install Gate) — T201 orchestrates the full install including OAuth initiation. T202 handles the specific OAuth callback and code exchange moment — a sub-flow triggered by the external provider's redirect. Different trigger (T201 = install request; T202 = OAuth provider callback). T202 can also fire independently for token refresh without a new install.

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F528, F535, F536 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F528 IOAuthExchangeService | CORE FABRIC (HTTP) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG encrypted) | Code exchange + token storage (delegates to F427) |
| F535 IIntegrationHealthService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Post-exchange health verification |
| F536 IIntegrationLogService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | OAuth event audit log |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates OAuth callback handler + PKCE verifier + token vault delegator + refresh scheduler |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: receive callback → validate state (CSRF check) → verify PKCE code_verifier → exchange code for tokens (F528 CORE FABRIC HTTP) → store tokens in FLOW-14 F427 (via F528) → schedule proactive refresh → run health check (F535) → log event (F536) → emit OAuthConnected |
| AF-3 | `oauth2-pkce-exchange`, `state-csrf-validation`, `proactive-token-refresh-scheduling` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-90 (Connector Auth pattern, FLOW-14 — F427 reuse), SK-116 (OAuth2 PKCE Exchange) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for PKCE validation and token storage delegation pattern |
| AF-6 | Verify state parameter validation is present, verify no token logging |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | State parameter validation (CSRF), PKCE code_verifier required, tokens never in logs or queue events |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-202-1 through IR-202-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | OAuth completion rate + token refresh proactive success rate + CSRF rejection rate |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-238 (OAuth tokens via F427 vault only — separate from FLOW-14 connector credential store for integration tokens)

### IRON RULES (IR-202-1 through IR-202-8)
1. State parameter MUST be validated (exact match to session-stored value) before code exchange — CSRF prevention
2. PKCE MUST be used: code_verifier MUST be sent with token request (implicit flow forbidden)
3. Token exchange MUST occur via CORE FABRIC HTTP client — never importing an OAuth SDK directly
4. Access and refresh tokens MUST be stored in FLOW-14 F427 (ICredentialVaultService) via F528 delegation
5. Tokens MUST NOT appear in queue events, logs, or ES records (only token metadata: expiry, scope)
6. Proactive refresh MUST be scheduled for 90% of token TTL (before expiry, not reactive)
7. F535 health check MUST verify the stored token works before emitting OAuthConnected
8. All OAuth events MUST be logged in F536 (audit trail: connected, refreshed, revoked)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-202-1 through QG-202-6)
1. CSRF: replay of state parameter from different session → rejected
2. PKCE: code exchange without code_verifier → rejected by generated code
3. Token storage: token accessible from F427 vault, not from F528/PG plain table
4. No token leak: queue events and logs scanned → zero token values present
5. Proactive refresh: token at 90% TTL → refresh fires before expiry
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | State parameter validation | — | No |
| MACHINE | PKCE required | — | No |
| MACHINE | Token storage via F427 | — | No |
| MACHINE | Tokens never in logs/events | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Proactive refresh threshold | 90% TTL | Yes — admin configurable |
| FREEDOM | Token scope | integration-specific | Yes — per integration |

---

## T203 — Webhook Relay Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** INGESTION  (reuses FLOW-14 T169 — same external push with HMAC verification)
**ENTRY:** Fires on ExternalWebhookReceived (HTTP POST to tenant's registered webhook endpoint)
**PURPOSE:** Receive external webhook payloads (from Zapier, Stripe callbacks, GitHub events, custom sources), verify HMAC signature, deduplicate, relay to the tenant app's internal event bus via QUEUE FABRIC, and log delivery. Reuses FLOW-14's webhook ingestion pattern but for integration-layer webhooks (not warehouse data ingestion).
**DISTINCT FROM:** T169 (Webhook Ingestion Gate, FLOW-14) — T169 ingests external data into the warehouse raw zone (immutable data store). T203 relays webhook events to the tenant app's queue for real-time processing. Different destination (T169 → warehouse ES; T203 → app QUEUE FABRIC), different purpose (warehouse ingestion vs app event relay), and different consumer (T169 feeds data pipeline; T203 feeds app business logic).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F529, F535, F536, F537 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F529 IWebhookRelayService | QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) | HMAC verify + dedup + relay |
| F535 IIntegrationHealthService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Source health tracking |
| F536 IIntegrationLogService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Delivery audit log |
| F537 IIntegrationRateLimitService | CORE FABRIC (cache) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Rate limit per webhook source |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates webhook receiver: HMAC verify → dedup → rate check → relay to queue → log |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: receive HTTP POST → verify HMAC (F529) → check dedup (Redis SETNX on externalEventId) → check rate limit (F537) → enqueue to app topic (QUEUE FABRIC) → log delivery (F536) → emit WebhookRelayed |
| AF-3 | `webhook-hmac-verification`, `webhook-dedup-setnx`, `rate-limit-guard`, `relay-to-app-queue` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-91 (Webhook Ingestion + HMAC, FLOW-14), SK-117 (Integration Webhook Relay + HMAC — new in P6C) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for HMAC verification and dedup logic |
| AF-6 | Verify HMAC check precedes any processing, verify no internal app URLs leaked to source |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | HMAC mandatory, no webhook source can enumerate internal tenant structure, payload PII scrub |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-203-1 through IR-203-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Relay latency + HMAC rejection rate + dedup collision rate |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-239 (Webhook relay port must not conflict with FLOW-14 F429 IWebhookReceiverService — separate consumer groups)

### IRON RULES (IR-203-1 through IR-203-8)
1. HMAC/signature MUST be verified before ANY processing of payload (CF-239 enforcement)
2. Dedup MUST use Redis SETNX with externalEventId key — same event delivered twice = relay once
3. Rate limit MUST be checked via F537 before relay — rate-exceeded webhooks → 429 response, not DLQ
4. Relay MUST go to QUEUE FABRIC (app topic) — never direct in-process function call to app code
5. Webhook source MUST NOT receive information about internal app structure (response: 200 OK only)
6. Failed relays (app queue unavailable) MUST be retried via DLQ — max 3 attempts
7. Delivery MUST be logged in F536 with externalEventId, source, timestamp, relay status
8. tenantId MUST be on all relay records and queue events (DNA-5)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-203-1 through QG-203-6)
1. HMAC: payload with invalid signature → rejected before any ES write or queue enqueue
2. Dedup: same externalEventId received 100× → exactly 1 relay to app queue
3. Rate limit: source exceeds rate limit → 429 returned, no relay, F536 logs rejection
4. Isolation: webhook handler returns 200 OK for all valid deliveries regardless of internal app state
5. DLQ retry: app queue unavailable → payload queued to DLQ and retried up to 3×
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | HMAC before processing | — | No |
| MACHINE | Relay via QUEUE FABRIC | — | No |
| MACHINE | No internal structure leak to source | — | No |
| FREEDOM | HMAC algorithm | SHA-256 | Yes — per source config |
| FREEDOM | Rate limit | 100 req/min | Yes — per source |
| FREEDOM | DLQ max retries | 3 | Yes — per integration |

---

## T204 — Integration Health Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** VALIDATION
**ENTRY:** Fires on IntegrationHealthCheckScheduled (periodic, from queue scheduler) OR IntegrationDegraded (from F535) OR TokenRefreshFailed (from F528)
**PURPOSE:** Continuously validate that installed integrations are healthy — OAuth tokens are valid, webhook endpoints are reachable, rate limits are not exhausted — and trigger remediation (token refresh, reconnect prompt, or graceful degradation) when health degrades.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T203 (Webhook Relay Gate) — T203 handles live inbound webhook processing. T204 monitors the health of the integration connection itself independently of live traffic. T204 fires on schedule even when no webhooks are flowing.

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F535, F528, F536, F537 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F535 IIntegrationHealthService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | Health check execution + status |
| F528 IOAuthExchangeService | CORE FABRIC (HTTP) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) | Token refresh on health failure |
| F536 IIntegrationLogService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Health event audit log |
| F537 IIntegrationRateLimitService | CORE FABRIC (cache) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Rate limit status check |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates health check worker: probe token validity → probe endpoint reachability → check rate budget → update health status → trigger remediation if degraded |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: schedule health check → probe token (F528 token status) → probe endpoint (CORE FABRIC HTTP HEAD) → check rate budget (F537) → update F535 status → if degraded: attempt token refresh (F528) → log (F536) → emit IntegrationHealthy or IntegrationDegraded |
| AF-3 | `integration-health-probe`, `token-validity-check`, `graceful-degradation` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-117 (Integration Webhook Relay — health is complementary), SK-121 (Circuit Breaker State — health transitions model) |
| AF-5 | 2 models for health check and auto-remediation logic |
| AF-6 | Verify health check does not count against integration rate limit (uses HEAD not GET) |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Health probe must not expose auth tokens in health check response |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-204-1 through IR-204-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Health check reliability + auto-remediation success rate + time-to-detect degradation |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-254 (F537 per-install rate limit must not conflict with FLOW-14 F430 per-connector-type rate limit)

### IRON RULES (IR-204-1 through IR-204-8)
1. Health check MUST run on schedule — at minimum every 5 minutes per installed integration
2. Health probe MUST use HEAD requests where possible — not full GET (rate-limit-friendly)
3. Token expiry detection MUST trigger proactive refresh (F528) — not wait for 401 on live traffic
4. Rate limit exhaustion MUST set integration status to DEGRADED (not FAILED) — different severity
5. Health events MUST be logged in F536
6. Auto-remediation attempts MUST be limited (max 3 auto-retries before MANUAL_REQUIRED status)
7. Health status transitions MUST emit queue events (IntegrationHealthy / IntegrationDegraded / IntegrationFailed)
8. tenantId MUST be on all health records (DNA-5)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-204-1 through QG-204-6)
1. Scheduled probe: health check fires for every active integration within 5-minute window
2. Token expiry: token at 90% TTL detected → proactive refresh fires before expiry
3. Degradation detection: simulate endpoint unreachable → DEGRADED status within 1 probe cycle
4. Auto-remediation: DEGRADED → auto-refresh succeeds → HEALTHY restored without human intervention
5. Rate limit: exhausted rate budget → DEGRADED (not FAILED) with appropriate reason code
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Scheduled health check required | — | No |
| MACHINE | Token expiry → proactive refresh | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Health check interval | 5 min | Yes — per integration |
| FREEDOM | Auto-remediation max retries | 3 | Yes — per tenant |

---

## T205 — External Trigger Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** INTEGRATION
**ENTRY:** Fires on ZapTriggerFired (from F530) OR GDriveFileChanged (from F531) OR SalesforceObjectChanged (from F532)
**PURPOSE:** Handle external integration triggers arriving from connected SaaS systems (Zapier zaps, Drive file changes, Salesforce CRM changes), transform the payload into the tenant app's internal event schema via AI mapping (F530 Zapier), route to the appropriate app workflow, and update integration cursor state.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T203 (Webhook Relay Gate) — T203 handles raw inbound HTTP webhook payloads with HMAC verification. T205 handles structured trigger events from installed integrations that have already been authenticated and catalogued. T205 involves semantic action mapping (AI ENGINE FABRIC for Zapier); T203 is pure relay.

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F530, F531, F532, F536, F537 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F530 IZapierConnectorService | QUEUE FABRIC + AI ENGINE FABRIC | Zap trigger/action semantic mapping |
| F531 IGDriveConnectorService | DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + QUEUE FABRIC | Drive sync cursor + event relay |
| F532 ISalesforceConnectorService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC | CRM object sync events |
| F536 IIntegrationLogService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Trigger event audit log |
| F537 IIntegrationRateLimitService | CORE FABRIC (cache) | Rate guard before external calls |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates trigger handler: source-specific payload normalization → AI mapping (Zapier) → route to app → cursor persist |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: consume trigger event → normalize payload to Dictionary (DNA-1) → if Zapier: AI map to app action (F530 via AI ENGINE FABRIC) → if Drive/SF: direct route → check rate (F537) → enqueue to app topic (QUEUE FABRIC) → persist cursor (F531/F532) → log (F536) |
| AF-3 | `integration-trigger-normalization`, `ai-action-mapping`, `sync-cursor-persist` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-116 (OAuth2 PKCE — trigger auth), SK-117 (Webhook Relay), SK-89 (Incremental Cursor — cursor state for Drive/SF) |
| AF-5 | 2+ models for AI action mapping quality (Zapier semantic mapping) |
| AF-6 | Verify cursor persisted before advancing, verify AI mapping uses fabric not direct provider |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Rate limit before every external API call, no cross-tenant trigger routing |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-205-1 through IR-205-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Trigger relay latency + AI mapping accuracy + cursor persistence reliability |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-239 (External trigger port must not conflict with FLOW-14 F435 webhook ingestion consumer groups)

### IRON RULES (IR-205-1 through IR-205-8)
1. Rate limit MUST be checked via F537 before any external API call (FLOW-14 F430 pattern)
2. Trigger payload MUST be normalized to dict[str, Any] via parse_document (DNA-1)
3. AI action mapping MUST use AI ENGINE FABRIC IAiProvider.GenerateAsync() — never direct LLM SDK
4. Sync cursor (Drive/Salesforce) MUST be persisted before advancing to next batch (EP-4 pattern)
5. Trigger routing MUST go to QUEUE FABRIC — never direct in-process function call
6. Each trigger source MUST have its own rate limit counter (F537 per-source keying)
7. Trigger log (F536) MUST capture source, normalized payload hash, routing destination, timestamp
8. tenantId MUST be on all trigger records (DNA-5)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-205-1 through QG-205-6)
1. Rate limit: trigger source exceeding rate limit → blocked before external call, F536 logged
2. Cursor durability: process killed mid-batch → on restart, cursor at last persisted position (no data loss or duplication)
3. AI mapping: Zapier action description → correct internal event type in >90% of test cases
4. Normalization: all trigger payloads stored as Dictionary — zero typed trigger model classes generated
5. Queue relay: trigger enqueued to app topic, not executed synchronously
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | Rate limit before external calls | — | No |
| MACHINE | Payload as Dictionary | — | No |
| MACHINE | Cursor persist before advance | — | No |
| FREEDOM | AI mapping model | config-driven (IAiProvider) | Yes — per tenant |
| FREEDOM | Trigger rate limit | 100/hr | Yes — per tier |
| FREEDOM | Action timeout | 30s | Yes — per action |

{

    "QUEUE FABRIC": ["Redis Streams"],
    "CORE FABRIC": ["HTTP client"],
    "AI ENGINE FABRIC": ["IAiProvider"]
  }
}

# FAMILY 71 — AI ADD-ONS & INTELLIGENT COMPONENTS
# Task Types T206–T210

---

## T206 — AI Chatbot Provision Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** AI_ADDON ← NEW ARCHETYPE
**ENTRY:** Fires on AiChatbotProvisionRequested event
**PURPOSE:** Provision a tenant-scoped AI chatbot instance for a generated app: spawn chatbot config (F539), initialize conversation context with app knowledge (F540 RAG index), provision sandbox (F547), configure FREEDOM parameters (system prompt, model, context strategy), and verify end-to-end inference works before emitting ChatbotProvisioned.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T187 (Plugin Installation Gate, FLOW-15 P4B) — T187 provisions sandboxed code plugins with CPU/memory quotas. T206 provisions a conversational AI component that calls out to AI ENGINE FABRIC (external LLM, not sandboxed code). Different isolation model: T187 = code sandbox; T206 = AI session + RAG context scope. T206 also differs from T83/T85 (FLOW-08 Deep Research) — T83/T85 generate app specs via research; T206 provisions a chatbot as a drop-in component into an already-running generated app.

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F538, F539, F540, F544, F546, F547 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F538 IAiAddonRegistryService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC | Add-on catalog + compatibility check |
| F539 IChatbotProvisionService | AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) | Chatbot instance creation |
| F540 IChatbotContextService | RAG FABRIC (IRagService) + DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) | App knowledge indexing + context strategy |
| F544 IAiAddonConfigService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | FREEDOM config per app |
| F546 IAiUsageTrackingService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) | Token usage tracking → billing |
| F547 IAiAddonSandboxService | CORE FABRIC (ResourceQuota) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) | Sandbox resource quota |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates chatbot provisioning orchestrator: catalog check → sandbox spawn → RAG index → config apply → end-to-end test |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: check catalog + compatibility (F538) → provision sandbox (F547) → create chatbot instance (F539) → index app knowledge into RAG (F540) → apply FREEDOM config (F544) → send test inference → verify token usage tracked (F546) → emit ChatbotProvisioned |
| AF-3 | `chatbot-provisioning`, `rag-knowledge-indexing`, `ai-addon-sandbox-quota`, `end-to-end-inference-test` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-118 (AI Chatbot Context Chain — new in P6C), SK-119 (AI Add-on Sandbox Quota), SK-30 (Managed Primitive Factory — chatbot as managed component) |
| AF-5 | 2+ models for chatbot config generation and RAG strategy selection |
| AF-6 | Verify all LLM calls via IAiProvider.GenerateAsync(), verify no direct openai.chat() generated |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | Tenant isolation in RAG context (no cross-tenant knowledge), token tracking enforced |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-206-1 through IR-206-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Chatbot provision success rate + RAG index accuracy + first-inference latency |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-240 (AI token usage F546 must feed billing F506 — not bypass metering pipeline)
CF-241 (AI add-on sandbox F547 must be separate enforcement from plugin sandbox F498)

### IRON RULES (IR-206-1 through IR-206-8)
1. ALL LLM calls MUST go through AI ENGINE FABRIC IAiProvider.GenerateAsync() — never openai.chat() or any SDK directly
2. RAG context MUST be tenant-scoped (IRagService.SearchAsync() with tenantId filter) — no cross-tenant knowledge access
3. Token usage MUST be emitted to F546 after every inference — no inference without usage tracking (CF-240)
4. Sandbox MUST be provisioned via F547 — separate from plugin sandbox F498 (CF-241)
5. App knowledge indexing MUST complete before ChatbotProvisioned event — no live chatbot without RAG index
6. Chatbot config MUST be stored as dict[str, Any] in F544 (DNA-1) — no typed ChatbotConfig model
7. Provisioning MUST be idempotent — re-provision same chatbot = return existing instance
8. tenantId MUST be on all chatbot records, RAG index scope, and usage tracking events (DNA-5)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-206-1 through QG-206-6)
1. Fabric compliance: generated provisioning code shows zero direct openai.chat() or SDK imports
2. RAG isolation: chatbot for tenant A cannot retrieve knowledge indexed for tenant B
3. Token tracking: test inference produces usage record in F546 with correct tokenCount and modelId
4. Sandbox separation: F547 sandbox quota enforced independently from F498 plugin sandbox (separate counters)
5. Idempotency: provision twice → second invocation returns existing chatbot record, no duplicate
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | LLM via AI ENGINE FABRIC only | — | No |
| MACHINE | Token usage tracked per inference | — | No |
| MACHINE | RAG tenant isolation | — | No |
| FREEDOM | LLM model | config-driven | Yes — per chatbot |
| FREEDOM | System prompt | default onboarding | Yes — per app |
| FREEDOM | RAG strategy | Tiered | Yes — FREEDOM config |
| FREEDOM | Max context window | 8k tokens | Yes — per tier |

## T207 — Prediction Add-on Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** AI_ADDON
**ENTRY:** Fires on PredictionAddonInstallRequested OR PredictionRunRequested (async, from app workflow)
**PURPOSE:** Register a prediction model configuration for a generated app (what to predict, which features to use, which AI model), run predictions on demand via AiDispatcher (multi-model if configured), store results, and track token usage. Supports batch prediction for bulk inference jobs.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T206 (AI Chatbot Provision Gate) — T206 provisions a conversational chatbot with ongoing session context. T207 provisions a stateless prediction component that answers point-in-time questions from structured input. Different interaction model (chat session vs request-response prediction) and different RAG usage (T206 uses RAG for conversational context; T207 uses RAG for feature lookup if needed).

### FACTORY DEPENDENCIES
F538, F541, F544, F546, F547 — resolved via CreateAsync()

### FABRIC RESOLUTION
| Factory | Fabric | Provider | Purpose |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| F538 IAiAddonRegistryService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC | Model catalog + compatibility |
| F541 IPredictionModelService | AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Prediction inference |
| F544 IAiAddonConfigService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES) | Model FREEDOM config |
| F546 IAiUsageTrackingService | DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) | Token usage → billing |
| F547 IAiAddonSandboxService | CORE FABRIC (ResourceQuota) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) | Sandbox quota enforcement |

### AF CONFIGURATION
| Station | Role |
|---------|------|
| AF-1 | Generates prediction model orchestrator: register config → run inference via AiDispatcher → store result → track usage |
| AF-2 | Decomposes: validate model config (F538) → apply FREEDOM config (F544) → provision sandbox (F547) → run inference via AiDispatcher (F541) → store result as Dictionary (DNA-1) → track tokens (F546) → emit PredictionCompleted |
| AF-3 | `prediction-model-config`, `multi-model-inference-dispatch`, `batch-prediction-pipeline` |
| AF-4 | Retrieves SK-43 (Multi-Strategy RAG, FLOW-09 — prediction may use RAG for feature lookup), SK-118 (AI Chatbot Context Chain — similar dispatch pattern), SK-119 (AI Add-on Sandbox Quota) |
| AF-5 | 2+ models via AiDispatcher for prediction quality (consensus or best-of) |
| AF-6 | Verify AiDispatcher used for multi-model, verify results as Dictionary not typed PredictionResult |
| AF-7 | DNA-1 through DNA-9 |
| AF-8 | No PII in prediction inputs stored without consent, tenant isolation on all prediction records |
| AF-9 | Validates IR-207-1 through IR-207-8 |
| AF-10 | Merge |
| AF-11 | Prediction latency + accuracy (compared to baseline) + token cost per prediction |

### BFA VALIDATION
CF-240 (Token usage F546 must feed billing F506)
CF-241 (Sandbox F547 separate from plugin sandbox F498)

### IRON RULES (IR-207-1 through IR-207-8)
1. Prediction inference MUST use AiDispatcher (multi-model) when configured — never single-provider hardcode
2. Prediction results MUST be stored as dict[str, Any] (DNA-1) — never typed PredictionResult class
3. Token usage MUST be emitted to F546 after every inference (CF-240)
4. Batch predictions MUST be chunked and queued via QUEUE FABRIC — never blocking large batch in memory
5. Model config MUST be stored in F544 (ES FREEDOM config) — never hardcoded in prediction code
6. Sandbox quota MUST be enforced via F547 (separate from F498) (CF-241)
7. Prediction results MUST include confidence interval in metadata (DataProcessResult metadata field)
8. tenantId MUST be on all prediction records, model config, and usage events (DNA-5)

### QUALITY GATES (QG-207-1 through QG-207-6)
1. Multi-model: prediction run with AiDispatcher config → 2+ model outputs aggregated
2. No typed models: generated code shows dict[str, Any] for all prediction results
3. Batch chunking: batch of 10k predictions → queued in chunks, not loaded into memory
4. Confidence: prediction result includes confidence_interval metadata field
5. Token tracking: inference produces F546 record with tokenCount, modelId, tenantId
6. DNA compliance: all 9 patterns pass

### MACHINE/FREEDOM
| Layer | Parameter | Default | Configurable |
|-------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| MACHINE | AiDispatcher for multi-model | — | No |
| MACHINE | Token usage tracked | — | No |
| MACHINE | Results as Dictionary | — | No |
| FREEDOM | Model selection | config-driven | Yes — per model config |
| FREEDOM | Multi-model mode | off | Yes — consensus or best-of |
| FREEDOM | Batch chunk size | 100 | Yes — admin configurable |
