```
  QG-263-3: All participants converge to same state within 2s
```

## TASK TYPE: T264 — Collaboration Permission Gate
```
ARCHETYPE: COLLABORATION
ENTRY: Canvas owner modifies sharing settings
PURPOSE: RBAC management — grant/revoke access, role assignment (owner/editor/viewer/commenter)
DISTINCT FROM: T262 (session join) — T264 handles permission CRUD independent of sessions

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F672, F671, F673 — all via CreateAsync()
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F672 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG + Redis) for permission records + cache
  F671 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC for comment permissions
  F673 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) for permission change audit

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis: generate RBAC service for collaborative canvases
  AF-2 Planning: validate-owner → modify-permissions → invalidate-cache → notify → audit
  AF-3 Prompt Library: "rbac-management", "permission-patterns"
  AF-4 RAG: SK-159 (Collaboration Patterns)
  AF-5 Multi-model: parallel RBAC model evaluation
  AF-6 Code review: permission escalation prevention
  AF-7 Compliance: DNA-1, DNA-2, DNA-5
  AF-8 Security: privilege escalation, permission bypass, unauthorized access
  AF-9 Judge: validates — no self-promotion, owner cannot be removed, permissions immediately effective
  AF-10 Merge: select RBAC implementation
  AF-11 Feedback: permission change frequency, access violation attempts

BFA VALIDATION: CF-324 (owner cannot be removed), CF-325 (permission check on every operation)
MACHINE: Owner role cannot be removed (only transferred). Permission cache invalidated synchronously.
FREEDOM: Default role for invited users, permission inheritance, guest access policy
IRON RULES:
  IR-264-1: Owner cannot remove own owner role — only transfer
  IR-264-2: Permission changes take effect IMMEDIATELY (cache invalidated)
  IR-264-3: Every canvas operation checks permission BEFORE execution
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-264-1: Permission grant/revoke < 500ms
  QG-264-2: Cache invalidation < 100ms
  QG-264-3: Zero unauthorized access after permission revocation
```

## TASK TYPE: T265 — Marketplace Publish Gate
```
ARCHETYPE: VISUAL_CREATION
ENTRY: User submits tested flow to marketplace
PURPOSE: Publish flow to marketplace after quality validation — categorize, license, list
DISTINCT FROM: T258 (test report) — T265 handles marketplace-specific listing

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F674, F675, F677, F678, F680 — all via CreateAsync()
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F674 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + RAG FABRIC for listing and recommendation
  F675 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) for ratings initialization
  F677 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) for category assignment
  F678 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + QUEUE FABRIC for license assignment
  F680 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) for analytics initialization

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis: generate marketplace publish service
  AF-2 Planning: validate-test-pass → assign-category → assign-license → create-listing → init-analytics
  AF-3 Prompt Library: "marketplace-publishing", "flow-categorization"
  AF-4 RAG: SK-134 (Idempotent Publish Pattern)
  AF-5 Multi-model: parallel category suggestion
  AF-6 Code review: listing integrity
  AF-7 Compliance: DNA-1, DNA-2, DNA-5
  AF-8 Security: malicious flow listing, license circumvention
  AF-9 Judge: validates — flow passed all quality gates, category appropriate, license valid
  AF-10 Merge: select categorization
  AF-11 Feedback: marketplace adoption, listing quality scores

BFA VALIDATION: CF-326 (test report required before listing), CF-327 (unique listing per flow version)
MACHINE: Flow MUST have passing test report to list. Listing is idempotent per version.
FREEDOM: Approval required before listing, featured slots, category selection
IRON RULES:
  IR-265-1: No marketplace listing without passing test report (T258)
  IR-265-2: Listing includes flow hash for integrity verification
  IR-265-3: License terms immutable after publication
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-265-1: Listing creation < 3s
  QG-265-2: Category suggestion accuracy > 80%
  QG-265-3: Flow immediately searchable after listing
```

## TASK TYPE: T266 — Marketplace Import & Fork Gate
```
ARCHETYPE: VISUAL_CREATION
ENTRY: User imports or forks a marketplace flow
PURPOSE: Import flow into tenant scope, fork with lineage tracking, resolve conflicts
DISTINCT FROM: T265 (publish) — T266 is the consumer-side of marketplace

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F676, F679, F678, F650 — all via CreateAsync()
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F676 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + CORE FABRIC for package import
  F679 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG) for fork with lineage
  F678 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) for license validation
  F650 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC for BFA registration of imported flow

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis: generate import/fork service with conflict resolution
  AF-2 Planning: validate-license → import-package → resolve-conflicts → register-BFA → fork-lineage
  AF-3 Prompt Library: "flow-import", "fork-lineage"
  AF-4 RAG: SK-134 (Idempotent Publish Pattern — reverse direction)
  AF-5 Multi-model: parallel conflict resolution strategies
  AF-6 Code review: import integrity
  AF-7 Compliance: DNA-1, DNA-5 (tenant scoping on import)
  AF-8 Security: malicious flow import, supply chain attack
  AF-9 Judge: validates — license valid, conflicts resolved, BFA registered, lineage tracked
  AF-10 Merge: select import strategy
  AF-11 Feedback: import success rate, conflict frequency

BFA VALIDATION: CF-328 (imported flow must register in BFA)
MACHINE: Imported flow gets new factory IDs (never reuse marketplace IDs in tenant scope).
FREEDOM: Auto-resolve conflicts or manual, fork upstream sync policy
IRON RULES:
  IR-266-1: Imported flow gets tenant-scoped factory IDs — never reuse marketplace IDs
  IR-266-2: License validated BEFORE import begins
  IR-266-3: Lineage tracking immutable — cannot hide fork origin
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-266-1: Import < 10s for typical flow
  QG-266-2: All factory dependencies resolvable after import
  QG-266-3: BFA check passes for imported flow
```

## TASK TYPE: T267 — Promotion Pipeline Advance Gate
```
ARCHETYPE: CODE_INJECTION
ENTRY: Flow passes current stage requirements in promotion pipeline
PURPOSE: Advance flow through GENERATED→TESTED→INJECTED→MINIMAL→CORE ladder
DISTINCT FROM: T253 (code injection — one-time) — T267 manages ongoing promotion lifecycle

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F681, F682, F683, F684, F686 — all via CreateAsync()
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F681 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + QUEUE FABRIC for promotion state machine
  F682 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG + Redis) for feature flag management
  F683 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (ES) for canary deployment
  F684 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) for approval workflow
  F686 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC for health monitoring

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis: generate promotion pipeline service with state machine
  AF-2 Planning: check-stage-requirements → request-approval → advance-stage → update-flags → monitor
  AF-3 Prompt Library: "promotion-pipeline", "canary-deployment"
  AF-4 RAG: SK-150 (Code Injection Patterns — promotion subset)
  AF-5 Multi-model: parallel health assessment
  AF-6 Code review: state machine correctness
  AF-7 Compliance: DNA-1, DNA-3, DNA-5, DNA-7, DNA-8
  AF-8 Security: unauthorized promotion, approval bypass
  AF-9 Judge: validates — all stage requirements met, approval obtained, health checks pass
  AF-10 Merge: select promotion strategy
  AF-11 Feedback: promotion success rate, time-in-stage metrics

BFA VALIDATION: CF-315 (quality gate required), CF-307 (feature flag required)
MACHINE: TESTED→INJECTED requires sandbox pass. INJECTED→MINIMAL requires canary success. MINIMAL→CORE requires approval.
FREEDOM: Auto-promote thresholds, canary percentage, cooldown between stages, approval chain
IRON RULES:
  IR-267-1: Promotion is forward-only — no stage skipping
  IR-267-2: MINIMAL→CORE requires explicit human approval
  IR-267-3: Canary health check MUST pass before traffic increase
  IR-267-4: Promotion events via transactional outbox (DNA-8)
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-267-1: Stage advancement < 30s
  QG-267-2: Canary health check accuracy > 99%
  QG-267-3: Rollback available at every stage
```

## TASK TYPE: T268 — Rollback & Health Gate
```
ARCHETYPE: CODE_INJECTION
ENTRY: Health monitor detects degradation OR user triggers manual rollback
PURPOSE: Instant rollback of promoted flow, health monitoring, incident tracking
DISTINCT FROM: T267 (promotion advance) — T268 handles the reverse direction

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F685, F686, F687, F681, F651 — all via CreateAsync()
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F685 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG) for rollback execution
  F686 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC for health metrics
  F687 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + QUEUE FABRIC for core demotion
  F681 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) for promotion state update
  F651 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) for rollback audit

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis: generate rollback service with health-triggered automation
  AF-2 Planning: detect-degradation → evaluate-severity → execute-rollback → verify → audit
  AF-3 Prompt Library: "rollback-execution", "health-monitoring"
  AF-4 RAG: SK-150 (Code Injection Patterns — rollback subset)
  AF-5 Multi-model: parallel health assessment for accurate degradation detection
  AF-6 Code review: rollback atomicity
  AF-7 Compliance: DNA-1, DNA-3, DNA-5
  AF-8 Security: unauthorized rollback, DDoS triggering false health alerts
  AF-9 Judge: validates — rollback is atomic, previous version restored, traffic rerouted
  AF-10 Merge: select rollback strategy
  AF-11 Feedback: rollback speed, false alert rate, recovery time

BFA VALIDATION: CF-307 (rollback must restore feature flag state)
MACHINE: Rollback MUST complete in < 2s. Previous version MUST be fully operational after rollback.
FREEDOM: Auto-rollback triggers (error rate threshold, latency threshold), notification channels
IRON RULES:
  IR-268-1: Rollback is ATOMIC — full restore or no change
  IR-268-2: Rollback MUST complete in < 2s
  IR-268-3: Previous version health verified BEFORE rollback considered complete
  IR-268-4: Rollback audit trail immutable
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-268-1: Rollback execution < 2s
  QG-268-2: Zero traffic to failed version after rollback
  QG-268-3: Previous version health check passes within 5s of rollback
```

## TASK TYPE: T269 — Component Descriptor Ingestion Gate
```
ARCHETYPE: CATALOG_INGESTION (NEW)
ENTRY: Fires on component.descriptor_changed event (Git webhook via F697)
PURPOSE: Ingest, validate, and publish component descriptor from Git to ES catalog.
         Builds dependency edges. Emits component.profile_validated event.
DISTINCT FROM: T169 (INGESTION archetype handles external push events — webhooks from
               3rd-party APIs). T269 handles internal Git descriptor ingestion with
               full schema validation + dependency graph update.
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F697, F698, F699, F700 — resolved via CreateAsync()
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F697 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES catalog index) + QUEUE FABRIC (events)
  F698 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG profile metadata)
  F699 → DATABASE FABRIC (Neo4j graph) + RAG FABRIC (similarity matching)
  F700 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES full-text search)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generate ingestion service from descriptor schema spec
  AF-2 (Planning): decompose into parse → validate → upsert → graph-update → publish
  AF-4 (RAG): search SK-161 (catalog ingestion patterns) and SK-162 (dependency graph)
  AF-6 (Code Review): verify no typed descriptor models (DNA-1), build_search_filter (DNA-2)
  AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-1 through DNA-9 scan
  AF-8 (Security): no secret values in descriptor, no PII in component profiles
  AF-9 (Judge): validate dedup key format, graph edge integrity, ES mapping compliance
  AF-11 (Feedback): store ingestion quality score for catalog completeness tracking
BFA VALIDATION: CF-329 (descriptor before environment), CF-330 (graph before promotion)
MACHINE (fixed):
  - Dedup key = {repo}@{sha}:{descriptor_path}
  - Profile version = semver from descriptor.version field
  - Dependency edges derived from descriptor.dependencies section
  - Published event always emitted (even for StoredInvalid — validation errors included)
FREEDOM (configurable):
  - Descriptor schema version (v1, v2 — backward compatible)
  - Validation strictness (warn vs block on missing fields)
  - Graph traversal depth for impact analysis
IRON RULES:
  IR-269-1: NEVER store typed descriptor models — dict[str, Any] only (DNA-1)
  IR-269-2: NEVER advance catalog state without dedup key check — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-269-3: NEVER skip dependency graph update on valid descriptor — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-269-4: NEVER store secret values found in descriptor content — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-269-5: NEVER skip tenantId scope on ES catalog writes (DNA-5) — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-269-6: NEVER emit profile_validated with incomplete profile version — BUILD FAILURE
QUALITY GATES (AF-9 checks):
  QG-269-1: Descriptor parses to Dictionary without type casting
  QG-269-2: All required profile fields present (owner, criticality, endpoints, auth)
  QG-269-3: Dependency edges reference valid component IDs
  QG-269-4: ES write confirmed with DataProcessResult.Success
  QG-269-5: Dedup table confirms no duplicate processing of same (repo,sha,path)
  QG-269-6: component.profile_validated event emitted with correct payload
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T270 — Dependency Graph Refresh Cycle
```
ARCHETYPE: MODELING
ENTRY: Fires on schedule (configurable interval) or on component.profile_validated event
PURPOSE: Recompute full dependency graph from all active component profiles.
         Detects orphaned components, circular dependencies, critical path changes.
DISTINCT FROM: T269 (T269 handles single-descriptor ingestion; T270 handles
               full-graph recomputation for consistency and health checks).
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F698, F699, F700 — resolved via CreateAsync()
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F698 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG profile metadata source)
  F699 → DATABASE FABRIC (Neo4j graph rebuild target)
  F700 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES — orphan/circular detection queries)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-2 (Planning): decompose into load → diff → rebuild → validate → report
  AF-4 (RAG): search SK-162 (graph refresh patterns)
  AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-5 scope isolation on all graph operations
  AF-9 (Judge): validate graph acyclicity, critical path validity
BFA VALIDATION: CF-330 (graph must be consistent before promotion gates)
IRON RULES:
  IR-270-1: NEVER mutate graph without diff + validation first — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-270-2: NEVER skip circular dependency detection — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-270-3: ALL graph nodes must have tenantId scope (DNA-5) — BUILD FAILURE
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-270-1: Graph refresh completes with DataProcessResult.Success
  QG-270-2: No circular dependencies detected (or explicit override with CF-330 bypass)
  QG-270-3: Orphan count delta reported and alertable
  QG-270-4: Critical path computation result cached for promotion gate use
```

## TASK TYPE: T271 — Environment Request Gate
```
ARCHETYPE: ENV_PROVISIONING (NEW)
ENTRY: Fires on env.requested event (from CI pipeline webhook via F710, or manual trigger)
PURPOSE: Validate environment request, check policy (CF-343 sensitive routing),
         reserve idempotency key, emit env.provision_step.queued to start saga.
DISTINCT FROM: T272 (T271 is the gate/validation; T272 is the durable saga execution).
               T271 runs in seconds; T272 may run for minutes.
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F701, F705, F707, F708 — resolved via CreateAsync()
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F701 → CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG env state)
  F705 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG config layers + Redis cache)
  F707 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (policy reasoning) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES policy store)
  F708 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG config snapshots)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-2 (Planning): validate → policy-check → idempotency-reserve → enqueue
  AF-4 (RAG): search SK-163 (environment validation patterns)
  AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-5 tenantId, DNA-3 DataProcessResult
  AF-9 (Judge): validate policy decision completeness, idempotency key uniqueness
BFA VALIDATION: CF-331 (config-before-provisioning), CF-343 (sensitive routing)
MACHINE: Idempotency key = (env_type, pr_number) or request UUID
FREEDOM: Policy strictness, allowed env profiles per tenant tier
IRON RULES:
  IR-271-1: NEVER proceed without PolicyDecision.Allow for sensitive profiles — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-271-2: NEVER provision without valid ConfigBundle — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-271-3: NEVER skip idempotency key reservation — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-271-4: local-sensitive profile MUST enforce zero-egress NetworkPolicy (CF-343) — BUILD FAILURE
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-271-1: PolicyDecision contains non-empty obligations list
  QG-271-2: ConfigBundle has all required keys for env profile
  QG-271-3: Idempotency key stored before env.provision_step.queued emitted
  QG-271-4: Environment state = Validating before gate exits
```

## TASK TYPE: T272 — IaC Provision Saga
```
ARCHETYPE: DURABLE_SAGA
ENTRY: Fires on env.provision_step.queued event
PURPOSE: Execute full infrastructure provisioning lifecycle:
         plan → apply → deploy → verify resources. Persist StepRun before every
         side effect (EP-4). Register compensation (destroy) plan before apply.
         Enforce concurrency limit per cloud account/region (CF-329).
DISTINCT FROM: T168 (FLOW-14 DURABLE_SAGA handles incremental sync; T272 handles
               infrastructure provisioning with IaC compensation semantics).
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F701, F702, F703, F713 — resolved via CreateAsync()
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F701 → CORE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG state)
  F702 → CORE FABRIC (HTTP — IaC runner) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG IaC outputs)
  F703 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (Redis TTL + PG instance)
  F713 → CORE FABRIC (HTTP — GitOps sync)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generate IaC wrapper service from provider spec
  AF-2 (Planning): plan → validate-plan → store-compensation → apply → gitops-sync → health-check
  AF-4 (RAG): search SK-164 (IaC saga patterns), SK-165 (compensation design)
  AF-6 (Code Review): verify no direct Terraform/Pulumi SDK imports (DR-113)
  AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-3 DataProcessResult, DNA-4 MicroserviceBase, DNA-5 scope
  AF-9 (Judge): validate compensation plan exists before apply, state persisted
MACHINE: State machine: Provisioning → Deploying → Testing → Ready | Failed → Deleting
IRON RULES:
  IR-272-1: NEVER apply IaC without storing compensation plan first (EP-4 pattern) — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-272-2: NEVER advance state without persisting StepRun — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-272-3: NEVER exceed concurrency limit per cloud account (CF-329) — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-272-4: NEVER import Terraform/Pulumi/Crossplane SDK directly (DR-113) — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-272-5: Failed state MUST trigger compensation cleanup — BUILD FAILURE if skipped
  IR-272-6: ALL operations include tenantId scope (DNA-5) — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-272-7: NEVER store secret values in IaC outputs — BUILD FAILURE
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-272-1: Compensation plan serialized and stored before Apply call
  QG-272-2: All StepRuns have output_json before next step starts
  QG-272-3: GitOps sync confirms reconciled state before Testing phase
  QG-272-4: Environment reaches Ready within configurable timeout
  QG-272-5: Failed environments trigger compensation and reach Deleted
  QG-272-6: Idempotency verified — re-run of same flow_instance_id is safe
```

```
ARCHETYPE: VALIDATION
ENTRY: Fires on env.ttl_expired event (from Redis TTL expiry via F703)
PURPOSE: Validate TTL expiry authenticity, transition env to Expiring state,
         trigger cleanup saga (compensation via F702). Prevent orphaned resources.

  AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-5 scope, DNA-3 result
  AF-9 (Judge): verify env not already Deleted before processing expiry
IRON RULES:
  IR-273-1: NEVER delete already-Deleted environment — idempotent cleanup only
  IR-273-2: NEVER skip cleanup on TTL expiry — BUILD FAILURE (resource leak)

QUALITY GATES:
```

## TASK TYPE: T274 — Config Layer Resolution Gate
```
ARCHETYPE: VALIDATION
ENTRY: Fires before any deployment step that requires resolved configuration
PURPOSE: Merge config layers (global → tier → tenant → env), validate all required
         keys present, validate all secret references exist in vault (F706),
         cache resolved ConfigBundle, snapshot config version for this deployment.
DISTINCT FROM: T275 (T274 is config resolution; T275 is policy evaluation).
               T274 always precedes T275 in pipeline sequence.
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F705, F706, F708 — resolved via CreateAsync()
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F705 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG layers + Redis cache)
  F706 → CORE FABRIC (HTTP — vault existence check)
  F708 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG config snapshots)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-2 (Planning): layer-merge → key-validate → secret-ref-validate → cache → snapshot
  AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-5 tenantId, DNA-3 DataProcessResult
  AF-9 (Judge): all required keys present, all secret refs verified
IRON RULES:
  IR-274-1: NEVER store secret VALUES in ConfigBundle — BUILD FAILURE (DR-116)
  IR-274-2: NEVER proceed with missing required keys — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-274-3: NEVER skip secret reference existence check — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-274-4: Config snapshot MUST be stored before deployment step begins — BUILD FAILURE
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-274-1: ConfigBundle contains no secret values — only reference paths
  QG-274-2: All required keys resolved for target env profile
  QG-274-3: All secret references return "exists" from vault check
  QG-274-4: Config version stored and linked to FlowInstance
  QG-274-5: Cache invalidation verified before resolution (not stale)
```

```
QUALITY GATES:
```

## TASK TYPE: T277 — Ephemeral Deploy + Smoke Suite
```
ARCHETYPE: ORCHESTRATION
ENTRY: Fires after T272 reaches Ready state (env.ready event)
PURPOSE: Deploy workload to ready ephemeral environment via GitOps sync (F713),
         then execute smoke + config test suites (F717, F718). Gate pipeline
         on passing ReadinessReport (F719).

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F713 → CORE FABRIC (HTTP — GitOps API)
  F714 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES — drift snapshots)
  F717 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (ES test runs)
  F718 → CORE FABRIC (HTTP — endpoints under test)
  F719 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG — readiness reports)
  F720 → CORE FABRIC (HTTP) + QUEUE FABRIC
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generate deployment orchestrator from env profile
  AF-2 (Planning): gitops-sync → wait-healthy → config-tests → smoke → integration → report
  AF-4 (RAG): search SK-167 (deployment orchestration), SK-168 (readiness patterns)
  AF-5 (Multi-model): parallel smoke + integration test execution
  AF-9 (Judge): readiness report completeness, all mandatory suites passed
IRON RULES:
  IR-277-1: NEVER promote without passing ReadinessReport — BUILD FAILURE (DR-117)
  IR-277-2: NEVER skip drift detection after GitOps sync — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-277-3: NEVER run tests before GitOps sync confirms Synced state — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-277-4: ReadinessReport MUST be immutable once generated — BUILD FAILURE if mutated
  IR-277-5: ALL test operations scoped to env_id and tenantId — BUILD FAILURE if missing
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-277-1: GitOps sync status = Synced before test suites start
  QG-277-2: Config test suite passes (all required keys, all vault refs exist)
  QG-277-3: Smoke suite passes (DB, queue, AI provider, RAG connectivity)
  QG-277-4: ReadinessReport.overall_pass = true before promotion allowed
  QG-277-5: Drift detection found no unexpected mutations
```

## TASK TYPE: T281 — Promotion Ladder Gate
```
ARCHETYPE: ORCHESTRATION
ENTRY: Fires on promotion.requested event (from CI or manual trigger via F712)
PURPOSE: Execute dev → stage → prod promotion sequence. Check readiness report,
         BFA validation, DR drill freshness (tier-1), artifact integrity, optional
         human approval. Record promotion decision. Trigger rollback on failure.

  F712 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG)
  F719 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG)
  F723 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES WORM — drill evidence)
  F711 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES — artifact metadata)
  F726 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG WORM + ES — audit)
IRON RULES:
  IR-281-1: NEVER promote without passing ReadinessReport (DR-117) — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-281-2: NEVER promote tier-1 component without fresh drill evidence within 7 days (DR-118) — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-281-3: NEVER promote if BFA stress test suite not passed — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-281-4: NEVER promote with unacknowledged drift (CF-338) — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-281-5: Rollback MUST be triggerable within 5 minutes of failed post-deploy check — BUILD FAILURE if not
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-281-1: ReadinessReport.overall_pass = true
  QG-281-2: Tier-1: DrillEvidence exists and is within 7 days
  QG-281-3: Artifact sha256 integrity check passes
  QG-281-4: Promotion decision recorded in promotion log (immutable)
  QG-281-5: Rollback plan stored and verified executable
```

## TASK TYPE: T283 — Restore Drill Saga
```
ARCHETYPE: RESTORE_DRILL (NEW)
ENTRY: Fires on schedule (weekly minimum for tier-1) or restore_drill.requested event
PURPOSE: Execute full restore drill: provision sandbox (F724) → restore backup (F721) →
         run smoke tests in sandbox (F718) → capture DrillResult → store evidence (F723)
         → destroy sandbox → update promotion eligibility.
DISTINCT FROM: T282 (T282 runs backup; T283 runs restore verification in isolated sandbox).
               Together they form the non-negotiable DR evidence pair.
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F721, F722, F723, F724, F718, F726 — resolved via CreateAsync()
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F721 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES) + QUEUE FABRIC
  F722 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG drill state)
  F723 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES WORM — immutable evidence)
  F724 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG sandbox state)
  F718 → CORE FABRIC (HTTP — smoke under test)
  F726 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG WORM + ES — audit)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generate drill orchestrator from drill spec
  AF-2 (Planning): provision-sandbox → restore → smoke → capture-result → store-evidence → destroy
  AF-4 (RAG): search SK-169 (DR drill patterns), SK-170 (sandbox isolation)
  AF-8 (Security): sandbox has zero production network access
  AF-9 (Judge): drill evidence completeness, RTO measurement validity
MACHINE: Drill state: Scheduled → SandboxProvisioning → BackupRunning → RestoreRunning → Verifying → Passed/Failed → Cleanup → Completed
FREEDOM: Drill scope (full vs targeted), tier-specific RTO targets, drill frequency
IRON RULES:
  IR-283-1: NEVER allow sandbox to have production network access — BUILD FAILURE (DR-118)
  IR-283-2: NEVER seed sandbox from anything other than backup artifact — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-283-3: NEVER store mutable evidence — DrillResult is append-only (DR-119) — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-283-4: RTO measurement MUST be captured (restore_start → smoke_pass timestamp) — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-283-5: NEVER skip sandbox destruction after drill — BUILD FAILURE (resource leak)
  IR-283-6: Tier-1 components MUST have drill within 7 days for promotion (DR-118) — BUILD FAILURE if expired
  IR-283-7: ALL drill operations scoped to tenantId (DNA-5) — BUILD FAILURE
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-283-1: Sandbox provisioned with zero-egress NetworkPolicy confirmed
  QG-283-2: Restore from backup artifact — NOT from production data
  QG-283-3: Smoke suite passes in sandbox (golden path verification)
  QG-283-4: DrillResult.rto_actual_minutes ≤ DrillResult.rto_target_minutes
  QG-283-5: DrillEvidence stored in ES WORM — immutable record confirmed
  QG-283-6: Audit event logged with drill_id, target, pass/fail, RTO
  QG-283-7: Sandbox destroyed — Deleted terminal state confirmed
  QG-283-8: Promotion eligibility updated for target component
```

## TASK TYPE: T286 — Tenant Offboarding Saga
```
ARCHETYPE: DURABLE_SAGA
ENTRY: Fires on tenant.offboarding_requested event (requires admin approval gate)
PURPOSE: Execute tenant offboarding lifecycle: suspend writes → export data →
         rotate/revoke keys → purge/tombstone data per retention policy →
         delete tenant resources + credentials → preserve audit logs →
         finalize billing → close tenant.
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F728, F729, F730, F733, F731, F723, F726 — resolved via CreateAsync()
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F728 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG — tenant master)
  F729 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG + Redis — binding cleanup)
  F730 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG)
  F733 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG lifecycle)
  F731 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG + ES)
  F723 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES WORM — preserve drill evidence)
  F726 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG WORM + ES — preserve audit logs)
IRON RULES:
  IR-286-1: NEVER delete audit logs during offboarding (DR-120) — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-286-2: NEVER delete drill evidence during offboarding (DR-119) — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-286-3: NEVER proceed past suspend without admin approval event — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-286-4: Key rotation MUST complete before data purge — BUILD FAILURE if out of order
  IR-286-5: Billing MUST be finalized before tenant_id deactivated — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-286-6: Offboarding is IRREVERSIBLE past purge step — must have confirmation event
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-286-1: Tenant status = Suspended before any data mutation
  QG-286-2: Export artifact created and stored before purge
  QG-286-3: All tenant credentials revoked and keys rotated
  QG-286-4: Data purge respects retention policy (tombstone vs delete)
  QG-286-5: Audit logs preserved — NOT deleted
  QG-286-6: Billing finalized and metering ledger closed
  QG-286-7: Tenant binding removed from resolver cache
```

## AF STATION MAPPING — FLOW-19

### INVENTORY SUB-ENGINE
- AF-4 (RAG Task Context): retrieves SK-161 through SK-174 for relevant patterns
- AF-3 (Prompt Library): CI/CD, IaC, GitOps, DR, Multi-Tenant prompts
- Key RAG queries: "catalog ingestion", "environment provisioning", "IaC saga compensation",
  "pipeline contract", "restore drill", "tenant onboarding idempotent"

### SYNTHESIS SUB-ENGINE
- AF-1 (Genesis): generates services from engine contracts (T269-T286)
- AF-5 (Multi-model): parallel generation of IaC wrapper, GitOps adapter, test suites
- AF-10 (Merge): combines multi-model outputs selecting highest DNA compliance score

### JUDGMENT SUB-ENGINE
- AF-6 (Code Review): no direct provider SDK imports; no typed models; build_search_filter
- AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-1 through DNA-9 scan on all generated services
- AF-8 (Security): no secrets in Git/descriptors/IaC outputs; BOLA/BOPLA checks
  NetworkPolicy enforcement; tenant isolation validation
- AF-9 (Judge): validates iron rules and quality gates per task type
- AF-11 (Feedback): stores generation quality → feeds future improvements

```json
      "on_failure": "emit descriptor.invalid event, store validation errors" },

      "on_failure": "alert — graph refresh failed, catalog still valid" },
    { "step": 3, "task_type": "T269", "factory": "F700", "fabric": "DATABASE(ES)",

  ],

}
```

# FLOW-20 TASK TYPES — Sponsored Content + Graph API

## T287 — Graph Read Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T287 — Graph Read Gate
ARCHETYPE: REQUEST_RESPONSE (synchronous, <200ms p99)
ENTRY: GET /{version}/{nodeId}/{edge}?fields=... with Bearer token
       F846 TenantEdgeResolver already resolved tenantId into scope context
PURPOSE: Execute a graph read: validate request, plan query, enforce permissions,
         fan out to domain services, return authorized partial or full result set.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T288 (Graph Write) — T287 is read-only, no mutation events emitted
  T299 (Schema Field Projection Gate) — T299 resolves projection metadata
        T287 executes the full read pipeline using T299 output

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F735, F736, F737, F741, F742, F756, F757, F758, F759
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F735 → DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (version manifest)
  F736 → CORE FABRIC (request normalisation)
  F737 → DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (cursor index)
  F741 → DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (schema + field-permission)
  F742 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (plan optimisation) + DATABASE FABRIC → ES
  F756 → RAG FABRIC (policy retrieval) + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (audit log)
  F757 → QUEUE FABRIC → Redis Streams (federation fan-out)
  F758 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (node cache)
  F759 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (edge cache)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generate graph read service on MicroserviceBase; uses F756 per-node/field
  AF-2 Planning:    S1(version) → S2(normalize) → S3(project fields) → S4(plan) →
                    S5(cache check) → S6(fan-out) → S7(permission filter) → S8(partial-error build)
  AF-3 Prompt Lib:  Graph API read-path domain prompts; partial-auth patterns
  AF-4 RAG:         SK-175 (graph auth pattern), SK-176 (partial-error pattern); T288 (write gate reuse)
  AF-5 Multi-model: Field projection correctness scored across models
  AF-6 Code Review: Verify no typed model returned; Dictionary-only results; allSettled semantics
  AF-7 Compliance:  DNA-1 parse_document (no typed GraphNode class), DNA-2 build_search_filter,
                    DNA-3 DataProcessResult, DNA-4 MicroserviceBase, DNA-5 Scope Isolation
  AF-8 Security:    F756 per-node/field auth verified; no field leakage on partial auth
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates all IRON RULES and QUALITY GATES
  AF-10 Merge:      Conservative score on divergence > 10% (DD-175 applied to code quality)
  AF-11 Feedback:   Graph read latency + partial-auth ratio stored for improvement

BFA VALIDATION:
  Gate: Node types must be registered in schema registry (F740) before read pipeline ships
  Gate: No T287 read returns data created by FLOW-13 finance engine without explicit DR-133 config
  Cross-check: GraphRead entities ≠ FLOW-10 CRM entities (different domains — PASS with annotation)
  Cross-check: T287 depth limit config (F734) ≠ conflict with FLOW-08 tenant quota (F849)

MACHINE:
  Version resolution: unknown version → 404-result (never 500)
  Tenant resolution: F846 scope context ALWAYS used; user header tenantId rejected
  Permission decision: F756 per-node per-edge per-field — never per-request only
  Depth limit: F734 FREEDOM config enforced; over-depth request → partial-error (not 400)
  Cache invalidation: write-through (not lazy) for F758/F759

FREEDOM:
  Depth limit per tenant/app tier (F734)
  Pagination page size per app tier (F737)
  Cache TTL per node type (F758)
  Deprecation warning window (F735 — default 90 days)

IRON RULES:
  IR-287-1: F846 tenantId from scope context ONLY. User-supplied tenant header = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-287-2: F756 called per node, per edge, per field. Single per-request decision = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-287-3: Partial authorization returns HTTP 200 with errors array, not HTTP 403.
  IR-287-4: F736 normalisation BEFORE F741 projection. Raw path used in projection = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-287-5: Depth limit violation → partial-error result. Hard rejection (400) = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-287-6: All factory calls via CreateAsync(). Direct instantiation = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-287-7: All results DataProcessResult[T]. Exception for permission deny = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-287-8: F757 fan-out via QUEUE FABRIC only. Direct HTTP to domain services = BUILD FAILURE.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-287-1: Depth limit enforcement verified by integration test (request exceeding limit)
  QG-287-2: Partial-auth format: { data: {...}, errors: [...] } — schema validated
  QG-287-3: F758/F759 cache hit verified for hot node types (popular pages/posts)
  QG-287-4: Tenant isolation — cross-tenant node access returns empty (not error-leaking)
  QG-287-5: F757 allSettled: one domain service timeout = partial result, not total failure
  QG-287-6: F735 deprecation header present in response for deprecated version requests
```

---

## T288 — Graph Write Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T288 — Graph Write Gate
ARCHETYPE: REQUEST_RESPONSE (synchronous, <500ms p99 incl. policy check)
ENTRY: POST/PUT/DELETE /{version}/{nodeId}/{edge} with write scope token
       F846 tenantId in scope context; F748 scope enforcement confirmed write scope
PURPOSE: Execute a graph write: validate scope, check content policy, enforce rate limit,
         write via domain service, emit mutation event for cache invalidation and
         downstream consumption (webhooks, feed, search index).
DISTINCT FROM:
  T287 (Graph Read) — T288 mutates state, emits events, invalidates cache
  T291 (Creative Ingestion) — T291 is ad-specific write with review pipeline
        T288 is general platform graph write (posts, comments, profile updates)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F735, F736, F746, F748, F756, F758, F759, F761, F762, F764
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F735 → DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (version manifest)
  F736 → CORE FABRIC (request normalisation)
  F746 → DATABASE FABRIC → PG (consent) + QUEUE FABRIC (withdrawal events)
  F748 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (scope cache) + ES (scope rules)
  F756 → RAG FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (audit)
  F758 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (node cache — invalidation on write)
  F759 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (edge cache — invalidation on write)
  F761 → DATABASE FABRIC → PG (subscription registry)
  F762 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (subscription filter index)
  F764 → QUEUE FABRIC + CORE FABRIC (HMAC signing)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generate graph write service; outbox pattern for mutation events
  AF-2 Planning:    S1(version) → S2(normalize) → S3(scope check) → S4(content policy) →
                    S5(permission check) → S6(rate limit) → S7(write) → S8(cache invalidate) →
                    S9(webhook fan-out)
  AF-3 Prompt Lib:  Graph write + outbox pattern; webhook delivery prompts
  AF-4 RAG:         SK-177 (webhook HMAC pattern); T289 (webhook delivery reuse)
  AF-6 Code Review: Atomic write + cache invalidation; HMAC on all webhook paths
  AF-7 Compliance:  DNA-1–5; DR-125 (HMAC mandatory); no direct cache manipulation post-write
  AF-8 Security:    F748 scope verified before any mutation; write scope never inferred
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates all IRON RULES
  AF-11 Feedback:   Write latency + webhook delivery success rate stored

BFA VALIDATION:
  Gate: Mutation events must not conflict with FLOW-04 post-publishing event schema
  Cross-check: Graph write post.created ≠ FLOW-04 ContentPublished (same domain — flag for dedup)
  Gate: Cache invalidation events do not conflict with FLOW-02 social graph cache keys

MACHINE:
  Scope enforcement: write scope MUST be present; admin cannot override scope requirement
  Mutation event: ALWAYS emitted after successful write for downstream cache + webhooks
  HMAC: ALL webhook deliveries signed — no unsigned delivery path

FREEDOM:
  Rate limit on writes per app/token (F850 FREEDOM config)
  Webhook subscription max per app (F761 FREEDOM config)
  Content policy sensitivity level (F782 FREEDOM config)

IRON RULES:
  IR-288-1: F748 scope check BEFORE any write operation. Write without scope = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-288-2: F758/F759 cache invalidation MUST fire on successful write. Stale cache = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-288-3: F764 webhook delivery MUST use HMAC signing. Unsigned dispatch = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-288-4: Write mutation event via QUEUE FABRIC only. Direct HTTP notify to subscribers = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-288-5: F746 consent checked for writes that publish to audience. Broadcast without consent = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-288-6: F756 permission check BEFORE write. No post-write permission rollback = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-288-7: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-288-8: All factory calls via CreateAsync().

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-288-1: Scope gate verified: token with read-only scope cannot write (integration test)
  QG-288-2: Cache invalidation: node cache miss after successful write (verified by T287 follow-up)
  QG-288-3: Webhook HMAC: subscriber can verify signature with shared secret
  QG-288-4: Mutation event in QUEUE FABRIC within 100ms of write (SLO test)
  QG-288-5: Idempotency: duplicate write with same idempotency key = no second event emitted
  QG-288-6: Rate limit: write beyond quota returns 429 DataProcessResult with Retry-After
```

---

## T289 — Webhook Delivery Orchestrator

```
TASK TYPE: T289 — Webhook Delivery Orchestrator
ARCHETYPE: ORCHESTRATION (event-driven, async, retry-with-backoff)
ENTRY: Mutation event consumed from QUEUE FABRIC (from T288 S9 fan-out)
       F762 filter resolves matching subscriptions
PURPOSE: Deliver platform events to subscribed developer apps with HMAC signature,
         deduplication, retry backoff, DLQ escalation, and delivery audit logging.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T288 (Graph Write) — T288 triggers the event; T289 owns delivery lifecycle
  T306 (Developer Analytics) — T289 logs delivery outcomes; T306 aggregates them

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F761, F762, F763, F764, F765, F844
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F761 → DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (subscription registry)
  F762 → DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (subscription filter index)
  F763 → QUEUE FABRIC → Redis Streams (retry queue, DLQ)
  F764 → QUEUE FABRIC (delivery) + CORE FABRIC (HMAC)
  F765 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (SETNX dedup)
  F844 → DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (developer dashboard aggregate)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generate delivery orchestrator on MicroserviceBase
  AF-2 Planning:    S1(filter subs) → S2(dedup check) → S3(sign payload) → S4(deliver) →
                    S5(log outcome) → S6(retry schedule if failed) → S7(DLQ if exhausted)
  AF-3 Prompt Lib:  Webhook delivery + retry backoff pattern prompts
  AF-4 RAG:         SK-177 (HMAC delivery pattern); T288 (event schema reuse)
  AF-6 Code Review: Retry idempotency; DLQ after max attempts; no silent drop
  AF-7 Compliance:  DR-125 (HMAC mandatory); DNA-3 DataProcessResult; DNA-5 scope isolation
  AF-8 Security:    HMAC on every delivery; dedup key prevents replay amplification
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates all IRON RULES
  AF-11 Feedback:   Delivery success rate, p99 delivery latency stored

BFA VALIDATION:
  Gate: Webhook event schema compatible with FLOW-04 post events (shared event bus)
  Cross-check: Delivery dedup keys do not collide with FLOW-08 notification dedup keys

MACHINE:
  HMAC: every delivery signed — no exception
  Max attempts: FREEDOM config (default 5); after max → DLQ + FAILED subscription status
  Retry backoff: exponential with jitter (MACHINE formula: 2^n × 1000ms ± 20% jitter)
  Delivery timeout per attempt: 30s (MACHINE — not configurable)

FREEDOM:
  Max retry attempts (default 5)
  Retry window (max delay cap, default 24h)
  Subscription event filter expressions (per-subscription FREEDOM)
  Priority lane assignment per advertiser tier (F785 FREEDOM config)

IRON RULES:
  IR-289-1: DeliverAsync MUST compute HMAC before any HTTP dispatch. No HMAC = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-289-2: F765 dedup check BEFORE delivery attempt. Duplicate delivery = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-289-3: F763 retry schedule MUST use exponential backoff. Linear retry = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-289-4: F763 DLQ after max attempts. Silent drop on exhaustion = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-289-5: Delivery outcome logged to F844 ALWAYS. Silent success = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-289-6: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-289-7: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-289-8: Subscription FAILED status set after DLQ. No auto-re-enable = BUILD FAILURE.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-289-1: HMAC verification: subscriber test using provided secret succeeds
  QG-289-2: Dedup: same deliveryId delivered twice → second attempt skipped
  QG-289-3: Retry: simulated endpoint 500 → retry with exponential backoff (integration test)
  QG-289-4: DLQ: after max attempts → subscription status = FAILED (state verified)
  QG-289-5: Delivery latency: p99 first attempt < 5s from event ingestion
  QG-289-6: Developer dashboard shows correct delivery success/failure stats
```

---

## T290 — Payment Method Registration Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T290 — Payment Method Registration Gate
ARCHETYPE: VALIDATION (synchronous, stateful, PCI-scoped)
ENTRY: POST /ad-accounts/{accountId}/payment-methods with raw payment instrument
       Caller has billing:write scope (F748 verified)
PURPOSE: Accept payment instrument, tokenize via external vault (never store raw PAN),
         bind tokenized reference to billing account, confirm PCI boundary compliance.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T296 (Spend Billing Gate) — T290 registers the method; T296 charges it
  T303 (Financial Reconciliation) — T303 reconciles charges; T290 is one-time registration

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F766, F769, F773, F838
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F766 → DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (ad account validation)
  F769 → DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (billing account update)
  F773 → DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (token reference storage, PCI out-of-scope)
  F838 → DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (audit log)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generate payment registration service; PCI tokenization boundary enforced
  AF-2 Planning:    S1(scope check) → S2(account validation) → S3(tokenize) → S4(bind) → S5(audit)
  AF-3 Prompt Lib:  PCI tokenization boundary prompts; never log raw card data
  AF-4 RAG:         SK-179 (payment tokenization pattern)
  AF-7 Compliance:  DR-126 (PCI boundary); AF-8 rejects any field named card_number/pan/cvv
  AF-8 Security:    Raw PAN cannot appear in any log, queue event, or stored field
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates all IRON RULES
  AF-11 Feedback:   Tokenization success rate, vault latency stored

BFA VALIDATION:
  Gate: F766 ad account must exist and not be suspended before payment method binding
  Cross-check: Payment token reference format compatible with FLOW-13 finance billing schema

MACHINE:
  Raw card data: NEVER stored, logged, or emitted to any queue
  PCI boundary: F773 is the ONLY service that receives raw payment data
  Tokenization: synchronous — no async tokenization path exists
  Audit log: every payment method registration logged to F838 regardless of outcome

FREEDOM:
  Supported payment instrument types (credit card / wire / invoice) — per account tier
  Multi-payment-method: max number per account (FREEDOM config)

IRON RULES:
  IR-290-1: Raw card data (card_number, CVV, full PAN) NEVER stored. = BUILD FAILURE if found.
  IR-290-2: F773 tokenization BEFORE any storage. Binding without token = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-290-3: F838 audit log MUST fire on every registration attempt (success or failure).
  IR-290-4: F766 account validation BEFORE tokenization. Orphaned token = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-290-5: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-290-6: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-290-7: Tokenization error → DataProcessResult Error (not HTTP 500).
  IR-290-8: F769 binding update is atomic with token storage. Partial bind = BUILD FAILURE.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-290-1: PCI scan: no log entry or queue event contains card_number/CVV (automated scan)
  QG-290-2: Orphaned token: vault token created but bind fails → cleanup triggered
  QG-290-3: Duplicate registration: same card tokenized twice → second token bound (no raw data)
  QG-290-4: Suspended account: payment method registration rejected with correct error result
  QG-290-5: Audit log: every attempt (success/fail) has matching audit entry in F838
```

---

## T291 — Creative Ingestion Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T291 — Creative Ingestion Gate
ARCHETYPE: VALIDATION (multi-step, async review trigger)
ENTRY: POST /ad-accounts/{accountId}/creatives with asset binary + metadata
       ads:write scope (F748); billing account active (F769)
PURPOSE: Ingest ad creative asset, validate format, dispatch transcoding if needed,
         score quality, auto-classify content, enqueue for review, set initial
         approval status to PENDING (blocking auction eligibility until approved).
DISTINCT FROM:
  T288 (Graph Write) — T288 is general content write; T291 is ad-specific creative
        ingestion with mandatory review pipeline (DD-178)
  T295 (Ad Review Gate) — T291 is ingestion entry point; T295 is the review decision step

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F776, F777, F778, F780, F781, F782, F785, F786, F801
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F776 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (metadata) + Object Store (binary)
  F777 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (variant generation)
  F778 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (quality scoring) + DATABASE FABRIC → ES
  F780 → CORE FABRIC (async transcode job)
  F781 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + RAG FABRIC (IAB taxonomy)
  F782 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (content policy rules, FREEDOM config)
  F785 → QUEUE FABRIC → Redis Streams (review queue)
  F786 → DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (review decision, append-only)
  F801 → DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (ad catalog)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generate creative ingestion service on MicroserviceBase
  AF-2 Planning:    S1(scope) → S2(format validate) → S3(store asset) → S4(transcode if video) →
                    S5(quality score) → S6(tag+classify) → S7(policy check) → S8(set PENDING) →
                    S9(enqueue review)
  AF-3 Prompt Lib:  Creative ingestion + content policy gate prompts
  AF-4 RAG:         SK-186 (creative review gate pattern); T295 (review pipeline reuse)
  AF-5 Multi-model: Quality scoring run across multiple models (DD-175 conservative divergence)
  AF-6 Code Review: PENDING status set before review enqueue; no auction before approval
  AF-7 Compliance:  DNA-1–5; DD-178 (approval before eligibility); IR-291-1 enforced
  AF-8 Security:    No unsigned asset accepted; hash validation on binary upload
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates all IRON RULES
  AF-11 Feedback:   Creative quality scores + review outcome rate stored for model improvement

BFA VALIDATION:
  Gate: F801 catalog update event must not conflict with FLOW-04 content moderation pipeline
  Cross-check: Creative asset IDs not colliding with FLOW-04 media asset namespace
  Gate: Review queue events compatible with existing QUEUE FABRIC consumer group schema

MACHINE:
  Approval status: initial = PENDING. NEVER starts as approved.
  Auction eligibility: BLOCKED until F786 records approved decision (DD-178)
  Content policy: evaluated before review enqueue — prohibited category → REJECTED immediately
  SHA-256 hash: computed on ingestion for deduplication and integrity

FREEDOM:
  Allowed creative formats per placement (FREEDOM config via F800)
  Quality score threshold for expedited vs standard review lane (F785 FREEDOM config)
  Variant auto-generation on/off per account tier (F777 FREEDOM config)

IRON RULES:
  IR-291-1: Initial review status = PENDING. Approved on ingestion = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-291-2: F782 policy check BEFORE F785 review enqueue. Policy violation = REJECT, not PENDING.
  IR-291-3: F801 catalog updated with PENDING status BEFORE review queue enqueue.
  IR-291-4: F780 transcode dispatched for video assets. Untranscoded video in catalog = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-291-5: SHA-256 hash computed and stored on every asset ingestion.
  IR-291-6: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-291-7: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-291-8: F776 asset store BEFORE F785 review enqueue. Enqueue without stored asset = BUILD FAILURE.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-291-1: Status gate: newly ingested creative has catalog status = PENDING (verified)
  QG-291-2: Policy gate: prohibited category creative rejected before reaching review queue
  QG-291-3: Transcode: video asset has all required format variants before PENDING set
  QG-291-4: Hash: duplicate hash creative identified and linked (not duplicated)
  QG-291-5: Quality score: available in ES before review enqueue (reviewer context enriched)
  QG-291-6: Auction ineligibility: T292 cannot select PENDING creative (integration test)
```

## T292 — Ad Auction Orchestrator

```
TASK TYPE: T292 — Ad Auction Orchestrator
ARCHETYPE: ORCHESTRATION (latency-critical, <50ms p99, stateless function)
ENTRY: Ad slot context + viewer context emitted by feed/search service
       F846 tenantId resolved; F793 consent gate confirmed BEFORE this task fires
PURPOSE: Run the full ad auction pipeline: load eligible candidates, compute quality
         scores, apply pricing model, enforce frequency/pacing, determine winner,
         insert into slot via F804, log impression, decrement budget async.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T293 (Impression Attribution) — T293 processes post-auction impression signals
        T292 is the real-time auction decision
  T298 (Tenant Quota Gate) — T298 enforces platform quotas; T292 is the auction itself

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F793, F795, F796, F797, F798, F799, F800, F802, F804, F805, F809, F810, F811
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F793 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (consent cache) + PG (authoritative)
  F795 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (catalog) + Redis (eligibility cache)
  F796 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (quality scoring) + DATABASE FABRIC → ES
  F797 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (pricing FREEDOM config)
  F798 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (INCR atomic counters)
  F799 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (pacing state)
  F800 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (slot definition FREEDOM config)
  F802 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES
  F804 → QUEUE FABRIC → Redis Streams (feed injection)
  F805 → QUEUE FABRIC → Redis Streams (impression log)
  F809 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES + Redis (eligibility cache)
  F810 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis ONLY (stateless auction, DD-166)
  F811 → QUEUE FABRIC (async budget decrement) + DATABASE FABRIC → Redis

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generate auction orchestrator on MicroserviceBase; Redis-only path enforced
  AF-2 Planning:    S1(consent gate) → S2(slot resolve) → S3(eligibility load) → S4(quality score) →
                    S5(pricing) → S6(freq cap) → S7(pacing signal) → S8(auction) →
                    S9(insert) → S10(impression log async) → S11(budget decrement async)
  AF-3 Prompt Lib:  Auction latency budget prompts; consent-before-targeting pattern
  AF-4 RAG:         SK-178 (stateless auction Redis pattern); SK-180 (consent gate pattern)
  AF-5 Multi-model: Quality scoring conservative divergence > 10% (DD-175)
  AF-6 Code Review: Redis-only in critical path; async for impression/budget (non-blocking)
  AF-7 Compliance:  DNA-1–5; DR-127 (50ms SLO); DR-128 (consent before targeting)
                    DR-130 (Redis-only auction state); DD-178 (approved creative only)
  AF-8 Security:    F793 consent gate cannot be bypassed; F809 approval status hard-filtered
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates all IRON RULES; p99 latency test in quality suite
  AF-10 Merge:      Conservative model score on divergence (DD-175)
  AF-11 Feedback:   Win rate, fill rate, quality score distribution, p99 latency stored

BFA VALIDATION:
  Gate: Impression log events must not conflict with FLOW-04 analytics event schema
  Gate: Feed injection slot IDs compatible with FLOW-04 feed distribution slot schema
  Cross-check: Auction budget counters (Redis) isolated from FLOW-13 financial ledger (PG)
  Gate: Consent check for targeting compatible with FLOW-08 consent schema

MACHINE:
  Consent gate (F793): BLOCKING before targeting evaluation (DD-167, DR-128)
  Creative approval (F809 + F786): APPROVED status required — hard filter (DD-178)
  Political verification (F806 via F802): dual gate — both classifier + verification (DD-168)
  Auction state: Redis ONLY in critical path — no PG/ES write (DD-166, DR-130)
  Impression + budget: async post-auction (non-blocking render path)
  No-fill: valid result; never error on empty auction

FREEDOM:
  Pricing model variant per placement (F797 FREEDOM config)
  Frequency cap values per campaign (F798 FREEDOM config)
  Quality score threshold for auction inclusion (F796 FREEDOM config)
  Multi-model quality conservative threshold (DD-175 — FREEDOM config: default 10%)
  Depth limit for ad slot targeting context (F734 extended to auction context)

IRON RULES:
  IR-292-1: F810 RunAuctionAsync reads Redis ONLY. Non-Redis fabric call = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-292-2: F809 eligibility check: creative with status ≠ "approved" excluded. No override = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-292-3: F793 consent check BEFORE F787 targeting evaluation. Reversed order = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-292-4: Political ad: both F803 classifier AND F806 verification pass. One gate only = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-292-5: F805 impression log via QUEUE FABRIC async. Synchronous impression write = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-292-6: F811 budget decrement via QUEUE FABRIC async. Synchronous decrement in auction = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-292-7: No-fill result = DataProcessResult[Success] with empty winner. Exception = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-292-8: All factory calls via CreateAsync().

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-292-1: Consent gate: viewer without targeting consent → no targeted ad delivered (integration test)
  QG-292-2: Approval gate: PENDING creative never wins auction (integration test)
  QG-292-3: p99 latency: RunAuctionAsync < 50ms (Redis-only load test)
  QG-292-4: Political dual gate: F806 missing verification → ad excluded regardless of classifier
  QG-292-5: No-fill: empty eligible set → no-fill result, no error, slot remains empty
  QG-292-6: Multi-model quality divergence > 10%: lower score used (DD-175 verified by unit test)
  QG-292-7: Budget exhaustion: campaign paused after last budget counter decremented
```

## T293 — Impression Attribution Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T293 — Impression Attribution Gate
ARCHETYPE: EVENT_PROCESSING (async, fraud-gated, append-only)
ENTRY: Impression event consumed from F805 (QUEUE FABRIC impression stream)
PURPOSE: Process raw impression event: enrich with viewability, apply fraud gate
         (blocking), attribute credit to ad touchpoints, emit billing event if billable.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T294 (Click Attribution) — T293 is impression path; T294 is click path
  T292 (Auction Orchestrator) — T292 produces impressions; T293 processes them
  T305 (Fraud Quarantine) — T305 handles quarantined events; T293 is the pre-quarantine gate

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F812, F817, F822, F823, F828, F830, F832
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F812 → QUEUE FABRIC (impression stream) + DATABASE FABRIC → ES
  F817 → DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (viewability log)
  F822 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (IVT detection)
  F823 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (click fraud — applied to impression context)
  F828 → QUEUE FABRIC → DLQ + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (quarantine audit)
  F830 → DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (spend ledger, append-only)
  F832 → QUEUE FABRIC (billing consumer) + DATABASE FABRIC → PG

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generate impression attribution service on MicroserviceBase
  AF-2 Planning:    S1(consume) → S2(viewability enrich) → S3(IVT score) → S4(fraud gate) →
                    S5(attribution) → S6(billing emit) → S7(ledger append)
  AF-3 Prompt Lib:  Fraud gate before billing pattern; append-only ledger prompts
  AF-4 RAG:         SK-183 (fraud gate blocking pattern); SK-181 (append-only ledger pattern)
  AF-6 Code Review: Fraud gate is blocking (not async filter); ledger is append-only
  AF-7 Compliance:  DD-169 (append-only ledger); DD-177 (fraud gate before billing); DR-131
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates all IRON RULES
  AF-11 Feedback:   IVT rate, viewability rate, attribution latency stored

BFA VALIDATION:
  Gate: Billing events must not conflict with FLOW-13 finance engine billing schema
  Cross-check: Impression attribution events compatible with FLOW-04 analytics events
  Gate: Spend ledger schema compatible with FLOW-13 general ledger integration (DR-131)

MACHINE:
  Fraud gate: BLOCKING before billing event emission (DD-177)
  Ledger: APPEND-ONLY (DD-169, DR-131)
  Viewability threshold: MRC standard (50% pixels, 1 continuous second) — MACHINE
  Attribution model: resolved from F818/F820 (FREEDOM config per advertiser)

FREEDOM:
  Attribution window (F821/F829 per advertiser account — DD-179)
  IVT confidence threshold for quarantine (F822/F823 FREEDOM config)
  Viewability strict mode (strict MRC vs relaxed) — per campaign FREEDOM config

IRON RULES:
  IR-293-1: F822/F823 fraud scoring BEFORE billing event emission. Reversed order = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-293-2: Fraud score above threshold → F828 quarantine BEFORE billing. Billing then quarantine = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-293-3: F830 ledger: AppendLedgerEntryAsync ONLY. UpdateLedgerEntry = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-293-4: Attribution window from F821/F829 FREEDOM config. Hardcoded window = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-293-5: QUEUE FABRIC consumption: AcknowledgeAsync only after ledger append success.
  IR-293-6: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-293-7: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-293-8: F832 billing event only emitted after fraud gate PASS. No pre-screening bill = BUILD FAILURE.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-293-1: Fraud gate: simulated high-IVT impression → quarantined, not billed
  QG-293-2: Ledger append: no UPDATE on spend_ledger table (PG schema constraint test)
  QG-293-3: Attribution window: advertiser with 28d window receives credit for 25d-old impression
  QG-293-4: Viewability: sub-threshold impression flagged as not billable in correct pricing models
  QG-293-5: QUEUE ACK: ACK only after ledger write (failure = re-consume, no float-bill test)
```

## T294 — Click Attribution Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T294 — Click Attribution Gate
ARCHETYPE: EVENT_PROCESSING (async, fraud-gated, higher fraud sensitivity than T293)
ENTRY: Click event consumed from F813 click log (QUEUE FABRIC)
PURPOSE: Process click event: validate against served impression (anti-fraud),
         deduplicate, apply fraud gate (blocking, higher sensitivity than impressions),
         attribute conversion credit, emit billing event.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T293 (Impression Attribution) — T294 is click path; fraud sensitivity is higher
        (clicks drive CPC billing; inflated clicks = direct revenue fraud)
  T305 (Fraud Quarantine) — T305 handles quarantined events post-gate; T294 is the gate

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F813, F822, F823, F824, F826, F827, F828, F830, F832
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F813 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (click log)
  F822 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES
  F823 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (click fraud primary detector)
  F824 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (bot detection)
  F826 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (device fingerprint)
  F827 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (behaviour anomaly)
  F828 → QUEUE FABRIC → DLQ + DATABASE FABRIC → ES
  F830 → DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (append-only ledger)
  F832 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → PG

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generate click attribution service; higher fraud threshold than T293
  AF-2 Planning:    S1(consume) → S2(impression correlation) → S3(dedup) → S4(bot check) →
                    S5(click fraud score) → S6(behaviour anomaly) → S7(fraud gate) →
                    S8(attribution) → S9(billing emit) → S10(ledger append)
  AF-4 RAG:         SK-183 (fraud gate blocking); SK-181 (append-only ledger)
  AF-7 Compliance:  DD-169; DD-177; DR-131; higher fraud sensitivity than T293
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates all IRON RULES; click-to-impression correlation test

BFA VALIDATION:
  Cross-check: Click events compatible with FLOW-04 post analytics schema
  Gate: CPC billing events compatible with FLOW-13 revenue recognition gate (DR-57)

MACHINE:
  Fraud gate: BLOCKING before billing (DD-177); stricter threshold than T293 (MACHINE: clicks drive direct revenue)
  Impression correlation: click without matching served impression → QUARANTINE
  Deduplication: same click event twice → second silently dropped (not billed)
  Ledger: APPEND-ONLY (DD-169)

FREEDOM:
  Click fraud sensitivity threshold (FREEDOM config per placement type)
  Attribution window (F821/F829 per advertiser — DD-179)
  Bot confidence threshold for quarantine (F824 FREEDOM config)

IRON RULES:
  IR-294-1: Impression correlation: click without matching F805 impression record → quarantine. No billing.
  IR-294-2: Fraud gate blocking BEFORE billing. Any bill before fraud gate = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-294-3: F830 AppendLedgerEntryAsync ONLY. Update = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-294-4: Dedup: same clickId billed twice = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-294-5: Attribution window from F821/F829. Hardcoded window = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-294-6: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-294-7: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-294-8: F828 quarantine before any billing attempt. No "bill then review" = BUILD FAILURE.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-294-1: Impression correlation: orphan click (no matching impression) → quarantined
  QG-294-2: Dedup: duplicate clickId → exactly one billing event emitted
  QG-294-3: Bot gate: simulated bot pattern → click quarantined, not billed
  QG-294-4: Ledger: no update on spend_ledger for clicks (PG constraint test)
  QG-294-5: Behaviour anomaly: velocity-based click fraud pattern → quarantine triggered
```

## T295 — Ad Review Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T295 — Ad Review Gate
ARCHETYPE: VALIDATION (multi-stage: automated + optional human escalation)
ENTRY: Review item dequeued from F785 (QUEUE FABRIC review queue, from T291 enqueue)
PURPOSE: Execute ad review pipeline: automated policy check, political dual-gate,
         brand safety score, human escalation if required, record append-only decision,
         update catalog approval status to APPROVED/REJECTED/RESTRICTED.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T291 (Creative Ingestion) — T291 sets status to PENDING; T295 resolves to final status
  T292 (Auction Orchestrator) — T292 checks the approval status set by T295

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F782, F783, F784, F785, F786, F801, F802, F803, F806, F807
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F782 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (content policy FREEDOM config)
  F783 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (prohibited category registry)
  F784 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (sensitive topic index)
  F785 → QUEUE FABRIC → Redis Streams (review queue)
  F786 → DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (review decision, append-only)
  F801 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (catalog approval status update)
  F802 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES
  F803 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (political classifier)
  F806 → DATABASE FABRIC → PG (political verification records)
  F807 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (brand safety)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generate review gate service; dual-gate pattern for political content
  AF-2 Planning:    S1(dequeue) → S2(prohibited check) → S3(sensitive topic detect) →
                    S4(political classifier) → S5(political verify if political) →
                    S6(brand safety score) → S7(auto-decide or escalate) →
                    S8(record decision) → S9(update catalog)
  AF-3 Prompt Lib:  Political dual-gate prompts; brand safety decision prompts
  AF-4 RAG:         SK-186 (creative review gate); SK-187 (political dual-gate pattern)
  AF-5 Multi-model: Brand safety scoring across models; conservative on divergence (DD-175)
  AF-7 Compliance:  DD-168 (political dual gate); DR-129; DD-178 (decision gate for auction)
  AF-8 Security:    Political verification checked regardless of classifier confidence
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates all IRON RULES; political dual-gate integration test

BFA VALIDATION:
  Gate: Review decisions must not conflict with FLOW-03 content moderation schema
  Cross-check: Ad review prohibited categories compatible with FLOW-04 content moderation rules
  Gate: Political verification records compatible with FLOW-08 identity schema

MACHINE:
  Prohibited category: immediate REJECT, no human escalation path overrides this
  Political dual gate: both classifier AND verification required (DD-168, DR-129)
  Decision: APPEND-ONLY record (DD-169); no modification
  Human escalation: MACHINE criteria (prohibited = no; sensitive topic detected = yes)

FREEDOM:
  Auto-approve threshold (brand safety score minimum — FREEDOM config)
  Human review SLA per priority lane (FREEDOM config via F785)
  Sensitive topic list (FREEDOM config via F784)
  Brand safety blocking categories per advertiser (F807 FREEDOM config)

IRON RULES:
  IR-295-1: Prohibited category → REJECT immediately. Human override of prohibited = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-295-2: Political ad: F803 classifier AND F806 verification BOTH required. One gate = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-295-3: F786 decision record: AppendDecisionAsync ONLY. Update/delete = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-295-4: F801 catalog status updated AFTER F786 decision recorded. Reversed order = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-295-5: Human escalation: decision not recorded until human approves/rejects. Auto-approve on timeout = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-295-6: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-295-7: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-295-8: Brand safety score computed BEFORE final decision. Decision without score = BUILD FAILURE.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-295-1: Political dual gate: ad with political keyword but no F806 verification → REJECTED
  QG-295-2: Prohibited category: tobacco ad → REJECTED immediately, no human escalation offered
  QG-295-3: Decision audit: approved creative has append-only decision trail (no updates)
  QG-295-4: Catalog sync: APPROVED decision in F786 → catalog status APPROVED within 5s
  QG-295-5: Auction gate: creative approved by T295 → now eligible in T292 (integration test)
```

## T296 — Spend Billing Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T296 — Spend Billing Gate
ARCHETYPE: FINANCIAL (append-only, fraud-screened events only)
ENTRY: Billing-eligible event consumed from F832 (post fraud gate from T293/T294)
PURPOSE: Evaluate billability (viewability threshold, engagement minimum), write
         append-only billing entry to spend ledger (F830), emit budget decrement
         event, trigger invoice threshold check.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T293/T294 (Attribution Gates) — T293/T294 route to T296 after fraud screening
             T296 is the financial record step
  T303 (Financial Reconciliation) — T303 reconciles end-of-period; T296 is per-event

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F830, F832, F833, F774, F811
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F830 → DATABASE FABRIC → PostgreSQL (append-only spend ledger)
  F832 → QUEUE FABRIC (billing consumer) + DATABASE FABRIC → PG
  F833 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (invoice narrative) + DATABASE FABRIC → PG
  F774 → DATABASE FABRIC → PG (budget) + Redis (live counter)
  F811 → QUEUE FABRIC (async budget decrement) + DATABASE FABRIC → Redis

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generate billing gate on MicroserviceBase; append-only enforced at schema level
  AF-2 Planning:    S1(consume) → S2(billability check) → S3(pricing calc) →
                    S4(ledger append) → S5(budget decrement async) → S6(invoice threshold check)
  AF-7 Compliance:  DD-169 (append-only); DR-131 (ledger schema contract)
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates all IRON RULES

BFA VALIDATION:
  Gate: Billing events compatible with FLOW-13 financial engine integration
  Cross-check: Spend ledger schema (F830) compatible with FLOW-14 data warehouse ingestion

MACHINE:
  Ledger: APPEND-ONLY (DD-169); no UPDATE/DELETE on billing records
  Budget decrement: async via F811 (never in billing critical path — DD-166)
  Fraud pre-screen: T296 only receives fraud-screened events (from T293/T294)

FREEDOM:
  Billability threshold per pricing model (CPM viewability, CPC engagement minimum)
  Invoice trigger threshold per account (FREEDOM config via F769)
  Currency and tax rate (FREEDOM config via F833)

IRON RULES:
  IR-296-1: F830 AppendLedgerEntryAsync ONLY. UpdateLedger = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-296-2: Non-fraud-screened event in T296 = BUILD FAILURE. T293/T294 gate must precede.
  IR-296-3: Budget decrement via F811 QUEUE event. Synchronous decrement in T296 = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-296-4: Billability result stored with ledger entry. Unbillable entry not in ledger.
  IR-296-5: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-296-6: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-296-7: Double-bill protection: same event_id billed twice = BUILD FAILURE (idempotency check).
  IR-296-8: QUEUE ACK only after successful ledger append.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-296-1: Append-only: no UPDATE on spend_ledger (DB constraint test)
  QG-296-2: Idempotency: same billing event processed twice → one ledger entry
  QG-296-3: Budget sync: decrement event reaches F811 within 200ms of ledger append
  QG-296-4: Invoice threshold: account hits 90% of monthly threshold → alert emitted
```
