## T297 — Flow Version Publish Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T297 — Flow Version Publish Gate
ARCHETYPE: PROVISIONING (immutable snapshot, version lifecycle management)
ENTRY: POST /flows/{flowId}/publish with spec snapshot + semver
       platform:admin scope (F748)
PURPOSE: Publish a new immutable flow version: validate spec, create snapshot,
         validate backward compatibility (F848), record immutable version document,
         update current version pointer. No modification of published versions.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T304 (Attribution Config Gate) — T304 configures FREEDOM config docs
        T297 versions entire flow specifications

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F839, F840, F847, F848
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F839 → DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (immutable flow version index)
  F840 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (governance policy)
  F847 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (BFA conflict index)
  F848 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (compatibility matrix)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generate flow versioning service; immutable doc ID pattern
  AF-2 Planning:    S1(scope check) → S2(spec validate) → S3(compat check) →
                    S4(BFA conflict check) → S5(publish snapshot) → S6(update current pointer)
  AF-7 Compliance:  DD-173 (immutable snapshots); DR-132 (version contract)
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates all IRON RULES

IRON RULES:
  IR-297-1: FlowVersion document ID = {flowId}:{semver}. Non-version-embedded ID = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-297-2: No UpdateVersionAsync method. Any update to published version = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-297-3: F848 compat check BEFORE F839 publish. Incompatible version published = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-297-4: F847 BFA conflict check BEFORE publish. Conflicting flow version = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-297-5: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-297-6: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-297-7: Status transition: DRAFT → REVIEW → PUBLISHED (unidirectional). Skipping REVIEW = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-297-8: Rollback = new PUBLISHED version with previous spec. Not modification.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-297-1: Immutability: published version ES doc cannot be overwritten (index mapping test)
  QG-297-2: Compat check: breaking interface change blocked before publish
  QG-297-3: BFA conflict: conflicting entity registration blocks publish
  QG-297-4: Rollback: rollback to v1 creates v3 with v1 spec (not modifying v1 or v2)
```

---

## T298 — Tenant Quota Enforcement Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T298 — Tenant Quota Enforcement Gate
ARCHETYPE: GUARDRAIL (per-tenant, non-shared pool, DD-176)
ENTRY: Every API request before domain processing; called by F850 rate limit router
PURPOSE: Enforce per-tenant API quotas (requests/min, requests/day, concurrent connections)
         using per-tenant Redis counters (not shared pool). Return 429 on exhaustion.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T300 (API Rate Limit Gate) — T300 is per-app/endpoint; T298 is per-tenant platform quota
  T287/T288 (Graph Gates) — those are request processing; T298 is the upstream quota check

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F849, F850, F851, F845
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F849 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (per-tenant quota counters)
  F850 → CORE FABRIC + Redis (rate limit routing)
  F851 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (burst detection) + ES (metrics)
  F845 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (error budget / SLO tracking)

IRON RULES:
  IR-298-1: Quota counters are per-tenant (not shared pool). Shared counter = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-298-2: Quota exhaustion returns DataProcessResult with 429 status. Exception = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-298-3: F851 noisy neighbor detection: burst above fair-share → throttle (not drop silently).
  IR-298-4: Quota limits from FREEDOM config only. Hardcoded limits = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-298-5: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-298-6: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-298-7: Retry-After header populated on 429 result. Missing header = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-298-8: Tenant identity from F846 scope context only. User-supplied tenant = BUILD FAILURE.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-298-1: Tenant isolation: tenant A quota exhaustion does not affect tenant B (concurrent test)
  QG-298-2: Noisy neighbor: tenant burst beyond fair share → throttled; others unaffected
  QG-298-3: FREEDOM config: quota limits updated via config (no code deploy required)
  QG-298-4: Error budget: quota enforcement events feed SLO tracking (F845 verified)
```

---

## T299 — Schema Field Projection Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T299 — Schema Field Projection Gate
ARCHETYPE: QUERY (metadata resolution, synchronous, <10ms)
ENTRY: Field projection context from T287/T288 S3 (F741 call)
PURPOSE: Resolve the authorized field set for a graph response: apply scope requirements,
         depth limit, privacy rules, and produce the projection mask used by T287 fan-out.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T287 (Graph Read) — T287 calls T299 as sub-step S3; T299 is the projection resolver only

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F734, F740, F741, F748, F755
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F734 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (depth FREEDOM config)
  F740 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (schema registry)
  F741 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (field projection + permissions)
  F748 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (scope cache)
  F755 → CORE FABRIC (partial error builder)

IRON RULES:
  IR-299-1: Depth limit from F734 FREEDOM config. Hardcoded depth = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-299-2: Fields requiring elevated scope silently excluded (not errored) unless explicitly requested.
  IR-299-3: Partial errors assembled by F755. Hand-crafted error array = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-299-4: Schema version pinned to request version. Cross-version field leak = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-299-5: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-299-6: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-299-7: Projection result is Dictionary (no typed FieldProjection class = BUILD FAILURE).
  IR-299-8: Empty requested fields → all authorized fields returned (implicit wildcard is safe default).

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-299-1: Elevated scope field excluded for basic token (integration test)
  QG-299-2: Depth limit enforced: nested beyond limit → partial error, not 400
  QG-299-3: Schema version pinning: v1 request cannot expose v2-only fields
```

---

## T300 — API Rate Limit Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T300 — API Rate Limit Gate
ARCHETYPE: GUARDRAIL (per-app, per-endpoint, token-bucket)
ENTRY: Every API request; evaluated after T298 (tenant quota) passes
PURPOSE: Enforce per-app, per-endpoint rate limits using token bucket algorithm.
         Separate from T298 (tenant quota) — this is app-level granular limiting.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T298 (Tenant Quota) — T298 is platform-wide tenant; T300 is per-app per-endpoint

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F739, F848, F849, F850
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F739 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (token bucket) + ES (pattern index)
  F848 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (compatibility matrix — rate limit schema compat check)
  F849 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (quota counters)
  F850 → CORE FABRIC + Redis (rate limit router)

IRON RULES:
  IR-300-1: Rate limits per-tenant (not shared). Shared limit = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-300-2: Rate limit config from FREEDOM config only. Hardcoded = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-300-3: 429 result includes Retry-After. Missing header = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-300-4: Abuse signal from F739 escalated to F841. Silent drop = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-300-5: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-300-6: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-300-7: Rate limit counter increment is atomic (Redis INCR). Non-atomic = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-300-8: Rate limit bypass for internal system tokens. No bypass for user app tokens.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-300-1: Token bucket: request N+1 over limit within window returns 429 with Retry-After
  QG-300-2: Per-app isolation: app A exhausted does not limit app B (same tenant)
  QG-300-3: FREEDOM config: limit change takes effect within 60s (no redeploy)
```

---

## T301 — Consent Verification Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T301 — Consent Verification Gate
ARCHETYPE: COMPLIANCE (blocking gate, privacy-first)
ENTRY: Targeting evaluation context from T292 S1 (before F787 targeting evaluation)
PURPOSE: Verify viewer targeting consent before any targeting evaluation occurs.
         No consent → skip all targeting (not filter after). Returns consent context
         dictionary for downstream evaluation (DD-167, DR-128).
DISTINCT FROM:
  T292 (Auction Orchestrator) — T292 calls T301 as first step; T301 owns consent logic only

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F746, F793, F838
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F746 → DATABASE FABRIC → PG (consent records) + QUEUE FABRIC (withdrawal events)
  F793 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (consent cache) + PG (authoritative)
  F838 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (audit log)

IRON RULES:
  IR-301-1: Consent check BEFORE targeting evaluation. Any targeting without consent check = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-301-2: No consent → SKIP all targeting. Post-filter pattern = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-301-3: F793 Redis cache read (fast path); F746 PG for authoritative (cache miss).
  IR-301-4: Consent status change via F746 event → F793 cache invalidated within 30s.
  IR-301-5: Consent verification logged to F838.
  IR-301-6: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-301-7: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-301-8: Consent withdrawal event MUST trigger immediate cache invalidation.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-301-1: No-consent viewer: targeting evaluation steps skipped entirely (trace verified)
  QG-301-2: Consent withdrawal: targeting blocked within 30s of withdrawal event
  QG-301-3: Cache coherence: F793 Redis matches F746 PG after cache miss refresh
```

---

## T302 — App OAuth Consent Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T302 — App OAuth Consent Gate
ARCHETYPE: IDENTITY (OAuth 2.0 + scope consent flow)
ENTRY: OAuth authorization request from developer app; user authentication confirmed
PURPOSE: Present scope consent UX, record user consent to requested app scopes,
         issue authorization code for token exchange. Sensitive scopes require
         review-approved app (F747 gate — DR-123).
DISTINCT FROM:
  T301 (Consent Verification) — T301 is runtime targeting consent; T302 is app scope grant

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F743, F744, F746, F747, F748
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F743 → DATABASE FABRIC → PG (app registry)
  F744 → DATABASE FABRIC → PG + Redis (token issuance)
  F746 → DATABASE FABRIC → PG (consent record) + QUEUE FABRIC
  F747 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (review status check)
  F748 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (scope enforcement)

IRON RULES:
  IR-302-1: Sensitive scope request from non-reviewed app → REJECT. No override = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-302-2: Consent record written BEFORE authorization code issued.
  IR-302-3: Restricted scope: human review approved in F747 required. Auto-approve = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-302-4: Token issued only after consent record committed.
  IR-302-5: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-302-6: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-302-7: PKCE required for public clients. No PKCE for public client = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-302-8: Consent scope audit logged to F838.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-302-1: Sensitive scope: unreviewed app gets sensitive scope rejected
  QG-302-2: PKCE: public client without code_challenge rejected
  QG-302-3: Consent record: authorization code issued only after consent committed to PG
```

---

## T303 — Financial Reconciliation Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T303 — Financial Reconciliation Gate
ARCHETYPE: FINANCIAL (period-end, append-only correction, SOC2 compliant)
ENTRY: Scheduled trigger (end-of-billing-period) or on-demand admin trigger
PURPOSE: Reconcile spend ledger (F830) against billing system totals, identify
         discrepancies, issue offset corrections (append-only), generate reconciliation
         report for audit.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T296 (Spend Billing Gate) — T296 is per-event billing; T303 is period reconciliation

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F830, F831, F834, F838
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F830 → DATABASE FABRIC → PG (spend ledger, append-only)
  F831 → DATABASE FABRIC → PG (reversal records, append-only)
  F834 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES + DW (reporting aggregate)
  F838 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (audit log)

IRON RULES:
  IR-303-1: Corrections are OFFSET ENTRIES in F830. Update/delete = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-303-2: Reconciliation report stored in audit log (F838).
  IR-303-3: Discrepancy threshold alert if variance > 0.1% of period spend (MACHINE).
  IR-303-4: Reversal records in F831 are append-only.
  IR-303-5: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-303-6: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-303-7: Reconciliation run idempotent: same period reconciled twice = same result.
  IR-303-8: Offset entry references original entry ID. Orphaned offset = BUILD FAILURE.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-303-1: Offset correction: original + offset = net correct amount (sum test)
  QG-303-2: Idempotency: reconciliation run twice on same period = no duplicate offsets
  QG-303-3: Audit: every reconciliation run has audit log entry with variance amount
```

## T304 — Attribution Window Config Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T304 — Attribution Window Config Gate
ARCHETYPE: CONFIGURATION (FREEDOM config update, per advertiser account)
ENTRY: PUT /ad-accounts/{accountId}/attribution-config with window spec
       ads:settings:write scope (F748)
PURPOSE: Update per-advertiser attribution window configuration in FREEDOM config
         (F829): click window, view window, attribution model, cross-device toggle.
         Config takes effect on next conversion attribution cycle.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T297 (Flow Version Publish) — T297 versions flow specs; T304 updates FREEDOM config

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F766, F821, F829, F838
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F766 → DATABASE FABRIC → PG (account validation)
  F821 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (window config read)
  F829 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (FREEDOM config, update)
  F838 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (audit log)

IRON RULES:
  IR-304-1: Attribution window stored in F829 FREEDOM config. Hardcoded window = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-304-2: F766 account validation BEFORE config update.
  IR-304-3: Config change logged to F838 with before/after values.
  IR-304-4: Click window: 1–90 days (MACHINE range). Out-of-range = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-304-5: View window: 0–7 days (0 = view attribution disabled). Out-of-range = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-304-6: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-304-7: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-304-8: Config update takes effect within 60s. Stale window after 60s = BUILD FAILURE.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-304-1: Range validation: 91-day click window rejected with correct error
  QG-304-2: Propagation: updated window used in next attribution cycle (T293/T294)
  QG-304-3: Audit: config change has before/after audit entry (F838)
```

## T305 — Fraud Quarantine Gate

```
TASK TYPE: T305 — Fraud Quarantine Gate
ARCHETYPE: SECURITY (blocking, append-only audit, IAB TechLab reporting)
ENTRY: Fraud-flagged event from T293/T294 fraud scoring step
PURPOSE: Quarantine fraudulent events synchronously before billing reaches F830.
         Store quarantine record, report to IAB TechLab fraud pipeline, credit
         advertiser account for quarantined impressions/clicks.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T293/T294 (Attribution Gates) — those detect fraud; T305 is the quarantine action

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F823, F828, F831, F834, F838
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F823 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (fraud evidence)
  F828 → QUEUE FABRIC → DLQ + DATABASE FABRIC → ES (quarantine audit)
  F831 → DATABASE FABRIC → PG (reversal records for post-billing fraud)
  F834 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES + DW (fraud impact reporting)
  F838 → DATABASE FABRIC → ES (audit log)

IRON RULES:
  IR-305-1: Quarantine BEFORE billing event emission. Post-bill quarantine = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-305-2: Quarantine record stored in F828 DLQ audit (append-only).
  IR-305-3: Advertiser credit issued for quarantined events (offset entry in F831).
  IR-305-4: IAB TechLab report emitted for quarantine batches (QUEUE FABRIC event).
  IR-305-5: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-305-6: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-305-7: Quarantine record contains fraud evidence (signal types + scores). Empty evidence = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-305-8: Credit issuance idempotent: float-quarantine of same event = one credit.

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-305-1: High-IVT impression: quarantined, billed = $0, credit issued (end-to-end test)
  QG-305-2: Double quarantine: same event flagged twice → one quarantine record, one credit
  QG-305-3: Audit: quarantine has fraud evidence, IAB report event, credit record (all 3 verified)
```

## T306 — Developer Analytics Aggregator

```
TASK TYPE: T306 — Developer Analytics Aggregator
ARCHETYPE: REPORTING (async aggregation, near-real-time, read-only output)
ENTRY: Scheduled trigger (every 5 min); consumes API request telemetry from F739/F850
PURPOSE: Aggregate API usage metrics per app per endpoint: request count, error rate,
         latency percentiles, quota utilisation. Store in F835 developer analytics index.
         Serve via F844 developer dashboard.
DISTINCT FROM:
  T289 (Webhook Delivery) — T289 logs delivery outcomes; T306 aggregates them
  T298 (Tenant Quota Gate) — T298 enforces quotas; T306 reports on quota consumption

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F739, F835, F844, F845
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F739 → DATABASE FABRIC → Redis (request telemetry) + ES (pattern index)
  F835 → DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (developer analytics aggregate index)
  F844 → DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (dashboard index)
  F845 → DATABASE FABRIC → Elasticsearch (error budget / SLO index)

IRON RULES:
  IR-306-1: Aggregation is APPEND-ONLY to F835. No update to historical aggregates = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-306-2: Aggregation window = 5 min (FREEDOM config default). Hardcoded window = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-306-3: F845 error budget updated in same aggregation run.
  IR-306-4: Data retention: aggregates kept per F840 governance policy (FREEDOM config).
  IR-306-5: All results DataProcessResult[T].
  IR-306-6: All factory calls via CreateAsync().
  IR-306-7: Dashboard query reads only from F844 aggregate. Direct query of raw telemetry = BUILD FAILURE.
  IR-306-8: Aggregation run idempotent: same window aggregated twice = same result (no duplicates).

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-306-1: Latency p99: aggregation run completes within 30s for 1M events
  QG-306-2: Dashboard accuracy: F844 aggregate matches raw event count within 0.1%
  QG-306-3: Error budget: SLO breach (> 1% error rate) triggers alert via F845
  QG-306-4: Idempotency: aggregation re-run for same window = no duplicate entries
```

## TASK TYPE: T307 — Form Schema Authoring Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T307
ARCHETYPE:          AUTHORING  ← FIRST USE (new archetype)
ENTRY:              Fires when admin initiates form create/edit in form builder UI
PURPOSE:            Validates, versions, and publishes form schema (fields, pages,
                    conditional logic) through AI-assisted authoring pipeline.
                    Produces platform-agnostic render spec for client consumption.
DISTINCT FROM:      T167 (Connector Registration — provisioning, not authoring)
                    T318 (Recipe Definition — automation DAG, not form schema)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F852 IFormSchemaService          — stores/retrieves schema from ES
  F853 IFieldDefinitionService     — validates field types against catalog
  F854 IConditionalLogicService    — stores and AI-suggests conditional rules
  F855 IFormVersioningService      — creates immutable version snapshot on publish
  F856 IFormPublishService         — transitions schema draft→published + emits event
  F857 IFormRenderSpecService      — generates platform-agnostic render spec
  F860 IAiFormAssistService        — AI-assisted field/schema generation

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F852 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch)
  F853 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch)
  F854 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC
  F855 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL)
  F856 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (ES)
  F857 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch)
  F860 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:       Generates IFormSchemaService implementation on top of ES fabric
  AF-2 Planning:      Decomposes schema authoring into validate→version→publish→render steps
  AF-3 Prompt Library: Retrieves form-authoring domain prompts
  AF-4 RAG:           Searches SK-189 (schema DAG validation), SK-197 (fabric-first UI),
                      SK-199 (conditional logic evaluator) for reusable patterns
  AF-5 Multi-model:   Runs competing models for AI schema suggestions (F860)
  AF-6 Code review:   Reviews generated IFormSchemaService against DNA patterns
  AF-7 Compliance:    Checks DNA-1 (no typed FormSchema), DNA-5 (tenantId on all ES writes)
  AF-8 Security:      Validates F859 permission checks, tenant scope isolation
  AF-9 Judge:         Validates iron rules, quality gates below
  AF-10 Merge:        Combines multi-model schema suggestions
  AF-11 Feedback:     Stores schema quality scores for AI improvement

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-380: Schema publish requires version snapshot (F855) before event (F856)
  CF-381: Render spec regenerated on every schema publish (F856→F857)
  CF-398: Schema namespaced per tenant — no cross-tenant schema read

MACHINE (fixed):
  - Schema stored as ES document (DNA-1)
  - Version created on every publish (DR via F855)
  - Render spec platform-agnostic (SK-197)
  - AI suggestions via IAiProvider.GenerateAsync() (never direct SDK)
  - tenantId on every ES write (DNA-5)

FREEDOM (configurable):
  - AI provider for schema suggestions (claude|openai|gemini|deepseek)
  - Maximum fields per form (FREEDOM config)
  - Versioning retention policy (keep N versions)
  - Conditional logic complexity limits

IRON RULES (violations = BUILD FAILURE):
  IR-307-1: Schema stored as dict[str, Any] — no typed FormSchema class
  IR-307-2: IAiProvider.GenerateAsync() only — never import openai/anthropic/gemini SDK
  IR-307-3: Version snapshot created BEFORE publish event emitted
  IR-307-4: Render spec contains zero platform-specific values (SK-197 compliant)
  IR-307-5: tenantId present on every ES index write in generated service
  IR-307-6: Conditional rules stored as ES document — never compiled into service code
  IR-307-7: F859 permission check executed before schema mutation
  IR-307-8: IFormSchemaService resolved via CreateAsync() — never directly instantiated

QUALITY GATES (AF-9 validates):
  QG-307-1: AI-suggested schemas return DataProcessResult[Dictionary]
  QG-307-2: Published render spec parseable by client without platform imports
  QG-307-3: Version rollback restores previous render spec correctly
  QG-307-4: Conditional rules evaluate without exception on empty field values
  QG-307-5: Schema publish idempotent (republish same version = no duplicate)
  QG-307-6: Cross-tenant schema read returns DataProcessResult.Failure
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T308 — Submission Intake Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T308
ARCHETYPE:          INGESTION
ENTRY:              HTTP POST from form client (web/mobile/API)
PURPOSE:            Captures raw form submission, deduplicates, and enqueues
                    for pipeline processing. Returns immediately — processing async.
DISTINCT FROM:      T169 (Webhook Ingestion — external push, HMAC verify)
                    T312 (Entry Persistence — downstream stage, not intake)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F864 ISubmissionIntakeService   — dedup + enqueue
  F865 IFieldNormalizerService    — raw payload normalization
  F861 IFormSessionService        — validates active session

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F864 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) + DATABASE FABRIC (Redis dedup)
  F865 → CORE FABRIC (ObjectProcessor)
  F861 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis sessions)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: Generates ISubmissionIntakeService — QUEUE FABRIC enqueue + Redis dedup
  AF-4: RAG retrieves SK-190 (submission stage-gate pattern)
  AF-7: DNA-1 (Dictionary), DNA-5 (tenantId on dedup key), DNA-3 (no throw)
  AF-9: Validates iron rules, quality gates

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-382: Anti-spam (F867) must run before entry persist (F868) — this stage pre-spam
  CF-383: Wizard session state isolated per tenant (F861)

IRON RULES:
  IR-308-1: Raw payload enqueued to QUEUE FABRIC before any synchronous processing
  IR-308-2: Dedup key = hash(formId + visitorId + payload) checked in Redis before enqueue
  IR-308-3: Return 202 Accepted immediately — never block on pipeline completion
  IR-308-4: tenantId on every Redis dedup key namespace
  IR-308-5: No typed SubmissionRequest class — raw payload as Dictionary
  IR-308-6: Session validation via F861 before intake
  IR-308-7: Duplicate submission returns DataProcessResult.Failure with DUPLICATE_SUBMISSION code
  IR-308-8: ISubmissionIntakeService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-308-1: 10k concurrent submissions handled without queue overflow (ST-216)
  QG-308-2: Dedup detection rate > 99.9% for identical replayed submissions
  QG-308-3: Response latency < 100ms (queue enqueue, not full pipeline)
  QG-308-4: Cross-tenant submission leak returns DataProcessResult.Failure
  QG-308-5: Intake handles malformed payload with DataProcessResult.Failure (no 500)
  QG-308-6: Redis dedup TTL matches submission idempotency window (config-driven)
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T309 — Field Normalization Step
```
TASK TYPE:          T309
ARCHETYPE:          TRANSFORM
ENTRY:              Fires from QUEUE FABRIC after submission intake
PURPOSE:            Normalizes raw field values per schema type definitions,
                    strips disallowed fields, resolves file references.
DISTINCT FROM:      T171 (Raw-to-Staging Transform — DWH zones, not form fields)
                    T323 (Data Mapping Transform — connector payload mapping)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F865 IFieldNormalizerService    — core normalization
  F852 IFormSchemaService         — schema lookup for type coercion

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F865 → CORE FABRIC (ObjectProcessor)
  F852 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch)

IRON RULES:
  IR-309-1: Output is dict[str, Any] — no typed NormalizedSubmission
  IR-309-2: Unknown fields stripped (not passed through) — security boundary
  IR-309-3: File references resolved to fileId tokens — never raw file bytes in payload
  IR-309-4: Schema lookup via F852 CreateAsync() — never direct ES client
  IR-309-5: tenantId on schema lookup
  IR-309-6: Normalization failure returns DataProcessResult.Failure with field errors
  IR-309-7: Empty fields skipped via build_search_filter (DNA-2)
  IR-309-8: Normalization is idempotent — running twice produces same output

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-309-1: All 40 field types (text, date, file, payment, repeater...) normalize correctly
  QG-309-2: Cross-field normalization preserves relationships (e.g. start < end date)
  QG-309-3: File ref normalization tokens are tenant-scoped
  QG-309-4: Malicious field injection (XSS payloads) neutralized at normalization
  QG-309-5: Idempotency: float normalization produces identical Dictionary output
  QG-309-6: Performance: 10k field normalizations/sec on standard hardware
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T310 — Submission Validation Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T310
ARCHETYPE:          VALIDATION
ENTRY:              Fires from QUEUE FABRIC after field normalization (T309)
PURPOSE:            Validates normalized submission against form rules: required
                    fields, regex patterns, cross-field rules, business constraints.
DISTINCT FROM:      T311 (Anti-Spam — security gate, not business validation)
                    T172 (Schema Drift Detection — DWH schema, not form fields)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F866 ISubmissionValidatorService  — rule evaluation
  F852 IFormSchemaService           — rule source lookup
  F854 IConditionalLogicService     — conditional required-field evaluation

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F866 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + RAG FABRIC (hybrid strategy)
  F852 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch)
  F854 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC

IRON RULES:
  IR-310-1: Validation runs AFTER normalization (T309) — never on raw payload
  IR-310-2: Validation rules loaded from ES — never hardcoded in service
  IR-310-3: Conditional required-field evaluation via F854 (not inline if/else)
  IR-310-4: All validation errors collected — never fail-fast on first error
  IR-310-5: Result is DataProcessResult[Dictionary] with errors[] array
  IR-310-6: tenantId on all ES rule lookups
  IR-310-7: RAG fabric retrieves validation patterns via IRagService.SearchAsync()
  IR-310-8: ISubmissionValidatorService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-310-1: All required field types validated (text, email, phone, date, file)
  QG-310-2: Regex validation patterns configurable per field (FREEDOM config)
  QG-310-3: Cross-field rules (e.g. end date after start date) evaluated correctly
  QG-310-4: Validation result errors array includes field IDs for UI highlighting
  QG-310-5: Conditional required fields re-evaluated on each step change
  QG-310-6: Validation performant: < 20ms for 100-field form
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T311 — Anti-Spam Security Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T311
ARCHETYPE:          VALIDATION
ENTRY:              Fires from QUEUE FABRIC after validation (T310), before persist
PURPOSE:            Composite spam/security check: honeypot, reCAPTCHA, rate limit,
                    AI scoring. Blocks submission if spam score exceeds threshold.
DISTINCT FROM:      T310 (Business Validation — field rules, not security)
                    T324 (File Upload Security — virus scan, not submission gate)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F867 IAntiSpamService   — composite spam gate

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F867 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (spam scoring) + DATABASE FABRIC (Redis rate limit)

IRON RULES:
  IR-311-1: Anti-spam runs BEFORE entry persistence (T312) — never after (DR-135, CF-382)
  IR-311-2: Composite gate: honeypot + reCAPTCHA + AI scoring — all three evaluated
  IR-311-3: Rate limit check via Redis before AI scoring (avoid wasted AI calls)
  IR-311-4: Spam result includes {score, blocked, reason} as Dictionary fields
  IR-311-5: Spam block returns DataProcessResult.Failure — submission not persisted
  IR-311-6: tenantId on Redis rate limit key namespace
  IR-311-7: AI scoring via IAiProvider.GenerateAsync() — never direct LLM import
  IR-311-8: Spam thresholds configurable per form (FREEDOM config)

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-311-1: False positive rate < 0.1% on known-legitimate submissions (ST-217)
  QG-311-2: Rate limit triggers correctly at configured threshold
  QG-311-3: AI spam score returned as normalized 0.0-1.0 float in result Dictionary
  QG-311-4: reCAPTCHA verification returns DataProcessResult.Failure on invalid token
  QG-311-5: Honeypot detection does not add latency > 5ms
  QG-311-6: Spam gate bypass attempt (missing gate stage) = BUILD FAILURE via AF-7
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T312 — Entry Persistence Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T312
ARCHETYPE:          PERSISTENCE  ← FIRST USE (new archetype)
ENTRY:              Fires from QUEUE FABRIC after anti-spam gate (T311) clears
PURPOSE:            Persists validated, spam-cleared entry to ES (primary) and
                    PG (audit). Emits ENTRY_PERSISTED event AFTER storage.
DISTINCT FROM:      T313 (Partial Entry Save — Redis interim, not final persist)
                    T169 (Webhook Ingestion — external push capture, not form persist)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F868 IEntryPersistenceService  — ES primary + PG audit write
  F877 IEntryAuditService        — immutable audit log
  F871 IEntryStatusService       — initial status set (unread)

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F868 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES primary) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG audit)
  F877 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG WORM) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES)
  F871 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch)

IRON RULES:
  IR-312-1: Entry persisted to ES BEFORE ENTRY_PERSISTED event emitted (DR-134, INV-15-1)
  IR-312-2: entryId = Ulid.NewUlid() — generated at persist, not at intake
  IR-312-3: Entry stored as dict[str, Any] — no typed Entry class
  IR-312-4: tenantId written to every ES document and PG audit record
  IR-312-5: PG audit record written in same transaction scope as ES write
  IR-312-6: Initial status = "unread" set via F871 on persist
  IR-312-7: ENTRY_PERSISTED event payload contains entryId + formId + tenantId only
  IR-312-8: IEntryPersistenceService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-312-1: ES document and PG audit both written before event emitted
  QG-312-2: entryId uniqueness guaranteed across 1M concurrent submissions
  QG-312-3: Entry retrieval by entryId returns exact persisted Dictionary
  QG-312-4: Cross-tenant entry read returns DataProcessResult.Failure
  QG-312-5: Persist failure rolls back (no orphaned PG audit without ES entry)
  QG-312-6: Audit trail queryable by entryId + tenantId within 100ms
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T313 — Partial Entry Save Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T313
ARCHETYPE:          PERSISTENCE
ENTRY:              Fires when user clicks "Save & Continue" on multi-step form
PURPOSE:            Saves in-progress form data to Redis with TTL.
                    Issues resume token. Promotes to full entry on completion.
DISTINCT FROM:      T312 (Final persist — completed submissions, PG primary)
                    T176 (Redis session state — form session, not partial entry)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F870 IPartialEntryService   — Redis TTL + PG promotion
  F862 IMultiStepWizardService — current step state

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F870 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis TTL) + DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL)
  F862 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis)

IRON RULES:
  IR-313-1: Partial entry stored ONLY in Redis (not ES, not PG) until completion
  IR-313-2: Redis TTL ≤ 48 hours — never configurable above 48h (DR-142)
  IR-313-3: Resume token = cryptographically random 32-byte hex (not entryId)
  IR-313-4: Promotion to PG on complete — Redis key deleted after promotion
  IR-313-5: On TTL expiry, partial deleted without promotion (no orphaned PG record)
  IR-313-6: tenantId on Redis key namespace and PG promotion record
  IR-313-7: Partial data stored as Dictionary — no typed PartialEntry class
  IR-313-8: IPartialEntryService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-313-1: Resume token valid for configured TTL window (ST-218)
  QG-313-2: Partial promoted to full entry preserves all field values exactly
  QG-313-3: TTL expiry deletes Redis key without PG orphan (ST-218)
  QG-313-4: Cross-tenant resume token returns DataProcessResult.Failure
  QG-313-5: 1M concurrent partial entries handled within Redis memory budget
  QG-313-6: Resume flow reconstructs wizard step from partial data correctly
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T314 — Notification Dispatch Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T314
ARCHETYPE:          NOTIFICATION  ← FIRST USE (new archetype)
ENTRY:              Fires from QUEUE FABRIC after ENTRY_PERSISTED event
PURPOSE:            Resolves notification templates with merge tags, routes to
                    correct recipients per routing rules, delivers multi-channel.
DISTINCT FROM:      T315 (Integration Feed — CRM/webhook push, not notification)
                    T316 (Webhook Feed — single channel push, not multi-channel notify)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F878 INotificationTemplateService  — template retrieval
  F879 IMergeTagResolverService      — tag resolution + AI assist
  F880 INotificationRouterService    — routing rule evaluation
  F881 INotificationDeliveryService  — multi-channel delivery
  F882 INotificationAttachmentService — file/PDF attachments

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F878 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch)
  F879 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC
  F880 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (ES routing rules)
  F881 → QUEUE FABRIC (multi-channel Redis Streams)
  F882 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + F874 (scan gate)

IRON RULES:
  IR-314-1: Notification dispatched AFTER ENTRY_PERSISTED event — never before (DR-134)
  IR-314-2: Merge tags resolved via F879 — never str.Replace() on typed model fields
  IR-314-3: Routing rules loaded from ES — never hardcoded recipient lists
  IR-314-4: Multi-channel delivery via QUEUE FABRIC — never synchronous HTTP in service
  IR-314-5: File attachment requires F874 MarkClean before attach (DR-141)
  IR-314-6: tenantId on all template and routing rule lookups
  IR-314-7: AI tag resolution via IAiProvider.GenerateAsync() — never direct LLM
  IR-314-8: INotificationDeliveryService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-314-1: Fan-out to 50 recipients from single entry (ST-219)
  QG-314-2: Merge tag resolution for all standard tags < 50ms
  QG-314-3: Unknown merge tags return DataProcessResult.Failure with missing-tag list
  QG-314-4: Channel routing applies conditional rules correctly (Finance team test)
  QG-314-5: PDF attachment generated + scanned before delivery
  QG-314-6: Delivery failure queued to DLQ with retry schedule
```

## TASK TYPE: T315 — Integration Feed Execution Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T315
ARCHETYPE:          FEED_EXECUTION  ← FIRST USE (new archetype)
ENTRY:              Fires from QUEUE FABRIC after notification dispatch (T314)
PURPOSE:            Evaluates feed conditions, maps data, executes integration
                    (CRM, mailing list, ticketing) with idempotency guarantee.
DISTINCT FROM:      T316 (Webhook Feed — single webhook push, not full feed)
                    T314 (Notification — internal email/SMS, not external integration)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F883 IFeedDefinitionService    — feed config lookup
  F884 IFeedConditionService     — condition evaluation
  F885 IFeedExecutorService      — idempotent execution + run log
  F887 ICrmFeedService           — CRM integration
  F888 IMailingListFeedService   — mailing list sync
  F898 IDataMappingService       — payload transformation

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F883 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch)
  F884 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC
  F885 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG run log)
  F887 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (ES connector config)
  F888 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (ES list config)
  F898 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES mapping) + AI ENGINE FABRIC

IRON RULES:
  IR-315-1: Feed condition evaluated BEFORE execution — never execute unconditionally
  IR-315-2: Redis idempotency key registered BEFORE feed execution (DR-138, INV-15-4)
  IR-315-3: Data mapping validated via F898 BEFORE push to external system (CF-396)
  IR-315-4: Feed execution result recorded in PG run log regardless of outcome
  IR-315-5: CRM payload as Dictionary — no typed CrmContact class (DNA-1)
  IR-315-6: tenantId on all condition rule and run log queries
  IR-315-7: Feed failure queued to DLQ — never lost (CF-388)
  IR-315-8: IFeedExecutorService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-315-1: CRM duplicate detection via idempotency key (ST-220)
  QG-315-2: Condition evaluation supports AND/OR/NOT operators
  QG-315-3: Field mapping transforms all data types (str, date, number, bool)
  QG-315-4: Run log queryable by feedId + entryId + tenantId
  QG-315-5: Feed replay (F885.ReplayRun) re-executes correctly with same outcome
  QG-315-6: Cross-tenant feed config read returns DataProcessResult.Failure
```

## TASK TYPE: T316 — Webhook Feed Dispatch Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T316
ARCHETYPE:          FEED_EXECUTION
ENTRY:              Fires from QUEUE FABRIC for webhook-type feeds
PURPOSE:            Sends HTTP POST to configured webhook endpoint with
                    mapped entry data. Retry with backoff on failure.
DISTINCT FROM:      T169 (Webhook Ingestion — inbound HMAC verify, not outbound push)
                    T315 (Full Feed — multi-integration, not single webhook)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F886 IWebhookFeedService    — outbound webhook
  F885 IFeedExecutorService   — idempotency + run log
  F898 IDataMappingService    — payload mapping

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F886 → QUEUE FABRIC + CORE FABRIC (HTTP)
  F885 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG)
  F898 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + AI ENGINE FABRIC

IRON RULES:
  IR-316-1: Webhook sent via QUEUE FABRIC — never synchronous HTTP in service
  IR-316-2: Idempotency key prevents duplicate webhook sends (CF-388)
  IR-316-3: Retry with exponential backoff — max 5 attempts via DLQ
  IR-316-4: Webhook payload mapped via F898 — no raw entry Dictionary sent directly
  IR-316-5: Endpoint URL stored in ES feed config — never hardcoded
  IR-316-6: tenantId included in webhook headers (X-Tenant-Id)
  IR-316-7: HTTP failure returns DataProcessResult.Failure with status + body
  IR-316-8: IWebhookFeedService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-316-1: Retry storm handled — DLQ overflow prevention (ST-221)
  QG-316-2: Idempotency: webhook not sent twice for same entryId + feedId
  QG-316-3: Endpoint verification validates URL format + reachability
  QG-316-4: Webhook payload includes X-Signature-256 HMAC header
  QG-316-5: Timeout handling: 30s timeout returns failure, not hang
  QG-316-6: Run history (F899) records all webhook attempts including failures
```

## TASK TYPE: T317 — Payment Feed Processing Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T317
ARCHETYPE:          ORCHESTRATION
ENTRY:              Fires from QUEUE FABRIC after PaymentStateChanged event
PURPOSE:            Processes payment state transitions (pending→captured→failed→refunded),
                    updates entry, triggers downstream feeds on payment success.
DISTINCT FROM:      T315 (Generic Feed — non-payment integrations)
                    T312 (Entry Persist — initial save, not payment state update)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F889 IPaymentFeedService    — payment state + PG log
  F868 IEntryPersistenceService — entry update with payment state
  F885 IFeedExecutorService   — downstream feed trigger

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F889 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG payment log)
  F868 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG audit)
  F885 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG)

IRON RULES:
  IR-317-1: Payment feed runs LAST in feed chain after other integrations (CF-389)
  IR-317-2: Payment state transitions follow allowed state machine (pending→captured only)
  IR-317-3: Entry updated with payment state BEFORE downstream feeds triggered
  IR-317-4: PG payment log written with gatewayRef, amount, currency as Dictionary
  IR-317-5: tenantId on all payment log and entry update records
  IR-317-6: Refund state triggers refund-specific notification (F878 template)
  IR-317-7: Payment state stored as Dictionary {status, gatewayRef, amount, currency}
  IR-317-8: IPaymentFeedService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-317-1: State machine rejects invalid transitions (captured→pending)
  QG-317-2: Double-capture prevention via idempotency key (gatewayRef)
  QG-317-3: Refund triggers correct downstream notification template
  QG-317-4: Payment log query by gatewayRef + tenantId returns correct record
  QG-317-5: Failed payment returns DataProcessResult.Failure with gateway error
  QG-317-6: Payment feed isolated per tenant (no cross-tenant payment data)
```

## TASK TYPE: T318 — Recipe Definition Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T318
ARCHETYPE:          AUTHORING
ENTRY:              Fires when admin creates/edits automation recipe in builder UI
PURPOSE:            Validates, stores, and activates automation recipe DAG
                    (trigger + steps + conditions) in Elasticsearch.
DISTINCT FROM:      T307 (Form Schema Authoring — form definition, not automation recipe)
                    T319 (Recipe Trigger Evaluation — runtime execution, not authoring)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F890 IRecipeDefinitionService  — ES recipe DAG store
  F891 IRecipeTriggerService     — trigger registration
  F892 IRecipeStepResolverService — step catalog validation

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F890 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch)
  F891 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (ES)
  F892 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + FLOW ENGINE FABRIC

IRON RULES:
  IR-318-1: Recipe DAG stored as ES document — same pattern as SK-392 (RagStrategyRegistry) (DR-139)
  IR-318-2: Recipe as Dictionary {trigger, steps[], conditions{}} — no typed RecipeClass
  IR-318-3: All step types validated against step catalog (F892) before activation
  IR-318-4: Trigger registered in QUEUE FABRIC trigger index on activation
  IR-318-5: tenantId on all recipe and trigger records
  IR-318-6: Recipe activation idempotent — re-activate same recipe = no duplicate
  IR-318-7: Step resolver via FLOW ENGINE FABRIC — extends SK-392 (RagStrategyRegistry) pattern
  IR-318-8: IRecipeDefinitionService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-318-1: DAG cycle detection — circular step dependencies = BUILD FAILURE
  QG-318-2: All referenced factory interfaces exist in catalog before activation
  QG-318-3: Recipe with 20+ steps activates without timeout (ST-222)
  QG-318-4: Trigger registration idempotent across QUEUE FABRIC restarts
  QG-318-5: Cross-tenant recipe read returns DataProcessResult.Failure
  QG-318-6: Recipe rollback deactivates trigger cleanly
```

## TASK TYPE: T319 — Recipe Trigger Evaluation Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T319
ARCHETYPE:          ORCHESTRATION
ENTRY:              Fires when QUEUE FABRIC delivers a trigger event
PURPOSE:            Evaluates whether incoming event matches registered recipe triggers.
                    Starts execution for matching recipes with idempotency check.
DISTINCT FROM:      T320 (Recipe Step Execution — individual step, not trigger eval)
                    T505 (Trigger Registration — authoring time, not runtime eval)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F891 IRecipeTriggerService       — trigger evaluation
  F895 IRecipeIdempotencyService   — dedup before execution

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F891 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (ES)
  F895 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis dedup)

IRON RULES:
  IR-319-1: Idempotency check BEFORE trigger fires execution (CF-392, INV-15-4)
  IR-319-2: Trigger evaluation reads from ES — never hardcoded in listener
  IR-319-3: Multiple recipes can match single event — all started independently
  IR-319-4: Trigger hash = hash(eventType + sourceId + timestamp-window)
  IR-319-5: tenantId on all trigger index queries
  IR-319-6: Matched recipe IDs returned as list[str] in DataProcessResult
  IR-319-7: No-match result = DataProcessResult.Success with empty list
  IR-319-8: IRecipeTriggerService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-319-1: Trigger evaluation < 10ms for 1000-recipe catalog
  QG-319-2: Idempotency key prevents duplicate execution on event replay
  QG-319-3: All matching recipes started in parallel (not sequential)
  QG-319-4: Trigger condition supports formId, eventType, field-value filters
  QG-319-5: No-match scenario logged but not error
  QG-319-6: Cross-tenant trigger match returns DataProcessResult.Failure
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T320 — Recipe Step Execution Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T320
ARCHETYPE:          ORCHESTRATION
ENTRY:              Fires from QUEUE FABRIC per recipe step event
PURPOSE:            Executes individual recipe step: resolve factory, call action,
                    record result, advance to next step.
DISTINCT FROM:      T319 (Trigger Evaluation — recipe start, not step execution)
                    T321 (Retry Gate — failure recovery, not normal execution)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F892 IRecipeStepResolverService  — step factory resolution
  F893 IRecipeExecutionService     — state management + next-step advance

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F892 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + FLOW ENGINE FABRIC
  F893 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG)

IRON RULES:
  IR-320-1: Step result recorded in PG run state before next step triggered
  IR-320-2: Step factory resolved via CreateAsync() — never hardcoded class
  IR-320-3: Step failure triggers T321 (Retry) — not inline retry loop
  IR-320-4: Step execution atomic — partial step state not visible to other consumers
  IR-320-5: tenantId on all run state reads/writes
  IR-320-6: Step result as Dictionary — no typed StepResult class
  IR-320-7: Max concurrent steps per recipe = FREEDOM config (default 10)
  IR-320-8: IRecipeExecutionService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-320-1: Recipe chain of 20+ steps completes without timeout (ST-222)
  QG-320-2: Step ordering preserved — step N+1 never fires before step N completes
  QG-320-3: Step result data available to subsequent steps via run state
  QG-320-4: Concurrent recipe executions (same recipe, different entries) isolated
  QG-320-5: Run state queryable mid-execution for observability
  QG-320-6: Step with conditional branch evaluates branch at execution time
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T321 — Recipe Retry Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T321
ARCHETYPE:          DURABLE_SAGA
ENTRY:              Fires from DLQ when recipe step fails
PURPOSE:            Implements exponential backoff retry for failed recipe steps.
                    Marks recipe as failed after exhausting retry attempts.
DISTINCT FROM:      T508 (Recipe Retry Service — the service itself)
                    T170 (Backfill Saga — DWH backfill, not recipe retry)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F894 IRecipeRetryService        — DLQ + backoff scheduling
  F893 IRecipeExecutionService    — run state update on exhaustion

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F894 → QUEUE FABRIC (DLQ) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG backoff log)
  F893 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC (PG)

IRON RULES:
  IR-321-1: Retry via DLQ ONLY — never inline retry loop (CF-393)
  IR-321-2: Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s (configurable via FREEDOM)
  IR-321-3: Max retry attempts configurable per recipe step (FREEDOM config)
  IR-321-4: After max attempts: recipe marked FAILED in run state, alert emitted
  IR-321-5: Retry job records attempt number + error in PG backoff log
  IR-321-6: tenantId on all retry and run state records
  IR-321-7: Retry is idempotent — duplicate retry job for same step = no-op
  IR-321-8: IRecipeRetryService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-321-1: Retry backoff intervals respect configured schedule
  QG-321-2: Exhaustion triggers alert notification (F878) to admin
  QG-321-3: Retry storm prevention — max concurrent retries per tenant capped
  QG-321-4: DLQ overflow handled gracefully (ST-221 pattern)
  QG-321-5: Retry job idempotency prevents duplicate step execution
  QG-321-6: Failed recipe run queryable with full retry history
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T322 — Connector Auth Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T322
ARCHETYPE:          PROVISIONING
ENTRY:              Fires when admin connects an external service integration
PURPOSE:            Completes OAuth PKCE flow, stores encrypted tokens in PG,
                    validates connector capabilities.
DISTINCT FROM:      T167 (DWH Connector Registration — warehouse connectors)
                    T326 (Form Tenant Provision — tenant setup, not connector auth)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F897 IOAuthConnectorService      — PKCE flow + PG encrypted token store
  F896 IConnectorDefinitionService — connector capability validation

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F897 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG encrypted) + CORE FABRIC (HTTP)
  F896 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch)

IRON RULES:
  IR-322-1: OAuth tokens stored in PG encrypted ONLY (DR-140, INV-15-5, CF-395)
  IR-322-2: Tokens never written to ES, Redis, or logs
  IR-322-3: PKCE code verifier generated per auth session — never reused
  IR-322-4: Token refresh scheduled before expiry (not on-demand)
  IR-322-5: Connector credentials never cross tenant boundary (CF-400, INV-15-10)
  IR-322-6: tenantId on all PG token records + RLS enforced
  IR-322-7: Connector capabilities validated against F896 catalog before token store
  IR-322-8: IOAuthConnectorService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-322-1: OAuth token refresh race condition handled (ST-223)
  QG-322-2: Token encryption verified at PG column level (not application-level only)
  QG-322-3: PKCE verifier collision probability < 1 in 2^128
  QG-322-4: Cross-tenant connector credential read returns DataProcessResult.Failure
  QG-322-5: Token expiry handled gracefully — service retries with refreshed token
  QG-322-6: Revoked connector tokens cleaned up within 1 TTL window
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T323 — Data Mapping Transform Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T323
ARCHETYPE:          TRANSFORM
ENTRY:              Fires before any feed pushes to external system
PURPOSE:            Transforms form entry Dictionary to target system payload format
                    via ES mapping rules. AI-assisted mapping suggestion.
DISTINCT FROM:      T309 (Field Normalization — internal form normalization)
                    T171 (DWH Raw-to-Staging — warehouse transform zones)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F898 IDataMappingService   — mapping rules + AI suggestions + transform

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F898 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES mapping rules) + AI ENGINE FABRIC

IRON RULES:
  IR-323-1: Mapping rules loaded from ES — never hardcoded (DR-136 pattern for mappings)
  IR-323-2: Transform output is Dictionary — no typed CRM/Webhook payload class
  IR-323-3: AI mapping suggestions via IAiProvider.GenerateAsync()
  IR-323-4: Mapping validated BEFORE push to external system (CF-396)
  IR-323-5: tenantId on all mapping rule lookups
  IR-323-6: Missing required target field returns DataProcessResult.Failure
  IR-323-7: Mapping is idempotent — same input always produces same output
  IR-323-8: IDataMappingService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-323-1: All standard data types mapped correctly (str, date, number, bool, array)
  QG-323-2: AI suggestion produces valid mapping for 90%+ of standard form→CRM combos
  QG-323-3: Missing required target field detected before external API call
  QG-323-4: Mapping idempotency verified across 1000 transform runs
  QG-323-5: Mapping performance < 5ms per entry transformation
  QG-323-6: Mapping version tracked — rollback to previous version supported
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T324 — File Upload Security Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T324
ARCHETYPE:          VALIDATION
ENTRY:              Fires from QUEUE FABRIC after file upload complete
PURPOSE:            Virus scans uploaded file, quarantines on threat detection,
                    marks clean for signed URL generation.
DISTINCT FROM:      T311 (Anti-Spam — submission-level, not file-level)
                    T489 (Signed URL — post-scan, not scan itself)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F873 IFileUploadService   — file metadata
  F874 IVirusScanService    — async scan queue
  F875 ISignedUrlService    — post-clean URL generation

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F873 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES meta) + CORE FABRIC (storage)
  F874 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + QUEUE FABRIC
  F875 → CORE FABRIC (Auth) + DATABASE FABRIC (Redis TTL)

IRON RULES:
  IR-324-1: Signed URL generation ONLY after MarkClean (DR-141, INV-15-6, CF-401)
  IR-324-2: Scan via QUEUE FABRIC — never inline in upload handler
  IR-324-3: Quarantine applied immediately on threat detection
  IR-324-4: File metadata stored as Dictionary {fileId, status, mimeType, sizeBytes}
  IR-324-5: tenantId on all file metadata and scan job records
  IR-324-6: Scan timeout = BUILD FAILURE if IVirusScanService not called before URL
  IR-324-7: Scan result includes {status, threats[], scanJobId} as Dictionary
  IR-324-8: IVirusScanService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-324-1: Scan queue saturation handled gracefully (ST-224)
  QG-324-2: Quarantined file inaccessible — signed URL returns DataProcessResult.Failure
  QG-324-3: Clean file URL TTL configurable per form (FREEDOM config)
  QG-324-4: Scan handles all common MIME types (pdf, docx, xlsx, jpg, png, zip)
  QG-324-5: Malicious zip extraction (zip bomb) detected and quarantined
  QG-324-6: Scan result queryable by fileId + tenantId
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T325 — Entry Export Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T325
ARCHETYPE:          ACTIVATION
ENTRY:              Fires when admin requests entry export
PURPOSE:            Exports form entries to CSV/JSON/XLSX asynchronously.
                    Applies tenant-scoped filter. Generates secure download link.
DISTINCT FROM:      T177 (Reverse ETL Activation — outbound to SaaS, not export)
                    T486 (Export Service — the service itself)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F872 IEntryExportService   — async export job + download
  F875 ISignedUrlService     — secure download link

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F872 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) + QUEUE FABRIC
  F875 → CORE FABRIC (Auth) + DATABASE FABRIC (Redis TTL)

IRON RULES:
  IR-325-1: Export runs async via QUEUE FABRIC — never synchronous large query
  IR-325-2: Export filter always includes tenantId (DNA-5) — no cross-tenant data
  IR-325-3: Export file delivered via signed URL (F875) — not direct download
  IR-325-4: Export format (csv/json/xlsx) resolved via FREEDOM config
  IR-325-5: Export job status queryable by exportJobId + tenantId
  IR-325-6: Export result as Dictionary {exportJobId, status, downloadUrl, expiresAt}
  IR-325-7: Export file deleted after signed URL TTL expires
  IR-325-8: IEntryExportService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-325-1: 1M entry export completes within 5 minutes
  QG-325-2: Export file format validated against schema (no malformed CSV)
  QG-325-3: Cross-tenant filter bypass returns DataProcessResult.Failure
  QG-325-4: Download URL expires correctly at configured TTL
  QG-325-5: Concurrent exports (same form, different admins) isolated correctly
  QG-325-6: Export resumes correctly after QUEUE FABRIC restart
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T326 — Form Tenant Provision Gate
```
TASK TYPE:          T326
ARCHETYPE:          PROVISIONING
ENTRY:              Fires when new tenant is onboarded to form platform
PURPOSE:            Provisions tenant form space: ES namespacing, PG RLS policies,
                    Redis key prefixes, connector credential isolation.
DISTINCT FROM:      T167 (Connector Registration — DWH connector, not form tenant)
                    T178 (Warehouse Tenant Provision — DWH, not forms)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F900 IFormTenantIsolationService  — full tenant provision
  F852 IFormSchemaService           — tenant ES index setup
  F868 IEntryPersistenceService     — tenant entry index setup
  F897 IOAuthConnectorService       — connector credential isolation

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F900 → CORE FABRIC + MT ISOLATION FABRIC (Skill 11) + DATABASE FABRIC (PG RLS)
  F852 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch)
  F868 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG)
  F897 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG encrypted)

IRON RULES:
  IR-326-1: Inherits FLOW-08 MT model + FLOW-14 warehouse MT model (DR-143)
  IR-326-2: ES form schema index namespaced per tenant (CF-398)
  IR-326-3: PG RLS policies applied for entry reads/writes (CF-399)
  IR-326-4: Connector credentials isolated per tenant at PG level (CF-400)
  IR-326-5: Redis key prefix registered for tenant (no key collision risk)
  IR-326-6: Provision failure rolls back — no partial tenant state
  IR-326-7: tenantId on all provisioning records (obviously DNA-5)
  IR-326-8: IFormTenantIsolationService resolved via CreateAsync()

QUALITY GATES:
  QG-326-1: Provision completes in < 500ms (all 5 isolation planes)
  QG-326-2: Cross-tenant schema read returns DataProcessResult.Failure post-provision
  QG-326-3: PG RLS policy blocks cross-tenant entry read at DB level
  QG-326-4: Connector credential isolation verified (ST-225)
  QG-326-5: Provision idempotent — re-provisioning same tenant = no-op
  QG-326-6: Deprovision cleans all 5 isolation planes completely
```

## NEW ARCHETYPES

|-----------|-------------|---------|

| CMS_LIFECYCLE | Schema + content lifecycle state machine | T331–T335 |

| MEDIA_PIPELINE | Upload → transform → CDN pipeline | T344–T346 |

## TASK TYPE: T327 — Page Tree Node Mutation
```
TASK TYPE:    T327 — Page Tree Node Mutation
ARCHETYPE:    PAGE_BUILDER
ENTRY:        Fires when editor submits node add/move/delete/update operation
PURPOSE:      Atomically mutate page tree node with version snapshot + collaboration lock check
DISTINCT FROM: T328 (component registration), T329 (layout resolve) — T327 is the write path
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F901, F905, F906, F940
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F901 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — page tree storage
  F905 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) — collaboration lock broadcast
  F906 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) — editor session validation
  F940 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC — audit trail
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates MicroserviceBase-extended PageTreeMutationService
  AF-2 (Planning): decomposes into lock-check → snapshot → mutate → broadcast
  AF-4 (RAG): searches SK-201 (Page Tree Versioning) for reusable patterns
  AF-6 (Code Review): validates ETag/If-Match implementation
  AF-7 (Compliance): checks DNA-1 (no PageNode typed model), DNA-5 (tenantId on all ops)
  AF-8 (Security): validates lock token ownership before write
  AF-9 (Judge): checks IR-901-1, IR-905-2, DNA-5 compliance
BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-402: page tree write must not conflict with active publish job (F917)
  CF-403: lock token must belong to requesting userId + tenantId
MACHINE (fixed):
  - Version snapshot always before mutation
  - ETag/If-Match concurrency control
  - tenantId + siteId composite key
FREEDOM (configurable):
  - collab.lock_ttl_seconds
  - visual_editor.max_tree_depth
IRON RULES:
  IR-T327-1: NO mutation without valid session (F906) + lock token (F905)
  IR-T327-2: version history stored APPEND-ONLY (IR-901-1 enforced)
  IR-T327-3: node operations return DataProcessResult — never throw
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-T327-1: mutation latency P99 < 200ms
  QG-T327-2: version snapshot written before mutation confirmed
  QG-T327-3: audit event enqueued within same transaction window
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T328 — Component Registration
```
TASK TYPE:    T328 — Component Registration
ARCHETYPE:    PAGE_BUILDER
ENTRY:        Fires when developer/admin registers a new UI component
PURPOSE:      Validate, version, and register component in registry for use by all tenants
DISTINCT FROM: T327 (page tree mutation) — T328 is admin-level component catalog management
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F902, F909, F939, F940
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F902 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — component registry
  F909 → RAG FABRIC (Hybrid) — AI schema validation + field suggestions
  F939 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — RBAC permission check
  F940 → QUEUE FABRIC + DATABASE — audit
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates ComponentRegistrationService extending MicroserviceBase
  AF-3 (Prompt Library): retrieves component validation prompts
  AF-4 (RAG): searches SK-202 (JSON Schema 2020-12 patterns) for reuse
  AF-7 (Compliance): checks DNA-1 (no typed Component class)
  AF-9 (Judge): validates schema format = JSON Schema 2020-12 (DD-192)
BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-404: component ID must not conflict with existing F902 registry
MACHINE: component immutability once published; version increment on update
FREEDOM: allowed component categories (FREEDOM config)
IRON RULES:
  IR-T328-1: schema format MUST be JSON Schema 2020-12 (DD-192) — build failure otherwise
  IR-T328-2: system components (tenantId=SYSTEM) can only be registered by SYSTEM role
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  QG-T328-1: schema validation passes JSON Schema 2020-12 validator
  QG-T328-2: component has at least 1 required prop defined
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T329 — Responsive Layout Resolution
```
TASK TYPE:    T329 — Responsive Layout Resolution
ARCHETYPE:    PAGE_BUILDER
ENTRY:        Fires on breakpoint change or new node render request
PURPOSE:      Resolve layout constraints for a node across desktop/tablet/mobile breakpoints
DISTINCT FROM: T327 (write path) — T329 is read-only layout resolution
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F903, F901, F906
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F903 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) — breakpoint cache
  F901 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — page tree source of truth
  F906 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) — session context
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: generates LayoutResolutionService
  AF-7: checks DNA-2 (build_search_filter for empty breakpoint fields)
  AF-9: validates IR-903-2 (read-only resolution)
BFA VALIDATION: CF-402 (must not block active publish)
MACHINE: cache-first resolution; ES fallback on cache miss
FREEDOM: breakpoint definitions (FREEDOM: visual_editor.breakpoints)
IRON RULES:
  IR-T329-1: layout resolution NEVER mutates page tree
  IR-T329-2: cache miss must re-populate from ES source of truth
QUALITY GATES: QG-T329-1: resolution latency P99 < 50ms
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T330 — Live Preview Render
```
TASK TYPE:    T330 — Live Preview Render
ARCHETYPE:    PAGE_BUILDER
ENTRY:        Fires on page tree save or CMS data change in editor context
PURPOSE:      Real-time SSR preview combining page tree + CMS data via AI-enhanced rendering
DISTINCT FROM: T339 (publish-time SSG) — T330 is always SSR, never cached artifact
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F904, F901, F910, F920
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F904 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — live preview rendering
  F901 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — page tree
  F910 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — CMS data query
  F920 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — render runtime
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: generates LivePreviewService
  AF-5 (Multi-model): parallel preview generation for quality comparison
  AF-9: validates IR-904-1 (always SSR, never SSG artifact — DD-193)
BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-405: preview must not read unpublished content from other tenants
MACHINE: always SSR; never returns cached publish artifact
FREEDOM: preview_render.ai_provider; preview.timeout_ms
IRON RULES:
  IR-T330-1: preview ALWAYS SSR — build failure if any SSG cache lookup occurs
  IR-T330-2: preview isolated by tenantId + sessionId
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-T330-1: preview render < 2s P95
  QG-T330-2: no cross-tenant data in preview response
```

## TASK TYPE: T331 — Post Type Schema Registration
```
TASK TYPE:    T331 — Post Type Schema Registration
ARCHETYPE:    CMS_LIFECYCLE
ENTRY:        Fires when admin creates or updates a PostType collection definition
PURPOSE:      Register new PostType schema, generate form schema, validate backward compat
DISTINCT FROM: T328 (component registry) — T331 manages content schemas, not UI components
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F907, F909, F912, F939, F940
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F907 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — PostType registry
  F909 → RAG FABRIC (Hybrid) — AI schema assistance
  F912 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — collection relations
  F939 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — RBAC check
  F940 → QUEUE + DATABASE — audit
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: generates PostTypeRegistrationService
  AF-3: retrieves CMS schema prompts
  AF-4: searches SK-202 (JSON Schema patterns), SK-203 (CMS relation patterns)
  AF-7: checks DNA-1 (no typed PostType class), DNA-5 (tenantId)
  AF-9: validates DD-192 (JSON Schema 2020-12 mandatory)
BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-404: PostType ID must not collide with existing registry entries
  CF-406: schema change triggers cache invalidation (F918)
MACHINE: additive-only schema updates; version increment
FREEDOM: allowed field types (FREEDOM: cms.allowed_field_types)
IRON RULES:
  IR-T331-1: schema MUST be JSON Schema 2020-12 — enforced at engine level
  IR-T331-2: schema changes MUST be backward-compatible (no field removal)
  IR-T331-3: AI-suggested fields require admin approval (IR-909-2)
QUALITY GATES: QG-T331-1: schema validates against JSON Schema 2020-12 meta-schema
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T332 — Post Create / Update
```
TASK TYPE:    T332 — Post Create / Update
ARCHETYPE:    CMS_LIFECYCLE
ENTRY:        Fires when author saves post content (create or update)
PURPOSE:      Persist post with schema validation, version snapshot, and autosave support
DISTINCT FROM: T333 (status transition) — T332 is content write; T333 is lifecycle change
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F908, F909, F911, F913, F940
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F908 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES primary + PG journal)
  F909 → RAG FABRIC (Hybrid) — schema validation
  F911 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL) — version journal
  F913 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — rich editor draft
  F940 → QUEUE + DATABASE — audit
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: generates PostWriteService
  AF-4: searches SK-204 (Optimistic Concurrency), SK-201 (version journal)
  AF-6: reviews ETag/If-Match usage
  AF-7: DNA-1 (no Post typed model), DNA-2 (build_search_filter), DNA-5 (tenantId)
  AF-9: validates IR-908-2 (version concurrency), IR-911-1 (append-only journal)
BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-407: post slug must not duplicate existing published slugs (F927)
  CF-408: category/tag references must exist before post saved
MACHINE: version increment on every update; ETag concurrency
FREEDOM: autosave interval; max content blocks per post
IRON RULES:
  IR-T332-1: concurrent update without matching version = DataProcessResult 409
  IR-T332-2: version journal APPEND-ONLY — no UPDATE/DELETE
  IR-T332-3: publishAt stored as RFC 3339 (DD-194)
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-T332-1: save latency P99 < 500ms
  QG-T332-2: version snapshot confirmed before response
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T333 — Post Status Transition
```
TASK TYPE:    T333 — Post Status Transition
ARCHETYPE:    CMS_LIFECYCLE
ENTRY:        Fires when editorial action triggers status change (Draft→Published etc.)
PURPOSE:      Enforce valid state machine transitions with audit trail and downstream event fan-out
DISTINCT FROM: T332 (content write) — T333 is lifecycle state change only
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F908, F916, F917, F941, F940
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F908 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG) — post state update
  F916 → FLOW ENGINE FABRIC — editorial workflow gate
  F917 → QUEUE FABRIC — enqueue publish job if transitioning to Published
  F941 → QUEUE FABRIC — schedule trigger if transitioning to Scheduled
  F940 → QUEUE + DATABASE — audit
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: generates PostStatusTransitionService
  AF-2: decomposes into validate → gate → transition → fan-out
  AF-7: checks DNA-3 (invalid transition = DataProcessResult error, never throw)
  AF-9: validates IR-908-1 (only valid transitions — build failure for illegal path)
BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-409: transition to Published must trigger cache invalidation (CF-406 chain)
  CF-410: slug change within transition must create redirect (F927 → F928)
MACHINE: transition validity enforced by engine state machine
FREEDOM: approval requirements per transition (FREEDOM: cms.workflow_config)
IRON RULES:
  IR-T333-1: ONLY valid transitions: Draft→Published, Draft→Scheduled, 
              Scheduled→Published, Published→Archived — all others = BUILD FAILURE
  IR-T333-2: Published status triggers F917 publish job enqueue (idempotent)
  IR-T333-3: Scheduled status triggers F941 scheduler with RFC 3339 time
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-T333-1: transition events reach QUEUE FABRIC within 100ms
  QG-T333-2: invalid transition attempt returns structured DataProcessResult error
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T334 — Editorial Workflow Gate
```
TASK TYPE:    T334 — Editorial Workflow Gate
ARCHETYPE:    CMS_LIFECYCLE
ENTRY:        Fires on review request from author; blocks publish path until resolved
PURPOSE:      Configurable approval workflow: Author → Editor → Publisher
DISTINCT FROM: T333 (status transition) — T334 is the human approval gate within the flow
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F916, F939, F940, F941
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F916 → FLOW ENGINE FABRIC (IFlowOrchestrator) — workflow DAG execution
  F939 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — RBAC role check
  F940 → QUEUE + DATABASE — audit
  F941 → QUEUE FABRIC — timeout/reminder scheduler
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: generates EditorialWorkflowGateService
  AF-3: retrieves workflow prompts
  AF-9: validates workflow config = FREEDOM (IR-916-1), not hardcoded
BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-412: workflow config must be in FREEDOM ES index — not in code
MACHINE: workflow state transitions (Submitted→InReview→Approved/Rejected)
FREEDOM: approval steps, role requirements, timeout duration
IRON RULES:
  IR-T334-1: workflow config MUST come from FREEDOM config (F938) — never hardcoded
  IR-T334-2: workflow timeout triggers automatic escalation event
QUALITY GATES: QG-T334-1: workflow state durable — survives service restart
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T335 — CMS Feed Query
```
TASK TYPE:    T335 — CMS Feed Query
ARCHETYPE:    CMS_LIFECYCLE
ENTRY:        Fires on widget render requesting blog feed / related posts
PURPOSE:      Execute configurable CMS content queries for display widgets
DISTINCT FROM: T329 (layout resolution) — T335 is CMS data retrieval, not UI layout
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F910, F908, F942
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F910 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — query execution
  F908 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) — post data
  F942 → DATABASE + RAG FABRIC (ES + Vector) — semantic search/related posts
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: generates CmsFeedQueryService
  AF-4: searches SK-205 (Feed Query Patterns)
  AF-7: DNA-2 (build_search_filter for empty category/tag), DNA-5 (tenantId)
  AF-9: validates all query results tenant-scoped
BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-413: feed must never surface unpublished posts to public endpoints
MACHINE: always filter status=Published for public feeds
FREEDOM: query limit, sort order, cache TTL
IRON RULES:
  IR-T335-1: public feeds filter status=Published — never expose Draft/Scheduled
  IR-T335-2: tenantId on every ES query — build_search_filter enforces (DNA-2)
QUALITY GATES: QG-T335-1: feed query P99 < 100ms; QG-T335-2: no unpublished content in public response
```

## TASK TYPE: T336 — Publish Job Orchestration
```
TASK TYPE:    T336 — Publish Job Orchestration
ARCHETYPE:    PUBLISH_SAGA
ENTRY:        Fires when post transitions to Published or publish job dequeued
PURPOSE:      Orchestrate full publish pipeline: render → CDN deploy → cache invalidate → sitemap → index
DISTINCT FROM: T333 (status transition trigger) — T336 is the pipeline execution after trigger
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F917, F919, F918, F921, F942, F940
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F917 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) — job lifecycle
  F919 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — SSG build trigger
  F918 → QUEUE + DATABASE FABRIC — cache invalidation
  F921 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — sitemap/RSS refresh
  F942 → DATABASE + RAG — search index update
  F940 → QUEUE + DATABASE — audit
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: generates PublishPipelineOrchestratorService
  AF-2: decomposes into 6 sequential steps with compensation on failure
  AF-4: searches SK-206 (Publish Saga Pattern), SK-207 (Idempotent Pipeline)
  AF-6: reviews idempotency-key usage
  AF-7: DNA-4 (MicroserviceBase), DNA-5 (tenantId on all ops)
  AF-8: security scan — validates CDN URL namespace isolation
  AF-9: validates IR-917-1 (idempotency), IR-917-3 (CloudEvents envelope)
BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-414: publish job must check BFA for cross-flow entity conflicts
  CF-415: concurrent publish jobs for same siteId prohibited
MACHINE: CloudEvents envelope; idempotency-key; compensation on partial failure
FREEDOM: publish pipeline steps enabled/disabled (FREEDOM: publishing.pipeline_steps)
IRON RULES:
  IR-T336-1: publish is idempotent — duplicate job = no-op (idempotency-key check)
  IR-T336-2: each pipeline step generates audit event via F940
  IR-T336-3: CloudEvents envelope on ALL job events (DD-194)
  IR-T336-4: failure at any step triggers compensation — rollback partial state
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-T336-1: full publish pipeline < 30s P95 (configurable)
  QG-T336-2: idempotency proven by duplicate-job test
  QG-T336-3: cache invalidation confirmed before job marked complete
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T337 — Scheduled Publish Trigger
```
TASK TYPE:    T337 — Scheduled Publish Trigger
ARCHETYPE:    PUBLISH_SAGA
ENTRY:        Fires at publishAt time by F941 ISchedulerService
PURPOSE:      Transition Scheduled post to Published and trigger publish pipeline
DISTINCT FROM: T336 (pipeline) — T337 is the time-based trigger
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F941, F908, F917
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F941 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) — scheduler event
  F908 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES + PG) — post status update
  F917 → QUEUE FABRIC — publish job enqueue
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: generates ScheduledPublishTriggerService
  AF-7: DNA-3 (timing race = DataProcessResult, never throw), DNA-5 (tenantId)
  AF-9: validates RFC 3339 timestamp (DD-194), idempotency
MACHINE: RFC 3339 timestamp; idempotency-key; Scheduled→Published transition
FREEDOM: scheduler.check_interval_seconds
IRON RULES:
  IR-T337-1: scheduler fires at or after publishAt — never before
  IR-T337-2: duplicate fire = idempotent (post already Published = no-op)
  IR-T337-3: publishAt in RFC 3339 format mandatory
QUALITY GATES: QG-T337-1: trigger delay < 60s from publishAt time
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T338 — Publish Rollback
```
TASK TYPE:    T338 — Publish Rollback
ARCHETYPE:    PUBLISH_SAGA
ENTRY:        Fires on admin rollback request to previous published version
PURPOSE:      Restore previous publish artifact without destroying history
DISTINCT FROM: T332 (content update) — T338 is a non-destructive publish pointer switch
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F922, F918, F919, F939, F940
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F922 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL) — version history
  F918 → QUEUE + DATABASE — cache invalidation
  F919 → AI ENGINE FABRIC — build artifact pointer switch
  F939 → DATABASE FABRIC — RBAC (publish.rollback permission)
  F940 → QUEUE + DATABASE — audit
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: generates PublishRollbackService
  AF-8: validates permission check (publish.rollback RBAC)
  AF-9: validates IR-922-1 (non-destructive rollback)
BFA VALIDATION: CF-416: rollback prohibited during active publish job
MACHINE: creates new rollback-publish version; immutable artifact preservation
IRON RULES:
  IR-T338-1: rollback is NON-DESTRUCTIVE — creates new version, never deletes
  IR-T338-2: requires publish.rollback RBAC permission (IR-922-2)
  IR-T338-3: rollback triggers cache invalidation (F918)
QUALITY GATES: QG-T338-1: rollback complete < 10s; QG-T338-2: version history preserved after rollback
```

```
TASK TYPE:    T339 — Static Site Generation
ARCHETYPE:    PUBLISH_SAGA
ENTRY:        Fires as part of publish pipeline (T336 step 2) or on manual rebuild
PURPOSE:      Generate static site artifacts for CDN delivery
DISTINCT FROM: T330 (live SSR preview) — T339 is SSG for published content only

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F919 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — AI-optimized build
  F920 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — render runtime
  F923 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) — design tokens
  F926 → QUEUE FABRIC — rebuild event coordination
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: generates StaticSiteGenerationService
  AF-5: parallel multi-model build optimization
  AF-9: validates DD-193 (SSG for published; SSR for preview)
MACHINE: SSG for published content only; artifacts immutable
IRON RULES:
  IR-T339-1: SSG artifacts tagged with tenantId + siteId + buildVersion
  IR-T339-2: SSG NEVER used for editor preview (DD-193)
QUALITY GATES: QG-T339-1: build artifacts deterministic for identical inputs
```

TASK TYPE:    T340 — Sitemap + RSS Refresh
ARCHETYPE:    PUBLISH_SAGA
ENTRY:        Fires as step in T336 publish pipeline (after CDN deploy)
PURPOSE:      Regenerate sitemap.xml and RSS feed for newly published/archived content
DISTINCT FROM: T339 (SSG) — T340 is sitemap/feed metadata, not page content

  F921 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch)

  F908 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) — published post data

  AF-9: validates IR-921-1 (regenerates on every publish)
MACHINE: regenerated on every publish event
IRON RULES:

  IR-T340-2: only Published posts appear in sitemap/RSS
