## TASK TYPE: T341 — Design Token Update + Propagation
```
TASK TYPE:    T341 — Design Token Update + Propagation
ARCHETYPE:    DESIGN_PROPAGATION
ENTRY:        Fires when admin saves new design token values
PURPOSE:      Update token store, compute affected pages, trigger partial rebuild
DISTINCT FROM: T342 (global style) — T341 is token-level; T342 is typography/spacing scale
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F923, F918, F926, F903, F940
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F923 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis) — token store + cache
  F918 → QUEUE + DATABASE — cache invalidation
  F926 → QUEUE FABRIC — rebuild trigger
  F903 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) — breakpoint cache invalidation
  F940 → QUEUE + DATABASE — audit
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: generates DesignTokenUpdateService
  AF-2: decomposes into update → invalidate → compute-scope → enqueue-rebuild
  AF-7: DNA-5 (tenantId on token cache keys)
  AF-9: validates CF-406 (token update must trigger cache invalidation)
BFA VALIDATION: CF-406 enforced here; CF-417: token update not allowed during active publish
MACHINE: token update always triggers partial rebuild scope computation
FREEDOM: design_system.token_rebuild_strategy (full vs partial)
IRON RULES:
  IR-T341-1: token update MUST trigger cache invalidation (F918) within same operation
  IR-T341-2: token change events published to QUEUE FABRIC BEFORE cache clear
  IR-T341-3: partial rebuild scope computed from dependency graph (IR-926-1)
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-T341-1: affected pages computed < 2s
  QG-T341-2: cache invalidation confirmed before rebuild enqueued
```

## TASK TYPE: T344 — Asset Upload + Processing
```
TASK TYPE:    T344 — Asset Upload + Processing
ARCHETYPE:    MEDIA_PIPELINE
ENTRY:        Fires when user initiates file upload in media library
PURPOSE:      Presigned URL upload → async transform pipeline → CDN registration
DISTINCT FROM: T345 (image transforms only) — T344 is full upload lifecycle
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F932, F933, F934, F935, F940
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F932 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) — upload lifecycle events
  F933 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — transforms + alt text
  F934 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) — CDN URL registration
  F935 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — media library record
  F940 → QUEUE + DATABASE — audit
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1: generates AssetUploadPipelineService
  AF-2: decomposes into initiate → upload → complete → transform → CDN → library
  AF-4: searches SK-208 (Media Pipeline Pattern)
  AF-7: DNA-5 (tenantId on all bucket paths)
  AF-8: validates file type + size limits
  AF-9: validates IR-932-2 (tenantId-scoped bucket path), IR-933-1 (immutable variants)
BFA VALIDATION: CF-418: asset IDs must not collide across tenants
MACHINE: async transforms; immutable variants; tenantId-scoped storage
FREEDOM: max upload size; allowed MIME types; transform quality settings
IRON RULES:
  IR-T344-1: presigned URL includes tenantId namespace (IR-932-2)
  IR-T344-2: transform variants stored as new assetId (IR-933-1) — original preserved
  IR-T344-3: transform pipeline runs async via QUEUE FABRIC
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-T344-1: presigned URL returned < 200ms
  QG-T344-2: WebP variant generated within 30s of upload
```

```
  QG-T346-2: all pages updated within 60s for < 1000 usages
```

## AF STATION MAPPING — FLOW-22 (11 × 20 task types)

| Task Type | AF-1 Genesis | AF-2 Plan | AF-3 Prompts | AF-4 RAG | AF-5 Multi-model | AF-6 Review | AF-7 Compliance | AF-8 Security | AF-9 Judge | AF-10 Merge | AF-11 Feedback |
|-----------|------------|-----------|-------------|---------|-----------------|-------------|----------------|--------------|-----------|------------|----------------|
| T327 | PageTreeMutationSvc | lock→snap→mutate→broadcast | editor prompts | SK-201 | — | ETag impl | DNA-1,DNA-5 | lock ownership | IR-901-1,CF-403 | — | latency score |
| T328 | ComponentRegSvc | validate→register | schema prompts | SK-202 | — | schema check | DNA-1 | — | DD-192,CF-404 | — | schema quality |
| T329 | LayoutResolutionSvc | — | layout prompts | SK-201 | — | read-only check | DNA-2 | — | IR-903-2 | — | latency |
| T330 | LivePreviewSvc | — | preview prompts | SK-209 | ✅ parallel preview | — | DNA-5 | tenant isolation | DD-193,CF-405 | ✅ quality | preview score |
| T331 | PostTypeRegSvc | validate→register | cms prompts | SK-202,SK-203 | — | additive-only | DNA-1,DNA-5 | — | DD-192,CF-404 | — | schema quality |
| T332 | PostWriteSvc | draft→validate→snapshot | content prompts | SK-204,SK-201 | — | ETag impl | DNA-1,DNA-2 | — | IR-908-2,CF-407 | — | write latency |
| T333 | PostTransitionSvc | validate→gate→transition→fanout | workflow prompts | SK-206 | — | state machine | DNA-3,DNA-5 | — | IR-908-1,CF-409 | — | transition latency |
| T334 | EditorialGateSvc | submit→review→approve/reject | approval prompts | SK-203 | — | — | DNA-4 | RBAC check | CF-412,IR-916-1 | — | approval time |
| T335 | CmsFeedQuerySvc | — | feed prompts | SK-205 | — | filter review | DNA-2,DNA-5 | — | CF-413,IR-T335-1 | — | query latency |
| T336 | PublishPipelineSvc | render→CDN→invalidate→sitemap→index | publish prompts | SK-206,SK-207 | — | idempotency | DNA-4,DNA-5 | CDN isolation | IR-917-1,CF-414 | ✅ merge | pipeline score |
| T337 | ScheduledPublishSvc | trigger→validate→transition | scheduler prompts | SK-207 | — | — | DNA-3 | — | DD-194,IR-T337-3 | — | timing accuracy |
| T338 | PublishRollbackSvc | validate→repoint→invalidate | rollback prompts | SK-206 | — | — | DNA-4 | RBAC check | IR-922-1,CF-416 | — | rollback time |
| T339 | StaticBuildSvc | tree→render→artifact | build prompts | SK-209 | ✅ build optimize | — | DNA-5 | — | DD-193,IR-T339-2 | ✅ quality | build quality |
| T340 | SitemapRssSvc | query→generate→submit | SEO prompts | SK-210 | — | slug check | DNA-5 | — | IR-921-1,CF-413 | — | coverage |
| T341 | DesignTokenUpdateSvc | update→invalidate→scope→rebuild | token prompts | SK-201 | — | cache review | DNA-5 | — | CF-406,CF-417 | — | propagation time |
| T342 | GlobalStylePropSvc | compute→propagate→queue-rebuild | style prompts | SK-201 | — | async check | DNA-5 | — | IR-924-1 | — | propagation scope |
| T343 | ThemeSwitchSvc | fork→swap→rebuild | theme prompts | SK-209 | — | — | DNA-5 | — | IR-925-2,IR-T343-2 | — | rebuild time |
| T344 | AssetUploadSvc | initiate→upload→transform→CDN | asset prompts | SK-208 | — | MIME validation | DNA-5 | file safety | IR-932-2,CF-418 | — | pipeline latency |
| T345 | ImageTransformSvc | transform→variant→CDN | transform prompts | SK-208 | ✅ AI quality | — | DNA-5 | alt-text safety | IR-933-1 | ✅ quality | transform quality |
| T346 | AssetReplacementSvc | preview→confirm→fanout→invalidate | replacement prompts | SK-208 | — | usage scan | DNA-5 | — | IR-936-2,CF-419 | — | fanout coverage |

# XIIGen TASK TYPES CATALOG — FLOW-23: Visual Editor Extended
## Delta File — T347–T366 only (20 new task types, 6 new templates)
## Extends: TASK_TYPES_CATALOG_MERGED.md (T1–T346, Templates 1–69)

---

## FLOW-23 TASK TYPE SUMMARY

| Range | Archetype | Domain |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| T347–T351 | TRANSFORMATION | Visual Canvas Core (node tree ops) |
| T352–T355 | ORCHESTRATION | Layout Engine (solver, breakpoints) |
| T356–T359 | INTEGRATION | Data Binding Bridge |
| T360–T362 | GUARDRAIL | Multi-Tenant Hardening |
| T363–T366 | GENERATION | Code Export & Deployment |

---

## TASK TYPE: T347 — Canvas Node Tree Composition Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** TRANSFORMATION
**ENTRY:** Fires when a flow definition targets `CANVAS_NODE_TREE` as output type
**PURPOSE:** Compose, persist, and version a UI node tree (Pages→Sections→Containers→Elements) for a given tenant. Enforces DNA-1 (no typed models) and DNA-5 (scope isolation) on all node documents.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T247 (FLOW-18 visual flow editor — logic nodes, not UI layout nodes)

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F945, F946, F951, F949 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F945 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (node tree documents)
- F946 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Redis (hot canvas state)
- F951 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (layer tree index)
- F949 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (version history)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-2 (Planning): decompose node tree into section/container/element subtasks
- AF-4 (RAG): retrieve SK-211 (NodeTreeService patterns), SK-212 (LayoutSolverService)
- AF-7 (Compliance): verify DNA-1 (Dictionary), DNA-5 (tenantId on every ES query)
- AF-9 (Judge): validate `schemaVersion` present; no `NodeTreeDocument` class; `build_search_filter` used

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-430: check canvas node IDs do not collide with FLOW-02 content node IDs
- CF-431: check `tenantId` scoping does not cross FLOW-01 user registration tenant boundary

**MACHINE (fixed):**
- Node persistence via `StoreDocument(dict[str, Any])` — DNA-1
- `tenantId` always injected into every query — DNA-5
- `schemaVersion` field required on every node document

**FREEDOM (configurable):**
- Default layout mode (FLEX_VERTICAL / GRID / ABSOLUTE) — admin-settable
- Max node depth (default: 10 levels)
- Version retention policy (default: 30 versions)

**IRON RULES:**
1. Node tree documents MUST use `dict[str, Any]` — no typed DTOs
2. Every database query MUST include `tenantId` filter — BUILD FAILURE if absent
3. `StoreDocument` return type MUST be `DataProcessResult[T]` — never throw for business logic
4. `parse_document` called on every retrieved document — no direct cast

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Node tree roundtrip: store → retrieve → validate schema integrity
- Version history: create 3 versions → verify rollback to v1
- Tenant isolation: two tenants → verify zero cross-tenant node visibility

---

## TASK TYPE: T348 — Symbol Library Reuse Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** TRANSFORMATION
**ENTRY:** Fires when a canvas node references a Symbol (reusable section: header, footer, blog card)
**PURPOSE:** Resolve, materialize, and scope symbol instances across the canvas. Symbols are stored once in F947, instantiated per tenant context. Changes to the symbol propagate to all instances.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T347 (raw node tree composition — no symbol resolution)

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F947, F948, F945, F952 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F947 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (symbol library index)
- F948 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (component variants)
- F945 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (node tree, materialized instances)
- F952 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) → Redis Streams (symbol update propagation events)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-4 (RAG): SK-211 (NodeTree patterns), SK-213 (ComponentConstraintService)
- AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-1, DNA-5 scoping on symbol library queries
- AF-9 (Judge): symbol instance count consistency; propagation event verified

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-432: symbol names must not conflict with FLOW-02 content template names
- CF-433: symbol update events via F952 must not collide with FLOW-04 notification events on same Redis Stream group

**MACHINE:** Symbol definition → instance materialization → propagation via Queue Fabric
**FREEDOM:** Symbol scope (global / tenant / project); propagation delay (sync/async)

**IRON RULES:**
1. Symbol definitions stored as `dict[str, Any]` — DNA-1
2. Symbol instances carry `symbolId` + `tenantId` + `instanceId` — DNA-5
3. Propagation MUST go through QUEUE FABRIC — never direct HTTP push to canvas clients
4. `DataProcessResult[T]` on all symbol resolve operations

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Update symbol → verify all instances receive propagation event within SLA
- Tenant isolation: Symbol from Tenant A not visible to Tenant B
- Variant resolution: 3 variants (featured/compact/hero) correctly resolved

---

## TASK TYPE: T349 — UI Layout Composition Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** TRANSFORMATION
**ENTRY:** Fires after T347 completes node tree; layout solver calculates bounding boxes, alignment candidates, and Flex/Grid constraints
**PURPOSE:** Deterministically resolve layout for a node tree given its `layoutMode`, padding, gap, and children constraints. Output is a resolved layout document stored back via DATABASE FABRIC.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T347 (persistence), T350 (breakpoint responsive overrides)

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F953, F956, F957, F958 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F953 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) → MicroserviceBase (pure layout computation)
- F956 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) → MicroserviceBase (alignment math)
- F957 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (constraint documents)
- F958 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) → MicroserviceBase (snap guide calculation)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-2 (Planning): decompose node tree into layout subtasks per container level
- AF-4 (RAG): SK-212 (LayoutSolverService patterns)
- AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-3 (DataProcessResult); DNA-4 (MicroserviceBase)
- AF-9 (Judge): layout idempotency; constraint evaluation correctness

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-434: layout solver output doc IDs must not overwrite FLOW-09 search result documents
- CF-435: snap guide events must not collide with FLOW-12 chat event stream keys

**MACHINE:** Flex/Grid layout math is deterministic — fixed algorithm
**FREEDOM:** Snap threshold (px), grid column defaults, alignment snap tolerance

**IRON RULES:**
1. Layout solver MUST extend MicroserviceBase — no standalone class
2. Layout result MUST be `DataProcessResult[dict[str, Any]]`
3. Constraint evaluation fetched via F957 (DATABASE FABRIC) — no hardcoded constraint map
4. `build_search_filter` used with `tenantId` + `nodeId` — DNA-2 + DNA-5

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Flex container with 5 children → correct bounding box calculation
- Constraint `lockLayout:true` → layout changes rejected by evaluator
- Snap guide: two nodes within threshold → snap transform applied

---

## TASK TYPE: T350 — Responsive Breakpoint Override Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** TRANSFORMATION
**ENTRY:** Fires when a designer switches breakpoint mode or when layout solver completes (T349)
**PURPOSE:** Apply per-breakpoint style patches on top of base node styles. Stores base style + override patches. Rendering picks correct patch at runtime. AI-assisted override suggestion via F954.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T349 (base layout solver, no breakpoint awareness)

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F954, F955, F953 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F954 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) → AiDispatcher (AI responsive suggestions)
- F955 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (breakpoint patch documents)
- F953 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) → MicroserviceBase (base layout reference)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-5 (Multi-model): AI models compete on best responsive layout suggestion for each breakpoint
- AF-9 (Judge): verify patch application order; verify base + patch = valid merged style
- AF-11 (Feedback): store model quality scores for responsive suggestion per breakpoint scenario

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-436: breakpoint patch documents must not conflict with FLOW-13 AI content pipeline style documents

**MACHINE:** Base style + patch merge algorithm is deterministic
**FREEDOM:** Breakpoint definitions (desktop/tablet/mobile or custom px values); AI suggestion acceptance threshold

**IRON RULES:**
1. Breakpoint patches stored as `dict[str, Any]` — DNA-1
2. Patch document MUST carry `tenantId` + `nodeId` + `breakpoint` key — DNA-5
3. AI suggestion is advisory — human or AF-9 approval required before storing
4. Merging base + patch MUST return `DataProcessResult[T]`

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Desktop → tablet → mobile: correct patch applied per breakpoint
- AI suggestion score ≥ 0.7 for acceptance threshold
- Invalid patch (removes required field) → rejected by constraint evaluator

---

## TASK TYPE: T351 — Canvas Version Control Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** ORCHESTRATION
**ENTRY:** Fires on canvas save, publish, or rollback command
**PURPOSE:** Manage canvas version history. Store immutable version snapshots in PostgreSQL via F949. Support rollback to any version. Diff between versions. Tag versions for publishing.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T347 (write path), T348 (symbol propagation — separate versioning concern)

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F949, F945, F952 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F949 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (version history — ACID transactions)
- F945 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (node tree current state)
- F952 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) → Redis Streams (version change events)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-5 tenant scoping on all version queries
- AF-9 (Judge): version immutability; rollback correctness; diff accuracy

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-437: version IDs must not collide with FLOW-18 flow version IDs

**MACHINE:** Version snapshot is immutable once created
**FREEDOM:** Max version retention count; auto-tag on publish

**IRON RULES:**
1. Version documents stored in PostgreSQL via DATABASE FABRIC — never direct Postgres import
2. Rollback MUST be atomic — `DataProcessResult[T]` wraps the transaction
3. Version event published to QUEUE FABRIC after commit — not before

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Create 5 versions → rollback to v2 → verify canvas state matches v2 snapshot
- Version event arrives in Redis Streams within 500ms of commit

---

## TASK TYPE: T352 — Multi-Model Layout Advisor Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** ORCHESTRATION
**ENTRY:** Fires when designer requests AI layout advice or when F954 generates responsive suggestions
**PURPOSE:** Run competing AI models (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini) to suggest optimal layout configurations. AF-10 merges outputs. AF-9 judges against design token constraints and iron rules.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T350 (applies a specific breakpoint patch), T349 (deterministic layout solver — no AI)

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F954, F977, F962 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F954 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) → AiDispatcher (multi-model orchestration)
- F977 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (design token constraints)
- F962 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) → AiDispatcher (content fallback generation)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-5 (Multi-model): 3+ models run in parallel on layout suggestion prompt
- AF-10 (Merge): aggregate layout suggestions; pick highest-scoring per quality criteria
- AF-9 (Judge): verify suggested layout respects `lockLayout` constraints and design token bounds
- AF-11 (Feedback): store winning model + score for layout advisory quality improvement

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-438: AI layout advisor output must not inject new factory dependencies not in F945–F981

**MACHINE:** Model selection, parallel run, merge algorithm
**FREEDOM:** Model set (which AI providers compete), scoring weights, acceptance threshold

**IRON RULES:**
1. AI models called via `IAiProvider.GenerateAsync()` — never `openai.chat()` directly
2. Layout suggestion output stored as `dict[str, Any]` — DNA-1
3. Suggestions treated as FREEDOM layer (advisory) — designer confirmation required for MACHINE changes

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- 3 models produce suggestions → AF-10 merges → score ≥ 0.7 layout accepted
- Model producing layout violating `lockLayout` constraint → rejected by AF-9

---

## TASK TYPE: T353 — Design Token Synchronization Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** TRANSFORMATION
**ENTRY:** Fires when global design tokens (colors, fonts, spacing, radii) are updated by admin
**PURPOSE:** Propagate updated design tokens to all canvas nodes, component variants, and running applications via F977. Ensures token changes are reflected without code deployment.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T349 (layout solver uses tokens, does not update them)

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F977, F948, F952, F945 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F977 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (design token index)
- F948 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (component variants that reference tokens)
- F952 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) → Redis Streams (token update events)
- F945 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (node tree token reference update)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-2 (build_search_filter for empty token fields skipped); DNA-5 tenant scope
- AF-9 (Judge): verify token propagation completeness — all nodes referencing changed token updated

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-439: token update events on F952 must not collide with FLOW-13 AI content style events

**MACHINE:** Token document schema; propagation event format
**FREEDOM:** Token inheritance rules (component overrides global); propagation scope (global/tenant/project)

**IRON RULES:**
1. Token documents stored as `dict[str, Any]` — DNA-1
2. Token propagation MUST go through QUEUE FABRIC (F952) — never direct HTTP sync
3. `build_search_filter` used for token queries — skips empty token fields — DNA-2

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Update primary color token → verify 100% of referencing nodes receive update event
- Component override token takes precedence over global token

---

## TASK TYPE: T354 — Alignment and Snapping Validation Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** GUARDRAIL
**ENTRY:** Fires during interactive canvas editing when nodes are moved or resized
**PURPOSE:** Real-time calculation of smart guides, edge/center snapping candidates, and distribution alignment. Ensures design consistency without locking the designer into rigid grids.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T349 (full layout solve — expensive), T354 is lightweight real-time guardrail

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F956, F958, F959 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F956 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) → MicroserviceBase (alignment math — pure computation)
- F958 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) → MicroserviceBase (snap guide calculation)
- F959 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Redis (z-index state — hot path)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-9 (Judge): snap guide accuracy; z-index conflict detection

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-440: z-index updates in F959 must not collide with FLOW-18 layer ordering documents

**MACHINE:** Snap threshold math; bounding box intersection detection
**FREEDOM:** Snap threshold (px); guide color; snap-to-grid vs snap-to-element toggle

**IRON RULES:**
1. Snap calculation MUST return `DataProcessResult[T]` — never throw on near-miss
2. Z-index operations via F959 (Redis via DATABASE FABRIC) — never direct Redis import

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Node within 4px of another node's edge → snap guide displayed, transform applied
- Z-index conflict (two nodes same z-index in same container) → warning emitted, not error

---

## TASK TYPE: T355 — Constraint Enforcement Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** GUARDRAIL
**ENTRY:** Fires before any node property update when `constraints.lockLayout` or role restrictions apply
**PURPOSE:** Enforce designer/editor role boundary. If `lockLayout:true`, reject layout changes from content-editor role. If `allowContentEdit:true`, allow text/image edits regardless of lock status.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T354 (visual snapping — no permission check), T360 (tenant-level isolation)

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F957, F967 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F957 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (constraint documents per node)
- F967 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) → MicroserviceBase (tenant isolation enforcer — role context)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-8 (Security): verify constraint enforcement not bypassable via API
- AF-9 (Judge): role boundary validation; constraint document integrity

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-441: constraint rules must not conflict with FLOW-01 permission model for user roles

**MACHINE:** `lockLayout` → reject layout change; `allowContentEdit` → allow text/image
**FREEDOM:** Role names (designer/editor/admin) — configurable via FREEDOM layer; constraint inheritance (parent → child)

**IRON RULES:**
1. Constraint evaluation MUST happen before every write — never post-hoc
2. Role context from F967 (MicroserviceBase auth context) — never from request body
3. Constraint violation returns `DataProcessResult.Failure` — never throws

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Editor role + `lockLayout:true` → padding change rejected
- Editor role + `allowContentEdit:true` → text change accepted
- Designer role + any constraint → all changes accepted

---

## TASK TYPE: T356 — CMS Data Binding Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** INTEGRATION
**ENTRY:** Fires when a node has `bindings` properties referencing CMS fields (e.g., `$.skill_05.latest_post.title`)
**PURPOSE:** Resolve binding paths to actual CMS content records via DATABASE FABRIC. Populate node with live data. Handle missing/null fields via F962 fallback.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T351 (canvas versioning), T358 (repeater loop execution)

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F960, F962, F966, F965 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F960 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (CMS content documents)
- F962 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) → AiDispatcher (AI fallback content generation)
- F966 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) → MicroserviceBase (field formatting: date, clamp, truncate)
- F965 → RAG FABRIC (SK-389 (HybridRagStrategy)) → Hybrid strategy (binding validation patterns)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-4 (RAG): SK-214 (DataBindingBridgeService patterns) via F965
- AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-1, DNA-5; binding paths must carry tenantId context
- AF-9 (Judge): field resolution accuracy; fallback trigger conditions; format correctness

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-442: CMS binding queries via F960 must not conflict with FLOW-02 content management queries on same ES index
- CF-443: AI fallback via F962 must not collide with FLOW-13 AI content pipeline jobs

**MACHINE:** JSONPath binding resolution algorithm; null field → trigger fallback
**FREEDOM:** Fallback strategy (AI/static/empty); field format rules (date locale, clamp length); null handling policy

**IRON RULES:**
1. Binding resolution MUST use `SearchDocuments` via F960 DATABASE FABRIC — no direct ES query
2. Missing CMS field → F962 AI fallback or configured static fallback — never raw null in rendered output
3. All field formatting via F966 `DataProcessResult[T]` — no format exceptions propagate to canvas
4. Binding document carries `tenantId` — DNA-5

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Post with 200-char title → clamp applied correctly
- Missing `coverImage` field → AI fallback or placeholder rendered
- Two tenants: binding resolves to own-tenant content only

---

## TASK TYPE: T357 — Template Mode Preview Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** INTEGRATION
**ENTRY:** Fires when designer activates "Template Mode" on a page template
**PURPOSE:** Switch canvas context from static design to live content preview. Fetch real content records via F961. Bind to page template. Preview with multiple content records (short/int titles, missing cover). Exit preview returns to design state.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T356 (runtime binding at render time), T357 is design-time preview only

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F961, F960, F964, F946 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F961 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (template mode context documents)
- F960 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (real content records)
- F964 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) → Redis Streams (data context propagation to canvas clients)
- F946 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Redis (canvas hot state — preview mode flag)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-4 (RAG): SK-215 (BreakpointResolverService) for responsive preview
- AF-9 (Judge): preview mode does not mutate production canvas state

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-444: template mode preview context must not write to FLOW-02 production content indices

**MACHINE:** Preview reads real content; writes are session-isolated, not persisted to production
**FREEDOM:** Sample content set (latest/random/specific record); number of preview records

**IRON RULES:**
1. Template Mode is READ-ONLY against production content — no write via F960 in preview mode
2. Preview state stored in Redis hot state (F946) — isolated from production canvas state (F945 ES)
3. Canvas clients notified of mode change via QUEUE FABRIC (F964) — never direct WebSocket call

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Preview 10 posts with varying title lengths → layout holds for all
- Exit preview → canvas returns exactly to pre-preview design state
- Preview does not modify F960 content documents

---

## TASK TYPE: T358 — Repeater Loop Execution Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** TRANSFORMATION
**ENTRY:** Fires when a canvas section has `type: REPEATER` with a content query binding
**PURPOSE:** Execute the repeater block: run content query via F963, paginate results, render card template for each item, handle empty-state rendering.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T356 (single-field binding), T357 (template preview — no pagination logic)

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F963, F960, F964, F966 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F963 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (repeater content queries with pagination)
- F960 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (content records per item)
- F964 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) → Redis Streams (rendered item events)
- F966 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) → MicroserviceBase (field formatting per item)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-2 (Planning): decompose repeater into pagination steps
- AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-2 (build_search_filter for query — empty fields skipped); DNA-5 tenant scope
- AF-9 (Judge): pagination consistency; empty-state handling; total count accuracy

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-445: repeater query via F963 must not conflict with FLOW-09 search discovery queries on same ES index

**MACHINE:** Query → paginate → render card → next page loop
**FREEDOM:** Page size; sort order; filter fields; empty-state template

**IRON RULES:**
1. Repeater queries use `build_search_filter` — empty filter fields auto-skipped — DNA-2
2. Rendered items carry `tenantId` — DNA-5
3. Pagination cursor stored as `DataProcessResult[T]` — no exception on last page

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- 50 content items, page size 10 → 5 pages rendered correctly
- Zero items → empty-state template rendered (not null/error)
- Tenant A repeater never returns Tenant B items

---

## TASK TYPE: T359 — Binding Validation Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** GUARDRAIL
**ENTRY:** Fires before a binding path is saved to a node document
**PURPOSE:** Validate binding paths (`$.skill_XX.field`) against known skill output schemas via RAG FABRIC. Prevent broken bindings from reaching production canvas.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T356 (runtime resolution), T359 is pre-save validation guardrail

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F965, F960 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F965 → RAG FABRIC (SK-389 (HybridRagStrategy)) → Hybrid strategy (skill schema validation patterns)
- F960 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (binding validation against live CMS schema)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-4 (RAG): SK-221 (FlowStateMachineService — binding state transitions)
- AF-9 (Judge): binding path validity; schema compatibility

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-446: binding validator must recognize all skill output paths from FLOW-01–FLOW-22

**MACHINE:** JSONPath syntax validation; skill output schema registry
**FREEDOM:** Strict vs lenient binding validation; unknown skill warnings vs errors

**IRON RULES:**
1. Invalid binding path → `DataProcessResult.Failure` with clear error — never silent null
2. Validation uses RAG FABRIC — never hardcoded skill schema map

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Valid path `$.skill_05.post.title` → accepted
- Invalid path `$.skill_99.nonexistent.field` → rejected with descriptive error
- Cross-tenant binding attempt → rejected

---

## TASK TYPE: T360 — Tenant Isolation Enforcement Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** GUARDRAIL
**ENTRY:** Fires at the start of EVERY canvas operation (before T347–T359)
**PURPOSE:** Enforce tenant boundary on all canvas operations. Validate `tenantId` in request context matches authenticated user's tenant. Block cross-tenant access. Enforce quota limits.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T355 (role/constraint enforcement within a tenant), T360 is cross-tenant boundary

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F967, F968, F970 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F967 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) → MicroserviceBase (auth context, tenantId extraction)
- F968 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Redis (quota counters — rate limit hot path)
- F970 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Redis (idempotency store)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-8 (Security): OWASP API1 (BOLA/IDOR) check; OWASP API4 (quota abuse) check
- AF-9 (Judge): zero cross-tenant leakage; quota enforcement accuracy

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-447: tenant isolation gate must precede ALL FLOW-23 task type executions
- CF-448: quota limits must not conflict with FLOW-15 DevOps quotas on same tenant

**MACHINE:** tenantId validation; quota check; idempotency key deduplication
**FREEDOM:** Quota limits per tenant tier (pool/silo/bridge); idempotency TTL

**IRON RULES:**
1. Missing or mismatched `tenantId` → immediate `DataProcessResult.Failure` — BUILD FAILURE
2. Quota exceeded → `DataProcessResult.Failure(QuotaExceeded)` — never silent pass
3. Duplicate idempotency key → return cached result — never re-execute side effect

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Tenant A token → cannot access Tenant B canvas node
- Quota at limit → next request rejected with quota error
- Duplicate `Idempotency-Key` → returns first result, no duplicate stored

---

## TASK TYPE: T361 — CloudEvents Envelope Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** GUARDRAIL
**ENTRY:** Fires before any inter-service canvas event is published to QUEUE FABRIC
**PURPOSE:** Wrap all canvas async events in CloudEvents envelope with `tenantId`, `specversion`, `type`, `source`, `id`, and `time` attributes. Enforce deduplication via idempotency store.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T360 (synchronous isolation check), T361 wraps async events

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F969, F970, F952 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F969 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) → Redis Streams (CloudEvents envelope publisher)
- F970 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Redis (idempotency deduplication store)
- F952 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) → Redis Streams (canvas event bus)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-8 (Security): verify CloudEvents envelope integrity; `tenantId` extension attribute present
- AF-9 (Judge): envelope schema validity; dedupe correctness

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-449: CloudEvents from canvas must use distinct `source` prefix from FLOW-15 DevOps events

**MACHINE:** CloudEvents v1.0 envelope schema; deduplication on `id` + `tenantId`
**FREEDOM:** Event retention TTL; dead-letter queue behavior

**IRON RULES:**
1. Every canvas async event MUST carry CloudEvents envelope — no raw queue messages
2. `tenantId` MUST be a CloudEvents extension attribute — never in payload only
3. Duplicate event `id` + `tenantId` → acknowledged without re-processing — idempotent

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- 1000 events published → zero missing `tenantId` extension attributes
- Duplicate event ID → deduplicated; consumer receives exactly once

---

## TASK TYPE: T362 — Tenant Tiering & Restore Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** ORCHESTRATION
**ENTRY:** Fires when tenant tier graduation criteria met or restore request received
**PURPOSE:** Graduate tenant from shared schema (pool) → separate schema (bridge) → separate database (silo). Execute tenant-scoped restore from PITR snapshot. Verify RLS policies post-migration.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T360 (runtime isolation check), T362 is infrastructure-level tier migration

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F971, F973, F974 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F971 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (tenant tier registry — ACID migrations)
- F973 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (PITR restore operations)
- F974 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) → MicroserviceBase (OTLP telemetry on migration events)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-9 (Judge): post-migration isolation verification; RLS policy correctness; restore completeness

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-447: tier migration must not disrupt active canvas operations on same tenant
- CF-448: restore point must not overwrite FLOW-18 visual flow definitions in same DB

**MACHINE:** Migration steps are atomic (ACID); rollback plan defined before execution
**FREEDOM:** Graduation criteria (compliance, SLA, workload intensity); restore RPO/RTO targets

**IRON RULES:**
1. Migration MUST be atomic — `DataProcessResult[T]` wraps full migration transaction
2. No migration without verified backup snapshot
3. Post-migration RLS policy verification MUST pass before tenant resumes operations

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Pool → bridge migration: zero cross-tenant data exposure post-migration
- Restore to T-24h: canvas state matches snapshot; no data newer than restore point

---

## TASK TYPE: T363 — UI Code Export Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** GENERATION
**ENTRY:** Fires when designer triggers "Export to Code" from canvas
**PURPOSE:** Transform node tree into production-ready frontend code using AiDispatcher multi-model consensus. Output: React/Tailwind, Angular, or Vue — configurable via FREEDOM layer. AF-6 code review + AF-7 DNA compliance check before export.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T352 (layout advisor — suggestions), T363 generates deployable code

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F975, F976, F978, F979 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F975 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) → AiDispatcher (multi-model code generation)
- F976 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-385 (IAiProvider)) → IAiProvider (framework-specific adapter)
- F978 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) → AiDispatcher (AF-6 code review + AF-9 quality gate)
- F979 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (artifact registry — generated code versions)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-1 (Genesis): generate code from node tree spec
- AF-5 (Multi-model): 3 models compete on framework output quality
- AF-6 (Code Review): automated review of generated code
- AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-1/3/4 compliance on generated service code
- AF-9 (Judge): validate generated code compiles; no direct provider imports; MicroserviceBase extended
- AF-10 (Merge): combine best-scoring code segments per AF-9
- AF-11 (Feedback): store generation quality for improvement

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-449: generated artifact IDs must not collide with FLOW-18 code injection artifacts

**MACHINE:** Code generation pipeline (AF-1 → AF-5 → AF-6 → AF-7 → AF-9 → AF-10)
**FREEDOM:** Target framework (React/Angular/Vue); styling system (Tailwind/CSS Modules/SCSS); export format (component/page/full-app)

**IRON RULES:**
1. Generated service code MUST extend MicroserviceBase — DNA-4
2. No direct framework imports hardcoded — F976 framework adapter resolves via config
3. Generated code stored in artifact registry (F979) via DATABASE FABRIC — never on filesystem
4. AF-9 quality gate MUST PASS before export available to designer

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Generated code compiles without errors
- No typed models in generated service layer — `dict[str, Any]` only
- MicroserviceBase extended in all service classes
- AF-9 quality score ≥ 0.8 before export enabled

---

## TASK TYPE: T364 — Design Token Export Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** GENERATION
**ENTRY:** Fires alongside T363 or when admin triggers "Sync Design Tokens to Production"
**PURPOSE:** Export design tokens (colors, fonts, spacing, radii, shadows) from Elasticsearch index (F977) to framework-specific token files (CSS custom properties, Tailwind config, style-dictionary JSON). Sync to running applications without code deploy.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T353 (canvas-internal token propagation), T364 targets external code/app output

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F977, F976, F980 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F977 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (design token source of truth)
- F976 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-385 (IAiProvider)) → IAiProvider (framework adapter for token format conversion)
- F980 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) → Redis Streams (deployment pipeline dispatch)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-7 (Compliance): token document DNA-1 compliance; tenant scope
- AF-9 (Judge): token export completeness; no platform-specific values hardcoded

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-439: token sync events on F980 must not collide with FLOW-15 DevOps deployment events

**MACHINE:** Token source → format converter → deployment pipeline dispatch
**FREEDOM:** Target format (CSS vars/Tailwind/style-dictionary); deployment trigger (manual/auto on token change)

**IRON RULES:**
1. Token export MUST use F977 (DATABASE FABRIC) as source — never hardcoded token values
2. Deployment dispatch via F980 (QUEUE FABRIC) — never direct HTTP to CI/CD

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- All 50 design tokens exported correctly to CSS custom properties
- Token update in ES → deployment event dispatched within 2 seconds

---

## TASK TYPE: T365 — Artifact Registry & Rollback Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** ORCHESTRATION
**ENTRY:** Fires after T363 export completes or on rollback request
**PURPOSE:** Manage versioned artifact registry (F979) for all exported code. Support rollback to previous export version. Diff between artifact versions.
**DISTINCT FROM:** T351 (canvas design version control), T365 manages generated code artifact versions

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F979, F981, F980 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F979 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (artifact registry index)
- F981 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (rollback transaction — ACID)
- F980 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue)) → Redis Streams (deployment pipeline events)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-9 (Judge): rollback completeness; artifact integrity; deployment event correctness

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-449: artifact IDs must not conflict with FLOW-18 injection artifacts

**MACHINE:** Artifact immutability; rollback is atomic PostgreSQL transaction
**FREEDOM:** Artifact retention count; auto-rollback trigger conditions

**IRON RULES:**
1. Artifacts stored as `dict[str, Any]` in Elasticsearch — DNA-1
2. Rollback MUST be atomic via F981 (PostgreSQL via DATABASE FABRIC)
3. Post-rollback deployment event dispatched via QUEUE FABRIC

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Store 3 artifact versions → rollback to v1 → deployment event dispatched
- Artifact integrity hash verified before rollback executed

---

## TASK TYPE: T366 — Full Visual Editor Flow DAG Execution Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** ORCHESTRATION
**ENTRY:** Fires when the FlowOrchestrator (SK-392 (RagStrategyRegistry)) executes a FLOW-23 flow definition
**PURPOSE:** Orchestrate the full Visual Editor flow: T360 (tenant isolation) → T347 (node tree) → T349 (layout) → T350 (breakpoints) → T356 (data binding) → T355 (constraints) → T363 (code export) → T365 (artifact registry). State machine: Designing → Review → Published.
**DISTINCT FROM:** All individual task types above (T366 orchestrates them as a complete DAG)

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F945, F953, F960, F967, F975, F949 — resolved via `CreateAsync()`

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F945 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (node tree state)
- F953 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) → MicroserviceBase (layout solver)
- F960 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → Elasticsearch (CMS data binding)
- F967 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase)) → MicroserviceBase (tenant isolation)
- F975 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) → AiDispatcher (code export)
- F949 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase)) → PostgreSQL (flow state machine — version/publish)

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-2 (Planning): decompose full canvas flow into DAG steps
- AF-4 (RAG): all SK-211–SK-222 patterns available
- AF-9 (Judge): full flow execution completeness; state machine transition correctness
- AF-11 (Feedback): store full flow generation quality score

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-430 through CF-449: all FLOW-23 conflict rules checked before DAG execution
- FLOW-01–FLOW-22 backward compatibility verified

**MACHINE:** DAG step order; state machine transitions (Designing→Review→Published)
**FREEDOM:** State machine guard conditions; publish approval workflow (auto/manual)

**IRON RULES:**
1. T360 (tenant isolation) MUST be first step — BUILD FAILURE if skipped
2. T363 (code export) MUST pass AF-9 before state machine advances to Published
3. All intermediate step results wrapped in `DataProcessResult[T]`
4. Full DAG stored as flow-definition JSON in Elasticsearch via FlowOrchestrator (SK-392 (RagStrategyRegistry))

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- Full flow execution: Designing → Review → Published in under 60 seconds for typical canvas
- T360 failure → entire DAG aborts (no partial canvas corruption)
- Published canvas: generated code artifact stored + deployment event dispatched

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FLOW-24: Learning Calendar Extension (Personal AI Tutor)
# Factories F982–F1027 | Task Types T367–T374 | Families 140–146
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## TASK TYPE: T367 — Student Onboarding & Knowledge Diagnosis
**ARCHETYPE:** ORCHESTRATION  
**ENTRY:** Fires on CalendarExtensionInstalled event OR ManualOnboardingTriggered event  
**PURPOSE:** Collect student profile + consent → run baseline questionnaire + per-subject quizzes → build initial Knowledge Map → generate 30-day lesson plan → create recurring calendar task  
**DISTINCT FROM:** T47 (User Registration — identity only, no knowledge map) | T289 (Webhook delivery — event routing, no diagnosis)  

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F982, F983, F984, F985, F986, F987, F988, F989, F990, F991, F992, F993, F994, F1020, F1021, F1023, F1024 — resolved via CreateAsync()  

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
```
F982 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL) — student profile store
F983 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL) — consent profile, minor gate
F984 → CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase) — auth context
F985 → CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase) — multi-tenant scope enforcement
F986 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — locale/curriculum config
F987 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — curriculum pack (school/biz/dev)
F988 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) — knowledge map store
F989 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL) — questionnaire definitions
F990 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL) — baseline quiz store + attempt append
F991 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — diagnostic scoring (multi-model)
F992 → RAG FABRIC (Vector strategy) — gap pattern matching
F993 → DATABASE FABRIC (Neo4j) — prerequisite validation
F994 → DATABASE FABRIC (Neo4j) — curriculum graph traversal
F1020 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL) — lesson plan storage
F1021 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — 30-day plan generation
F1023 → CORE FABRIC (external connector) — Google Calendar event creation
F1024 → CORE FABRIC (external connector) — Outlook Calendar event creation
```

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
```
AF-1 (Genesis): Generates onboarding orchestration service extending MicroserviceBase
AF-2 (Planning): Decomposes into: [collect-profile → validate-consent → run-questionnaire → 
                   score-diagnosis → build-knowledge-map → generate-plan → create-calendar-task]
AF-3 (Prompt Library): Retrieves: onboarding-flow, knowledge-diagnosis, consent-gate prompts
AF-4 (RAG): Searches SK-223 (knowledge-map-builder), SK-224 (consent-gate), SK-226 (curriculum-graph)
AF-5 (Multi-model): Parallel scoring of questionnaire responses via F991
AF-6 (Code Review): Reviews: consent gate enforcement, minor flag handling, factory resolution
AF-7 (Compliance): Enforces DNA-1 (no typed models), DNA-5 (tenantId on all queries)
AF-8 (Security): Validates: consent gate cannot be bypassed, minor data isolation
AF-9 (Judge): Validates iron rules — see below
AF-10 (Merge): Combines multi-model diagnostic scores (conservative consensus)
AF-11 (Feedback): Stores onboarding completion rate, diagnosis accuracy metrics
```

**BFA VALIDATION:**
```
CF-458: StudentProfile entity overlaps with FLOW-01 (User Registration) — tenantId scope enforced
CF-459: KnowledgeMap entity NEW — register in BFA index
CF-460: CalendarTask creation conflicts with FLOW-03 (Event Creation) — calendar slot coordination
CF-461: ConsentProfile blocking gate — minor flag must propagate to all downstream tasks
```

**MACHINE / FREEDOM:**
```
MACHINE (fixed, non-negotiable):
  - Minor status = READ-ONLY server-side (F983). No client override.
  - Consent gate MUST fire before any questionnaire starts (F983.RequiresParentalConsentAsync)
  - Diagnostic scoring is server-side only (F991). No client-submitted scores.
  - tenantId present on every DB query (F985 enforces)
  - Graph traversal for prerequisites (F993) runs before plan generation (F1021)

FREEDOM (ES config, admin-configurable, no literals in code):
  - questionnaire_length (5/10/15 questions)
  - baseline_quiz_questions_per_subject
  - initial_plan_horizon_days (default 30)
  - calendar_provider (google|outlook|caldav) — per tenant config
  - curriculum_pack (school_grade_X|business_owner|developer)
```

**IRON RULES:**
```
IR-367-1: ConsentProfile MUST be checked before any data collection. BUILD FAILURE if bypassed.
IR-367-2: Minor = true triggers parental consent gate. No data collection before consent confirmed.
IR-367-3: KnowledgeMap score MUST be computed by F991 (server-side). Never accept client-supplied scores.
IR-367-4: CalendarTask creation uses calendar provider from tenant config (F986), not hardcoded Google/Outlook.
IR-367-5: All DB queries include tenantId. Zero-tenantId query = BUILD FAILURE.
IR-367-6: Attempt records (F990) are append-only. No UPDATE/DELETE. BUILD FAILURE if violated.
```

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9 Judge):**
```
QG-367-1: Knowledge Map produced with score for every configured subject (non-null)
QG-367-2: 30-day plan generated with at least 1 topic per active day
QG-367-3: Calendar task created and confirmed (or error logged to DLQ — not silent failure)
QG-367-4: Consent status stored in F983 before any questionnaire data persisted
QG-367-5: All factory calls use CreateAsync() — no direct class instantiation
```

## TASK TYPE: T368 — Daily Lesson Generation Pipeline
**ARCHETYPE:** ORCHESTRATION + AI_GENERATION  
**ENTRY:** Fires on DailyLessonScheduleTrigger (cron or CalendarFreeSlotFound event)  
**PURPOSE:** Select next topic from KnowledgeMap + prerequisite graph → fetch RAG atoms → compose micro-lesson via LLM → pass safety gate → publish lesson → upsert calendar event → notify student  
**DISTINCT FROM:** T367 (onboarding diagnosis — runs once) | T50 (Content Management — generic content, no curriculum graph) | T178 (AI Content Pipeline — no curriculum-aware difficulty adaptation)  

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F986, F988, F992, F993, F994, F995, F997, F998, F999, F1000, F1001, F1002, F1003, F1004, F1005, F1006, F1007, F1020, F1022, F1025, F1026 — resolved via CreateAsync()  

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
```
F986 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) — locale + difficulty config
F988 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) — read knowledge map (topic scores)
F992 → RAG FABRIC (Vector) — similar gap patterns
F993 → DATABASE FABRIC (Neo4j) — prerequisite readiness check
F994 → DATABASE FABRIC (Neo4j) — curriculum graph traversal
F995 → RAG FABRIC (Hybrid) — topic ontology search
F997 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — mastery path planning
F998 → RAG FABRIC (Vector) — learning atom retrieval (definitions/examples/stories)
F999 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — topic progress read
F1000 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — lesson composition (multi-model)
F1001 → RAG FABRIC (Vector) — RAG atom fetcher
F1002 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — safety gate (BLOCKING)
F1003 → DATABASE FABRIC (MongoDB) — publish lesson
F1004 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — localization
F1005 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — difficulty adaptation
F1006 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) — lesson catalog index
F1007 → DATABASE FABRIC (MongoDB) — lesson content variant store
F1020 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — lesson plan read
F1022 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) — calendar sync queue (durable)
F1025 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — quiet hours check
F1026 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) — reminder queue
```

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
```
AF-1 (Genesis): Generates DailyLessonGeneratorService extending MicroserviceBase
AF-2 (Planning): Decomposes into: [check-quiet-hours → select-topic → validate-prerequisites → 
                   fetch-atoms → compose-lesson → safety-gate → adapt-difficulty → 
                   publish → sync-calendar → enqueue-reminder]
AF-3 (Prompt Library): Retrieves: lesson-composer, safety-gate, difficulty-adapter prompts
AF-4 (RAG): SK-225 (lesson-composer), SK-226 (curriculum-graph-rag), SK-227 (difficulty-adapter)
AF-5 (Multi-model): Parallel lesson composition via F1000 (AiDispatcher)
AF-6 (Code Review): Reviews: safety gate placement (must be after compose, before publish)
AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-1 (no typed models), DNA-2 (empty field skip in lesson search)
AF-8 (Security): Minor flag check before composition; no LLM call before consent verified
AF-9 (Judge): Validates lesson published only after safety gate pass
AF-10 (Merge): Conservative lesson merge — judge selects highest quality + safety score
AF-11 (Feedback): Stores composition quality, safety gate pass rate, model performance
```

**BFA VALIDATION:**
```
CF-460: Calendar event upsert overlaps with FLOW-03 calendar slots — coordinate via F1022 queue
CF-462: LessonPublished event NEW — register in BFA index for FLOW-24 downstream
CF-463: Topic selection uses KnowledgeMap — must not conflict with F988 concurrent writes from T369
```

**MACHINE / FREEDOM:**
```
MACHINE:
  - Safety gate (F1002) MUST run before publish. No bypass.
  - Minor flag from F983 passed to F1002. Stricter policy applied. Non-negotiable.
  - Calendar sync goes through queue (F1022) — never direct API call in service code
  - RAG atoms fetched before LLM call (no hallucinated facts without retrieval)

FREEDOM:
  - lesson_length_minutes (5|7|10)
  - include_story (true|false), include_example_count (1|2|3)
  - composition_model (claude|openai|gemini — per tenant config)
  - rag_atom_count (3|5|7)
  - calendar_sync_provider (google|outlook|caldav)
  - reminder_policy (soft|hard|none)
```

**IRON RULES:**
```
IR-368-1: Safety gate (F1002) MUST precede publish (F1003). Reversed order = BUILD FAILURE.
IR-368-2: Minor=true → F1002 uses strict_minor policy. No configurable override.
IR-368-3: Lesson composition (F1000) calls AiDispatcher only — no direct openai/anthropic SDK.
IR-368-4: Calendar sync enqueued to QUEUE FABRIC (F1022) — never direct HTTP to Google/Outlook.
IR-368-5: RAG atoms (F1001) fetched before compose (F1000) — no compose without retrieval context.
IR-368-6: Lesson published with citation references for research-mode atoms (DD-179 pattern).
```

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
```
QG-368-1: Safety gate score ≥ threshold (FREEDOM config) before publish
QG-368-2: Lesson contains ≥ 1 example and ≥ 1 quiz question
QG-368-3: Difficulty matches student's current topic score from F988
QG-368-4: Calendar event created or DLQ entry written (no silent calendar failure)
QG-368-5: Lesson indexed in F1006 catalog (searchable)
```

## TASK TYPE: T369 — Quiz Submission → Hybrid Gamification Gate
**ARCHETYPE:** HYBRID_SYNC_ASYNC  
**ENTRY:** Fires on StudentSubmitsQuiz event  
**PURPOSE:** SYNC path: grade answers server-side → return points preview + badge preview ≤1s. ASYNC path: append ledger event → update streak → evaluate badges → update knowledge map → adapt tomorrow's plan → reschedule calendar if needed.  
**DISTINCT FROM:** T44 (basic gamification in FLOW-05) | T46 (spaced repetition scheduler — planning only, no grading)  

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F983, F988, F999, F1008, F1009, F1010, F1011, F1012, F1013, F1014, F1015, F1016, F1017, F1018, F1019, F1020, F1021, F1027 — resolved via CreateAsync()  

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
```
F983 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — consent + minor status for point display rules
F988 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) — knowledge map update (ASYNC)
F999 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — topic progress update (ASYNC)
F1008 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — N/A (quiz already generated; grader uses rubric)
F1009 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — quiz session state
F1010 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — attempt store (append-only, ASYNC)
F1011 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — server-side grader (SYNC step)
F1012 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — adaptation analysis (ASYNC)
F1013 → RAG FABRIC (FanOut) — quiz template lookup (pre-session)
F1014 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — ledger append event (ASYNC)
F1015 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) — badge definition lookup
F1016 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) — badge unlock check queue (ASYNC)
F1017 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis + PG) — streak update (ASYNC)
F1018 → CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase) — points calculation (SYNC step)
F1019 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis) — leaderboard update (ASYNC)
F1020 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — plan read for adaptation
F1021 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — tomorrow's plan adaptation (ASYNC)
F1027 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — streak protection check (ASYNC)
```

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
```
AF-1 (Genesis): Generates QuizCompletionService (hybrid sync/async) extending MicroserviceBase
AF-2 (Planning): Decomposes into SYNC path (F1011+F1018 → return preview) and ASYNC path (queue all state updates)
AF-4 (RAG): SK-228 (gamification-ledger), SK-229 (streak-manager), SK-224 (hybrid-sync-async)
AF-7 (Compliance): Validates hybrid contract — sync returns in ≤1s, async via queue
AF-8 (Security): Server-side grading gate — no client score accepted
AF-9 (Judge): Validates atomic ledger append, no UPDATE on attempts
```

**BFA VALIDATION:**
```
CF-464: KnowledgeMap write (F988) conflicts with T368 concurrent read — serialized via event order
CF-465: Ledger append (F1014) is append-only — BFA validates no UPDATE/DELETE on ledger table
CF-466: Streak update (F1017) uses timezone from F982 — CF ensures no clock manipulation
```

**MACHINE / FREEDOM:**
```
MACHINE:
  - F1011 grades server-side. Zero client score input accepted.
  - F1014 appends only. No UPDATE/DELETE on ledger.
  - F1010 attempts are immutable.
  - F1017 streak uses profile timezone (F982), not request header.
  - Hybrid sync/async boundary: SYNC returns in ≤1s. All state mutation = ASYNC.

FREEDOM:
  - base_points_per_correct, streak_multiplier_cap, bonus_thresholds (ES config via F1018)
  - badge_thresholds (ES config via F1015)
  - streak_protection_enabled, grace_period_hours (F1027 / F1017 config)
  - adaptation_trigger_threshold (% score at which plan adapts)
  - leaderboard_scope (class|school|tenant|global)
```

**IRON RULES:**
```
IR-369-1: SYNC path MUST NOT write to database (no ledger, no attempt store in sync path). Returns preview only.
IR-369-2: Points calculated by F1018 (MACHINE formula). Client-submitted pointsEarned = BUILD FAILURE.
IR-369-3: F1010 attempts: append-only. Any UPDATE/DELETE = BUILD FAILURE.
IR-369-4: F1014 ledger: append-only. Any UPDATE/DELETE = BUILD FAILURE.
IR-369-5: F1017 streak timezone = F982 profile timezone. Request-header timezone = BUILD FAILURE.
IR-369-6: ASYNC path uses QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) — all events durable, DLQ on failure.
```

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
```
QG-369-1: SYNC response contains points_preview and badge_preview within ≤1s
QG-369-2: ASYNC path events confirmed enqueued before SYNC returns
QG-369-3: Knowledge map updated with new topic score (ASYNC completion)
QG-369-4: Tomorrow's plan adapted if score < adaptation_trigger_threshold
QG-369-5: Ledger event includes quizAttemptId for audit trail
```

## TASK TYPE: T370 — Missed Lesson Recovery Flow
**ARCHETYPE:** EVENT_PROCESSING  
**ENTRY:** Fires on CalendarEventExpired event where lesson was not completed  
**PURPOSE:** Check quiet hours → offer 2-minute recap micro-lesson → reschedule next available slot → apply streak protection policy if configured  
**DISTINCT FROM:** T368 (lesson generation — creates new lesson) | T369 (quiz completion — gamification)  

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F982, F983, F1003, F1006, F1017, F1020, F1021, F1022, F1025, F1026, F1027 — resolved via CreateAsync()  

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
```
F982 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — student profile + timezone
F983 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — minor status for reminder policy
F1003 → DATABASE FABRIC (MongoDB) — retrieve existing published lesson
F1006 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) — catalog search for recap variant
F1017 → DATABASE FABRIC (Redis+PG) — streak state read
F1020 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — lesson plan read
F1021 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — reschedule plan recompute
F1022 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) — calendar sync queue
F1025 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — quiet hours check
F1026 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) — reminder escalation queue
F1027 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — streak protection application
```

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
```
AF-1 (Genesis): Generates MissedLessonRecoveryService extending MicroserviceBase
AF-2 (Planning): [check-quiet-hours → check-streak-protection → offer-recap → reschedule → enqueue-calendar-sync]
AF-4 (RAG): SK-229 (streak-manager), SK-230 (calendar-sync), SK-231 (reminder-orchestrator)
AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-5 scope isolation on all queries
AF-9 (Judge): Validates streak not broken if protection applies; no lesson re-created (reuses F1003)
```

**MACHINE / FREEDOM:**
```
MACHINE:
  - Streak protection applied by server (F1027), not client request
  - Quiet hours enforced before any reminder sent (F1025)
  - Recap uses existing published lesson (F1003) — no new AI generation for missed lesson

FREEDOM:
  - streak_protection_enabled (bool), protection_uses_per_month (int)
  - reminder_escalation_policy (soft|hard)
  - recap_duration_minutes
  - reschedule_lookahead_hours
```

**IRON RULES:**
```
IR-370-1: No new lesson composition for missed lesson. Recap reuses F1003 published content.
IR-370-2: F1025 quiet hours check BEFORE any reminder dispatch.
IR-370-3: F1027 streak protection consumes one use token — server tracks count. No client manipulation.
IR-370-4: Calendar reschedule via F1022 queue (durable) — no direct calendar API call.
```

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
```
QG-370-1: Quiet hours respected (no reminder during quiet period)
QG-370-2: Streak protection applied if eligible (uses_remaining > 0)
QG-370-3: Next lesson slot rescheduled in calendar (or DLQ if calendar unavailable)
```

## TASK TYPE: T372 — Developer Pack: Weekly Research Digest Builder
**ARCHETYPE:** ORCHESTRATION + AI_GENERATION  
**ENTRY:** Fires on WeeklyResearchScheduleTrigger (cron, e.g. Sunday 10:00 per config)  
**PURPOSE:** Pick research themes from developer knowledge map → ingest trusted sources whitelist → summarize + extract claims with citations → store as RAG atoms → publish weekly digest → schedule calendar block  
**DISTINCT FROM:** T368 (daily lesson — curriculum-driven) | T178 (AI content pipeline — no curriculum graph or citation requirement)  
**NOTE:** Developer domain pack only. Skipped for school/business packs unless enabled via FREEDOM config.  

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F986, F987, F988, F994, F997, F998, F1000, F1002, F1003, F1006, F1020, F1022, F1026 — resolved via CreateAsync()  

**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
```
F986 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) — locale/pack config
F987 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) — developer curriculum pack
F988 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) — developer knowledge map
F994 → DATABASE FABRIC (Neo4j) — skill tree traversal
F997 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — research theme selection
F998 → RAG FABRIC (Vector) — existing atom search (avoid duplication)
F1000 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — summarize + extract claims
F1002 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) — quality/citation gate (BLOCKING)
F1003 → DATABASE FABRIC (MongoDB) — publish digest
F1006 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) — index digest in lesson catalog
F1020 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — plan read
F1022 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) — calendar sync
F1026 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams) — weekly block reminder
```

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
```
AF-1 (Genesis): Generates WeeklyResearchDigestService extending MicroserviceBase
AF-3 (Prompt Library): Retrieves: research-summarizer, citation-extractor prompts
AF-4 (RAG): SK-226 (curriculum-graph-rag), SK-225 (lesson-composer) — reused
AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-1, DNA-5; citation gate enforced before publish
AF-9 (Judge): Validates citations present, no hallucinated facts, quality score ≥ threshold
```

**MACHINE / FREEDOM:**
```
MACHINE:
  - F1002 citation gate MUST pass — digest without citations = BUILD FAILURE
  - Research ingestion from whitelist sources only (no open web scraping without approval)
  - "Unknown if unsure" policy — AI must not present uncertain claims as facts

FREEDOM:
  - sources_whitelist (per tenant/org in ES config)
  - research_cadence (weekly|biweekly), trigger_time
  - themes_per_digest (3|5|7)
  - digest_length_minutes
```

**IRON RULES:**
```
IR-372-1: Every claim in digest MUST include sourceRef citation. No citation = safety gate fail = BUILD FAILURE.
IR-372-2: Sources must be in whitelist (F986 config). No hardcoded source URLs in service code.
IR-372-3: F1002 safety gate + citation gate both MUST pass before F1003 publish.
```

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
```

QG-372-2: Digest summarizes ≥ 3 research themes
QG-372-3: New atoms stored in F998 (RAG store updated for future lessons)
QG-372-4: Calendar block created for weekly review
```

```
F1020 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — current lesson plan

F1023 → CORE FABRIC (external connector) — Google Calendar free/busy query
F1024 → CORE FABRIC (external connector) — Outlook Calendar free/busy query
F1025 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG) — quiet hours
```

```
  - Calendar provider resolved from F982 profile (server config), not client header
  - Quiet hours (F1025) enforced unconditionally
  - All calendar API calls go through F1022 queue — never direct in service code

FREEDOM:

  - lesson_duration_minutes
```

**IRON RULES:**
```

IR-373-2: Calendar API calls enqueued via F1022 (Queue Fabric). No direct HTTP to calendar APIs.
IR-373-3: Quiet hours (F1025) always checked. No scheduling during quiet period.
```

## FLOW TEMPLATES — FLOW-24

### Template 76: student-onboarding-knowledge-map-v1
**Trigger:** CalendarExtensionInstalled | ManualOnboardingTriggered  
**DAG:**
```json
{
  "templateId": "student-onboarding-knowledge-map-v1",
  "version": "1.0",
  "trigger": "CalendarExtensionInstalled",
  "steps": [
```
