# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FLOW-25: Business Flow Arbiter — Engine Contracts
# Task Types: T375–T388 | Templates: 80–82
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## ENGINE CONTRACTS (T375–T388)

---

### TASK TYPE: T375 — Proposed Change Intake Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          INGESTION
ENTRY:              Fires on proposed change submission (entity/schema/flow/code change)
PURPOSE:            Parse raw change payload, normalize to canonical ProposedChange document,
                    persist immutably, emit parsed.change event to arbitration pipeline
DISTINCT FROM:      T308 (Form Submission Intake) — this is engine-level, not form-level
FACTORY DEPS:       F1028:IProposedChangeParserService
                    F1034:IChangeDocumentPersistenceService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1028 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — diff normalization)
                    F1034 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL — immutable change store)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generates IProposedChangeParserService per supported diff_format
  AF-7 Compliance:  Validates DNA-1 (Dictionary, not typed ProposedChange class)
  AF-9 Judge:       Checks: canonical change doc has required fields (change_type, diff, actor, tenant_id)
BFA VALIDATION:     CF-473: change_type must be one of: ENTITY_SCHEMA | FLOW_DAG | API_CONTRACT | CODE_AST
                    CF-474: proposed_change_id must be globally unique (UUID v4)
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          4 supported change types (fixed). Immutability of persisted record.
  FREEDOM:          Supported diff formats per change_type (admin-configurable)
IRON RULES:
  IR-375-1: ProposedChange document MUST be persisted to PG BEFORE any downstream event is emitted
  IR-375-2: diff_blob_ref MUST be content-addressed (sha256 hash) — no mutable blob references
  IR-375-3: actor field MUST be validated against auth context (from MicroserviceBase)
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - canonical change doc validates against JSON schema
  - diff_format is one of the supported set
  - immutable PG record written successfully
  - parsed.change event emitted to QUEUE FABRIC
```

---

### TASK TYPE: T376 — Dependency Index Query Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          IMPACT_ANALYSIS
ENTRY:              Fires after parsed.change event; reads from bfa_dependency_index
PURPOSE:            Query dependency index for all flows/entities/APIs that overlap with
                    the extracted entity/API references in the proposed change.
                    Returns blast radius candidate set (not yet validated for true conflict).
DISTINCT FROM:      T108 (Search & Discovery — entity search); this is BFA-internal governance
FACTORY DEPS:       F1029:IEntityReferenceExtractorService
                    F1030:IApiSurfaceExtractorService
                    F1035:IDependencyIndexQueryService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1029 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch)
                    F1030 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch)
                    F1035 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch → bfa_dependency_index)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-2 Planning:    Decomposes extraction into entity refs + API surface refs in parallel
  AF-4 RAG:         Searches SK-233, SK-234 for entity extraction patterns
  AF-7 Compliance:  Validates build_search_filter usage (DNA-2: empty field skipping)
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates blast radius candidate set is non-null (even if empty = no conflict)
BFA VALIDATION:     CF-475: dependency_index MUST be current (last updated < flow_registry last publish)
                    CF-476: tenant_id MUST be present on all index queries (Scope Isolation)
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          Entity/API reference extraction logic (deterministic)
  FREEDOM:          Max blast radius candidate set size (default: 500, admin-configurable)
IRON RULES:
  IR-376-1: Every ES query MUST include tenantId filter (DNA-5 Scope Isolation)
  IR-376-2: build_search_filter MUST skip empty entity_class and empty access_type fields (DNA-2)
  IR-376-3: Empty blast radius candidate set = NONE severity path; MUST NOT escalate further
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - entity references extracted for all supported change types
  - dependency index queried with tenantId scope
  - blast radius candidate set written to session state (F1044)
  - candidate set size within FREEDOM config limit
```

---

### TASK TYPE: T377 — Static Conflict Detection Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          IMPACT_ANALYSIS
ENTRY:              Fires after blast radius candidate set is ready
PURPOSE:            Apply deterministic CF rules against extracted entity references and
                    blast radius candidates. Classify each candidate as: TRUE_CONFLICT |
                    ADDITIVE_ONLY | NO_CONFLICT. No AI involved — 100% repeatable.
DISTINCT FROM:      T378 (Semantic Analysis — AI-powered, advisory); T377 is deterministic and primary
FACTORY DEPS:       F1036:IStaticConflictRulesEngineService
                    F1037:IBlastRadiusCalculatorService
                    F1038:IBreakingChangeValidatorService
                    F1032:ISchemaDiffClassifierService
                    F1033:IStateTransitionAnalyzerService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1036 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — bfa_rules_index)
                    F1037 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — multi-hop graph traversal)
                    F1038 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — live flow definitions)
                    F1032 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — schema version history)
                    F1033 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — flow state machine specs)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generates IStaticConflictRulesEngineService with current CF rule set
  AF-4 RAG:         Searches SK-235 (StaticConflictDetector patterns)
  AF-7 Compliance:  Validates no typed conflict models (DNA-1)
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates: every blast radius candidate has explicit TRUE_CONFLICT | NO_CONFLICT classification
BFA VALIDATION:     CF-477: schema field removal classified as BREAKING before validation step
                    CF-478: state transition removal classified as BREAKING before validation step
                    CF-479: static conflict result MUST reference specific CF rule that triggered it
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          Classification logic (BREAKING/ADDITIVE/COMPATIBLE) — fixed CF rules
  FREEDOM:          Severity overrides for specific entity classes (admin-configurable)
IRON RULES:
  IR-377-1: Static conflict detection MUST complete before AI semantic analysis begins (T377 → T378 ordering)
  IR-377-2: If any static result = CRITICAL, arbitration MUST proceed to PENDING_RESOLUTION
             regardless of AI advisory result
  IR-377-3: Every conflict result MUST cite the specific CF rule(s) that triggered it
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - all blast radius candidates classified
  - CRITICAL findings cite CF rule number
  - transitive impact calculated (min 1 hop, max = FREEDOM config)
  - schema diff classification completed for ENTITY_SCHEMA change type
```

---

### TASK TYPE: T378 — Semantic Impact Analysis Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          IMPACT_ANALYSIS
ENTRY:              Fires after static conflict detection completes (T377 result = non-NONE)
PURPOSE:            Send context bundle + proposed change to AiDispatcher for parallel
                    multi-model semantic analysis. Detect hidden business logic conflicts
                    not caught by static rules. Output must validate against ImpactReport schema.
DISTINCT FROM:      T377 (Static Detection — deterministic); T378 is advisory AI analysis
FACTORY DEPS:       F1039:ISemanticConflictAnalyzerService
                    F1040:ISemanticOutputValidatorService
                    F1050:IFlowRegistryReaderService
                    F1051:ISchemaRegistryReaderService
                    F1052:IDocumentationVectorSearchService
                    F1054:IBFAContextBundlerService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1039 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher) → AiDispatcher → Claude/OpenAI/Gemini parallel)
                    F1040 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — ImpactReport JSON schema)
                    F1050 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — flow registry)
                    F1051 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — schema registry)
                    F1052 → RAG FABRIC (SK-389 (HybridRagStrategy) → IRagService → Hybrid strategy)
                    F1054 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — context bundle cache)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-3 Prompt:      Retrieves BFA arbiter system prompt from prompt library
                    "You are a Business Logic Arbiter. Proposed change: {diff}. Context: {bundle}.
                     Detect logical collisions, broken dependencies, invalid state changes.
                     Output ONLY valid ImpactReport JSON."
  AF-5 Multi-model: Runs Claude + OpenAI + Gemini in parallel; AiDispatcher aggregates
  AF-6 Code Review: Validates semantic output is not hallucinated (evidence links must be real)
  AF-10 Merge:      Merges multi-model outputs; consensus-based severity scoring
  AF-11 Feedback:   Stores generation quality score for retrospective review (F1071)
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates ImpactReport JSON schema compliance + evidence link presence
BFA VALIDATION:     CF-480: semantic analysis MUST only run after static detection (T377) completes
                    CF-481: AI output without evidence links = LOW severity maximum (no HIGH/CRITICAL from AI alone)
                    CF-482: context bundle MUST include at least 1 impacted flow definition
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          ImpactReport JSON schema (output contract — fixed)
  FREEDOM:          AI model selection and weights (admin-configures provider priority)
  FREEDOM:          Max context bundle size tokens (default: 100k, admin-configurable)
IRON RULES:
  IR-378-1: AI output MUST validate against ImpactReport JSON schema before acceptance
  IR-378-2: Semantic analysis result is ADVISORY; static gate (T377) CRITICAL takes precedence
  IR-378-3: If 2/3 models agree on severity level, that severity is accepted; otherwise → LOW
  IR-378-4: AI analysis MUST NOT access live production databases directly
             (context bundle is the only input — no direct DB calls from AI fabric)
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - ImpactReport JSON schema validation passed
  - multi-model consensus reached (2/3 models)
  - evidence links present for any HIGH/CRITICAL AI finding
  - context bundle was complete (all 4 context categories: flows, schemas, docs, design records)
```

---

### TASK TYPE: T379 — Conflict Severity Classification Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          IMPACT_ANALYSIS
ENTRY:              Fires after T377 (static) and T378 (semantic) both complete
PURPOSE:            Aggregate static + AI results into single composite severity.
                    Apply severity escalation rules. Final severity determines arbitration path.
DISTINCT FROM:      T377/T378 (individual analysis steps); T379 is the aggregation step
FACTORY DEPS:       F1041:ISeverityAggregatorService
                    F1042:IConflictReportAssemblerService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1041 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — severity rules config)
                    F1042 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL — conflict report store)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates composite severity is one of: CRITICAL|HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW|NONE
                    NONE → skip arbitration, publish directly
                    CRITICAL → mandatory PENDING_RESOLUTION (no auto-proceed)
BFA VALIDATION:     CF-483: CRITICAL composite severity MUST block publish until resolution
                    CF-484: NONE composite severity MUST unblock publish path immediately
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          CRITICAL always blocks (non-negotiable)
  FREEDOM:          HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW block vs warn-only (per-tenant FREEDOM config via F1065)
IRON RULES:
  IR-379-1: Composite severity CRITICAL → state machine MUST transition to PENDING_RESOLUTION
  IR-379-2: Composite severity NONE → state machine MUST skip arbitration, proceed to publish
  IR-379-3: ConflictReport document MUST be persisted to PG before state transition event
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - composite severity is one of 5 valid values
  - ConflictReport persisted to PG with all required fields
  - severity escalation rules applied from FREEDOM config (not hardcoded)
```

---

### TASK TYPE: T380 — Blast Radius Calculation Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          BLAST_RADIUS
ENTRY:              Fires in parallel with T377/T378 (can run concurrently)
PURPOSE:            Calculate transitive dependency impact: direct flows + flows that depend
                    on those flows (up to N hops, FREEDOM config). Produces blast_radius_report:
                    { direct_impacts, transitive_impacts, total_unique_flows, max_hop_reached }
DISTINCT FROM:      T376 (direct dependency query); T380 traverses the full dependency graph
FACTORY DEPS:       F1037:IBlastRadiusCalculatorService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1037 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — graph traversal)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-2 Planning:    Plans traversal strategy (BFS vs DFS) based on graph density
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates blast radius report includes hop count and total unique flows
BFA VALIDATION:     CF-485: blast radius traversal depth MUST respect FREEDOM config max (default: 3)
                    CF-486: circular dependency detection MUST be implemented (visited set)
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          Circular dependency detection (fixed — prevents infinite loops)
  FREEDOM:          Max traversal depth (default: 3), max unique flows threshold (default: 1000)
IRON RULES:
  IR-380-1: If circular dependency detected, log and continue (do not throw) — DataProcessResult[T]
  IR-380-2: Blast radius report MUST be attached to ConflictReport before assembly (T379)
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - blast radius report includes direct_impacts count
  - transitive_impacts count
  - circular dependency detection ran
  - hop depth <= FREEDOM config max
```

---

### TASK TYPE: T381 — Arbitration State Machine Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          ARBITRATION
ENTRY:              Fires on every state transition event from arbitration lifecycle
PURPOSE:            Orchestrate the BFA state machine:
                    IDLE → EXTRACTING → DETECTING → SEVERITY_AGGREGATING →
                    PENDING_RESOLUTION | SKIP_ARBITRATION → APPLYING_RESOLUTION →
                    RESOLVED | REJECTED | ERROR
                    Persist state BEFORE emitting next event (DNA-8 Outbox).
DISTINCT FROM:      T319 (Recipe Trigger Evaluation — FLOW-21); T381 is BFA-specific orchestration
FACTORY DEPS:       F1043:IArbitrationStateMachineService
                    F1044:IArbitrationSessionPersistenceService
                    F1048:IArbitrationTimeoutService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1043 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams — state transition events)
                    F1044 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL — bfa_sessions table)
                    F1048 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams — delayed timeout message)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generates state machine implementation from state transition spec
  AF-7 Compliance:  Validates DNA-8 (Outbox) pattern on every transition
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates all 8 valid states are reachable; no orphan states
BFA VALIDATION:     CF-487: state MUST be persisted to PG before queue event emitted
                    CF-488: timeout MUST be scheduled on PENDING_RESOLUTION entry (default: 24h)
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          8-state machine with fixed valid transitions
  FREEDOM:          Timeout duration (24h default), auto-reject on timeout (enable/disable)
IRON RULES:
  IR-381-1: State machine MUST implement persist-before-emit (DNA-8) on every transition
  IR-381-2: Invalid state transitions MUST return DataProcessResult[T] failure (no throw)
  IR-381-3: Resumable: any session can be rehydrated from last persisted state in < 60s
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - all 8 valid states covered in contract
  - persist-before-emit verified on each transition
  - timeout scheduled correctly
  - recovery test: session rehydration from PG state < 60s
```

---

### TASK TYPE: T382 — Impact Report Generation Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          SYNTHESIS
ENTRY:              Fires when state = PENDING_RESOLUTION and ConflictReport assembled
PURPOSE:            Generate human-readable structured impact report from ConflictReport document.
                    Report includes: severity badge, impacted flows list with descriptions,
                    blast radius summary, 4-option decision menu, evidence links.
DISTINCT FROM:      T378 (AI semantic analysis); T382 generates the USER-FACING report
FACTORY DEPS:       F1057:IImpactReportRendererService
                    F1058:IDecisionOptionBuilderService
                    F1056:IBFAHistoricalDecisionService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1057 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — impact report templates)
                    F1058 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — resolution option configs)
                    F1056 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL — bfa_decision_history)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-3 Prompt:      Retrieves impact report rendering prompt (human-readable description of impacts)
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates: report has severity_badge, impacted_flows[] (min 1 if CRITICAL),
                    options[] (exactly 4), evidence_links[] if HIGH/CRITICAL
BFA VALIDATION:     CF-489: FORCE_PROCEED option MUST be hidden unless actor has bfa:override permission
                    CF-490: precedent suggestion shown only if F1056 returns 3+ matching precedents
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          4 decision options (fixed). Severity badge schema (fixed).
  FREEDOM:          Report template per channel (Web/CLI/Chat — admin-configurable)
                    Precedent suggestion enable/disable (per tenant)
IRON RULES:
  IR-382-1: Report MUST include at least 1 impacted flow for CRITICAL/HIGH severity
  IR-382-2: FORCE_PROCEED option MUST only be rendered for actors with bfa:override permission
  IR-382-3: Evidence links MUST reference real flow/schema document IDs (no fabricated references)
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - report renders in all 3 channels: Web, CLI, Chat
  - FORCE_PROCEED hidden for standard actor
  - FORCE_PROCEED visible for override actor
  - precedent suggestion appears when 3+ matching historical decisions found
```

---

### TASK TYPE: T383 — Human Resolution Capture Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          ARBITRATION
ENTRY:              Fires when user submits decision (from any channel)
PURPOSE:            Receive and validate user decision. Idempotent capture (SETNX on session_id).
                    Validate decision is one of 4 allowed options. If FORCE_PROCEED,
                    validate elevated permission and mandatory rationale.
                    Emit bfa.decision.captured event to state machine.
DISTINCT FROM:      T322 (Connector Auth Gate — FLOW-21); T383 is BFA decision capture
FACTORY DEPS:       F1059:IDecisionCaptureService
                    F1046:IUserDecisionReceiverService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1059 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams — decision captured event)
                    F1046 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams — decision receiver)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-7 Compliance:  Validates DNA-7 (Idempotency) — SETNX on session_id
  AF-8 Security:    Validates actor permission for FORCE_PROCEED
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates decision is one of 4 valid options + rationale present for FORCE_PROCEED
BFA VALIDATION:     CF-491: decision MUST be one of: REFACTOR_FLOWS | REJECT_CHANGE | COMPAT_MODE | FORCE_PROCEED
                    CF-492: FORCE_PROCEED MUST have rationale (min 50 chars) — IR-384-1
                    CF-493: duplicate decision submission for same session = idempotent no-op (not error)
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          4 valid decision options (fixed). Idempotency SETNX (fixed).
  FREEDOM:          Rationale minimum length (default: 50 chars, admin-configurable)
IRON RULES:
  IR-383-1: SETNX on session_id MUST prevent duplicate decision processing (DNA-7)
  IR-383-2: FORCE_PROCEED without rationale MUST return DataProcessResult failure (CF-492)
  IR-383-3: Actor identity MUST be re-validated against auth context (not cached from report rendering)
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - idempotency tested: duplicate submission returns success with existing decision
  - FORCE_PROCEED blocked without rationale
  - FORCE_PROCEED blocked without bfa:override permission
  - decision event emitted to state machine after capture
```

---

### TASK TYPE: T384 — Resolution Application Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          ARBITRATION
ENTRY:              Fires when bfa.decision.captured event received by state machine
PURPOSE:            Apply the user's decision:
                    REFACTOR_FLOWS → queue refactor tasks for affected flows (via FlowOrchestrator)
                    REJECT_CHANGE → mark proposed change rejected, unblock original publish flow
                    COMPAT_MODE → register compat wrapper, mark fields obsolete, set sunset date
                    FORCE_PROCEED → log privileged override to F1070, proceed with publish
DISTINCT FROM:      T320 (Recipe Step Execution — FLOW-21); T384 is BFA resolution application
FACTORY DEPS:       F1047:IResolutionApplierService
                    F1061:IAutoRefactorCoordinatorService
                    F1062:ICompatibilityWrapperService
                    F1070:IForceOverrideTrackerService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1047 → FLOW ENGINE FABRIC (SK-392 (RagStrategyRegistry) → FlowOrchestrator)
                    F1061 → FLOW ENGINE FABRIC (SK-392 (RagStrategyRegistry) → FlowOrchestrator — refactor sub-flow)
                    F1062 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — bfa_compat_registry)
                    F1070 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL — bfa_override_log)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 Genesis:     Generates resolution application service from decision schema
  AF-8 Security:    Double-validates FORCE_PROCEED actor permission at application time
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates correct resolution path executed per decision type
BFA VALIDATION:     CF-494: FORCE_PROCEED MUST write to bfa_override_log before publish unblocks
                    CF-495: REFACTOR_FLOWS MUST queue refactor tasks before state transitions to RESOLVED
                    CF-496: COMPAT_MODE MUST write compat wrapper to bfa_compat_registry with sunset_date
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          4-path resolution logic (fixed per decision type)
  FREEDOM:          Compat mode sunset period (default: 90 days, admin-configurable per entity)
IRON RULES:
  IR-384-1: FORCE_PROCEED without rationale in override log = BUILD FAILURE
  IR-384-2: All 4 resolution paths MUST complete before state transitions to RESOLVED/REJECTED
  IR-384-3: REFACTOR_FLOWS must handle partial failure gracefully (some flows refactored, some not)
             → DataProcessResult with partial success, trigger escalation for failed flows
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - all 4 resolution paths tested independently
  - FORCE_PROCEED writes to override log atomically with publish unblock
  - COMPAT_MODE sunset date set and sunset alert scheduled
  - REFACTOR_FLOWS partial failure handled (DataProcessResult partial success)
```

---

### TASK TYPE: T385 — Decision Audit Trail Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          GOVERNANCE
ENTRY:              Fires on every arbitration resolution (any decision type)
PURPOSE:            Write immutable audit trail record to both tenant audit log (F1067)
                    and global override log if FORCE_PROCEED (F1070).
                    Emit compliance event to observability pipeline (F1073).
DISTINCT FROM:      T312 (Entry Persistence — FLOW-21); T385 is BFA-specific governance audit
FACTORY DEPS:       F1067:IBFATenantAuditTrailService
                    F1070:IForceOverrideTrackerService (if FORCE_PROCEED)
                    F1073:IBFAOpenTelemetryService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1067 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL — bfa_tenant_audit_log)
                    F1070 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL — bfa_override_log)
                    F1073 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams — telemetry event stream)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-7 Compliance:  Validates DNA-9 (per-field audit logging) for all decision fields
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates audit record contains all required fields
BFA VALIDATION:     CF-497: audit record MUST be written before arbitration session closes
                    CF-498: FORCE_PROCEED audit MUST be written to BOTH tenant log AND global log
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          Audit record schema (fixed). Immutability (fixed).
  FREEDOM:          Audit retention period (GDPR-configurable per tenant via F1065)
IRON RULES:
  IR-385-1: Audit record MUST be immutable (INSERT-only, no UPDATE/DELETE on audit tables)
  IR-385-2: FORCE_PROCEED MUST appear in both tenant audit log AND global override log
  IR-385-3: Audit record MUST include: session_id, tenant_id, decision, actor, timestamp, rationale, affected_flows[]
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - audit record written to PG successfully
  - FORCE_PROCEED appears in both logs
  - all required fields present
  - telemetry span emitted for end-to-end trace correlation
```

---

### TASK TYPE: T386 — BFA Multi-Tenant Isolation Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          GOVERNANCE
ENTRY:              Fires on BFA tenant provision request AND as periodic health check
PURPOSE:            Provision BFA capabilities per tenant (F1065). Validate that all
                    BFA dependency index queries are tenant-scoped (F1066).
                    Periodic health check scans for any unscoped queries.
DISTINCT FROM:      T326 (Form Tenant Provision — FLOW-21); T386 is BFA-specific MT governance
FACTORY DEPS:       F1065:IBFATenantProvisionService
                    F1066:IBFATenantIsolationValidatorService
                    F1063:IBFATenantConflictIndexService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1065 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL — bfa_tenant_config)
                    F1066 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — audit log scan)
                    F1063 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — tenant conflict index)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-7 Compliance:  Validates DNA-5 (Scope Isolation) on all tenant-scoped BFA queries
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates tenant provision record complete, isolation validator reports 0 unscoped queries
BFA VALIDATION:     CF-499: tenant provision MUST set severity threshold and resolution channel before BFA enabled
                    CF-500: isolation validator health check MUST run every 15m (configurable)
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          tenantId required on all BFA queries (non-negotiable)
  FREEDOM:          BFA enable/disable per tenant, severity threshold, resolution channel
IRON RULES:
  IR-386-1: BFA MUST NOT process arbitration for unconfigured tenant (F1065 config required first)
  IR-386-2: Any unscoped query detected by F1066 = CRITICAL alert + auto-disable BFA for that query path
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - tenant provision record includes all required config fields
  - isolation validator detects 0 unscoped queries in test scenario
  - health check scheduled and executing
```

---

### TASK TYPE: T387 — Cross-Tenant Conflict Guard Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          GOVERNANCE
ENTRY:              Fires on platform-level changes affecting shared factory interfaces or global schemas
PURPOSE:            Detect conflicts in shared platform components (factory registries, global
                    event types, platform-level BFA rules) that could affect multiple tenants.
                    Platform admin only. Never exposes per-tenant data.
DISTINCT FROM:      T377 (Static Conflict Detection — tenant-scoped); T387 is platform-level cross-tenant guard
FACTORY DEPS:       F1064:ICrossTenantConflictGuardService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1064 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — platform-level conflict index)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-8 Security:    Validates actor has platform_admin role before any cross-tenant analysis
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates no per-tenant data is included in cross-tenant analysis output
BFA VALIDATION:     CF-501: cross-tenant guard analysis output MUST be aggregated (no per-tenant data)
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          platform_admin role requirement (non-negotiable)
  FREEDOM:          Platform-level CF rule set (platform admin can add rules, not remove)
IRON RULES:
  IR-387-1: Cross-tenant guard MUST NOT return any tenant-specific data in its output
  IR-387-2: Actor MUST have platform_admin role (checked via MicroserviceBase auth context)
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - output contains only aggregated platform metrics (no tenant-specific rows)
  - platform_admin role validated
```

---

### TASK TYPE: T388 — BFA Analytics Emit Gate
```
ARCHETYPE:          GOVERNANCE
ENTRY:              Fires after every arbitration session closes (RESOLVED/REJECTED/ERROR)
PURPOSE:            Emit analytics events to BFA metrics pipeline:
                    - tenant metrics (F1068): arbitration_rate, block_rate, resolution_time
                    - platform metrics (F1069): aggregated platform-wide
                    - pattern learning (F1072): index resolved decision as learnable pattern
                    - telemetry (F1073): emit final span for closed arbitration session
DISTINCT FROM:      T385 (Audit Trail — persistence); T388 is analytics and learning
FACTORY DEPS:       F1068:IBFATenantMetricsService
                    F1069:IBFAPlatformMetricsService
                    F1072:IBFAPatternLearningService
                    F1073:IBFAOpenTelemetryService
FABRIC RESOLUTION:  F1068 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — tenant metrics index)
                    F1069 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch — platform metrics index)
                    F1072 → RAG FABRIC (SK-389 (HybridRagStrategy) → IRagService → vector indexing)
                    F1073 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams — telemetry stream)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-11 Feedback:   Stores analytics data for generation quality improvement (F1071)
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates all 4 metric paths executed; no cross-tenant data in tenant metrics
MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:          Metrics schema (fixed)
  FREEDOM:          Aggregation window sizes (1m/1h/1d — admin-configurable per tenant)
IRON RULES:
  IR-388-1: Analytics emit MUST NOT block arbitration session close (async, fire-and-forget acceptable)
  IR-388-2: Tenant metrics MUST be tenant-scoped (tenantId on all index writes)
  IR-388-3: Pattern learning MUST be tenant-scoped (patterns not shared across tenants by default)
QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - all 4 metric paths emit successfully
  - tenant metrics contain tenantId
  - telemetry span closed with final state
  - pattern indexed in RAG fabric
```

| AF-10 Merge | Merges multi-model semantic analysis outputs (T378); consensus-based severity |
| AF-11 Feedback | Stores arbitration decision quality scores for retrospective improvement (F1071) |

# FAMILY 154 — Gap Detection & Capability Registry (T389–T392)

## TASK TYPE: T389 — Capability Gap Detection Gate
**ARCHETYPE:** INVENTORY
**ENTRY:** Fires when FlowOrchestrator (SK-392 (RagStrategyRegistry)) cannot resolve a required factory interface, OR operator issues `self_build(capability)`, OR scheduled scan
**PURPOSE:** Parse user intent via AI ENGINE FABRIC, diff against capability registry in DATABASE FABRIC, classify gaps as MISSING_FACTORY | MISSING_OPERATION | MISSING_PROVIDER, emit CapabilityGapDetected event via QUEUE FABRIC
**DISTINCT FROM:** T390 (plans flow DAG; T389 only detects what's missing)
**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F1075 (ICapabilityGapDetectorService), F1076 (ICapabilityRegistryService) — resolved via CreateAsync()
**FABRIC RESOLUTION:** F1075 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) + DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → ES); F1076 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis locks)
**AF CONFIGURATION:** AF-1 Genesis (MicroserviceBase, dict[str, Any]), AF-2 Planning, AF-4 RAG (SK-251), AF-7 Compliance (DNA-1,2,5), AF-9 Judge (IR-1075-1/2/3)
**BFA VALIDATION:** CF-510 (prevent false-positive gaps), CF-511 (no cross-tenant gap reads)
**MACHINE/FREEDOM:** MACHINE: gap categories fixed enum, AI ENGINE required, event via QUEUE. FREEDOM: confidence threshold (0.7), max gaps (10), scan interval
**IRON RULES:** IR-1075-1: AI required for intent parsing (no regex) = BUILD FAILURE. IR-1075-2: all gaps classified = BUILD FAILURE. IR-1075-3: tenantId required = BUILD FAILURE
**QUALITY GATES:** QG-T389-1: non-empty gap list with classifications. QG-T389-2: gaps reference fabric interfaces. QG-T389-3: event emitted via QUEUE. QG-T389-4: tenantId present

---

## TASK TYPE: T390 — Flow Planning & DAG Decomposition
**ARCHETYPE:** ORCHESTRATION
**ENTRY:** Fires after T389 emits CapabilityGapDetected
**PURPOSE:** Decompose user intent into acyclic DAG using AI ENGINE FABRIC, traverse capability graph for reuse candidates, produce FlowPlan document in DATABASE FABRIC
**DISTINCT FROM:** T389 (detects gaps); T390 plans how to build the complete flow
**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F1077 (IFlowPlannerService), F1078 (ICapabilityGraphService) — resolved via CreateAsync()
**FABRIC RESOLUTION:** F1077 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES); F1078 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis cache)
**AF CONFIGURATION:** AF-1 Genesis, AF-4 RAG (SK-260, SK-266), AF-5 Multi-model (Claude+OpenAI+Gemini), AF-7 Compliance (DNA-1,5), AF-9 Judge
**BFA VALIDATION:** CF-512 (no duplicate DAGs), CF-513 (sub-flow depth limit)
**MACHINE/FREEDOM:** MACHINE: DAG acyclic, sub-flow max depth 3 (DR-195). FREEDOM: depth configurable, parallel/sequential mode
**IRON RULES:** IR-1077-1: AI required for DAG planning = BUILD FAILURE. IR-1077-2: depth overflow = BUILD FAILURE. IR-1077-3: tenantId required
**QUALITY GATES:** QG-T390-1: valid acyclic DAG. QG-T390-2: gap nodes reference factories. QG-T390-3: depth within limit. QG-T390-4: traversal within timeout

---

## TASK TYPE: T391 — Reuse Decision & Scanning
**ARCHETYPE:** INVENTORY
**ENTRY:** Fires after T390 produces FlowPlan with gap nodes
**PURPOSE:** Scan skill library via RAG FABRIC, evaluate reuse candidates, classify each gap as COPY | ADAPT | REWRITE
**DISTINCT FROM:** T389 (detects gaps), T390 (plans flow); T391 determines HOW to fill each gap
**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F1079 (IReuseDecisionMatrixService) — resolved via CreateAsync()
**FABRIC RESOLUTION:** F1079 → RAG FABRIC (SK-388/SK-389 (IRagService/HybridRag) — IRagService across SK-1–SK-250) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES)
**AF CONFIGURATION:** AF-1 Genesis, AF-4 RAG (SK-260 + all skills), AF-7 Compliance (DNA-1,3), AF-9 Judge
**BFA VALIDATION:** CF-514 (prevent unauthorized adaptation of existing skills)
**MACHINE/FREEDOM:** MACHINE: categories COPY|ADAPT|REWRITE fixed, RAG mandatory before REWRITE. FREEDOM: similarity thresholds (COPY 0.9, ADAPT 0.6), max skills (20)
**IRON RULES:** IR-1079-1: RAG scan mandatory before reuse decision = BUILD FAILURE. IR-1079-2: every gap classified = BUILD FAILURE
**QUALITY GATES:** QG-T391-1: every gap has reuse decision + confidence. QG-T391-2: COPY refs valid skill. QG-T391-3: ADAPT includes delta. QG-T391-4: REWRITE justified

---

## TASK TYPE: T392 — SelfBuildRun Initialization & State Machine
**ARCHETYPE:** ORCHESTRATION
**ENTRY:** Fires after T391 produces reuse plan
**PURPOSE:** Create SelfBuildRun document (dict[str, Any]) state TRIGGERED, persist to DATABASE FABRIC, initialize phase history/retry counters, emit SelfBuildRunStarted event
**DISTINCT FROM:** T389-T391 (analysis); T392 creates the trackable runtime entity
**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F1080 (ISelfBuildRunStateService), F1081 (ICapabilityGapNotificationService) — resolved via CreateAsync()
**FABRIC RESOLUTION:** F1080 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES + Redis locks); F1081 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams)
**AF CONFIGURATION:** AF-1 Genesis, AF-4 RAG (SK-259), AF-7 Compliance (DNA-1,5,7), AF-9 Judge
**BFA VALIDATION:** CF-515 (prevent concurrent build resource conflicts per tenant)
**MACHINE/FREEDOM:** MACHINE: state machine (TRIGGERED→...→COMPLETED), idempotency runId+phaseId (DD-252). FREEDOM: max concurrent runs (3), timeout (60 min)
**IRON RULES:** IR-1080-1: invalid state transition = BUILD FAILURE. IR-1080-2: phase idempotency required = BUILD FAILURE
**QUALITY GATES:** QG-T392-1: SelfBuildRun created. QG-T392-2: phase history initialized. QG-T392-3: event emitted. QG-T392-4: concurrent limit enforced

---

# FAMILY 155 — Contract & Artifact Generator (T393–T396)

## TASK TYPE: T393 — Contract Generation Orchestrator
**ARCHETYPE:** SYNTHESIS
**ENTRY:** Fires after T392 sets state to GAPS_DETECTED
**PURPOSE:** Orchestrate generation of all engine artifacts per gap: factory spec, task type contract, event schemas, API contracts via AI ENGINE FABRIC
**DISTINCT FROM:** T394 (generates specific artifacts); T393 orchestrates the pipeline
**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F1082 (IContractGeneratorService), F1083 (IFactorySpecGeneratorService) — resolved via CreateAsync()
**FABRIC RESOLUTION:** F1082 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES); F1083 → AI ENGINE FABRIC + DATABASE FABRIC
**AF CONFIGURATION:** AF-1 Genesis, AF-3 Prompt Library, AF-4 RAG (SK-252), AF-5 Multi-model, AF-7 Compliance (DNA-1,6), AF-9 Judge
**BFA VALIDATION:** CF-516 (no factory ID collision), CF-517 (fabric resolution required)
**MACHINE/FREEDOM:** MACHINE: full format required, no ID reuse. FREEDOM: template version

# FLOW TEMPLATES — FLOW-26 (Templates 83–89)

## Template 83 — SelfBuildRun Master (self-build-skill-v1)
**Phases:** 6 zones with loopbacks; **Steps:** T389→T412 full pipeline

## DNA COMPLIANCE (FLOW-26)

| DNA Pattern | Enforcement in FLOW-26 |
|------------|----------------------|
| DNA-1 parse_document | All 28 factories: dict[str, Any]. Gaps, state, evidence, promotions — all parsed. |
| DNA-2 build_search_filter | All ES queries via build_search_filter; empty fields auto-skipped. |
| DNA-3 DataProcessResult[T] | All factory methods return DPR<T>; no business-logic throws. |
| DNA-4 MicroserviceBase | All 28 services extend MicroserviceBase (19 components). |
| DNA-5 Scope Isolation | tenantId on every query. Sandbox, builds, promotions — all tenant-scoped. |
| DNA-6 DynamicController | No entity-specific controllers. All APIs via DynamicController. |
| DNA-7 Idempotency | SelfBuildRun idempotent by (runId, phaseId). Retries idempotent. |
| DNA-8 Outbox | SelfBuildRunStarted, CapabilityGapDetected, PromotionCompleted — persist-before-emit. |
| DNA-9 Extended | Per-field audit on promotion decisions, overrides, evidence. |

# FLOW-27 — TASK TYPES CATALOG
## Human Interaction Gate + Dependency Scheduler + Visual Runtime State + Multi-Tenant Group Tasks
## Extension of: TASK_TYPES_CATALOG_MERGED.md (T1-T412 unchanged)
## FLOW-27 Task Types: T413–T422
## Templates: 90–84
## Status: AUTHORITATIVE — FLOW-27 ONLY

---

## TASK TYPE: T413 — HumanInteractionGate Node

```
ARCHETYPE:          HUMAN_GATE
ENTRY:              Fires when orchestrator reaches a node with nodeType=HumanInteractionGate
                    in the flow DAG. Can be triggered from any flow template.
PURPOSE:            Suspend the current node's execution path, create a durable UserTask
                    record (via F1103), capture execution context snapshot (via F1105),
                    emit UserTaskCreated notification (via F1124), and suspend until answered.
                    Non-blocking policy: continue with defaults without waiting.
                    Optional policy: skip if time exceeds dueAt.
DISTINCT FROM:      T379 (Conflict Resolution Gate — BFA arbitration, not user input)
                    T285 (Tenant Onboarding Saga — system-level saga, not interactive gate)
                    Previous HumanApprovalGate concept (T413 is the formalized engine contract)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1103: IHumanInteractionGateService     (open gate, get status, skip, expire, cancel)
  F1104: IUserTaskRegistryService          (store/retrieve UserTask document)
  F1105: ITaskContextSnapshotService       (capture immutable context snapshot)
  F1106: ITaskAssignmentResolverService    (resolve eligible users from policy)
  F1115: IDependencySchedulerService       (re-evaluate nodes after gate closes)
  F1128: IUserTaskAnswerProcessorService   (process incoming answer)

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1103 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch user-tasks) +
           QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams hig-events)
  F1104 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch user-tasks)
  F1105 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch task-context-snapshots)
  F1106 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL assignment-policies)
  F1115 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams ready-queue)
  F1128 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch user-tasks) +
           QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams hig-events)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-2 Planning:     Decomposes gate into: open → capture → assign → notify → wait → resume
  AF-7 Compliance:   Checks DNA-5 (tenantId on all documents), DNA-1 (Dictionary storage),
                     DNA-3 (DataProcessResult on all methods)
  AF-9 Judge:        Validates: contextSnapshot captured before gate opens,
                     assigneePolicy resolves > 0 eligible users, blockingPolicy declared,
                     resumeTargets[] populated from DAG analysis

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-545: UserTask cross-tenant visibility — tenantId must isolate all task queries
  CF-546: Duplicate gate prevention — same nodeId+runId cannot have 2 open tasks
  CF-550: Dependent node pre-fire guard — downstream nodes MUST NOT start while gate is blocking
  CF-555: CompletionGate must not close while required gates open

MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE (non-configurable):
    - tenantId required on all documents
    - contextSnapshot captured before gate enters waiting_for_user
    - resumeTargets computed from DAG (not manually set)
    - UserTaskAnswered event MUST trigger DependencyScheduler
  FREEDOM (admin-configurable):
    - blockingPolicy: blocking | non_blocking | optional
    - assigneePolicy: user | group | role | anyOf | allOf | quorum
    - dueAt (expiry time)
    - priority
    - questionType and expectedAnswerSchema

IRON RULES:
  IR-T413-1: Gate MUST capture contextSnapshot BEFORE entering waiting_for_user state
  IR-T413-2: Gate MUST emit UserTaskCreated to QUEUE FABRIC atomically (outbox pattern)
  IR-T413-3: non_blocking gate MUST proceed with defaults if dueAt passes — NEVER block run
  IR-T413-4: blocking gate MUST block ALL transitive dependents until answered or expired
  IR-T413-5: ALL documents MUST include tenantId — BUILD FAILURE if missing

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  ✓ contextSnapshot doc exists in ES before gate status = waiting_for_user
  ✓ assigneePolicy resolves to at least 1 eligible user within tenant
  ✓ resumeTargets[] correctly identifies downstream nodes from waitFor DAG
  ✓ blockingPolicy declared and valid enum value
  ✓ UserTaskCreated event confirmed in QUEUE FABRIC within 500ms of gate opening
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T414 — GroupUserTask Assignment Gate

```
ARCHETYPE:          ASSIGNMENT
ENTRY:              Fires as sub-step of T413 (HumanInteractionGate) when assigneePolicy.type
                    is group | role | anyOf | allOf | quorum
PURPOSE:            Resolve group/role membership to actual user IDs within tenant.
                    Manage claim lifecycle for single_claim tasks.
                    Track quorum progress for allOf/quorum policies.
                    Prevent duplicate or conflicting answers via claim management.
DISTINCT FROM:      T413 (opens the gate; T414 manages who can answer it)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1106: ITaskAssignmentResolverService  (resolve eligible users)
  F1107: ITaskClaimService               (claim/release/expire management)
  F1108: ITaskQuorumService              (record answers, evaluate quorum)
  F1125: IGroupMembershipResolverService (group/role membership lookup)

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1106 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL assignment-policies + group-membership)
  F1107 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL task-claims) +
           QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams claim-events)
  F1108 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL task-quorums)
  F1125 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL group-membership)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-7 Compliance:   Checks cross-tenant group resolution is forbidden (CF-552)
  AF-9 Judge:        Validates quorum.needed <= group size at assignment time,
                     claim TTL > 0 when claimMode != none,
                     allOf answers deduplicated by userId

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-547: Quorum answer race condition guard
  CF-548: Claim TTL expiry conflict rule
  CF-552: Group membership must be tenant-scoped
  CF-556: Claim release orphan prevention
  CF-561: Quorum partial answer preservation
  CF-565: allOf answer completeness gate

MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:
    - Group membership queried within tenantId only
    - Duplicate answers from same user rejected (idempotency by userId+taskId)
    - All recorded answers preserved after quorum (audit trail)
    - Claim TTL expiry emits ClaimExpired and auto-releases
  FREEDOM:
    - claimMode: none | single_claim | multi_claim
    - claimTtlSec (default: 1800)
    - allowReassign: bool
    - quorum.needed, quorum.outOf

IRON RULES:
  IR-T414-1: quorum.needed > group size at assignment time = IMMEDIATE FAILURE with explanation
  IR-T414-2: Cross-tenant group reference in assigneePolicy = BUILD FAILURE
  IR-T414-3: single_claim: only 1 active claim at a time (database-enforced)
  IR-T414-4: Answer from non-eligible user = rejected with 403 equivalent DataProcessResult

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  ✓ ResolveEligibleUsersAsync returns > 0 results (or clear "no eligible users" failure)
  ✓ For quorum: needed <= actual group size
  ✓ Claim release on TTL confirmed (ST-329 scenario passes)
  ✓ Duplicate answer rejected (ST-326 scenario passes)
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T415 — DependencyAwareScheduler

```
ARCHETYPE:          ORCHESTRATION
ENTRY:              Fires after ANY node state change event (NodeCompleted, NodeFailed,
                    NodeSkipped, UserTaskAnswered, UserTaskExpired)
PURPOSE:            Re-evaluate all BLOCKED/PENDING nodes in the run to find newly READY
                    nodes. Enqueue them for execution. Ensure independent branches keep
                    running even when one branch is WAITING_FOR_USER.
                    This is the engine's "keep working" guarantee.
DISTINCT FROM:      T40 (Three-Way Join Gate — specific join; T415 is the universal scheduler)
                    T33 (Two-way convergence — T415 handles any DAG topology)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1115: IDependencySchedulerService     (evaluate ready nodes, enqueue)
  F1116: INodeReadinessEvaluatorService  (evaluate single node readiness)
  F1117: IBlockedNodeResolverService     (find nodes unblocked by completed node)
  F1118: IJoinPolicyEnforcerService      (evaluate join semantics)
  F1119: IDagWalkerService               (cycle detection, transitive deps)

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1115 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams ready-queue)
  F1116 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch node-snapshots + flow-definitions)
  F1117 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams resume-events)
  F1118 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch flow-definitions)
  F1119 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch flow-definitions)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-2 Planning:   Decomposes into: load DAG → evaluate each node → sort by readiness →
                   apply join policy → enqueue READY → emit BlockedNodeResolved
  AF-9 Judge:      Validates: no cycles in DAG (F1119), join policy correctly applied,
                   WAITING_FOR_USER nodes correctly kept BLOCKED (not incorrectly made READY)

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-550: Dependent node pre-fire guard
  CF-554: allSettled non-blocking branch rule
  CF-562: DependencyScheduler cycle detection rule
  CF-564: Cross-flow HumanTask propagation fence

MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:
    - Node READY only when ALL required waitFor satisfied (no exceptions)
    - Join policy from flow-definition (not overridable at runtime)
    - DAG cycle = immediate run failure
  FREEDOM:
    - Join mode per join node: all | allSettled | required+optional
    - progressWeight per node (for progress calculation)

IRON RULES:
  IR-T415-1: DAG cycle detected = run FAILED immediately with clear error
  IR-T415-2: NEVER enqueue a node as READY if any required dependency is non-terminal
  IR-T415-3: WAITING_FOR_USER gate must NOT propagate as READY to dependent nodes
  IR-T415-4: DependencyScheduler MUST run after every node state change event

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  ✓ Cycle detection runs at flow load time (not just at execution time)
  ✓ allSettled join correctly proceeds with partial results (FLOW-05 pattern reused)
  ✓ Non-blocking HIG branch continues while other branches run
  ✓ After UserTaskAnswered: transitive dependents re-evaluated and READY ones enqueued
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T416 — RunProgressAggregator

```
ARCHETYPE:          MONITORING
ENTRY:              Fires on any NodeEvent (NodeStarted, SubStepCompleted, NodeProgress,
                    NodeCompleted, NodeFailed, NodeSkipped, etc.)
PURPOSE:            Maintain the materialized RunSnapshot and NodeSnapshot views.
                    Compute progress % using weighted-sum algorithm.
                    Provide real-time progress data for the UI graph API.
DISTINCT FROM:      T418 (NodeStateProjector — handles event sourcing; T416 handles aggregation)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1109: IRunSnapshotService           (store/read RunSnapshot)
  F1110: INodeSnapshotService          (store/read NodeSnapshot)
  F1111: INodeEventEmitterService      (emit NodeProgress events)
  F1112: IFlowProgressAggregatorService (compute progress %)

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1109 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch run-snapshots)
  F1110 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch node-snapshots)
  F1111 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams node-events)
  F1112 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch run-snapshots + node-snapshots)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-7 Compliance:   Validates DNA-5 (tenantId on all snapshots)
  AF-9 Judge:        Validates progress % stays in [0.0, 1.0], summary counts sum correctly,
                     snapshot updated within 1s of triggering event

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-549: Stale snapshot invalidation rule
  CF-551: Run terminal state idempotency rule
  CF-557: NodeSnapshot tenant isolation rule
  CF-559: SubFlow progress rollup integrity rule

MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:
    - Progress algorithm: Σ(weight × statusWeight) / Σ(weight)
    - statusWeight: SUCCEEDED=1.0, RUNNING=0.5, all others=0.0
    - Snapshot updated on every NodeEvent
  FREEDOM:
    - progressWeight per node (default: 1)
    - SubFlow progress from childRun rollup vs provider telemetry (configurable)

IRON RULES:
  IR-T416-1: RunSnapshot.tenantId and NodeSnapshot.tenantId NEVER null
  IR-T416-2: Progress % NEVER exceeds 1.0 regardless of weight configuration
  IR-T416-3: SubFlow progress = child RunSnapshot.progressPct (recursive)

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  ✓ Progress % correctly computed for mix of SUCCEEDED/RUNNING/WAITING/FAILED nodes
  ✓ SubFlow node shows child run's rollup progress
  ✓ SKIPPED nodes contribute 1.0 × weight to progress (intentionally not executed)
  ✓ Snapshot tenant isolation: query without tenantId returns empty
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T417 — CompletionGateValidator

```
ARCHETYPE:          VALIDATION
ENTRY:              Fires when ALL nodes in run reach terminal status (no more READY/RUNNING/PENDING)
                    OR when explicitly called by orchestrator after last node completes.
PURPOSE:            Validate ALL completion criteria before marking run as READY:
                    required terminal nodes succeeded, zero open required UserTasks,
                    all AF-9 JudgeService verdicts approved. If not ready, classify why.
DISTINCT FROM:      T417 validates run-level completion. T413 validates node-level gate.

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1120: ICompletionGateService         (evaluate + mark complete)
  F1121: IRunReadinessValidatorService  (list all blockers)
  F1122: IRunTerminalStateService       (emit terminal state events)
  F1123: IPendingGateResolverService    (count open required gates)

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1120 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch run-snapshots + user-tasks)
  F1121 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch run-snapshots + user-tasks)
  F1122 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch run-snapshots) +
           QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams)
  F1123 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch user-tasks)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-9 Judge:  Validates completion rule strictly:
               1) ALL terminal nodes: SUCCEEDED
               2) Open required UserTasks = 0
               3) JudgeService approved on all required gates
               REJECTS run-ready if any condition fails

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-555: CompletionGate must not fire while required gates open
  CF-563: RunReadiness required terminal node rule

MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:
    - All 3 completion conditions required (non-negotiable)
    - MarkRunReadyAsync MUST call EvaluateCompletionAsync first
  FREEDOM:
    - Which nodes are "required terminal nodes" (from flow definition)
    - Which phase gates require JudgeService approval (from flow definition)

IRON RULES:
  IR-T417-1: NEVER mark run READY if open required UserTasks > 0
  IR-T417-2: NEVER mark run READY if any required terminal node is non-SUCCEEDED
  IR-T417-3: NEVER mark run READY if JudgeService has pending/rejected verdict on required gate
  IR-T417-4: MarkRunReadyAsync is idempotent (already-READY run = no-op, not error)

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  ✓ Optional blocking gates (blockingPolicy=optional) do NOT prevent READY
  ✓ non_blocking gates with defaults DO NOT count as "open required gates"
  ✓ Run FAILED state: CompletionGate correctly classifies as FailedNeedsRepair
  ✓ PendingUserInput status shown when blocking gates are open
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T418 — NodeStateProjector

```
ARCHETYPE:          EVENT_SOURCING
ENTRY:              Consumes all NodeEvents from QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams node-events)
PURPOSE:            Project immutable NodeEvents → mutable NodeSnapshot.
                    Implements event-sourcing read model. Ensures UI always gets consistent
                    snapshot even during concurrent updates. Write path and read path separated.
DISTINCT FROM:      T416 (aggregates progress %; T418 handles event→snapshot projection)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1110: INodeSnapshotService     (upsert NodeSnapshot)
  F1111: INodeEventEmitterService (consume events from node-events stream)
  F1109: IRunSnapshotService      (update RunSnapshot.summary after each projection)

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1110 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch node-snapshots)
  F1111 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams node-events)
  F1109 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch run-snapshots)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-7 Compliance:  Validates idempotent projection (same event applied twice = same result)
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates event order handling (out-of-order event does not corrupt state)

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-551: Run terminal state idempotency rule

MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:
    - Projection is deterministic: same events = same snapshot
    - Events consumed via consumer group (exactly-once semantics)
    - Snapshot version increments with each applied event

IRON RULES:
  IR-T418-1: Projection MUST be idempotent (eventId deduplicated)
  IR-T418-2: Consumer group MUST include tenantId in consumer name for isolation
  IR-T418-3: NEVER discard events — dead letter queue for projection failures

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  ✓ Duplicate event does not change snapshot (idempotency check)
  ✓ Out-of-order event handled gracefully (version check)
  ✓ Snapshot tenant isolation enforced at projection level
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T419 — SubFlowDrilldownBridge

```
ARCHETYPE:          NAVIGATION
ENTRY:              Called when UI requests childRun details for a SubFlow node
                    OR when DependencyScheduler needs to aggregate SubFlow progress
PURPOSE:            Bridge between parent run context and child run context.
                    Provides breadcrumb navigation: ParentRun → Node → ChildRun.
                    Aggregates child RunSnapshot progress into parent NodeSnapshot.
                    Exposes same RunGraph API for child runs (recursive).
DISTINCT FROM:      T416 (progress aggregation; T419 handles navigation + bridging)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1109: IRunSnapshotService       (get child RunSnapshot for rollup)
  F1110: INodeSnapshotService      (update parent NodeSnapshot with child progress)
  F1113: IRunGraphQueryService     (get child run graph for drill-down)
  F1114: INodeDebugSurfaceService  (provide trace debug links for child run)

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1109 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch run-snapshots)
  F1110 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch node-snapshots)
  F1113 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch node-snapshots + flow-definitions)
  F1114 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch trace-debug)

MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:
    - breadcrumb is computed from run hierarchy (not user-configured)
    - child progress rollup = childRun.progressPct
  FREEDOM:
    - Max drill-down depth (default: 10 levels)

IRON RULES:
  IR-T419-1: Child run MUST belong to same tenantId as parent (CF-564)
  IR-T419-2: Drill-down API MUST respect tenant scope — child run in different tenant = 404

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  ✓ Breadcrumb correctly shows ParentRun → NodeName → ChildRun
  ✓ Child run graph uses same node status enum as parent
  ✓ SubFlow node progress = child progressPct (not hard-coded)
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T420 — TaskClaimManager

```
ARCHETYPE:          COORDINATION
ENTRY:              Fires on UserTask claim request or claim TTL check
PURPOSE:            Manage single_claim / multi_claim lifecycle. Prevents conflicting answers.
                    Handles TTL expiry with auto-release and notification.
                    Supports reassignment with audit trail.
DISTINCT FROM:      T414 (assignment resolves who can answer; T420 manages who is answering now)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1107: ITaskClaimService             (claim/release/expire)
  F1104: IUserTaskRegistryService      (update task claim status)
  F1106: ITaskAssignmentResolverService (verify eligibility before claim)
  F1124: ITaskNotificationOrchestratorService (notify of claim/release)

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1107 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL task-claims) +
           QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams claim-events)
  F1104 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch user-tasks)
  F1106 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL assignment-policies)
  F1124 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams notification-events)

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-548: Claim TTL expiry conflict rule
  CF-556: Claim release orphan prevention
  CF-560: UserTask reassignment audit fence

IRON RULES:
  IR-T420-1: single_claim: optimistic lock — second claim attempt returns conflict result
  IR-T420-2: Claim expiry MUST be scheduled at claim time (TTL-based trigger)
  IR-T420-3: Reassignment MUST create audit event (who reassigned, when, from whom, to whom)

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  ✓ Concurrent claim from 2 users: exactly 1 succeeds (ST-328 scenario)
  ✓ TTL expiry releases claim and notifies group (ST-329 scenario)
  ✓ Reassignment audit event stored (CF-560)
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T421 — NotificationFanoutOrchestrator

```
ARCHETYPE:          NOTIFICATION
ENTRY:              Fires on UserTaskCreated event (from QUEUE FABRIC hig-events stream)
PURPOSE:            Fan-out notifications to all eligible recipients for a UserTask.
                    Resolve group/role members within tenant. Deduplicate per channel/recipient.
                    Include rich context payload (trace links, decision description, answer URL).
                    Support reminder notifications as dueAt approaches.
DISTINCT FROM:      T413 (opens gate; T421 handles notification side-effect of opening)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1124: ITaskNotificationOrchestratorService (orchestrate full pipeline)
  F1125: IGroupMembershipResolverService      (resolve recipients)
  F1126: INotificationDedupeService           (prevent duplicate sends)
  F1127: INotificationDeepLinkService         (build context + answer URLs)
  # F68: INotificationProvider (Management fabric — existing, reused)

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1124 → QUEUE FABRIC (SK-383 (RedisStreamQueue) → Redis Streams notification-events)
  F1125 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → PostgreSQL group-membership)
  F1126 → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Redis notif-dedup-cache)
  F1127 → CORE FABRIC (SK-379 (MicroserviceBase) → config)
  # F68 → existing Management fabric notification providers (Slack, Email, In-App, Teams...)

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-553: Notification anti-spam fence
  CF-552: Group membership tenant scope rule

MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:
    - Recipients resolved within tenantId only (cross-tenant = forbidden)
    - Dedup key = taskId + channel + recipient (per-combination)
    - viewContextUrl and answerUrl always included
  FREEDOM:
    - Notification channels (in-app / email / Slack / Teams — via F68)
    - Reminder schedule (hours before dueAt)
    - Urgency levels mapping

IRON RULES:
  IR-T421-1: Cross-tenant recipient resolution = BUILD FAILURE
  IR-T421-2: viewContextUrl and answerUrl MUST be in every notification payload
  IR-T421-3: Anti-spam: one active notification per (taskId + channel + recipient)

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  ✓ Duplicate send prevented for same taskId+channel+recipient (ST-338 scenario)
  ✓ Group dissolved after task assigned — graceful degradation (ST-335 scenario)
  ✓ Notification payload includes all required fields (viewContextUrl, answerUrl, dueAt)
```

---

## TASK TYPE: T422 — MultiTenantTaskVisibilityGate

```
ARCHETYPE:          GOVERNANCE
ENTRY:              Fires on any user-tasks query API call or UserTask list request
PURPOSE:            Enforce tenant isolation on ALL UserTask visibility operations.
                    Ensure users only see tasks for their tenant, their assignments,
                    and their permission level (viewer vs answerer). Enforce DNA-5.
DISTINCT FROM:      T386 (BFA Multi-Tenant Isolation — BFA-specific; T422 is UserTask-specific)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1104: IUserTaskRegistryService      (tenant-scoped task queries)
  F1106: ITaskAssignmentResolverService (visibility check)
  F1110: INodeSnapshotService          (tenant-scoped node queries)
  F1109: IRunSnapshotService           (tenant-scoped run queries)

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  All → DATABASE FABRIC (SK-382 (ElasticsearchDatabase) → Elasticsearch, PostgreSQL) — all with tenantId filter

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-7 Compliance:  Validates DNA-5 (tenantId on every query), rejects unscoped queries
  AF-8 Security:    Validates OWASP API1 — object-level authorization on every taskId lookup
  AF-9 Judge:       Validates: query without tenantId returns empty (not error), never leaks

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-545: UserTask cross-tenant visibility fence
  CF-557: NodeSnapshot tenant isolation rule

MACHINE/FREEDOM:
  MACHINE:
    - tenantId MANDATORY on every query (DNA-5)
    - IsEligibleToViewAsync must pass before returning task details
    - Cross-tenant object ID access = 403/404 (never 500)

IRON RULES:
  IR-T422-1: Any query without tenantId = DataProcessResult.Failure (not empty result)
  IR-T422-2: User accessing taskId from different tenant = 404 (not 403, to prevent enumeration)
  IR-T422-3: Admin role does NOT override tenant boundary (only super-admin with audit log)

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  ✓ Tenant A user cannot see Tenant B tasks (cross-tenant test passes)
  ✓ Non-eligible user gets 404 on task detail (OWASP API1 compliant)
  ✓ ListUserTasksAsync with no tenantId returns DataProcessResult.Failure
```

## TEMPLATE 90 — human-interaction-flow-v1

**Purpose:** DAG template showing how any flow embeds HumanInteractionGate nodes
with dependency-aware scheduling and non-blocking branch semantics.

```json
{
  "templateId": "human-interaction-flow-v1",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Flow template with human interaction gates, dependency scheduler, non-blocking branches",
  "nodes": [
    {
      "nodeId": "start",
      "nodeType": "StartNode",
      "waitFor": []
    },
    {
      "nodeId": "auto-branch-a",
      "nodeType": "AutomatedTask",
      "waitFor": ["start"],
      "progressWeight": 3
    },
    {
      "nodeId": "auto-branch-b",
      "nodeType": "AutomatedTask",
      "waitFor": ["start"],
      "progressWeight": 2
    },
    {
      "nodeId": "human-gate-1",
      "nodeType": "HumanInteractionGate",
      "taskType": "T413",
      "waitFor": ["start"],
      "blockingPolicy": "blocking",
      "assigneePolicy": { "type": "group", "targets": ["architects"] },
      "progressWeight": 1
    },
    {
      "nodeId": "post-human-task",
      "nodeType": "AutomatedTask",
      "waitFor": ["human-gate-1"],
      "progressWeight": 2
    },
    {
      "nodeId": "join",
      "nodeType": "JoinGate",
      "taskType": "T415",
      "joinPolicy": "required+optional",
      "required": ["auto-branch-a", "post-human-task"],
      "optional": ["auto-branch-b"],
      "waitFor": ["auto-branch-a", "post-human-task", "auto-branch-b"]
    },
    {
      "nodeId": "completion",
      "nodeType": "CompletionGate",
      "taskType": "T417",
      "waitFor": ["join"],
      "requiredTerminalNodes": ["join"],
      "requiredJudgeGates": []
    }
  ]
}
```

**Key behaviors demonstrated:**
- auto-branch-a and auto-branch-b run immediately after start
- human-gate-1 runs concurrently (non-blocking to auto-branches)
- post-human-task is BLOCKED until human-gate-1 is ANSWERED
- join waits for auto-branch-a (required) + post-human-task (required) + auto-branch-b (optional)
- Engine keeps working on auto-branches while human-gate-1 waits

---

## TEMPLATE 91 — progress-run-dashboard-v1

**Purpose:** API contract + snapshot schema for UI progress dashboard.
Fabric-first: zero platform-specific rendering — pure data contract.

```json
{
  "templateId": "progress-run-dashboard-v1",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "apiContracts": {
    "getRunGraph": {
      "method": "GET",
      "path": "/v1/tenants/{tenantId}/runs/{runId}/graph",
      "factory": "F1113",
      "responseShape": {
        "nodes": [{
          "id": "str",
          "type": "str",
          "nodeName": "str",
          "status": "PENDING|READY|RUNNING|WAITING_FOR_USER|BLOCKED|SUCCEEDED|FAILED|SKIPPED|CANCELLED",
          "progress": "float [0,1]",
          "progressPct": "str (e.g. '67%')",
          "blockedBy": ["nodeId"],
          "taskId": "str|null",
          "childRunIds": ["runId"],
          "subStepCount": "int",
          "subStepDone": "int",
          "assigneeDisplay": "str|null (e.g. 'Architects', 'Claimed by Dana')"
        }],
        "edges": [{ "from": "nodeId", "to": "nodeId" }],
        "summary": {
          "total": "int", "completed": "int", "running": "int",
          "waitingUser": "int", "blocked": "int", "failed": "int", "skipped": "int"
        },
        "progressPct": "float",
        "runStatus": "str"
      }
    },
    "getNodeDetail": {
      "method": "GET",
      "path": "/v1/tenants/{tenantId}/runs/{runId}/nodes/{nodeId}",
      "factory": "F1113",
      "tabs": ["summary", "subSteps", "inputsOutputs", "judgeGates", "childExecution"]
    }
  },
  "nodeChipRenderingRules": {
    "PENDING":            { "color": "gray",   "icon": "clock",     "label": "Pending" },
    "READY":              { "color": "blue",   "icon": "ready",     "label": "Ready" },
    "RUNNING":            { "color": "blue",   "icon": "spinner",   "label": "Running {pct}%" },
    "WAITING_FOR_USER":   { "color": "amber",  "icon": "user",      "label": "Waiting: {assigneeDisplay}" },
    "BLOCKED":            { "color": "orange", "icon": "lock",      "label": "Blocked by {blockedBy[0]}" },
    "SUCCEEDED":          { "color": "green",  "icon": "check",     "label": "Done" },
    "FAILED":             { "color": "red",    "icon": "x",         "label": "Failed" },
    "SKIPPED":            { "color": "gray",   "icon": "skip",      "label": "Skipped" },
    "CANCELLED":          { "color": "gray",   "icon": "cancel",    "label": "Cancelled" }
  }
}
```
