# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FLOW-32: SHARABLE FLOWS & RAG TEMPLATE MARKETPLACE
# Added: 2026-03-03 | T516-T535, Templates 116-119
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# FLOW-32: SHARABLE FLOWS & RAG TEMPLATE MARKETPLACE — TASK TYPES CATALOG
## Date: 2026-03-03 | Task Types: T516-T535 | Templates: 116-119
## Extends: TASK_TYPES_CATALOG_MERGED.md (T1-T515)

---

## FLOW-32 REVENUE PATHS

### A. Publish Template Package
`T516 (sanitize) → T517 (validate manifest) → T518 (sign) → T519 (list on marketplace)`

### B. Install / Bind Template
`T520 (discover) → T521 (resolve deps) → T522 (install: snapshot|linked|fork) → T523 (bind connectors)`

### C. Upgrade Linked Installation
`T524 (detect upgrade) → T525 (diff + migrate) → T526 (BFA revalidate) → T527 (rollback gate)`

### D. RAG Blueprint Package
`T528 (export blueprint) → T529 (validate blueprint) → T530 (install blueprint)`

### E. Revenue & Analytics
`T531 (meter events) → T532 (aggregate + settle) → T533 (analytics) → T534 (fraud detect) → T535 (sandbox execute)`

---

TASK TYPE: T516 — Template Package Publish Gate
ARCHETYPE: VALIDATION
ENTRY: Fires when publisher submits package for review
PURPOSE: Scan package for secrets/PII, validate export allowlist, run DLP scanner against all artifacts in bundle. Uses AI ENGINE FABRIC for deep PII detection (not regex only). Blocks packages containing any secret_ref values, production connection strings, or tenant-specific document IDs.
DISTINCT FROM: T219 (product listing — no sanitization), T468 (prompt validation — no package structure)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1339, F1348, F1349, F1350, F1351, F1416
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1339→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, template_packages index)
  F1348→AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher, deep PII/secret detection)
  F1349→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, scan_results index)
  F1350→CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase, allowlist rules — in-process)
  F1351→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, scan_audit index)
  F1416→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, artifact_signatures index)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates sanitizer service on DATABASE + AI ENGINE FABRIC
  AF-4 (RAG): searches SK-37 (safe-code), SK-330 (template-packaging)
  AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-1 (no typed models), DNA-3 (DataProcessResult), DNA-5 (scope)
  AF-8 (Security): validates no production credentials leak
  AF-9 (Judge): validates zero secrets in output, all allowlist rules pass
BFA VALIDATION: CF-715 (template vs marketplace entity conflict), CF-716 (vs FLOW-17 freelancer listing), CF-717 (vs FLOW-18 flow definition namespace)
MACHINE:
  Export allowlist cannot be overridden per tenant
  Secrets detection is non-negotiable (IRON RULE)
  PII scanning uses AI ENGINE FABRIC, never regex-only
  Scan audit stored permanently (compliance requirement)
FREEDOM:
  scanSensitivity: dropdown (low | medium | high)
  autoRejectOnFail: bool (default: true)
  customAllowlistExtensions: list of additional allowed artifact types
  notifyOnBlockedPublish: bool (default: true)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Package with detected secrets = IMMEDIATE REJECT (no override)
  IR-2: Package without valid manifest = BUILD FAILURE
  IR-3: Version semver must be valid and unique per package
  IR-4: All artifact hashes must match declared content-addressable storage
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: Zero secrets detected (AF-9 validates)
  QG-2: Manifest schema validates against JSON schema registry
  QG-3: All declared factory dependencies exist in factory registry
  QG-4: Artifact signed before publish (AF-8 validates cosign)
  QG-5: DLP scan report stored with package version

---

TASK TYPE: T517 — Template Manifest Validation
ARCHETYPE: VALIDATION
ENTRY: After T516 passes sanitization gate
PURPOSE: Deep-validate manifest structure: all declared factories must exist in global registry, parameter schemas must be well-typed (str|number|enum|secret_ref|connection_ref|dataset_ref), dependency tree must be acyclic, required connectors declared, minimum engine version specified.
DISTINCT FROM: T516 (sanitization — T517 is structural/semantic), T468 (prompt schema — not template manifest)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1340, F1341, F1343, F1339
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1340→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, template_manifests index)
  F1341→CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase, semver logic — in-process)
  F1343→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, template_parameter_schemas index)
  F1339→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, template_packages — cross-ref)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-2 (Planning): decomposes manifest into validation sub-tasks
  AF-4 (RAG): searches SK-331 (manifest-validation patterns)
  AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-1 (Dictionary validation), DNA-2 (filter construction)
  AF-9 (Judge): validates dependency graph acyclicity, parameter completeness
BFA VALIDATION: CF-718 (manifest factory refs vs actual factory registry)
MACHINE:
  Dependency cycle = BUILD FAILURE (non-negotiable)
  Missing factory reference = BUILD FAILURE
  Parameter types restricted to 6 allowed types
FREEDOM:
  strictMode: bool (default: true — relaxed allows warnings instead of errors)
  maxDependencyDepth: number (1-10, default: 5)
  allowExperimentalParameters: bool (default: false)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Cyclic dependency declaration = REJECT
  IR-2: Referencing non-existent factory = REJECT
  IR-3: secret_ref parameters NEVER have default values
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: All factories in manifest exist in F1-F1418 registry
  QG-2: Parameter schema is JSON Schema Draft 7+ compliant
  QG-3: Dependency graph is a DAG (AF-9 validates)

---

TASK TYPE: T518 — Template Artifact Signing
ARCHETYPE: SECURITY
ENTRY: After T517 passes manifest validation
PURPOSE: Compute content-addressable hash of all artifacts, generate cosign signature, create SBOM (software bill of materials), produce SLSA provenance attestation. Store immutable signature record. Verification required on every install.
DISTINCT FROM: T468 (prompt signing — different artifact type), T235 (code signing — not template)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1416, F1417, F1418, F1342
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1416→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, artifact_signatures index)
  F1417→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, sbom_records index)
  F1418→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, attestations index)
  F1342→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, template_artifacts — content-addressable)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates signing service on DATABASE FABRIC
  AF-8 (Security): validates hash integrity, signature chain
  AF-9 (Judge): validates SLSA level compliance
BFA VALIDATION: CF-719 (signature namespace vs FLOW-19 CI/CD signing)
MACHINE:
  SHA-256 minimum hash algorithm (non-negotiable)
  Signatures immutable once created
  SBOM includes all factory dependencies declared in manifest
FREEDOM:
  signatureAlgorithm: dropdown (cosign | pgp | sigstore)
  slsaLevel: dropdown (L1 | L2 | L3)
  requireProvenanceAttestation: bool (default: true)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Unsigned artifacts cannot be published = BUILD FAILURE
  IR-2: Tampered artifact (hash mismatch on install) = IMMEDIATE BLOCK
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: Content hash matches declared manifest hash
  QG-2: SBOM covers 100% of declared dependencies
  QG-3: Provenance attestation chain is valid

---

TASK TYPE: T519 — Marketplace Listing Publication
ARCHETYPE: DATA-WRITE
ENTRY: After T518 signs artifacts
PURPOSE: Create marketplace listing document with metadata, screenshots, pricing policy, license terms, category tags, and visibility rules. Emits template.published event. Indexes for full-text search and faceted navigation.
DISTINCT FROM: T219 (product listing — different entity), T160 (content post — no pricing/licensing)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1356, F1357, F1358, F1345, F1346
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1356→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, marketplace_listings index)
  F1357→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, marketplace_categories index)
  F1358→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, marketplace_ratings — initialized empty)
  F1345→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, template_licenses index)
  F1346→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, template_visibility index)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates listing service on DATABASE FABRIC
  AF-4 (RAG): searches SK-332 (marketplace-patterns), SK-110 (marketplace-listing from FLOW-17)
  AF-9 (Judge): validates listing completeness, pricing sanity
BFA VALIDATION: CF-720 (listing entity vs FLOW-16 product), CF-721 (vs FLOW-17 freelancer service)
MACHINE:
  tenantId on every listing (DNA-5)
  Pricing validated: free OR valid pricing model with non-negative amounts
  Visibility rules enforced (private/unlisted/public)
FREEDOM:
  featuredListingEnabled: bool
  maxScreenshots: number (1-20)
  categoryLimit: number (1-5 per listing)
  autoApproveListings: bool (default: false)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Listing without signed artifact = BUILD FAILURE
  IR-2: Pricing policy required for non-free templates
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: Listing has title, description, at least 1 category
  QG-2: Associated artifact is signed (verified against F1416)
  QG-3: License terms present and valid

---

TASK TYPE: T520 — Template Discovery & Search
ARCHETYPE: DATA-READ
ENTRY: Consumer tenant browses marketplace
PURPOSE: Full-text search across listings with faceted filtering (category, price range, rating, install count, compatibility). Respects visibility rules — private templates not shown to unauthorized tenants. Ranked by relevance + quality score composite.
DISTINCT FROM: T220 (product search — different entity/index), T162 (content search — no pricing facets)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1356, F1360, F1361, F1346, F1399
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1356→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, marketplace_listings — search)
  F1360→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, marketplace_search_analytics index)
  F1361→CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase, ranking logic — in-process)
  F1346→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, template_visibility — access check)
  F1399→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, quality_scores — composite score)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-4 (RAG): searches SK-332 (marketplace discovery patterns)
  AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-2 (build_search_filter for empty facets)
  AF-9 (Judge): validates search results respect visibility rules
BFA VALIDATION: CF-722 (search index vs FLOW-16 product search namespace)
MACHINE:
  Visibility enforcement on every query (DNA-5 + F1346 check)
  build_search_filter auto-skips empty facets (DNA-2)
  Quality score bounded [0.0, 1.0] — no cardinality explosion
FREEDOM:
  defaultSortOrder: dropdown (relevance | newest | highest_rated | most_installed)
  resultsPerPage: number (10-100)
  enableFeaturedSection: bool
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Private templates NEVER appear in public search results
  IR-2: Unlisted templates only via direct link
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: Search returns only listings consumer is authorized to see
  QG-2: build_search_filter skips all null/empty fields

---

TASK TYPE: T521 — Template Dependency Resolution
ARCHETYPE: ORCHESTRATION
ENTRY: Consumer selects template for installation
PURPOSE: Resolve full dependency tree from manifest. Check each declared factory exists in consumer tenant's engine. Identify missing connectors that need rebinding. Compute compatibility with consumer's current engine version. Report all blockers before proceeding.
DISTINCT FROM: T517 (publisher-side validation), T163 (content dependency — not factory graph)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1340, F1365, F1366, F1367
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1340→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, template_manifests — read deps)
  F1365→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, installation_records index)
  F1366→CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase, dependency resolver — in-process)
  F1367→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, connector_bindings index)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-2 (Planning): decomposes dependency tree into resolution steps
  AF-4 (RAG): searches SK-333 (dependency-resolution patterns)
  AF-9 (Judge): validates all dependencies resolvable or clearly blocked
BFA VALIDATION: CF-723 (dependency tree vs consumer's existing factory set)
MACHINE:
  Missing required dependency = BLOCK (cannot proceed without resolution)
  Circular dependency = BUILD FAILURE
  Engine version compatibility is semver-range checked
FREEDOM:
  allowOptionalDependencySkip: bool (default: true)
  autoBindMatchingConnectors: bool (default: false)
  showDetailedDependencyTree: bool
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Installation with unresolved required dependency = BLOCKED
  IR-2: Engine version mismatch (major version) = BLOCKED
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: All required dependencies resolved or explicitly blocked with reason
  QG-2: Dependency tree stored for audit

---

TASK TYPE: T522 — Template Installation Executor
ARCHETYPE: ORCHESTRATION
ENTRY: After T521 resolves all dependencies
PURPOSE: Execute installation in one of three modes: SNAPSHOT (clone flow definitions into consumer tenant — no ongoing link), LINKED (install with pointer to publisher version — runs pinned), FORK (break linked copy into local snapshot). Creates installation record. Emits template.installed metering event for revenue tracking.
DISTINCT FROM: T163 (content install — not template), T303 (app install — different lifecycle)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1365, F1368, F1369, F1370, F1390
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1365→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, installation_records index)
  F1368→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, installed_flow_definitions — cloned DAGs)
  F1369→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, installed_config_docs — cloned FREEDOM config)
  F1370→QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams, template.installed event)
  F1390→QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams, metering event for revenue attribution)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates install executor on DATABASE + QUEUE FABRIC
  AF-4 (RAG): searches SK-334 (install-mode patterns)
  AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-5 (installed assets scoped to consumer tenant)
  AF-9 (Judge): validates installation integrity, mode correctness
BFA VALIDATION: CF-724 (installed flow vs consumer's existing flows), CF-725 (metering event vs FLOW-08 billing)
MACHINE:
  SNAPSHOT mode: deep clone, no publisher link
  LINKED mode: reference to publisher+version, pinned semver
  FORK mode: convert linked to snapshot (one-way, irreversible)
  Metering event ALWAYS emitted (revenue tracking is non-negotiable)
  Installed assets carry consumerTenantId, NEVER publisherTenantId
FREEDOM:
  defaultInstallMode: dropdown (snapshot | linked)
  allowForkFromLinked: bool (default: true)
  installApprovalRequired: bool (default: false)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Installed assets MUST carry consumer's tenantId (not publisher's)
  IR-2: LINKED mode pins exact semver — no floating versions
  IR-3: Metering event must fire before installation acknowledged as complete
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: Installation record created with mode, source version, timestamp
  QG-2: All cloned assets scoped to consumer tenant
  QG-3: Metering event confirmed delivered to queue

---

TASK TYPE: T523 — Connector Rebinding Wizard
ARCHETYPE: CONFIGURATION
ENTRY: During T522 when installed template has connector parameters
PURPOSE: Interactive connector rebinding: map publisher's abstract connector_ref parameters to consumer's actual connections (DB credentials, API keys, queue endpoints). Uses secret_ref indirection — actual secrets NEVER stored in template config. Validates each binding against connector type schema.
DISTINCT FROM: T522 (installation — T523 is parameter binding only), T468 (prompt binding — different parameter type)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1367, F1371, F1372, F1373, F1343
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1367→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, connector_bindings — store bindings)
  F1371→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, consumer_connector_registry — available connections)
  F1372→CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase, binding validator — in-process)
  F1373→QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams, binding.completed event)
  F1343→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, template_parameter_schemas — expected types)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates binding wizard on DATABASE + CORE FABRIC
  AF-4 (RAG): searches SK-335 (connector-binding patterns)
  AF-9 (Judge): validates all required connectors bound
BFA VALIDATION: CF-726 (connector binding vs FLOW-15 credential vault namespace)
MACHINE:
  secret_ref always stores reference ID, never actual secret value
  connection_ref validated against consumer's registered connections
  Type mismatch (e.g., binding PostgreSQL ref to Redis connector) = REJECT
FREEDOM:
  autoMatchByType: bool (auto-bind if consumer has exactly 1 matching connector type)
  requireExplicitApproval: bool (default: true for production connectors)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Actual secret values NEVER appear in binding documents
  IR-2: Type mismatch in connector binding = BUILD FAILURE
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: All required connector_ref parameters bound
  QG-2: All bindings type-checked against schema
  QG-3: No secret values in binding records (AF-8 validates)

---

TASK TYPE: T524 — Linked Version Upgrade Detection
ARCHETYPE: EVENT-DRIVEN
ENTRY: Publisher publishes new version of a template that has linked installations
PURPOSE: Detect all linked installations pinned to older versions. Evaluate upgrade policy per consumer (notify-only, auto-patch, auto-minor, forced-security). Emit appropriate events: notification, auto-upgrade trigger, or forced-security-patch trigger. Respects consumer's upgrade policy preferences.
DISTINCT FROM: T525 (diff/migration — T524 is detection only), T160 (content update — not template version)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1374, F1375, F1365, F1344, F1347
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1374→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, upgrade_detections index)
  F1375→QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams, upgrade.detected events)
  F1365→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, installation_records — find linked installs)
  F1344→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, upgrade_policies — read consumer preferences)
  F1347→QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams, deprecation notifications)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-2 (Planning): partition linked installs by upgrade policy
  AF-9 (Judge): validates upgrade policy correctly applied per consumer
BFA VALIDATION: CF-727 (upgrade event vs FLOW-18 flow version events)
MACHINE:
  Forced security patches bypass consumer preferences (IRON RULE)
  Detection runs asynchronously on publish — does not block publisher
  Each linked installation evaluated independently
FREEDOM:
  scanFrequency: dropdown (on_publish | hourly | daily)
  maxUpgradesPerBatch: number (10-1000)
  notificationChannels: list (email | webhook | in-app)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Security-critical patches force upgrade notification regardless of consumer policy
  IR-2: Detection never modifies installation — only emits events
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: All linked installations for affected package detected
  QG-2: Correct upgrade policy applied per consumer
  QG-3: Events emitted with full version context (from-version, to-version, reason)

---

TASK TYPE: T525 — Template Diff & Migration Engine
ARCHETYPE: TRANSFORMATION
ENTRY: After T524 triggers upgrade (manual approval or auto)
PURPOSE: Compute structural diff between current installed version and target version. Generate migration plan: added/removed/modified nodes in flow DAG, changed parameters, new connector requirements. Execute migration in a transaction — all-or-nothing. Preserves consumer's custom bindings and FREEDOM overrides.
DISTINCT FROM: T524 (detection — T525 executes migration), T163 (content migration — not flow structure)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1376, F1377, F1378, F1379
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1376→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, migration_plans index)
  F1377→CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase, diff engine — in-process)
  F1378→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, installed_flow_definitions — modify)
  F1379→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, migration_history index)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates diff/migration service on DATABASE FABRIC
  AF-4 (RAG): searches SK-336 (migration patterns)
  AF-5 (Multi-model): competing models generate migration plan for complex diffs
  AF-9 (Judge): validates migration preserves consumer customizations
BFA VALIDATION: CF-728 (migration vs consumer's BFA registry)
MACHINE:
  Migration is atomic — all-or-nothing (no partial upgrades)
  Consumer FREEDOM overrides preserved during migration
  Migration history stored for audit and rollback
FREEDOM:
  autoMigrateNonBreaking: bool (default: true for patch/minor)
  requireApprovalForMajor: bool (default: true)
  preserveCustomBindings: dropdown (always | prompt | never)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Partial migration = ROLLBACK (never leave installation in inconsistent state)
  IR-2: Consumer's custom FREEDOM values NEVER overwritten without explicit consent
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: Diff accurately identifies all changes between versions
  QG-2: Migration plan is reversible (rollback data stored)
  QG-3: Consumer customizations verified preserved post-migration

---

TASK TYPE: T526 — Post-Upgrade BFA Revalidation
ARCHETYPE: VALIDATION
ENTRY: After T525 completes migration
PURPOSE: Re-run BFA conflict detection on the upgraded installation against consumer tenant's full flow set. Detects conflicts introduced by new version (new entities, events, API paths that collide with consumer's existing flows). If conflicts found, flag for review or trigger auto-rollback.
DISTINCT FROM: T525 (migration execution), T527 (rollback gate — T526 is revalidation)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1380, F1381, F1365
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1380→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, bfa_revalidation_results index)
  F1381→QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams, revalidation.complete event)
  F1365→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, installation_records — update status)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-7 (Compliance): re-runs DNA checks on upgraded installation
  AF-9 (Judge): validates zero new BFA conflicts OR all conflicts acknowledged
BFA VALIDATION: CF-729 (upgraded template vs ALL consumer's existing flows — dynamic check)
MACHINE:
  Revalidation is mandatory — cannot skip (IRON RULE)
  Critical BFA conflict = auto-rollback trigger
  Warning-level conflicts = flag for review
FREEDOM:
  autoRollbackOnCritical: bool (default: true)
  allowWarningContinue: bool (default: true)
  revalidationTimeout: number (seconds, 30-600)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Revalidation MUST run after every upgrade — skipping = BUILD FAILURE
  IR-2: Critical BFA conflict + autoRollback = immediate T527 trigger
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: BFA scan covers all consumer's installed flows
  QG-2: Conflict report stored with severity classifications
  QG-3: Installation status updated (validated | conflicts_found | rolled_back)

---

TASK TYPE: T527 — Upgrade Rollback Gate
ARCHETYPE: RECOVERY
ENTRY: When T526 detects critical BFA conflict OR consumer manually triggers rollback
PURPOSE: Reverse migration using stored rollback data. Restore previous version's flow definitions, config documents, and connector bindings. Update installation record to previous version. Emit rollback event for audit.
DISTINCT FROM: T526 (revalidation — T527 is actual rollback), T525 (forward migration — T527 is reverse)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1379, F1378, F1365, F1381
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1379→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, migration_history — rollback data)
  F1378→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, installed_flow_definitions — restore)
  F1365→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, installation_records — version revert)
  F1381→QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams, rollback.complete event)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-9 (Judge): validates rollback restored to exact previous state
BFA VALIDATION: CF-730 (rolled-back state must match pre-upgrade BFA state)
MACHINE:
  Rollback is atomic — all-or-nothing
  Installation pinned to previous version after rollback
  Rollback data retained even after rollback (audit requirement)
FREEDOM:
  maxRollbackRetries: number (1-3)
  notifyPublisherOnRollback: bool (default: true)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Rollback must restore exact previous state — no partial restore
  IR-2: Rollback data is NEVER deleted (compliance/audit)
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: Post-rollback state matches pre-upgrade snapshot
  QG-2: Installation record updated to previous version
  QG-3: Rollback event emitted with reason

---

TASK TYPE: T528 — RAG Blueprint Export
ARCHETYPE: DATA-WRITE
ENTRY: Publisher wants to share RAG configuration as template
PURPOSE: Export RAG strategy configuration as a blueprint: chunking rules, routing strategy, ranking weights, prompt IDs (references not content), metadata schema, embedding model preference. Never exports actual documents, embeddings, or query logs. Separates HOW to retrieve from WHAT was retrieved.
DISTINCT FROM: T530 (install blueprint — T528 is export), T468 (prompt export — not RAG strategy)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1382, F1383, F1384, F1385
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1382→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, rag_blueprints index)
  F1383→RAG FABRIC (IRagService, strategy config extraction)
  F1384→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, rag_metadata_schemas index)
  F1385→CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase, blueprint serializer — in-process)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates blueprint export on DATABASE + RAG FABRIC
  AF-4 (RAG): searches SK-337 (rag-blueprint patterns), existing RAG skills
  AF-8 (Security): validates no data leaks in blueprint
  AF-9 (Judge): validates blueprint contains only strategy, never data
BFA VALIDATION: CF-731 (blueprint vs FLOW-29 RAG deep research config namespace)
MACHINE:
  Blueprint NEVER contains: actual documents, embeddings, query logs, user data
  Prompt IDs are references — actual prompt content stays in publisher's prompt library
  Strategy names must be from approved IRagService.SearchAsync() strategies
FREEDOM:
  includeEmbeddingModelPreference: bool (default: true)
  includeCustomRankingWeights: bool (default: true)
  exportFormat: dropdown (json | yaml)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Blueprint with actual document content = IMMEDIATE REJECT
  IR-2: Blueprint with embedding vectors = IMMEDIATE REJECT
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: Zero data content in blueprint (AF-8 validates)
  QG-2: All referenced strategies exist in IRagService
  QG-3: Metadata schema is valid JSON Schema

---

TASK TYPE: T529 — RAG Blueprint Validation
ARCHETYPE: VALIDATION
ENTRY: After T528 exports blueprint OR during marketplace review
PURPOSE: Deep-validate RAG blueprint: strategy references are valid (one of 7 IRagService strategies), metadata schema is well-formed, prompt IDs exist in publisher's prompt library, embedding model preference is supported, chunking rules are syntactically valid.
DISTINCT FROM: T528 (export — T529 is validation), T517 (manifest validation — not RAG-specific)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1386, F1387, F1382
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1386→CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase, blueprint validator — in-process)
  F1387→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, rag_validation_results index)
  F1382→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, rag_blueprints — read for validation)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-7 (Compliance): validates against RAG FABRIC strategy catalog
  AF-9 (Judge): validates completeness and correctness
BFA VALIDATION: CF-732 (validated blueprint must not conflict with consumer's existing RAG config)
MACHINE:
  Strategy must be one of: Split, FanOut, Tiered, Hybrid, Graph, Vector, Multi
  Invalid strategy reference = REJECT
FREEDOM:
  allowExperimentalStrategies: bool (default: false)
  validationStrictness: dropdown (warn | error)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Invalid strategy reference = BUILD FAILURE
  IR-2: Validation result stored for every blueprint (audit)
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: All strategies valid against IRagService catalog
  QG-2: Metadata schema validates
  QG-3: Prompt ID references resolvable

---

TASK TYPE: T530 — RAG Blueprint Installation
ARCHETYPE: ORCHESTRATION
ENTRY: Consumer installs template with RAG blueprint component
PURPOSE: Apply RAG blueprint to consumer's tenant: configure IRagService with blueprint's strategy, apply chunking rules, set ranking weights, register metadata schema, bind prompt references to consumer's prompt library. Does NOT populate data — consumer populates their own indices.
DISTINCT FROM: T529 (validation — T530 is installation), T522 (flow installation — T530 is RAG-specific)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1388, F1389, F1383, F1367
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1388→RAG FABRIC (IRagService, strategy configuration — apply)
  F1389→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, consumer RAG config — store per-tenant)
  F1383→RAG FABRIC (IRagService, strategy config reading)
  F1367→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, connector_bindings — RAG bindings)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates RAG installer on RAG + DATABASE FABRIC
  AF-4 (RAG): searches SK-337 (rag-blueprint patterns)
  AF-9 (Judge): validates RAG config applied correctly to consumer tenant
BFA VALIDATION: CF-733 (installed RAG config vs consumer's existing RAG flows)
MACHINE:
  Consumer's existing RAG data untouched (IRON RULE)
  Blueprint applies CONFIGURATION, never CONTENT
  Consumer must populate their own data indices
FREEDOM:
  mergeWithExistingConfig: dropdown (replace | merge | additive)
  autoPopulateSampleData: bool (default: false — sandbox only)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: RAG installation NEVER modifies consumer's existing data indices
  IR-2: Consumer's tenantId used for all config storage (DNA-5)
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: RAG strategy correctly configured in consumer's IRagService
  QG-2: Consumer tenant's data indices untouched
  QG-3: Binding record created for prompt references

---

TASK TYPE: T531 — Template Usage Metering
ARCHETYPE: EVENT-DRIVEN
ENTRY: On every template execution, installation, or significant action
PURPOSE: Emit bounded metering events with template-specific dimensions (packageId, publisherTenantId, consumerTenantId, action type, version). Uses existing metering infrastructure (extends T90 pattern) with new template.* metric dimensions. Events feed into revenue attribution for affiliate/revenue share.
DISTINCT FROM: T90 (generic metering — T531 adds template dimensions), T532 (aggregation — T531 is event emission)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1390, F1391, F1392
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1390→QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams, metering event emission)
  F1391→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, usage_aggregations index)
  F1392→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, settlements index — feed)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates metering service on QUEUE + DATABASE FABRIC
  AF-4 (RAG): searches SK-338 (metering patterns), SK-89 (existing metering)
  AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-5 (metering scoped), DNA-2 (bounded dimensions)
BFA VALIDATION: CF-725 (metering vs FLOW-08 billing events)
MACHINE:
  Dimensions bounded (max 10 per event) — no cardinality explosion
  publisherTenantId and consumerTenantId always separate fields
  Metering events append-only (immutable stream)
FREEDOM:
  meteringGranularity: dropdown (per_execution | per_hour | per_day)
  additionalDimensions: list (up to 5 custom dimensions)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Metering event MUST include both publisher and consumer tenant IDs
  IR-2: Bounded dimensions only — unbounded cardinality = BUILD FAILURE
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: Events delivered to queue (confirmed)
  QG-2: Dimensions within cardinality bounds
  QG-3: All required fields present

---

TASK TYPE: T532 — Revenue Aggregation & Settlement
ARCHETYPE: COMPUTATION
ENTRY: Scheduled (daily/weekly) OR on-demand settlement trigger
PURPOSE: Aggregate metering events into revenue periods. Calculate publisher share based on licensing model (per-install, per-run, revenue-share percentage). Generate settlement records with detailed breakdown. Feed into payout pipeline.
DISTINCT FROM: T531 (event emission — T532 is aggregation), T90 (generic billing — T532 is template-specific)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1391, F1392, F1393, F1394, F1345
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1391→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, usage_aggregations — read events)
  F1392→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, settlements — store settlement records)
  F1393→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, payouts — create payout entries)
  F1394→CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase, revenue calculator — in-process)
  F1345→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, template_licenses — pricing rules)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates settlement service on DATABASE FABRIC
  AF-9 (Judge): validates settlement math accuracy
BFA VALIDATION: CF-734 (settlement vs FLOW-08 payment processing)
MACHINE:
  Revenue share capped at 50% (platform minimum retention)
  Settlement records immutable once generated
  Double-settlement detection required (idempotency — DNA-7)
FREEDOM:
  settlementFrequency: dropdown (daily | weekly | monthly)
  minimumPayoutThreshold: number (amount before payout triggered)
  payoutMethod: dropdown (bank_transfer | platform_credit)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Revenue share NEVER exceeds 50% — BUILD FAILURE
  IR-2: Settlement records are immutable — no modification after creation
  IR-3: Duplicate settlement detection (DNA-7 idempotency) required
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: Settlement math verified (AF-9 cross-checks)
  QG-2: All metering events covered (no gaps in aggregation period)
  QG-3: Idempotency key prevents float-settlement

---

TASK TYPE: T533 — Template Analytics & Quality Scoring
ARCHETYPE: COMPUTATION
ENTRY: Scheduled (hourly/daily) analytics aggregation
PURPOSE: Compute quality scores for templates: success rate (executions without errors), average latency, rollback rate, install-to-active ratio, rating average, fraud signal score. All metrics bounded (no per-user cardinality explosion). Feed into marketplace ranking and publisher dashboard.
DISTINCT FROM: T532 (revenue — T533 is quality), T220 (product analytics — different entity)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1398, F1399, F1400, F1401
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1398→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, funnel_events — read)
  F1399→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, quality_scores — store)
  F1400→CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase, quality calculator — in-process)
  F1401→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, trust_incidents — fraud signals)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates analytics on DATABASE FABRIC
  AF-9 (Judge): validates score computation, checks for gaming patterns
BFA VALIDATION: CF-735 (analytics vs FLOW-13 data warehouse metrics)
MACHINE:
  Quality score bounded [0.0, 1.0]
  Metrics aggregated per-package (not per-user — bounded dimensions)
  Fraud signals auto-flagged above threshold
FREEDOM:
  qualityWeights: configurable per metric (success_rate, latency, rollback_rate, rating)
  fraudThreshold: number (0.0-1.0)
  analyticsRetentionDays: number (30-365)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Metrics NEVER stored at per-user granularity (cardinality rule)
  IR-2: Quality score always recalculated, never cached stale
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: All metric components within expected ranges
  QG-2: Fraud signals checked against pattern library
  QG-3: Analytics stored with bounded dimensions

---

TASK TYPE: T534 — Template Fraud Detection
ARCHETYPE: ANALYSIS
ENTRY: Triggered by T533 anomaly signals OR on suspicious activity patterns
PURPOSE: Detect marketplace fraud: fake reviews/ratings, install farming (inflating install count), clone/plagiarism of existing templates, revenue manipulation (self-install loops). Uses AI ENGINE FABRIC for similarity detection. Emits trust incidents.
DISTINCT FROM: T533 (analytics — T534 is fraud-specific), T235 (code security — not marketplace fraud)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1401, F1402, F1403, F1404
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1401→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, trust_incidents index)
  F1402→AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher, similarity + anomaly detection)
  F1403→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, fraud_patterns index)
  F1404→QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams, fraud.detected event)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-5 (Multi-model): competing models analyze fraud patterns
  AF-8 (Security): validates detection accuracy
  AF-9 (Judge): confirms fraud signal before escalation
BFA VALIDATION: CF-736 (fraud detection vs FLOW-17 freelancer trust system)
MACHINE:
  AI-powered similarity detection for clone/plagiarism
  Self-install loop detection by tracking publisher→consumer tenant cycles
  Trust incidents require human review before action
FREEDOM:
  autoSuspendOnHighFraud: bool (default: false — review first)
  similarityThreshold: number (0.0-1.0, default: 0.85)
  anomalyDetectionWindow: dropdown (24h | 7d | 30d)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Fraud accusation MUST have evidence trail before any action
  IR-2: Human review required before template suspension
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: Evidence trail for every fraud signal
  QG-2: Similarity score computed and stored
  QG-3: No false-positive auto-suspension (human gate required)

---

TASK TYPE: T535 — Template Sandbox Execution
ARCHETYPE: EXECUTION
ENTRY: Consumer wants to test template before install OR publisher wants to demo
PURPOSE: Execute template in isolated sandbox with synthetic data, stubbed connectors, and network egress allowlist. No production data accessible. Time-limited execution (configurable). Generates execution report with success/failure, latency, resource usage. Sandbox destroyed after test.
DISTINCT FROM: T522 (real install — T535 is sandbox only), T303 (app sandbox — different isolation model)
FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1405, F1406, F1407, F1408, F1409, F1410
FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1405→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, sandbox_envs index)
  F1406→CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase, sandbox provisioner — in-process)
  F1407→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, synthetic_datasets index)
  F1408→QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams, sandbox.execute event)
  F1409→DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch, sandbox_results index)
  F1410→CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase, sandbox destroyer — in-process)
AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generates sandbox service on DATABASE + QUEUE + CORE FABRIC
  AF-8 (Security): validates sandbox isolation (no production access)
  AF-9 (Judge): validates sandbox execution results
BFA VALIDATION: CF-737 (sandbox vs FLOW-18 flow execution namespace), CF-738 (sandbox vs FLOW-19 CI/CD sandbox)
MACHINE:
  Sandbox NEVER accesses production data (IRON RULE)
  Time-limited execution: max configurable timeout
  Network egress restricted to allowlist
  Sandbox environment destroyed after execution
FREEDOM:
  maxExecutionTimeSeconds: number (30-600)
  syntheticDataSize: dropdown (small | medium | large)
  networkEgressAllowlist: list (configurable per template)
  sandboxRetentionMinutes: number (5-60, then auto-destroy)
IRON RULES:
  IR-1: Sandbox NEVER connects to production connectors = IMMEDIATE BLOCK
  IR-2: Sandbox auto-destroyed after retention period (no leaked resources)
  IR-3: Network egress limited to declared allowlist
QUALITY GATES:
  QG-1: Sandbox isolation verified (no production data access)
  QG-2: Execution report generated with metrics
  QG-3: Sandbox destroyed after completion (verified)

## FLOW-32 TEMPLATE INDEX

| Template | Name | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| 116 | template-publish-v1.json | DAG: T516→T517→T518→T519 |
| 117 | template-install-v1.json | DAG: T520→T521→T522→T523 |

# FLOW-33 — System Initiation: Self-Building Bootstrap Engine
# Added: 2026-03-03 | Post FLOW-32 state
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# FLOW-33 — System Initiation: Self-Building Bootstrap Engine
## TASK TYPES CATALOG — Engine Contract Extension

**FLOW Reference:** FLOW-33  
**Backward Compatibility:** T1-T535 UNCHANGED  
**New Task Types:** T536-T542  
**New Templates:** Template 120-55  

---

## TASK TYPE: T536 — Platform Bootstrap Orchestration

**ARCHETYPE:** ORCHESTRATION  
**ENTRY:** Fires on `PlatformBootRequested` OR when bootstrap sentinel is missing/outdated in Elasticsearch  
**PURPOSE:** Execute the full platform bootstrap sequence — schema registration, plan bundle import, GraphRAG seeding, core flow compilation, smoke test — in a resumable, idempotent DAG. This is the first real flow the system ever runs.

**DISTINCT FROM:**
- T103 (Tenant Onboarding) — T103 onboards a business tenant; T536 bootstraps the SYSTEM itself
- T53 (Flow Compile) — T53 compiles a single flow; T536 orchestrates the entire bootstrap pipeline including T53
- T54 (Flow Run) — T54 runs a compiled flow; T536 triggers T54 as its smoke test step

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F1419, F1420, F1421, F1423, F190, F191, F192, F194 — resolved via CreateAsync()  
**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F1419 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) + QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams)
- F1420 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) + RAG FABRIC (Vector)
- F1421 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL + Elasticsearch)
- F1423 → DATABASE FABRIC via F65 (GraphAI/Neo4j)
- F190 → FLOW ENGINE FABRIC (FlowDefinition storage in ES)
- F191 → FLOW ENGINE FABRIC (FlowValidation)
- F192 → FLOW ENGINE FABRIC (FlowRuntime)
- F194 → DATABASE FABRIC (SchemaRegistry in ES)

**FLOW TEMPLATE:** Template 120 — `platform-bootstrap-v1`

**Flow DAG (no-code orchestration):**
```
Trigger: PlatformBootRequested (or first-run sentinel absent)
  ↓
1. check_sentinel           F1419:CheckBootstrapStatus
   ↓ (if already CORE, skip to terminal)
2. register_core_schemas    F194:RegisterSchema
   Output: CoreSchemasRegistered
  ↓
3. import_plan_bundle       F1420:ValidatePlanBundle → F1420:IngestSourceDocuments → F1420:CompileRegistry
   Output: PlanBundleImported
  ↓
4. seed_graph_catalog       F1423:InitializeGraphSchema → F1423:SeedCatalogGraph
   Output: GraphCatalogSeeded
  ↓
5. coverage_scan            F191:CheckFactoryRegistry
   Output: CoverageScanCompleted
  ↓
  [Decision: missingFactories > 0?]
   ↳ YES → emit_capability_gaps (Queue: CapabilityGapDetected)
           → wait_for_gaps_resolved (wait event: CapabilityGapsResolved)
   ↳ NO  → compile_core_flows
  ↓
6. compile_core_flows       T53 via F190:StoreDefinition
   Output: CoreFlowsCompiled
  ↓
7. publish_core_flows       F190:PublishDefinition
   Output: CoreFlowsPublished
  ↓
8. smoke_run                T54 via F192:StartRun (FLOW-SMOKE-01)
   Output: SmokeRunCompleted
  ↓
9. bootstrap_done           F1419:SetBootstrappedSentinel
   Emits: BootstrapCompleted
```

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-2 (Planning): decomposes T536 into phase-by-phase steps, validates against oracle counts
- AF-4 (RAG): retrieves SK-346 (Bootstrap Pattern), SK-347 (GraphRAG Init), SK-01 (Core Interfaces)
- AF-9 (Judge): validates DAG completeness, checks all factory dependencies resolve, verifies sentinel logic
- AF-11 (Feedback): stores bootstrap phase timings and coverage scan results for future improvement

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-739: Validate PlanBundle ranges match session state oracle BEFORE any registry writes
- CF-740: Bootstrap sentinel MUST be written BEFORE BootstrapCompleted is emitted (outbox)
- CF-741: Schema registration for BootstrapCompleted schema MUST precede any event that emits it
- No conflict with FLOW-01–FLOW-32: T536 is SYSTEM scope, uses its own event namespace

**MACHINE/FREEDOM:**
- MACHINE: Phase order (schema → bundle → graph → coverage → compile → smoke → sentinel) — fixed
- MACHINE: Idempotency key = `platform-bootstrap-v1` — cannot change
- FREEDOM: `smokeFlowId`, `phaseTimeoutSeconds`, `requiredGateScore`, `graphValidationThreshold`

**IRON RULES:**
- MUST be idempotent — re-running produces identical state, no float-writes
- MUST abort if oracle validation fails (F1420:ValidatePlanBundle returns valid=false)
- MUST NOT emit BootstrapCompleted before smoke run passes
- MUST persist phase state to ES before emitting phase event (outbox pattern)

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9 checks):**
- All 7 registry types populated with docCount matching oracle ranges
- GraphCatalogSeeded: familiesCount ≥ 83, factoriesCount ≥ 630, edgesCount > 0
- SmokeRun: statusCode = 'COMPLETED', no DLQ events
- BootstrapSentinel: version = desired version, completedAt set

## TASK TYPE: T537 — Plan Bundle Install Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** VALIDATION_GATE  
**ENTRY:** Fires after `PlanBundleUploaded` event — validates bundle before any registry mutation  
**PURPOSE:** Three-stage validation gate — (1) range integrity check vs session state oracle, (2) referential integrity check (all task dependencies exist in factory registry), (3) schema conformance check on extracted records. Fail-fast before any ES writes.

**DISTINCT FROM:**
- T536 (Bootstrap Orchestration) — T536 calls T537 as a sub-step; T537 can run standalone for incremental updates
- T68 (Schema Validation) — T68 validates event schemas; T537 validates plan bundle structural integrity

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F1420, F194, F191 — resolved via CreateAsync()  
**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F1420 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch) + RAG FABRIC (Vector)
- F194 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES — SchemaRegistry)
- F191 → FLOW ENGINE FABRIC (validation service)

**FLOW TEMPLATE:** Template 121 — `install-plan-bundle-v1`

**Flow DAG (no-code):**
```
1. parse_bundle_manifest   → extract ranges, sourceFiles, checksums
2. validate_ranges         F1420:ValidatePlanBundle
   → check F1-F1418, T1-T535, SK-1-SK-345, CF-1-CF-738, ST-1-ST-454, DD-1-DD-335, DR-1-DR-249
3. check_referential_integrity
   → every task dependency exists in factory range
   → every template references existing task types
   → every BFA rule references existing entities/events
4. validate_schema_conformance   F194:ValidateSchema
   → each extracted record conforms to registry schema
5. [Gate: all checks pass?]
   ↳ YES → emit PlanBundleValidated
   ↳ NO  → emit PlanBundleRejected (with structured error report) → DLQ
```

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-4 (RAG): retrieves SK-349 (Registry Compilation Pattern) + SK-350 (Referential Integrity)
- AF-9 (Judge): checks validation completeness — are all 7 artifact types checked?

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-739: Range oracle check MUST run BEFORE referential integrity check
- CF-742: Rejected bundles MUST NOT partially update registries — rollback required

**MACHINE/FREEDOM:**
- MACHINE: All 3 validation stages mandatory and in order
- FREEDOM: `strictMode` (allow minor range gaps), `checksumAlgorithm`, `rejectionWebhook`

**IRON RULES:**
- MUST check ranges BEFORE writing anything to ES
- Partial validation failures MUST produce structured error report with exact gap location

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- All artifact type ranges validated (7 types)
- Zero referential integrity violations
- Schema conformance: 100% of extracted records pass

---

## TASK TYPE: T538 — GraphRAG Catalog Seed

**ARCHETYPE:** INFRASTRUCTURE_INIT  
**ENTRY:** Fires after `PlanBundleImported` during bootstrap, OR when graph integrity check detects missing nodes  
**PURPOSE:** Creates and validates the two-layer GraphRAG from the compiled registries. Catalog Graph (static plan connectivity) + Impact Graph (dynamic provider-test-flow links). Multi-tenant partitioned. Self-healing — reruns are safe.

**DISTINCT FROM:**
- T59 (Graph RAG Query) — T59 queries the graph; T538 builds and seeds it
- T537 (Install Gate) — T537 validates the bundle; T538 writes the graph from validated registries

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F1423, F1421, F1420, F65 — resolved via CreateAsync()  
**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F1423 → DATABASE FABRIC via F65 (IGraphAiProvider — Neo4j/Neptune provider)
- F1421 → DATABASE FABRIC (PostgreSQL + ES — Implementation Registry)
- F1420 → DATABASE FABRIC (Elasticsearch — source registries)
- F65 → DATABASE FABRIC (GraphAI fabric — resolved at runtime via config)

**FLOW TEMPLATE:** Template 122 — `seed-graph-catalog-v1`

**Flow DAG (no-code):**
```
1. init_graph_schema       F1423:InitializeGraphSchema
   → create constraints + indexes (unique id per label, scope+tenantId index)
2. create_family_nodes     → parse family_registry → F1423 graph upsert
3. create_factory_nodes    → parse factory_registry → F1423 graph upsert  
4. create_method_nodes     → derive methods from factory records → F1423 graph upsert
5. create_tasktype_nodes   → parse task_type_registry → F1423 graph upsert
6. create_skill_nodes      → parse skill_registry → F1423 graph upsert
7. create_template_nodes   → parse flow_template_registry → F1423 graph upsert
8. create_bfarule_nodes    → parse bfa_rule_registry → F1423 graph upsert
9. wire_catalog_edges      → HAS_FACTORY, HAS_METHOD, DEPENDS_ON, USES_SKILL, 
                              IMPLEMENTS_TASK, USES_FACTORY, APPLIES_TO
10. validate_graph         F1423:ValidateGraphIntegrity
    → counts match oracle, no dangling edges
11. store_query_templates  → Q1/Q2/Q3 stored in prompt_registry (F1428)
12. emit GraphCatalogSeeded { familiesCount, factoriesCount, edgesCount }
```

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-4 (RAG): retrieves SK-347 (GraphRAG Init Pattern), SK-348 (Graph Schema)
- AF-9 (Judge): validates node counts ≥ oracle values, all query templates resolvable

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-743: Graph seeding MUST use F1423 via CreateAsync() — never Neo4j driver directly
- CF-744: tenantId = 'SYSTEM' on ALL catalog graph nodes — cross-tenant contamination = FAIL
- CF-745: Graph integrity check MUST run before emitting GraphCatalogSeeded

**MACHINE/FREEDOM:**
- MACHINE: Node/edge types and relationship names — fixed (documented in F1423)
- MACHINE: Scope field on every node — non-negotiable
- FREEDOM: `graphProvider` (Neo4j/Neptune), `batchSize`, `parallelNodeCreation`

**IRON RULES:**
- MUST validate counts ≥ oracle BEFORE emitting success event
- MUST NOT create cross-scope edges (SYSTEM ↔ TENANT)
- Q1/Q2/Q3 query templates MUST be stored and resolvable before seeding is marked complete

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- familiesCount ≥ 83 (existing) + 2 (new FLOW-33)
- factoriesCount ≥ 630 (existing) + 10 (new FLOW-33)
- All DEPENDS_ON edges resolve to existing Factory nodes
- Q1/Q2/Q3 execute without errors against seeded graph

---

## TASK TYPE: T539 — Implement-Family Meta-Loop

**ARCHETYPE:** GENERATION_ORCHESTRATION  
**ENTRY:** Fires on `FamilyImplementationRequested { familyId, targetProviders[], priority }`  
**PURPOSE:** The central "self-building" loop. Given a family spec, orchestrates ContextPack retrieval → multi-model generation → 5-arbiter validation → retry loop → local deploy → smoke test → artifact publication. Can run N families in parallel ("factories side by side").

**DISTINCT FROM:**
- T44+ (specific flow task types) — those generate domain flows; T539 generates the factories themselves
- T58 (Self-Extend) — T58 generates new engine contracts; T539 implements an already-specified factory family

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F1422, F1424, F1425, F1426, F1427, F1428, F55, F192, F1421 — resolved via CreateAsync()  
**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F1422 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + RAG FABRIC (Hybrid)
- F1424 → FLOW ENGINE FABRIC + AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher)
- F1425 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES)
- F1426 → RAG FABRIC (Hybrid: Graph+Vector+Merge) + DATABASE FABRIC via F65
- F1427 → DATABASE FABRIC via F65 (Impact Graph) + QUEUE FABRIC
- F1428 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + RAG FABRIC (Vector)
- F55 → EXECUTION FABRIC (CiCd — local runner mode)
- F192 → FLOW ENGINE FABRIC (FlowRuntime)
- F1421 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG+ES — Implementation Registry)

**FLOW TEMPLATE:** Template 123 — `implement-family-v1`

**Flow DAG (no-code):**
```
Trigger: FamilyImplementationRequested
  ↓
1. load_family_spec         F1422:GetFamilySkillPack
2. build_context_pack       F1426:BuildForFamily (Hybrid RAG)
3. build_node_prompt        F1428:BuildNodePrompt (AF1 template + ContextPack)
  ↓
4. generate_candidates      AF-5: AiDispatcher multi-model parallel
   [OpenAI / Claude / Gemini / DeepSeek] → N candidate implementations
5. merge_candidates         AF-10: select/merge best candidate
  ↓
6. arbiter_loop             F1425:RunArbiters (all 5 must pass)
   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   │ A: COVERAGE  B: SECURITY  C: DNA  D: TEST  E: BFA │
   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   [Arbiter passes? YES → continue]
   [ANY fails → F1425:BuildArbiterPrompt (with failure trace) → back to step 4]
   [Iteration > maxIterations → FamilyImplementationFailed → DLQ]
  ↓
7. local_deploy             F55:ICiCdProvider (local runner: dotnet build + dotnet test)
8. e2e_smoke_run            F192:StartRun (smoke flow for this family)
9. publish_artifacts        F1421:UpdateImplementationStatus (→ INJECTED tier)
10. store_feedback          AF-11: F1428:RecordPromptFeedback
11. emit FamilyImplementationCompleted { familyId, iterations, judgeScore }
```

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-1 (Genesis): generates implementation code from ContextPack + node prompt
- AF-4 (RAG): F1426:BuildForFamily — retrieves all family context
- AF-5 (Multi-model): runs N providers in parallel (config-driven count)
- AF-6 (Code Review): automated review pass before arbiters
- AF-7 (Compliance): DNA-1..9 scan before arbiters
- AF-8 (Security): security scan (part of ARBITER-B)
- AF-9 (Judge): overall quality gate after all arbiters pass
- AF-10 (Merge): combines multi-model outputs
- AF-11 (Feedback): stores per-run outcome for prompt evolution

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-746: Generated code MUST register all entities in BFA indices before promotion
- CF-747: Multi-model candidates MUST be isolated — no cross-contamination between models
- CF-748: Arbiter retry loop MUST propagate exact failure trace to next generation attempt
- Checks against FLOW-01–FLOW-32: entity/event name collisions with existing BFA indices

**MACHINE/FREEDOM:**
- MACHINE: Arbiter order and all 5 arbiters mandatory
- MACHINE: AF station order (AF-5 → AF-10 → AF-7 → AF-9) within loop
- MACHINE: Maximum iterations = 10 (no config override)
- FREEDOM: `modelProviders[]`, `parallelModelCount`, `contextPackTokenBudget`, 
           `deployTarget`, `smokeFlowId`, `promotionTierOnSuccess`

**IRON RULES:**
- ALL 5 arbiters MUST pass — no "partial pass" (e.g., BFA failing is not acceptable even if DNA passes)
- Generated code MUST NOT import specific providers (PostgreSQL, Redis, OpenAI) — always through fabric
- Generated code MUST extend MicroserviceBase — no exceptions
- Failure trace from arbiter MUST be verbatim in next generation prompt — not summarized

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- judgeScore ≥ minJudgeScore (freedom config, default 85/100)
- Local deploy: 0 build errors, 0 test failures
- Smoke run: all flow nodes complete, no DLQ events
- Implementation Registry: status = INJECTED with evidence (commitHash + ciRunId)

---

## TASK TYPE: T540 — Arbiter Consensus Gate

**ARCHETYPE:** VALIDATION_GATE  
**ENTRY:** Fires within T539 implement-family loop after each candidate generation  
**PURPOSE:** The 5-arbiter validation engine as a standalone task type — can be called independently for validating any generated artifact, not just factory implementations. Returns structured pass/fail with exact failure traces per arbiter.

**DISTINCT FROM:**
- T539 (Implement-Family) — T539 orchestrates the full loop; T540 is the arbiter gate node within it
- T45 (Quality Gate in other flows) — T540 is specialized for code generation artifacts with DNA + fabric rules

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F1425, F1426, F1428 — resolved via CreateAsync()  
**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F1425 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (AiDispatcher) + DATABASE FABRIC (ES)
- F1426 → RAG FABRIC (Hybrid) + DATABASE FABRIC via F65
- F1428 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES)

**FLOW TEMPLATE:** Template 124 — `arbiter-consensus-v1`

**Flow DAG (no-code):**
```
1. build_arbiter_context    F1426:BuildForFactory (get relevant BFA rules, DNA checklist)
2. run_arbiters_parallel    F1425:RunArbiters
   [COVERAGE] [SECURITY] [DNA] [TESTING] [BFA] — run in parallel (faster)
3. collect_results          → { allPassed, results[{arbiterId, passed, score, failureTrace}] }
4. [Decision: allPassed?]
   ↳ YES → emit ArbitersAllPassed { runId, minScore }
   ↳ NO  → emit ArbitersFailed { runId, failedArbiters[], traces[] }
              → store failure in implementation_registry (for trend analysis)
```

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-5 (Multi-model): Each arbiter uses AiDispatcher with model specialized for that arbiter type
- AF-9 (Judge): post-arbiter overall quality check (score aggregation)
- AF-11 (Feedback): stores per-arbiter scores + traces for prompt improvement

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-748: Failure traces MUST be verbatim — no AI paraphrasing of the failure reason
- CF-749: Arbiter prompts MUST cite which ContextPack skills they used

**MACHINE/FREEDOM:**
- MACHINE: 5 arbiters always run (even if early ones fail — collect all failures)
- FREEDOM: `arbiterModelMap` (which model runs which arbiter), `minPassScore`

**IRON RULES:**
- Must collect ALL arbiter results before emitting decision (no short-circuit)
- Failure trace must include: arbiterId, specific DNA/BFA rule violated, line reference if available

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- All 5 arbiter results present in output
- Failure traces non-empty for failed arbiters

---

## TASK TYPE: T541 — Regression Impact Trigger

**ARCHETYPE:** CHANGE_DETECTION  
**ENTRY:** Fires on `FactoryContractChanged` or `ProviderImplementationChanged` events (from repo PR merge)  
**PURPOSE:** When a factory interface changes OR a provider adds/modifies a method, compute the full blast radius (providers + flows + test suites that must rerun) and trigger a regression run. Implements the "elastic must be retested when mongo gets a new method" rule.

**DISTINCT FROM:**
- T539 (Implement-Family) — T539 builds new; T541 retests existing when change is detected
- T54 (Flow Run) — T54 runs a specific flow; T541 determines WHICH flows need rerunning

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F1427, F1423, F1421, F55, F192 — resolved via CreateAsync()  
**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F1427 → DATABASE FABRIC via F65 (Impact Graph) + QUEUE FABRIC (regression events)
- F1423 → DATABASE FABRIC via F65 (GraphAI — impact queries)
- F1421 → DATABASE FABRIC (PG+ES — implementation registry)
- F55 → EXECUTION FABRIC (CiCd — trigger test runs)
- F192 → FLOW ENGINE FABRIC (run smoke flows)

**FLOW TEMPLATE:** Template 125 — `regression-impact-v1`

**Flow DAG (no-code):**
```
Trigger: FactoryContractChanged { factoryId, changedMethods[], type }
  ↓
1. compute_impact           F1427:ComputeImpact
   → impactedProviders[], impactedFlows[], impactedTestSuites[], compatibilityPatches[]
  ↓
2. check_compatibility      → any provider missing new methods?
   ↳ YES → emit CompatibilityPatchRequired (per missing provider) → T539 loop
   ↳ NO  → continue
  ↓
3. [Fork: parallel regression branches]
   Branch A: F55 trigger contract tests for ALL impacted providers
   Branch B: F192 run smoke flows for ALL impacted flow templates
  ↓
4. join_regression_results  → { allPassed: bool, failedProviders[], failedFlows[] }
  ↓
5. [Decision: allPassed?]
   ↳ YES → emit RegressionPassed { factoryId, coveredProviders, coveredFlows }
   ↳ NO  → emit RegressionFailed (with structured failure list) → create fix tasks
```

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-4 (RAG): F1426:BuildForRegression — fetches impact context from graph
- AF-9 (Judge): validates completeness of impact computation vs graph oracle

**BFA VALIDATION:**
- CF-750: Regression scope MUST include ALL providers implementing the changed interface
- CF-739 (referenced): Regression results MUST be stored before closing the change event

**MACHINE/FREEDOM:**
- MACHINE: ALL impacted providers and flows must be tested — no selective skipping
- FREEDOM: `parallelTestWorkers`, `testTimeout`, `regressionFailureWebhook`

**IRON RULES:**
- MUST NOT close a FactoryContractChanged event without regression completion (or explicit override)
- MUST detect "missing method in provider" as a compatibility patch requirement — not a test failure

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- impactedProviders count matches Impact Graph query result
- All contract test suites for impacted providers executed

---

## TASK TYPE: T542 — ContextPack Assembly

**ARCHETYPE:** RETRIEVAL  
**ENTRY:** Called by any AF node that needs RAG context before generation  
**PURPOSE:** Standalone task type for Hybrid RAG ContextPack assembly. Can be called with any combination of familyId / factoryId / taskTypeId / customConstraints. Always returns a structured ContextPack within tokenBudget. Powers "deterministic, not random" generation.

**DISTINCT FROM:**
- T59 (Graph RAG Query) — T59 is a raw graph query; T542 assembles a full ContextPack (graph + vector + merge)
- T539/T540/T541 — those call T542 as a sub-step; T542 is reusable by any new task type

**FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:** F1426, F1422, F1423, F1428 — resolved via CreateAsync()  
**FABRIC RESOLUTION:**
- F1426 → RAG FABRIC (Hybrid) + DATABASE FABRIC via F65 (Graph)
- F1422 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + RAG FABRIC (Vector — skill retrieval)
- F1423 → DATABASE FABRIC via F65 (GraphAI — catalog queries)
- F1428 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES — prompt pack retrieval)

**Flow DAG (no-code):**
```
Input: { scope, familyId?, factoryIds[]?, taskTypeId?, ragStrategies[], tokenBudget }
  ↓
1. graph_query              F1423:ExecuteGraphQuery (Q1 or Q2 or Q3 per input params)
2. vector_search            F1422:BuildContextPack (vector search filtered by graph node IDs)
3. merge_deduplicate        → combine, rank by relevance, trim to tokenBudget
4. attach_prompt_templates  F1428:GetBasePrompt (node-type-specific template)
5. return ContextPack (schema: skills[], taskContracts[], bfaRules[], 
                        codeSnippets[], priorJudgments[], providerMatrix[])
```

**AF CONFIGURATION:**
- AF-4 (RAG): this IS the AF-4 implementation
- AF-9 (Judge): validates ContextPack completeness (min 1 skill, min 1 taskContract)

**MACHINE/FREEDOM:**
- MACHINE: ContextPack schema (required fields non-negotiable)
- MACHINE: Graph query runs BEFORE vector search (not interchangeable)
- FREEDOM: `ragStrategies`, `tokenBudget`, `maxSkillCount`, `includePriorJudgments`

**IRON RULES:**
- MUST cite which skills are in the ContextPack — generation nodes MUST reference them
- MUST include at least one BFA rule if taskTypeId references factories with BFA hooks
- Vector search MUST be filtered by graph node IDs — no unfiltered semantic-only retrieval

**QUALITY GATES (AF-9):**
- skills[] non-empty
- taskContracts[] non-empty
- tokenUsed ≤ tokenBudget
- graphLinkedIds populated (traceability back to graph)

```yaml
  next_doc: SKILLS_FACTORY_RAG (SK-346-SK-354)
```

## T543 — CANONICAL SKILL EXTRACTION GATE
```
TASK TYPE: T543
NAME: Canonical Skill Extraction Gate
ARCHETYPE: EXTRACTION
ENTRY: Source skill document identified (SK-id provided by admin or trigger)
PURPOSE: Extract a Canonical Skill Spec from an existing Python / React Native skill definition; store as language-neutral behavioral contract document.
DISTINCT FROM: T544 (T543 extracts from existing skill; T544 attaches variant descriptors to an already-extracted canonical spec)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1429:ICanonicalSkillSpecService — store canonical spec
  F1433:ISkillMetadataIndexService — index for AF-4 retrieval
  F1434:ISkillGoldenTestStoreService — store golden test vectors
  F1432:ISkillContractValidatorService — validate contract completeness

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1429 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) via IDatabaseService.StoreDocument
  F1433 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) via IDatabaseService.StoreDocument
  F1434 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) via IDatabaseService.StoreDocument
  F1432 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher)) via IAiProvider.GenerateAsync

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-2 (Planning): decompose source skill into: contracts, events, UI model, test vectors, MACHINE/FREEDOM split
  AF-4 (RAG): retrieve SK-355 (Canonical Skill Spec Format) as extraction template
  AF-5 (Multi-model): run Claude + GPT-4 in parallel to extract contract; consensus on completeness
  AF-7 (Compliance): confirm canonical spec contains no typed models (DNA-1), tenant scope rules, DataProcessResult envelope
  AF-9 (Judge): validate spec has: inputs/outputs as Dictionary, CloudEvents definition, golden test vectors (min 3), MACHINE/FREEDOM declarations
  AF-11 (Feedback): store extraction quality score for improvement

BFA VALIDATION:
  Check CF-751: source skill → canonical spec lineage recorded
  Check CF-752: canonical spec does not duplicate business logic from existing families

MACHINE / FREEDOM:
  MACHINE (fixed):
    - Output is always dict[str, Any] (or language-equivalent map)
    - CloudEvents envelope required for all async events
    - Min 3 golden test vectors required before spec is valid
    - tenantId always present in scope rules
  FREEDOM (configurable):
    - Source skill language (Python, RN — where extraction starts)
    - Target stack preferences (recorded but not enforced at extraction time)
    - Extra domain keywords for AF-4 retrieval tagging

IRON RULES:
  IRON-543-1: Canonical spec MUST NOT contain language-specific type names (class, struct, interface — use "map/dict")
  IRON-543-2: Every input/output MUST be documented as Dictionary key-value pair with type annotation
  IRON-543-3: At least 3 golden test vectors (positive + negative + edge case) required
  IRON-543-4: CloudEvents envelope MUST be defined for all async triggers
  VIOLATION = BUILD FAILURE; extraction job aborted

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - Contract completeness score ≥ 0.85 (Claude + GPT-4 consensus)
  - All DNA-1 through DNA-6 patterns documented in spec
  - Tenant scope rules present and non-empty
  - Golden test vectors validated against JSON Schema
  - Source lineage recorded in F1435

OUTPUT: Canonical Skill Spec document (ES) with canonicalId, version, sourceSkillId, goldenTests[], contractSchema, eventsSchema, machineFreedomMap
```

```
  FREEDOM: per-variant constraints, packaging preferences, hosting notes

IRON RULES:

  IRON-544-3: WordPress theme variant descriptor MUST include: themeJsonScope, templatePartsExpected, businessLogicPolicy: "none"
```

```
ENTRY: Canonical spec + variant descriptors complete
PURPOSE: Create the canonical conformance test suite (golden vectors → JSON Schema validation + CloudEvents envelope checks) that ALL variant implementations must pass.

  F1466:IConformanceTestOrchestratorService — register test suite
  F1469:IApiConformanceCheckerService — generate OpenAPI boundary checks
  F1470:IEventEnvelopeValidatorService — generate CloudEvents envelope checks

  F1434 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES)
  F1466 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams)
  F1469 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher))
  F1470 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-2 (Planning): decompose canonical test scenarios into: API conformance, event conformance, payload JSON Schema validation
  AF-8 (Security): check test suite includes negative cases: missing tenant, missing idempotencyKey, oversized payload
  AF-9 (Judge): validate suite coverage ≥ 3 scenarios per variant; positive + negative + edge

IRON RULES:
  IRON-545-1: Every conformance suite MUST include at least 1 test that verifies tenant scope isolation
  IRON-545-2: idempotency stability test required (same key → idempotent result)
  IRON-545-3: suite stored as canonical artifact under same family ID (not per-variant)

OUTPUT: Conformance test suite document (ES) with suiteId, familyId, scenarios[], openApiCheck, cloudEventsCheck
```

```
  IRON-556-2: Settings registration MUST be on admin_init hook (not plugins_loaded, not init)
  IRON-556-3: Block MUST register on BOTH server (register_block_type in PHP) and client (registerBlockType in JS)
  IRON-556-4: REST endpoint MUST have permission_callback — absence = BUILD FAILURE
  IRON-556-5: NO secrets in WP options or PHP config files
  IRON-556-6: ALL business logic routed to XIIGen API — plugin is presentation + config layer ONLY
```

```
  F1456 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher))
  F1457 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher))
  F1458 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES)
  F1459 → RAG FABRIC (SK-388 (IRagService))

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): generate theme using SK-366 (WordPress Theme Adapter Pattern) scaffold
  AF-4 (RAG): retrieve SK-366, SK-371 (design token export patterns)
  AF-7 (Compliance): theme.json is ONLY mechanism for global styles; no inline PHP styles
  AF-8 (Security): no API credentials in theme files
  AF-9 (Judge): template structure correct (templates/, parts/ folders); theme.json valid schema

IRON RULES:
  IRON-557-1: theme.json is the ONLY global style mechanism — no wp_enqueue_style with custom CSS for global tokens
  IRON-557-2: Templates MUST be in templates/ folder; template parts in parts/ folder
  IRON-557-3: ZERO business logic in theme — routing to plugin or XIIGen API only
  IRON-557-4: If theme requires REST proxy: companion plugin declaration MUST be included in documentation

OUTPUT: WordPress theme ZIP (ES + file store); variant SK-{id}#client#wordpress_theme registered as GENERATED
```

## T558 — WORDPRESS SECURITY & AUTH GATE
```
TASK TYPE: T558
NAME: WordPress Security & Auth Gate
ARCHETYPE: VALIDATION
ENTRY: T556 or T557 packaging complete
PURPOSE: Security validation for all WordPress artifacts: no secrets exposure, correct auth strategy, nonce usage, REST permissions.

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES: F1447, F1440, F1454
FABRIC RESOLUTION: F1447 → CORE FABRIC; F1440 → DATABASE FABRIC; F1454 → AI ENGINE FABRIC

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-8 (Security): deep security scan of all WordPress PHP + JS files
  AF-9 (Judge): checklist validation

IRON RULES:
  IRON-558-1: ZERO secrets in client bundle or WP options
  IRON-558-2: API auth goes through XIIGen gateway — never embed API keys in plugin
  IRON-558-3: nonce validation required for all form submissions from WP admin

OUTPUT: Security gate report; PASS/FAIL per artifact
```

## T559 — CROSS-VARIANT CONFORMANCE RUNNER
```
TASK TYPE: T559
NAME: Cross-Variant Conformance Runner
ARCHETYPE: VALIDATION
ENTRY: Any variant promoted from GENERATED → INJECTED (or on schedule)
PURPOSE: Run canonical test suite against ALL variants of a skill family simultaneously; produce coverage matrix; flag regressions.

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1466:IConformanceTestOrchestratorService
  F1467:IGoldenTestReplayService
  F1468:IVariantTestReporterService

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1466 → QUEUE FABRIC (Redis Streams)
  F1467 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES)
  F1468 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES)

IRON RULES:
  IRON-559-1: variant CANNOT advance promotion ladder if conformance suite < 100% pass
  IRON-559-2: conformance runner MUST run on ALL variants simultaneously (parallel queue dispatch)

OUTPUT: Conformance matrix (ES): familyId → {variantId → passRate, failedTests[]}
```

```
TASK TYPE: T560
NAME: Variant Promotion Ladder Gate
ARCHETYPE: ORCHESTRATION
ENTRY: Variant passes T559 conformance; promotion requested
PURPOSE: Evaluate variant against quality gates and advance through GENERATED → INJECTED → MINIMAL → CORE.

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1476:IVariantPromotionGateService
  F1477:IVariantStatusTransitionService
  F1479:IPromotionAuditService

FABRIC RESOLUTION:

  F1477 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES) + QUEUE FABRIC
  F1479 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES)

IRON RULES:
  IRON-560-1: conformance = 100% required for any promotion
  IRON-560-2: CORE promotion requires human approval (not automated)
  IRON-560-3: promotion audit trail is immutable (append-only, never delete)

OUTPUT: Promotion event (QUEUE); updated variant status in F1436
```

```
TASK TYPE: T564

PURPOSE: Master DAG orchestrator for the full multi-target translation flow. Dispatches T543→T545, then parallel T546–T554 per selected targets, then T556–T558 if WordPress, then T559–T560, then T561–T563 (Phase B).

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1480:IMultiTargetTranslationOrchestratorService
  F1481:ITranslationTraceService
  F1482:ITranslationFeedbackInjectorService

  F1481 → DATABASE FABRIC (ES)
  F1482 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (SK-386 (AiDispatcher) / AF-11)

TEMPLATE: Template 126 — Multi-Target Translation DAG (see MASTER_EXECUTION_PLAN)

IRON RULES:
  IRON-564-1: T543 (extraction) MUST complete before ANY T546–T554 (generation) starts
  IRON-564-2: T545 (conformance seed) MUST complete before T559 (runner) starts
  IRON-564-3: T561–T563 (Graph RAG) MUST only run AFTER all requested variants reach INJECTED status
  IRON-564-4: trace ID persisted; orchestrator MUST be resumable from any checkpoint (F1481)

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - All requested variants reach at least GENERATED status
  - Cross-variant conformance matrix produced
  - Trace ID recorded for full audit trail

OUTPUT: Translation job summary document (ES); all variants registered; optional Graph RAG nodes created
```

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FLOW-35 TASK TYPES: T565–T566
# Added: 2026-03-05
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## T565 — SECRET RESOLUTION GATE
```
TASK TYPE: T565
NAME: Secret Resolution Gate
ARCHETYPE: INFRASTRUCTURE
ENTRY: Bootstrap phase — before fabric resolver initialization
PURPOSE: Resolve all $secret: and $env: references in config through ISecretsService, producing plain-text config dicts for all downstream fabric resolvers.
DISTINCT FROM: T43 (config validation — checks shape, not resolves secrets)

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1484:ISecretsService — resolves individual secret paths
  F1487:ConfigBuilder — walks config dicts, finds $secret:/$env: refs, calls ISecretsService

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1484 → SECRETS FABRIC (AWS Secrets Manager provider in production, EnvVar in dev, InMemory in tests)
  F1487 → CORE FABRIC (MicroserviceBase-compatible utility)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): Generates ConfigBuilder walking logic + secret reference parser
  AF-4 (RAG): Retrieves SK-402 (Secrets Fabric patterns) + SK-403 (ConfigBuilder patterns)
  AF-7 (Compliance): Validates no plaintext secrets in logs, DNA-1 (dict-based), DNA-3 (DPR returns)
  AF-9 (Judge): Validates all $secret: refs resolved, zero plaintext in output config, scope verified

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-789: Secret path collision across tenants — two tenants requesting same AWS SM path must get tenant-scoped values
  CF-790: Config resolution failure cascade — partial secret failure must not crash bootstrap (DEGRADED, not DOWN)

MACHINE (fixed — non-configurable):
  - Resolution algorithm: walk dict recursively, match $secret:/$env: patterns, resolve through ISecretsService
  - Cache invalidation: TTL-based, no manual invalidation
  - Retry policy: 3 attempts with exponential backoff for AWS SM calls
  - No plaintext secrets in any log output (IRON RULE)

FREEDOM (admin-configurable via FREEDOM layer):
  - TTL duration (default 300s, configurable per tenant)
  - Provider selection (aws_secrets_manager | env_var | in_memory)
  - Secret path prefixes (default: xiigen/{fabric}/{provider}_key)
  - Fallback provider chain order

IRON RULES:
  IRON-565-1: No plaintext secrets in logs — violation = BUILD FAILURE
  IRON-565-2: scope_id/tenant_id required on every secret retrieval call
  IRON-565-3: No caching without TTL — infinite cache = violation
  IRON-565-4: $secret: references must be fully resolved before any fabric resolver receives config
  IRON-565-5: Failed resolution returns DataProcessResult.failure — never throws exception

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - All $secret: references in config resolved (zero unresolved refs in output)
  - Zero plaintext secrets in any log output (grep verification)
  - scope_id present on every ISecretsService call
  - DataProcessResult used for all returns (never raise)
  - Backward compatible — existing config without $secret: refs passes unchanged

OUTPUT: Fully-resolved config dict (all $secret:/$env: refs replaced with plain values) ready for fabric resolvers
```

---

## T566 — PROVIDER HEALTH CASCADE
```
TASK TYPE: T566
NAME: Provider Health Cascade
ARCHETYPE: INFRASTRUCTURE
ENTRY: Bootstrap complete — all fabrics initialized (or partially initialized with DEGRADED status)
PURPOSE: Aggregate health status across all 7 fabrics, report per-fabric and overall system health, support graceful degradation.

FACTORY DEPENDENCIES:
  F1489:BootstrapSequence — 7-phase startup orchestrator
  F1490:HealthAggregator — cross-fabric health reporter
  F1484:ISecretsService — health check for secrets fabric
  F1488:GrokProvider — health check for new AI provider

FABRIC RESOLUTION:
  F1489 → CORE FABRIC (orchestrates all 7 fabrics in order)
  F1490 → CORE FABRIC (reads health from all fabric resolvers)
  F1484 → SECRETS FABRIC (health = can resolve a test secret)
  F1488 → AI ENGINE FABRIC (health = small generate call succeeds)

AF CONFIGURATION:
  AF-1 (Genesis): Generates bootstrap sequence + health aggregation logic
  AF-7 (Compliance): Validates graceful degradation — partial failure = DEGRADED, not crash
  AF-9 (Judge): Validates 7-phase ordering, no circular dependencies, health report completeness

BFA VALIDATION:
  CF-789: Secret path collision (inherited from T565)
  CF-790: Config resolution cascade (inherited from T565)
  ST-499: Bootstrap with partial secret failure — verify DEGRADED status, not crash

MACHINE (fixed):
  - Bootstrap phase order: Secrets → Config → DB → Queue → AI → RAG → Flow (always this order)
  - Health status enum: HEALTHY | DEGRADED | DOWN | UNKNOWN
  - Overall health = min(all fabric health statuses)
  - Health check interval: on-demand (no background polling)

FREEDOM (admin-configurable):
  - secrets_required mode (true = abort if secrets fail; false = DEGRADED but continue)
  - Per-fabric health check timeout (default 5s)
  - Health report format (json | dict)

IRON RULES:
  IRON-566-1: Bootstrap must be ordered — secrets before config, config before fabric resolvers
  IRON-566-2: Partial fabric failure = DEGRADED status, NOT system crash
  IRON-566-3: Health report must include all 7 fabrics even if some are DOWN
  IRON-566-4: scope_id required on health check calls (DNA-5)
  IRON-566-5: Health report data is dict[str, Any] (DNA-1 — no typed health models)

QUALITY GATES (AF-9):
  - All 7 fabrics represented in health report
  - Partial failure produces DEGRADED (not crash)
  - Bootstrap resumes from checkpoint on retry
  - Config template with $secret: refs fully resolved before fabric init
  - Zero provider SDK imports in bootstrap module (fabric purity verified)

OUTPUT: System health report (dict[str, Any]) with per-fabric status + overall status; Bootstrap complete event (CloudEvents envelope)
```
